However, it is often crowded, so I recommend making a reservation in advance.

Name: GOOD MORNING CAFE NOWADAYS
Address: Shinjuku City, Daikyocho, 31−4 Brillia ist 千駄ヶ谷 1F
Author: Max Kennard
Review: Nice little cafe with terrace seating. Happy hour is from 3 - 6 pm and all the drinks are 500 yen. They also offer a homemade lemon sour.

Name: Cafe Veloce
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 7 Chome−10−7 加賀谷ビル 1F
Author: Elena Kuznetsova
Review:
The coffee was good, and the staff answered my questions about food, even though I speak little Japanese. However, when a delivery guy called me, and I talked to him for short time (I just asked him to leave my package outside, it was like seconds), I got scolded by the staff!
There are no visible signs saying that the phone cannot be used in the shop!
And a group of people nearby was making much more noise that I did.
I understand that talking on a phone can bother other clients. But at least put up stickers "no calls please" instead of waving hands and getting angry with your clients. I felt really upset about the situation, and I won't come again to this place.

Name: Cafe Veloce
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 7 Chome−10−7 加賀谷ビル 1F
Author: Kevin Aviles
Review: Very kind staff, perfect to relax and prices are great. I got the melon soda float and it was delicious, as well as ice tea both very good!

Name: Cafe Veloce
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 7 Chome−10−7 加賀谷ビル 1F
Author: Ruth RH
Review:
The atmosphere was fine, nothing special but nothing bad. However, the tuna sandwich was awful. Worse though was their “mocha”. I truly believe the barista made a mistake and gave me cocoa (watery cocoa at that). There was absolutely no espresso in it.

First, she argued, explaining what mocha was. 🤣🤣🤣. Then, she said, “this is Japanese mocha.”  Well, not according to Tully’s, Doutor, Pronto, … and the numerous smaller shops in Kyoto, Osaka, Nara, Kanagawa, …. You get the idea.

Lastly, “this is Veloce mocha”. Well, ok… Veloce mocha does not contain the espresso one pays for. OR, more likely, she simply made a mistake and would not simply admit to it.

Name: Cafe Veloce
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 7 Chome−10−7 加賀谷ビル 1F
Author: Michael Wood
Review: I highly recommend the waffles, we had both the berry and chocolate. Service was amazing and beyond friendly to travelers.

Name: Cafe Veloce
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 7 Chome−10−7 加賀谷ビル 1F
Author: pmai91
Review: Super friendly staff. Food and drinks were delicious. Place is cozy and I'd like to come again 😁

Name: Kamata Cafe
Address: 5 Chome-14-5 Nishikamata, Ota City
Author: Martin Moelle
Review:
We came here as part of our Spitz pilgrimage. We were not at all disappointed.
The coffee, cake and sandwiches we had were excellent. The choice of coffee is extensive and good. They roast their own beans and know how to make a good cup. Recommended.

Name: Kamata Cafe
Address: 5 Chome-14-5 Nishikamata, Ota City
Author: Willy Oei
Review:
It’s my last day in Tokyo, and I just want to have a good coffee, wifi in the nice cafe. Randomly search it from google map, and here I am.

Love the food 🥪🥓🍳🍵☕️ I enjoyed the iced matcha, croque Madame, and also ice latte. Love the good crowds and overheard everyone is leaving with happy words, “Ooh your coffee is so good!” , “I love my foods that goes well with the coffee”, etc👍🏼

Good job, cafe Kamata! Keep it up.

Name: Kamata Cafe
Address: 5 Chome-14-5 Nishikamata, Ota City
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: A nice cafe that turns into a bar in the evening (but you can still get coffee and sweets). It’s open late which is a plus. I went after dinner and wanted a coffee and something sweet to eat. I ordered the pour over coffee which was done well. I also ordered the caramel basque cheesecake and it was delicious. There’s bar and table seating, and the vibe is laidback. Service was fast and friendly. A little walk from the station but worth a visit if you have some time.

Name: Kamata Cafe
Address: 5 Chome-14-5 Nishikamata, Ota City
Author: Michael A
Review:
Rostery and café by enthusiast owner and skilled barrista. Extreme quality consciousness with eye for details while remaining unpretentious. Chick and modern interior with a spacious and unassuming design. A genuine plane with a genuine feel. Excellent cappuccino from espresso (who's is not the norm in Japan) and homemade brulee pudding with coffee jelly. 600 yen for the cappuccino, which I happily paid for given the quality (current mean is about 500 yen). Highest recommendation.

Update one year later: Visited again. Prices have increased as has the staff size, but the quality is still superior. The owner has upped the latte art yet another notch. Impressed.

Name: Kamata Cafe
Address: 5 Chome-14-5 Nishikamata, Ota City
Author: Gabriel Nito
Review:
Amazing hand-drip coffee with a variety of single-origin in-house roasted beans!! My partner and I travelled from New Zealand (a country well-known for great coffee) to Japan and stumbled upon this cafe near Kamata station, Tokyo (~4min walk). Very impressed and surprised by their superb coffee.

Coffee: We tried the iced versions of the Kakumei blend and Mexico beans. Both delicious and matched the flavour notes described on the tasting cards (see photos) very well! Note: tasting cards are in Japanese so we used Papago to translate the text. They also have some very yummy desserts 😍

Service: Staff are super-accommodating and kind; and understand a bit of English! They are happy to provide explanations about the coffee (albeit in Japanese).

Atmosphere: Quiet and calm vibe with super  cool and clean rustic interior design. The restroom is well-maintained and clean as expected in Japan. It’s bidet-equipped!

This is now a must-go for us whenever we’re in Ota-city Tokyo. ☕️🤎

Name: Cafe Bohemia
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 36−22 PartⅡ, 1F Noa Shibuya
Author: Xiro Ar (Alphonz)
Review:
I don’t have an enough impression to actually write a review since we were here for partaking a third party foodie event. This place was a chartered venue for that.

Besides the supper course buffet went fine with exotic Turkish plates and the host’s informative talks, the ambience inside and the interior also went great with the event theme, “Turkish Mayhane”. The foods coming up to the buffet tables were fine, too, though they were supposed to have been prepared by the host (a world music’s critic and foodie), not by the restaurant chefs.

Not much to say about the staff and the service in general since there were barely conversation or interaction with them other than they were replenishing buffet dishes.
Well, I think they were supposed to be able to willingly serve us with smile and converse words, now I suppose that kind of “semi-silent treatment” was the restaurant’s standard protocol for their atmosphere setting as “Bohemian hideouts”. Hope I’m right about it.

From its best location with dark but glaring image, this place should be worth visiting for once or twice, but weekend evenings could be crowded or chartered full-house, so scouting may be necessary.

Name: Cafe Bohemia
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 36−22 PartⅡ, 1F Noa Shibuya
Author: Lukas R
Review: Terrace seats are nice and a rarity in the area and Tokyo in general. Nibbles okay, Shisha alright. The staff's attitude is mostly terrible tho, maybe it's the working conditions but most of them clearly don't wanna go beyond the bare minimum or interact with customers as people.

Name: Cafe Bohemia
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 36−22 PartⅡ, 1F Noa Shibuya
Author: Akshaya Kumar Sharma
Review: Fantastic place; we loved the ambience. The inside seating is comfortable for families with babies; the music was great and not too loud. We recommend trying the crème brûlée and hummus with olives and garlic bread. The flavor left our taste buds wanting for more!

Name: Cafe Bohemia
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 36−22 PartⅡ, 1F Noa Shibuya
Author: Carrie May
Review:
Positives: I do like that you can bring Pets if you sit outside. And they brought a water bowl out too. It’s hard to find pet friendly places in Japan so this is a big plus. The Chicken Tandoori was good and flavorful . There is non traditional belly dancing. Nice ambiance.
Negatives:  Like many of the other reviews my experience was similar, the customer service was lacking. I really wanted to try the hookah so I ordered it, but the server forgot to bring the hookah and my beverage. I reminded him of the beverage when he came out with the food.  I thought maybe we needed to wait a little bit longer for hookah. By the time we were done eating, they still hadn’t brought the hookah. When I went to pay I had to remind them they never brought the shisha but it’s still on the bill. The cashier was apologetic however our server didn’t say anything. The server  also tried to sit us away from other people and I thought, it was because I have my dog, but it was very cold, and we were in a corner away from the heaters. Another server (female) noticed that we were trying to look for our server and she allowed us to move. I think there is good customer service from over servers, but maybe the some   need more training or was having a bad night.

Name: Cafe Bohemia
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 36−22 PartⅡ, 1F Noa Shibuya
Author: Phil Robertson
Review: Absolutely terrible service. We were seated at the back of the restaurant in pitch darkness, making it very difficult to read the menus, and waiting staff were of little help. When my drink eventually arrived, the waitress gave it to the wrong person, and made no attempt to correct the mistake, leaving me wondering where my drink had gone. When my food arrived, there was no proper cutlery, and after various additional requests (water, pepper) the waitress developed an attitude. Finally, we were informed that unless we paid our bill by 10:00, the price would go up by 10%—despite the fact that we had already finished eating and drinking. (Never come across this system before.) All-in-all, a fairly miserable pre-Christmas night out. Won't be coming back.

Name: 10° Cafe
Address: 3 Chome-12-8 Takada, Toshima City
Author: Christy Mae Betos
Review:
One of the best cafes fit for working! If you’re near Takadanobaba Station and you need a cafe with multiple sockets to plug in your devices, free fast wifi, and multiple floors for you to choose from… 10°Cafe is the place you’re looking for. Quiet, aesthetic, great food and drinks; anyone can work here productively.
I love their hot coffees!! ☕️
P.S. Ordering system is via QR code

Name: 10° Cafe
Address: 3 Chome-12-8 Takada, Toshima City
Author: Maya W
Review:
This place could be great, unfortunately it’s just not.

It is marketed as a work cafe and admittedly has good wifi and plenty of outlets; but there are often groups loudly talking, which makes working difficult even with noise-cancelling earbuds. It gets rammed at the weekend with a weird mix of people trying to study and groups having brunch or lunch, making the space very loud.

The taco rice is good, the French toast is soggy, and the coffee is mediocre stuff from a bean-to-cup machine rather than proper hand drip or espresso. The disappointing coffee really is a dealbreaker for a cafe. The lemonade also isn’t particularly good.

Apparently you have to order something every two hours, but no one tells you that when you enter. When it’s busy food takes a long time to come and staff are slow to clear tables.

Better places to work nearby: Hoshino if you don’t need power or wifi; Case coworking if you do.

Name: 10° Cafe
Address: 3 Chome-12-8 Takada, Toshima City
Author: Giacomo Corino
Review:
Really cozy place, with free wifi and outlets to charge your pc.
I would recommend it if you want a relaxing place to work or study while sipping a cup of coffee. The prices after 11am become quite expensive though so make sure to go there in the early morning.

Name: 10° Cafe
Address: 3 Chome-12-8 Takada, Toshima City
Author: Kristiana Cook
Review: The food was delicious and the prices were fair. I ordered the taco rice bowl and a chai latte which were both great. It's a great place to study too.

Name: 10° Cafe
Address: 3 Chome-12-8 Takada, Toshima City
Author: Ben Guild
Review:
Frustrating Wi-Fi. It requires you to click a button to access the internet multiple times randomly per hour, and causes interruptions 👎🏻

Also they raised the prices here 🫤

Name: cafe WALL
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−9−5, Gold Build, 3FB
Author: ROSRO
Review:
Reason for rating is I was told to wait, there were three tables open (multiple seat) and 1 single table.

They had me wait in the shared common area by the elevator which was incredibly hot.

After about 15 minutes they came out to tell me there was no space and I’d have to wait about an hour.

Disappointed as I really wanted to try this place.   If I am ever able to get in I will revise the rating to appropriately reflect food.

Name: cafe WALL
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−9−5, Gold Build, 3FB
Author: Cherrier ToTravel
Review:
I love the honey coffee with kawaii drawing on top, taste good as well.

You may make an request for any celebrity in advance and the owner is responsive and helpful!

Cake is nice but I cannot have the seasonal parfait I want🥹sold out so sad

Name: cafe WALL
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−9−5, Gold Build, 3FB
Author: y_ann _dr
Review: Went here for dinner. Food was great and i enjoyed it so much. ❤️

Name: cafe WALL
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−9−5, Gold Build, 3FB
Author: Patrick Gailey
Review: Highly recommend. This spot is on the third floor. Really cute vibes! Homemade scones and cakes are delicious. Super friendly staff!

Name: cafe WALL
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−9−5, Gold Build, 3FB
Author: Siri L
Review: Free wifi and I could charge my phone

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Ali Chua
Review:
Grabbed a coffee here, it's consistent across branches in other countries. Would prefer if they offered skimmed milk.

It's decently priced and there was too long a queue. Saw the staff making the drip coffee and he is very conscientious about it.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Skye Cai
Review:
Tried their cakes which is quite disappointing.
The cheese cake doesn’t taste made with good milk and cheese. That it doesn’t melt in the mouth or has heavy cheese flavours.
The brownie is not tasty too. Super hard and not soft.
The better one is the coconut pound cake that has strong coconut taste.
In overall not much services but good atmosphere. Merchandises are expensive.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Rakhmadi Kusumo
Review:
Visiting Blue Bottle Coffee at the bustling B2 Ginza Six Mall can be a challenging experience due to its popularity. The cafe was extremely packed during my visit, resulting in long waits for both seating and ordering. The crowd, characteristic of such a renowned spot, unfortunately detracts from the usually serene coffee experience one might expect from Blue Bottle.

I ordered a cappuccino and an iced latte. While both drinks were up to the high standards Blue Bottle is known for, with the right balance of flavors and expertly brewed coffee, the overall experience was overshadowed by the excessive wait times and the chaotic environment. Additionally, a significant error occurred during my transaction, as I was charged twice for my order, further complicating my visit.

Given the stress and inconvenience of navigating the crowded space and dealing with billing errors, I would recommend seeking out a quieter, less crowded venue for those looking to enjoy their coffee in peace. While Blue Bottle Coffee offers quality beverages, the Ginza Six location might not be the best choice for those who prefer a more relaxed setting to truly savor their coffee.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Hi Goat Oh Deer迷鹿与高羊
Review:
Blue bottle is like the better version of Starbucks franchise.

Nola Float (736Y with tax) smooth coffee, Coffee not sweet, but the ice cream is sweet. Not bad.

The place is pretty crowded and noisy though.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Eileen Neo (Sgsohotleh)
Review: Not as iconic compared to the rest of the Blue Bottle Coffee Cafe! But the interior of the design is still very beautiful and minimalistic. Coffee wise, on the slightly pricey side. I guess due to branding. They also carry their own merchandise too, if you are a fan of blue bottle. In the outlet, we tried the basque cheesecake! It’s so so good! Ended up, I thought the cheesecake was a better choice compared to the coffee. Sadly, they don’t allow takeaway for the cheesecake. Definitely a must visit, given its popularity. 💯

Name: Vegewest Cafe & Bar
Address: Shibuya City, Daikanyamacho, 13−4 セレサ代官山 1F
Author: Paul Dentico
Review: Fantastic little place. My wife and I went for lunch. Both had salads and both were excellent. We shared a smoothie which was also really tasty. We could tell someone puts a lot of effort into serving great food with great service in a lovely atmosphere. Stop here and you will be pleased.

Name: Vegewest Cafe & Bar
Address: Shibuya City, Daikanyamacho, 13−4 セレサ代官山 1F
Author: Desmond Chan
Review: Please to chill!  Quiet and cozy!  Enjoy their very healthy food!  Love the deco!

Name: Vegewest Cafe & Bar
Address: Shibuya City, Daikanyamacho, 13−4 セレサ代官山 1F
Author: Cathy M
Review: Great quality wine (1000-1200 per glass) and food. Came here expecting to drink just one glass, but ending up ordering more because the selection was so good!

Name: Vegewest Cafe & Bar
Address: Shibuya City, Daikanyamacho, 13−4 セレサ代官山 1F
Author: George Karystianis
Review: Lovely small cafe, in a quiet corner of Daikayama. Interesting and more organic based menu. The pudding was excellent! To the ladies that might be wondering why I did not finish my coffee though: there were some really annoying people speaking so loud, I could not enjoy it! Sumimasen, nothing to do with the coffee quality!

Name: Vegewest Cafe & Bar
Address: Shibuya City, Daikanyamacho, 13−4 セレサ代官山 1F
Author: Keenan Ganaesan
Review: Stumbled upon this lovely place while we were walking and ordered a smoothie which tasted super good.Hidden gem in this area.

Name: EVERYONEs CAFE
Address: 8-4 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: No Cloud
Review: An amazing experience: we went twice and the staff was great each time. The products are fresh, the deserts are amazing and make you feel full. I would 100% recommend. Note that the price is quite high compared to normal restaurants but we thought it was worth it.

Name: EVERYONEs CAFE
Address: 8-4 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Derek Medeiros
Review: This is my favorite cafe EVER. I wish I could go here everyday, unfortunately I don’t live in Japan. Pancakes were incredible, iced coffee was the best I’ve ever had. Service was perfect. Exactly why I love Japan. Thank you! 😊

Name: EVERYONEs CAFE
Address: 8-4 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Ryan Alsaihaty
Review: Great! If you are visiting the zoo or the nearby museums, this is a good option for food. There was some wait during my visit, but it was not long (about 15-20 minutes)

Name: EVERYONEs CAFE
Address: 8-4 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Eve’s Daughter
Review: We had a wonderful brunch at this cafe just before hitting the museums and park. There is a machine outside the restaurant entrance where one will need to get a number and join a queue. There’s a waiting area though the wait was not too long we were seated after just a few minutes. The food was amazing and serving sizes were more than adequate. I would highly recommend this place when at Ueno!

Name: EVERYONEs CAFE
Address: 8-4 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: 李Way
Review: The curry rice is very delicious. The curry sauce is very layered. It contains crispy diced onions and high-quality vegetables. The dessert soufflé is also delicious. The fresh cream is not greasy and delicious. The store is clean and bright and the price is reasonable. It is a good choice for visiting the park.

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Shinjuku Marui Honkan
Address: B1, Note that only west entrance is open in the morning, 3 Chome-30-13 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Luca Perrino
Review:
Difficult to review this kind of shops
Fpr sure overpriced
Since located in the basement of a crowdy mall, atmosphere is not the best.
Food, depends. Lasagna was very good. Cheese cake, quite good. Choko mint drink. Delicious. But sandwich... no. Bread was crunchy and dry. Supposed to have vegan cheeae inside but no trace of it. Spinach had no taste. Definitely avoid spinach sandwich go for lasagna.
They sell american cbd cookies for 800 jpy. Understand cbd but 800 jpy for a cookie... uh?

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Shinjuku Marui Honkan
Address: B1, Note that only west entrance is open in the morning, 3 Chome-30-13 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: David Yoshihara
Review: I was in search of some vegan breakfast options, and this place came up as open around 8 am.  It was a bit tricky to find as the main building that it is located in does not open until 10 am or so.  It is in the basement (B1) level and was pretty quiet in the morning hours.  That meant my order came quickly and was piping hot.  I ordered the lasagna and quiche and walked back to my AirBnB in about 15 minutes and the food was still just as hot.  As other reviewers have commented, the lasagna hits and the quiche is just as good.  If you're vegan (and even if you're not), please give this place a try.  The service person spoke English well and although a little pricier, it is nice to know that the ingredients are all vegan.  I'm told the lasagna is even gluten-free.

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Shinjuku Marui Honkan
Address: B1, Note that only west entrance is open in the morning, 3 Chome-30-13 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Wendy L
Review: I was so happy to have found this place when I was searching for a vegan place to eat in Shinjuku because they have GF lasagna!! Lasagna is one of my favorite foods but it is so difficult to find one that is both vegan AND gluten free. It was absolutely delicious!! I had already eaten too many sweets that day, otherwise I would have also tried their GF cheesecake. Location was convenient and service was friendly as well. I can't wait to come back the next time I'm in Tokyo!

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Shinjuku Marui Honkan
Address: B1, Note that only west entrance is open in the morning, 3 Chome-30-13 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Adrien Wain
Review:
Amazing food and amazing service both times we visited during our trip to Tokyo.

Highly recommend the Lasagna and the Cheesecake!

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Shinjuku Marui Honkan
Address: B1, Note that only west entrance is open in the morning, 3 Chome-30-13 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Kaustubh Maloo
Review: Lasagna was delicious and served hot. Banana chocolate muffin felt like homemade, not too sweet and easy to eat. Lemonade was refreshing and matcha was classy. Good place overall for a quick bite.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Roppongi Cafe
Address: 7 Chome-7-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: Linas Gabrielaitis
Review:
A perfect place to grab a coffee before visiting Tokyo National Arts Center.

There are not a lot of tables inside and that's good, not very crowdy during lunch break.

The spacious exterior outside is under a roof, you can sit outside and enjoy the nature.

Overall a very pleasant atmosphere, good coffee and a quiet environment.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Roppongi Cafe
Address: 7 Chome-7-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: John de Souza
Review: Wonderful place. Love the outdoor seating especially on the rocks. The staff is. Wet friendly and kind. The coffee is always amazing. I also really like the fresh Belgian waffle. Sit, sip and enjoy.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Roppongi Cafe
Address: 7 Chome-7-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: nina e
Review: After finally having the chance to visit the famous Japanese cafe ‘Blue Bottle,’ I thoroughly enjoyed their delightful parfait dessert, tasty hot chocolate, and flavorful avocado toast. However, the cafe tends to get quite crowded, making it a bit challenging to find a seat.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Roppongi Cafe
Address: 7 Chome-7-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: Shao-Yu Yen
Review:
Slightly creamy taste in latte 😋😋😋

Americano is so so; just ordinarily good.

impressed by its Apple Store vibe

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Roppongi Cafe
Address: 7 Chome-7-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: Arnold
Review: A nice, quiet, and spacious location is perfect for an escape from the inner city. Coffee is great. Could serve it a bit warmer. But the taste is great.  Thanks BB.

Name: A&A Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Totsukamachi, 1 Chome−102−11 ニューライフ早稲田 １F
Author: Der Gärtner
Review: Delicious food, cosy atmosphere. The service was excellent. High recommended place for lunch or dinner.

Name: A&A Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Totsukamachi, 1 Chome−102−11 ニューライフ早稲田 １F
Author: 謝宛蓁
Review:
My favorite pasta near Morning University, it tastes delicious. The lunch special is around ¥1,000 and comes with soup and salad. There are limited flavors of pasta every day, and rice is also available (around ¥1,200). The three times I visited this semester, I always sat alone at the bar table, and it was easily full at noon.
💳Can swipe card & paypay👌

🥗Salad: The first time I tried it was a slightly oily sesame-flavored vinegar, and then I also had an egg salad.
🥣Attached soup: clear soup with a bit of tomato flavor

🍗ハワイアングリルチキンプレート (Hawaiian style grilled chicken rice) ¥1200
There are crispy garlic and cilantro on the lightly buttered risotto. Although it is not the same as the Hawaii that Taiwan knows, it is Nanyang style anyway XD
Perfect with the soft-boiled poached eggs. The side salad part feels a bit repetitive like the pre-meal salad. I ate a lot so I was a little full. The grilled chicken part is pan-fried chicken drumstick.

🍋レモンスカッシュ（lemon bubbles）¥250
It's a bit sweet, like lemon candy, but I don't recommend it.

🍝Daily replacement ¥1100
I forgot the name of this day-limited grouper pasta. The garlic chili noodles (ペペロンチーノ) stir-fried with zucchini and cabbage have the aroma of garlic, pepper and chili, and the stir-fry is very good. The fish pieces are not very fishy, ​​and the fat is still attached, which is very delicious.

🍝鶏ひき肉とIbaraki-produced かぼちゃのカレーflavored パスタ (Pumpkin Chicken Minced Curry Noodles) ¥1,000
It’s also limited to that day, and it smells like curry as soon as you enter. XDD The sauce is yellow Indian curry. There is a lot of minced chicken meat, and it is seasoned with spices (I thought it looked like eggs when I first tasted it). Pumpkin tastes like potato, or emperor beans taste pink. I don’t really like pumpkin. It tastes a bit forced, and the pumpkin flavor is very strong.

🍮パンナコッタ（cheese）+¥150
There are mini desserts to choose from. They are really mini, but the price is enough. The taste is tropical sauce (? Mango, pineapple and the like. The cheese itself is not particularly creamy but not bad. The small one can be eaten in ten bites.

The two people inside looked very busy, mainly an uncle chef was cooking + a beautiful sister who looked like Gui Lunmei was helping & in charge of the outside. When the seats were full, you had to wait for about 15 minutes. If it was too slow, you would be late for class in the afternoon XDD

Name: A&A Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Totsukamachi, 1 Chome−102−11 ニューライフ早稲田 １F
Author: FIVE- A
Review:
2024.08.17 (Sat) Weather: Sunny Open 11:00
We arrived at 11:35 AM. There were two groups of customers in the store. It was a nice cafe with a very nice atmosphere.

I had the daily lunch (cold tomato pasta) for 1,300 yen. The tomato-based seasoning gave it a strong taste, and it was even more delicious than I expected, with lots of tomatoes and maybe sardines. If it comes with salad and soup, it would be reasonable.

Payment can also be made using credit card or PayPal.

Name: A&A Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Totsukamachi, 1 Chome−102−11 ニューライフ早稲田 １F
Author: hayasta ikefukuro
Review:
I used it for lunch and tea time and ordered Japanese-style mentaiko spaghetti. The flavor is a little strong, which I like. The mentaiko had a chewy texture that easily coated the noodles, making it delicious. The set includes salad and soup.
I ordered cheesecake and iced coffee for dessert after dinner. It's super moist and falls apart when you try to cut it, but the flavor is rich and delicious. It was served with citrus, haskap, and citrus chips, and it looked stylish and good. The inside of the restaurant was spacious and had the feel of a stylish Italian restaurant. Payment also supports PayPal.
1st visit → Private reservation
Second visit → Out of omelet rice
I was disappointed that I couldn't choose the menu I wanted when I visited.

Name: A&A Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Totsukamachi, 1 Chome−102−11 ニューライフ早稲田 １F
Author: gourmet show
Review:
The store I was going to was closed, so I happened to find it nearby, so I went in.
It was around 2:00 pm, around the end of lunch time, but there were several customers in groups and groups of 2-3 people.
It was a restaurant that seemed to be loved by the locals.
The female staff was also very nice and made me feel comfortable.
The price range is reasonable, and it seems like it will be useful to nearby university students.
There was also Wi-Fi.
There was some power supply, but I don't know if it's okay to use it.
I want to use it again.
thank you very much.

Name: JAPAN RAIL CAFE TOKYO
Address: 1-chōme-9 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City
Author: Michelle Caggiano
Review: We got a sprite float and a rice bowl. Float was good and not too sweet, my husband said the bowl was alright but not his favorite!

Name: JAPAN RAIL CAFE TOKYO
Address: 1-chōme-9 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City
Author: andyloe
Review: Convenient location for a coffee or breakfast right in the middle of the train station. Meaning it can get crowded at times. Salmon breakfast and toast were decent and filling. A few high chair seats at the concern which gives some sort of a private space to be eating which having a view of the traffic.

Name: JAPAN RAIL CAFE TOKYO
Address: 1-chōme-9 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City
Author: Joydeep Choudhury
Review: Located at the entrance of the Yaesu central entrance with very nice decor, wide spread seating and high ceiling but there’s no exclusive toilet at this facility. There are a few assortments of bentos, sake, juice and coffee. But overall the place is a bit pricey than its taste and quality. Definitely when there’s more than one Starbucks around in Tokyo Station, this cafe will not be the one I’ll try out most often.

Name: JAPAN RAIL CAFE TOKYO
Address: 1-chōme-9 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City
Author: Fabi Rupprecht
Review:
Ordered a large cup of tea for take-out.
Had to wait 25min for it. After multiple times asking, I received a small cup of tea for dine-in.
They were friendly and tried to help, but still annoying

Name: JAPAN RAIL CAFE TOKYO
Address: 1-chōme-9 Marunouchi, Chiyoda City
Author: Ken O'Friel
Review: A new cafe at the entrance of the Yaesu central entrance. Very spread out seating, like the high ceilings. Can buy a few bentos here. Nice selection of sake too. But drinks are quite expensive, ¥500 for a hot cup of tea. Nice but would not be my first choice cafe at Tokyo station

Name: Cafe April
Address: ライオンズプラザ池尻大橋 1F, 1 Chome-2-10 Ohashi, Meguro City
Author: Eric Albanese
Review: Very very very cute and peaceful cafe. The coffee was good, but the croffles blew my tiny little peanut brain. I will be thinking about the croffle for a very long time and I don’t know if I’ll be a happy man again until I return to Cafe April.

Name: Cafe April
Address: ライオンズプラザ池尻大橋 1F, 1 Chome-2-10 Ohashi, Meguro City
Author: Shuan
Review: It is a new Korean café in an otherwise quiet neighborhood. The fresh apple tea was a neat idea, and their kimpa was tasty. The atmosphere has space for improvement. You have to go to the cashier to order. They offer specialty coffee and have their own roaster.

Name: Cafe April
Address: ライオンズプラザ池尻大橋 1F, 1 Chome-2-10 Ohashi, Meguro City
Author: C K
Review: The food was good, and the mood was quiet and cozy.

Name: Cafe April
Address: ライオンズプラザ池尻大橋 1F, 1 Chome-2-10 Ohashi, Meguro City
Author: Emile Woon
Review: Beautiful, chic and cozy atmosphere. Every item on the menu is worth a try, Personally tried Oreo cheesecake and Tiramisu croffle, I will be back ♥️

Name: Cafe April
Address: ライオンズプラザ池尻大橋 1F, 1 Chome-2-10 Ohashi, Meguro City
Author: Sheela Norazlan
Review: Very very delicious foods n drinks..🤤🤤🤤

Name: BURDIGALA CAFE
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−9−1 大丸東京店 ５階
Author: Cyril ClavMan
Review:
Went there for an early lunch, and to try the croque monsieur 🥪
I was surprise by the size, big for Japan, and it was really good 👍🏼
The price is reasonable for the lunch set, with a soup and a tea 🫖 service is good and quick, as it was not full yet when I ordered
Managed to have a table with view, which is actually cool 👍🏼

Name: BURDIGALA CAFE
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−9−1 大丸東京店 ５階
Author: Abe F
Review:
Great atmosphere and food. However, the pasta can be improved as it has a strong fishy smell from the ingredients used.

Would totally recommend the egg toast!

Name: BURDIGALA CAFE
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−9−1 大丸東京店 ５階
Author: Shalby Reduan
Review: Should allow kids to eat their own food while the parents can enjoy food from the cafe. It is supposed to be a classy cafe but not considerate & definitely not customer focused.

Name: BURDIGALA CAFE
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−9−1 大丸東京店 ５階
Author: Dave Ma
Review: Nice place for a break.

Name: BURDIGALA CAFE
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−9−1 大丸東京店 ５階
Author: Jiyoung / ジヨン (지영)
Review:
There was such a beautiful, calm and spacious cafe at Tokyo Station! I was surprised.
I visited in the morning, and there were plenty of seats inside, and I felt it was the perfect place for a businessman to find a calm and relaxing atmosphere, with people working there and people having meetings with multiple people. Ta.

There is a wide variety of cakes, and they seem to offer breads and cakes from the same company's bakery 🎂 I will come again when I use the Shinkansen or on weekends!

Name: La Cour Cafe
Address: 2 Chome-11-9 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino
Author: Jessica Thomas
Review: We came here to get out of the rain at first, but I’m so happy we found this place! We had omurice and tea and felt very comforted by the delicious food and clean environment. They have large windows to observe while you relax.

Name: La Cour Cafe
Address: 2 Chome-11-9 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino
Author: Alice
Review:
Came to this place spontaneously for some drinks and cake and it definitely didn't disappoint!
It's a quite small space so we had to wait for a little bit until a table opened up but it adds to the cute and calm presence of the café~
The drink menu is huge so it's safe to say there's something for every taste - no matter if it's coffee, tea, softdrinks or anything else. I've had the iced cinnamon milk tea and even though it wasn't sweet enough for me at the beginning, I added some syrup my friend got for her coffee and it tasted amazing!
All the cakes seem to be made fresh as well (I think there's a selection of cakes that changes daily) and they're heavenly sweet and creamy.

Name: La Cour Cafe
Address: 2 Chome-11-9 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino
Author: Zheng Renjie
Review: Nice cosy cafe where we sat by the glass windows to watch the busy street. Nice coffee and royal milk tea, and tasty cakes. The baked cheese cake was especially nice.

Name: La Cour Cafe
Address: 2 Chome-11-9 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino
Author: Rosario Herrera (Pinkvali)
Review: It was very nice for a long chat with my friend while enjoying a cup of coffee and a piece of cake. Very friendly staff and the food was tasty and oishii

Name: La Cour Cafe
Address: 2 Chome-11-9 Kichijoji Honcho, Musashino
Author: Rei-AynD’ru Fleming
Review: i enjoyed the vibe and aesthetic of this cafe. very peaceful and usually not too busy. they have such an amazing array of coffees and their cakes and foods are to die for!

Name: Caffè Veloce - Ikebukuro Station East Entrance
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−36−5 第２成澤ビル1F
Author: Christinmb
Review: The location is nice and it wasn't as crowded as all the places nearby. The cake was very tasty. The coffee was nice but a bit cold imo.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Ikebukuro Station East Entrance
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−36−5 第２成澤ビル1F
Author: A Undine
Review:
Breakfast set is better than lunch set.
Coffee is normal but the sandwiches taste very good!

Name: Caffè Veloce - Ikebukuro Station East Entrance
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−36−5 第２成澤ビル1F
Author: Kyle Li
Review:
Not to lie, I was there because I was doing laundry next door. After spending time here, I can say it is a very nice cafe, if you are looking for an authentic blend-in cafe experience, this is it! There are many single seats. If you have a big party, you will have to pull tables together. People and staff are super nice and down to earth. My wife and son came a little later, and we had to sit separately. A kind grandma gave her seat to us so we can sit together.

If you are in Higashi Ikebukuro and looking for a non-toursy place for a quick coffee break, this is the place for you.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Ikebukuro Station East Entrance
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−36−5 第２成澤ビル1F
Author: Melissa smith
Review: Coveralls ran small but a fun place to spend time. Destruction room was a little small but axe throwing was fun.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Ikebukuro Station East Entrance
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−36−5 第２成澤ビル1F
Author: y
Review:
A cheap and delicious cafe.
Coffee is the cheapest.
Smoking and non-smoking tables are separate.
There is no parking.

Name: CAFE LIFE
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiodenmacho, 3−9 グリーンパーク日本橋 1F
Author: 川瀬レカ
Review: One of the best coffee I had here.

Name: CAFE LIFE
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiodenmacho, 3−9 グリーンパーク日本橋 1F
Author: Donaldo Peton
Review: Very nice Spanish Jamon. I will try whole sandwich next time!

Name: CAFE LIFE
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiodenmacho, 3−9 グリーンパーク日本橋 1F
Author: Jag Khangura
Review: Fresh bread. Great coffee.

Name: CAFE LIFE
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiodenmacho, 3−9 グリーンパーク日本橋 1F
Author: Aya Sasaki
Review: Very very cozy place 💕

Name: CAFE LIFE
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiodenmacho, 3−9 グリーンパーク日本橋 1F
Author: Clar
Review: Yummy sandwiches

Name: Cafe Veloce Shōtō shop
Address: 1 Chome-5-1 Shoto, Shibuya City
Author: Tomasz Bocheński
Review: Coffee was ok as well as desserts. Although place feels really bad lot of people and heavily crowded. Some of guests behave really loud. What I would recommend is to grab takeaway coffee and leave. For a plus is free WiFi if you are tourist.

Name: Cafe Veloce Shōtō shop
Address: 1 Chome-5-1 Shoto, Shibuya City
Author: Renato Brandao
Review: Today I joined a friend who was having a coffee at Café Veloce in Shibuya. I was just waiting for him to finish so we could have lunch in a nearby restaurant. To be honest, I would rather wait outside, because the place stunk of cigarettes, but my friend was having a coffee, so I sat down. A waitress came to tell me I couldn't sit down unless I bought something. 本当に失礼でした！The place was almost empty!!! There was absolutely no need for that employee to be so rude. I hope this old-fashioned, unhealthy and unwelcoming establishment will close soon.

Name: Cafe Veloce Shōtō shop
Address: 1 Chome-5-1 Shoto, Shibuya City
Author: David Glover
Review: Lots of seating space (separated smoking and non-smoking) however there is not a great selection of drinks or snacks to eat. About 30% of clientele on one Thursday afternoon were salary men taking naps.

Name: Cafe Veloce Shōtō shop
Address: 1 Chome-5-1 Shoto, Shibuya City
Author: Richard Webster
Review: Handy location for a break, but can be very busy. Fast service, but very popular & too many smoking seats, plus no door separating the non-smoking section, shame.

Name: Cafe Veloce Shōtō shop
Address: 1 Chome-5-1 Shoto, Shibuya City
Author: YUKA
Review:
Standard, no fuss.
Good WiFi for free. Plenty of seating

Name: Beyond the Box
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−9−14 加登ビル 1階
Author: Martlet
Review:
Went there for lunch. Nice little cafe with great desserts and coffee. It's a bit pricey, though. The lunch set was OK but nothing special.

The service was attentive and friendly. Very welcoming.

Name: Beyond the Box
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−9−14 加登ビル 1階
Author: Tomoko A.
Review:
I think they should inform their customers that it is a 90-minute service before they sit down.
I guess it is better than ‘not being told’.
Due to circumstances, I ordered only a cafe latte, but it got cold quickly.

Name: Beyond the Box
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−9−14 加登ビル 1階
Author: Massimo Burhanuddin
Review: The service is great! And you can bring your pets inside :)

Name: Beyond the Box
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−9−14 加登ビル 1階
Author: Julian Zoller
Review: Very good coffee and food. I recommend the Philly Cheesesteak. Very friendly staff.

Name: Beyond the Box
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−9−14 加登ビル 1階
Author: iamivamic
Review: that was one great oat milk latte 👍

Name: Nui.
Address: 2 Chome-14-13 Kuramae, Taito City
Author: Rebekah Crotty
Review: Probably the best basic French toast I’ve ever had. Very good food and flavor. Also got the passion fruit cheese cake. It’s very dense a little dry but everything else was perfect.

Name: Nui.
Address: 2 Chome-14-13 Kuramae, Taito City
Author: Jason Chuei
Review: Warehouse/industrial style cafe, located in Kuramae. Seating across the ground floor and a mezzanine. Eclectic wooden furniture and decor. Kitschy paraphernalia dotted around. Flower and glassware stand in one corner.

Name: Nui.
Address: 2 Chome-14-13 Kuramae, Taito City
Author: Mateusz Sawastian
Review: Great spot for European-style brunches. Quality ingredients, well prepared too. This place has crunchy bacon and fresh sourdough bread. Anyone who's been to a Japanese restaurant knows that their bacon is usually quite sad and floppy, while sourdough bread is practically nonexistent, so Nui's was a great surprise in that regard. Most of the customers seem to be foreigners. The staff speaks fluent English. Quite pricey though, with a breakfast with drinks for two totaling around 3000 JPY.

Name: Nui.
Address: 2 Chome-14-13 Kuramae, Taito City
Author: Sas B.
Review: Very expensive for what it was. The brunch plates were disappointingly small and cold. It also took over half an hour for our food to come out despite it being quiet. I’ve worked hospo before and I understand sometimes things don’t go as quickly as you’d like so I don’t mind how long it took. The interior is very cool and handcrafted and the staff are very nice. This would be a nice place to get some work done or hangout with friends.

Name: Nui.
Address: 2 Chome-14-13 Kuramae, Taito City
Author: Erwin Szeto
Review: Very solid. Eggs cooked perfect. I’m enjoying my matcha latte w soy milk. Vibe is a good one. Lots of young travellers as this is a hostel after all

Name: THE REVIVAL HOUSE
Address: 1 Chome-5-11 Kaminarimon, Taito City
Author: Rea Rubio-Stock
Review: We always like to go where the locals go. We were not disappointed for our first stop. We love the atmosphere and relaxing music, she was very friendly to us. They have small food choice but taste good which is very good for us, this gives us energy before the long walk. Most important, its allowed to smoke outside☺️

Name: THE REVIVAL HOUSE
Address: 1 Chome-5-11 Kaminarimon, Taito City
Author: Ray Funahashi
Review:
The Revival House is an awesome cafe / bar /eatery and hidden gem in the heart of Tokyo. I had the BEST cup of coffee of my Japan trip here! My hot Americano, served in their signature smooth blue mug, was simply perfect!

Their eats are equally phenomenal—They have a rotating menu and I tried the Green curry, and was blown away by the rich, intense flavor!

The first floor is a cozy bar space and they also have a second floor to chill out in. The barista is super nice and speaks some English, which is great!

The location is super convenient too. It’s a 5-minute walk to the iconic Senso-ji Temple, 10 minutes to the historic Asakusa Shrine, and 8 minutes to Nakamise-dori Street—a busy street filled with shops, souvenirs, and local snacks.

If you're in Tokyo, definitely make a quick stop at this café. I guarantee you'll love it!

Name: THE REVIVAL HOUSE
Address: 1 Chome-5-11 Kaminarimon, Taito City
Author: Leslie Pollock
Review: We stumbled upon this cozy coffee bar after an exhausting afternoon of shopping in kappabashi, the kitchen supply district. The owner is serious about her coffee, it looks delish! We got matcha lattes which were also superb. The place is also known for being dog-friendly. She serves a lunch set and has cake and snacks too. I really love the vibe there, like going to a neighbor but you don’t have yo drink alcohol.  However,  they had a few bottles. I also noticed they were making some fruit infused syrups.

Name: THE REVIVAL HOUSE
Address: 1 Chome-5-11 Kaminarimon, Taito City
Author: Leonard Lam
Review:
The ambience is perfectly cozy, the female owner/staff is so kind and patience to make the recommendation ☺️
Better ask and try their seasoning drinks coz it's mix with diff ingredients as I don't heard that clear but still wish to try it, u won't disappoint for sure.（meal was come with cup of soup and I thought that's my drink😳)
2nd floor is suitable for group of people or if u want some private space when there's no one upstairs.
Ps: the seasoning drink I ordered taste like hot ginger tea and it's totally perfect for me during winter time

Name: THE REVIVAL HOUSE
Address: 1 Chome-5-11 Kaminarimon, Taito City
Author: Phoebus Torralba
Review:
This is a great café/bar that serves good food, great cocktails, and a fantastic atmosphere.

The people who man the café and bar are quite personable.

I forgot to take a photo of the drink, but if you happen to like summer cocktails, ask the bartender to make you their Okinawan Breeze.

The second floor also serves as a rentable space where you can hold events with friends or functions.

I am recommend giving thus place a try.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Meguro
Address: Meguro City, Shimomeguro, 1 Chome−2−21 泰山堂ビル B 1
Author: Khanh Dinh
Review:
A coffee shop with internet.
2nd floor has smoking room.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Meguro
Address: Meguro City, Shimomeguro, 1 Chome−2−21 泰山堂ビル B 1
Author: Ak
Review: Pretty cheap cafe for this area. Some of the seats have power outlets.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Meguro
Address: Meguro City, Shimomeguro, 1 Chome−2−21 泰山堂ビル B 1
Author: PY H
Review: Napoleon pasta is really good. The clerk can speak English, which is appreciated. It is a wonderful place to relax and enjoy the food. Recommend this café when you pass by for the blooming  Sakura.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Meguro
Address: Meguro City, Shimomeguro, 1 Chome−2−21 泰山堂ビル B 1
Author: Lisa Landry
Review: Quiet neighborhood eatery. Limited menu but nice place for a quick bite.

Name: Caffè Veloce - Meguro
Address: Meguro City, Shimomeguro, 1 Chome−2−21 泰山堂ビル B 1
Author: Victoria Latman
Review: Very relaxing, pleasant place. Food was delicious.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ikebukuro
Address: 2 Chome-23-7 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Charles C
Review: This location is not in an obvious location but quite lovely. It has a great view of the park across the street. Staff is very friendly and patient. My cappuccino was velvet-smooth. Seating is limited here, but I find the benches outside great for chilling out at too. Thank you!!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ikebukuro
Address: 2 Chome-23-7 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Vaji Nasution
Review:
Blue Bottle Coffee in Ikebukuro might be a bit tucked away, but it's a hidden gem worth discovering, especially for its prime view overlooking Minami Ikebukuro Park. This quaint café might be on the smaller side, making it ideal for a quick coffee stop during busy times, rather than long, leisurely visits.

The coffee here lives up to the high standards Blue Bottle is known for, with each cup brewed to perfection. The minimalist, charming interior is not only stylish but also incredibly Instagram-worthy, making it a great spot for those looking to enhance their feed with a chic, coffee-themed post.

In conclusion, Blue Bottle Coffee Ikebukuro offers a delightful experience with its excellent coffee and scenic park views, despite its compact size. It's a must-visit for coffee lovers and Instagram enthusiasts alike when in the area.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ikebukuro
Address: 2 Chome-23-7 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Emily Liu
Review:
Quiet and nice.
Take it out and enjoy in the park across the coffee shop, perfect!!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ikebukuro
Address: 2 Chome-23-7 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Alec Villavicencio
Review:
1) Great location
-  their location is in front of a small park with many trees and seating. It’s perfect because when the cherry blossoms bloom, you have the perfect view and take good pictures. Their frontage is a glass panel so you can see the reflection of the cherry blossoms. Iykyk!

2) Good coffee
- nuff said! I love their coffee. Got their pour over and the taste notes were consistent with their description

3) Clean bathroom
- fortunately, i was able to take a ‘number 2’ seamlessly! Wew! Haha.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ikebukuro
Address: 2 Chome-23-7 Minamiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Ed Vides
Review: Walking around randomly as I tend to do. Walked past this little coffee shop and I couldn't resist. They have an English menu and the prices are worth what you get. Bonus it's next to a little park to sit and enjoy your brew.

Name: Q CAFE by RoyalGardenCafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 1 Chome−1−2 6F
Author: 金Ck
Review:
Royal Garden Cafe Hibiya

Came for a rest in this cafe when we were tired from walking around Hibiya.

Very nice surroundings,  great ambience.  Overlooks the big pond around Hibiya Park.

We ordered the tiramisu cake set and a potato starter.  The potato was a winner, to me at least.  Sightly crispy on the outside, and hot like crazy on the inside.

The tiramisu set came with a coffee.  Cake was creamy and peppered with cookies bits and tasted alright.

Service was OK, staffed with young people, they were nice.

Price was alright, not cheap but we can accept it as it was in mid town Tokyo and had a nice decor within.

If tired after walking too much, this could be a good pit stop coupled with a walk around the Garden Park View.

Name: Q CAFE by RoyalGardenCafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 1 Chome−1−2 6F
Author: Thanh Hoang
Review:
A perfect place for brunch/lunch.
Set menu between ¥2200-¥3200
Good food and drinks, attentive staffs.
Perfect view towards the Imperial.
6th floor.

Name: Q CAFE by RoyalGardenCafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 1 Chome−1−2 6F
Author: george kuo
Review: Best Burgers and drinks, and we will visit here every time we come to Town

Name: Q CAFE by RoyalGardenCafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 1 Chome−1−2 6F
Author: J M
Review:
I thought the food here was really well prepared and delicious. Good customer service and reasonably priced.

I order a hamburger set lunch + 200 yen for dessert and the total was around 1,600 yen per person. The hamburger was really juicy and delicious. The chocolate dessert thing that was served could’ve been better, it was a little dry.

Overall, really good and will be back again sometime to try the outside seating.

Thanks 🙏

Name: Q CAFE by RoyalGardenCafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 1 Chome−1−2 6F
Author: Bogdan Pirvu
Review: The food was great - I had the steak - and given the amazing view, the prices are quite reasonable.

Name: torch cafe
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 2 Chome−1−12 ２F
Author: Felipe Velasquez
Review: The menu is limited but it’s a great spot if you want to get away from the summer heat, maybe wait for someone. The apple cinnamon tea wasn’t strong enough in flavor to be ¥600 but I liked their hazelnut latte. And it was empty for a Wednesday afternoon, unlike Starbucks or Tully’s. I’ll come back again in the future.

Name: torch cafe
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 2 Chome−1−12 ２F
Author: Massimo Burhanuddin
Review: A retro looking cafe. They might need to replace some sofas in my opinion. Like other reviewers mentioned, you need to order the food online. Staff was pretty nice. I would recommend to visit at night if you like to try this cafe since the atmosphere feels more cozy when it is a little dark.

Name: torch cafe
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 2 Chome−1−12 ２F
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: Came here for a late evening coffee and dessert. It was mostly empty, which was fine by me. I had to go around to the bar area to get the servers attention, which was a little odd. You order through their online menu as well. The coffee was so so but the cheesecake was rather good. Nice ambiance to sit and chill.

Name: torch cafe
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 2 Chome−1−12 ２F
Author: 林奕圻
Review: I tried their basque terrine. It’s quite nice. The cafe has a great atmosphere. The staffs are really nice. However the seats are a bit too close to one another.

Name: torch cafe
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 2 Chome−1−12 ２F
Author: nick ng
Review:
Like many cafes in Ebisu, it has barely any sort of interior decoration except trinkets here and there. A cafe meant for talking rather than doing work as you would in Doutor etc.
There is wifi though

Name: BISTRO J_O
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−4−6 銀座Velvia館 ９F
Author: Napol C
Review:
Very cozy and nice food. staffs are also very kind to little kids. A little bit difficult to call staffs from the table due to huge open space, which needs to wait until some staffs walk passed by.

The restaurant has child seat for small kid (mine was 2 yo at time)

Wine selection was not bad but not too high class.  good to pare with dishes on the menu.

course menu was awesome!

Name: BISTRO J_O
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−4−6 銀座Velvia館 ９F
Author: Niko P
Review: I had a great lunch in this restaurant. The food was good and reasonably priced, the service was good and the restaurant had nice decor and great atmosphere. Highly recommended.

Name: BISTRO J_O
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−4−6 銀座Velvia館 ９F
Author: Hend Al Falasi
Review: Reception counter unfriendly and not willing to make reservations

Name: BISTRO J_O
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−4−6 銀座Velvia館 ９F
Author: F
Review: Tasty coffee

Name: BISTRO J_O
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−4−6 銀座Velvia館 ９F
Author: Louis 5566
Review:
This restaurant is located on the top floor of Belvia, where Ginza's LOFT is located. There seems to be a cafe attached with the same concept.
I went there during lunch time on a weekday, even though it was a little late. This place is open for lunch until 4:00 pm, so it's convenient if you're late.

Lunch includes regular sets and courses. For the set, you can choose pasta, or change to meat or fish as a main course for a small additional charge.

I was able to ask about today's pasta and fish at the reception desk. A staff member will guide you into the hall, and it is a large hall with a very open space feel. Most of the seats are at tables, but there are also sofa seats against the wall for two or one person.

This time, the fish was sea bass, so I ordered that. First of all, the appetizer comes with salad and bread. It was delicious with not much domestic seasoning. The fish had a good white wine sauce, and it also came with black pepper and salt, so it was nice to be able to eat it while changing the taste.
The dolce was also delicious with lots of berries and a sourness that wasn't too sweet.

There were only women there, but I guess it was only during the lunch period. It didn't feel like it was lacking in volume, and the staff was polite, so I thought it would be a good option for a course or dinner.

Name: Ｍｉｔａｓ café
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−10−8 マニエラ銀座ビル １Ｆ
Author: Fer M
Review:
Very nice place!  from the beginning I felt very warm and welcomed by all the staff, especially from the new server (a girl) who helped me to order and clarify some doubts, very attentive and kind 😊.
The food was exactly what I ordered and expected, that was because of the good communication.
I really like the vibes of this place, I recommend it!

Name: Ｍｉｔａｓ café
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−10−8 マニエラ銀座ビル １Ｆ
Author: P T
Review: We had the maple au lait iced & the homemade mango soda. Definitely recommend! The staff were super friendly and explained what the lunch meals were.

Name: Ｍｉｔａｓ café
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−10−8 マニエラ銀座ビル １Ｆ
Author: Melody Chan
Review: Lovely dessert and helpful staff☺️

Name: Ｍｉｔａｓ café
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−10−8 マニエラ銀座ビル １Ｆ
Author: Renae Bell
Review: I cannot say enough GREAT things about Mitas Cafe.  Everything we ate was so incredibly delicious.  BBQ shrimp.  Salads.  Mac & cheese dish.  Pasta.  We simply had to go back the next evening!  Great wine.  Great atmosphere.  Great service.  Great food.   We highly recommend it and if you're reading this, you really need to just go!  :)

Name: Ｍｉｔａｓ café
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−10−8 マニエラ銀座ビル １Ｆ
Author: Melissa Sears
Review: Maple au lait and cafe mocha are delicious!

Name: Cafe la Voie
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 1 Chome−23−1 1F
Author: Edmond Fong
Review: Ok, this place is the closest cafe to keio plaza hotel. I thought the prices were great. A shot of Japanese whiskey for about $6 American. The food menu is admittedly limited. The service is kind of slow (well, not kind of, it is slow,) but the atmosphere is wonderful. The service is friendly.  The price is decent. It's really an oasis. If you don't want to drink at your hotel because of the extravagant prices, come here, relax at a third of the price and check your emails while you are waiting

Name: Cafe la Voie
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 1 Chome−23−1 1F
Author: Charlotty Lam'a
Review: Passed by this place because it was near the hotel we were staying at. Gave it a shot because the place looked intriguing from the outside. Upon entering, there were a bunch of vacant seats, however staff said “no. full.” to us, before looking around and quickly changing their mind and leading us to a 2 seater table. We sat down and were only given a drinks menu, had to stand up and ask for the lunch menu. We were quite disappointed to see that there were only 3 food options for lunch. We ordered their iced matcha latte, and had to wait 30 minutes for it, having to confront the staff twice as well. The cafe was not busy at all. We were even more disappointed after tasting the drinks; the most bland matcha latte we’ve had our entire trip in Tokyo. We have tasted better matcha from street vendors, I’ve had better matcha in white countries like Australia. If you are a tourist, please save your time and money, you can literally get a better lunch experience at 7-eleven.

Name: Cafe la Voie
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 1 Chome−23−1 1F
Author: takmien Miah
Review: Food was amazing but service was the worst had to wait 30 mins for a salmon avocado sandwich then other customers that just came in was served and  had their food within 10 mins while I had to ask wasn’t acknowledged smh get better servers or ur going to lose business

Name: Cafe la Voie
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 1 Chome−23−1 1F
Author: Omar Gonzalez
Review: Amazing food and staff. They have both  Japanese  and western style foods

Name: Cafe la Voie
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishishinjuku, 1 Chome−23−1 1F
Author: Ali Caglayan (Zaz)
Review: This might just be my first Google review ever, but I felt compelled to share my experience. Unfortunately, I have to say that this cafe is, hands down, the worst one I've been to in Tokyo. Even convenience store food is a step up from here. It's quite disappointing, to say the least.

Name: WIRED CAFE Dining Lounge Wing Takanawa Shop
Address: Minato City, Takanawa, 4 Chome−10−18 ウィング高輪WESTⅡ2F
Author: まん丸月
Review:
I can give 5stars for Desert.. it was really good.
Coffee was also tasted good.
And I tired Berry cocktail.. which was also good.
But when it comes to service.. the staff were not friendly enough..
We were 7 people visited this cafe during winter vacations.. we asked them to provide room where 7 can sit together..
But they didn’t responded properly.. (might be language problem) they really didn’t assist well.. which I felt bit sad about the service..
Other than this.. all the orders came fast and could able to enjoy the food..

Name: WIRED CAFE Dining Lounge Wing Takanawa Shop
Address: Minato City, Takanawa, 4 Chome−10−18 ウィング高輪WESTⅡ2F
Author: roy fleming
Review:
The beers were icy cold. The food was tasty and most of all, the girls who served us were so friendly and helpful. Service was also very fast even though they were busy.
We also thought prices were very reasonable.

Name: WIRED CAFE Dining Lounge Wing Takanawa Shop
Address: Minato City, Takanawa, 4 Chome−10−18 ウィング高輪WESTⅡ2F
Author: Sophie Lesinska
Review: I enjoyed this café as an alternative to  tourist oriented venues. Filled with young  Japapanese students, it served tasty breakfast and decent coffee. The staff spoke little English, but we made mutual efforts to use Google translate and everything worked out. I felt like this was my real Tokyo experience.

Name: WIRED CAFE Dining Lounge Wing Takanawa Shop
Address: Minato City, Takanawa, 4 Chome−10−18 ウィング高輪WESTⅡ2F
Author: Fabrizio Bozzato
Review: Solo customer, got proposed a “punitive seat” while there were better seats available. I let myself out. Two male staff laughed at me. All the same, that establishment did not get my patronage and money.

Name: WIRED CAFE Dining Lounge Wing Takanawa Shop
Address: Minato City, Takanawa, 4 Chome−10−18 ウィング高輪WESTⅡ2F
Author: Maharani Vidyaningrum
Review: My orders came quickly, and everything was delicious! One of the staff members spoke English very well. I will definitely go again.

Name: Suzu Cafe Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−6−5 銀座トレシャスビル 6F
Author: Nikhil Shankar
Review: It is wonderful that this cafe is open so late, as we were able to come here for decaf coffee and dessert after a dinner in Ginza. The atmosphere was very nice and while the food and drink was not superlative, it was very respectable. Given that the American 5-star review appears to be a 4-star in Japan, my guess is that this place would have 4.5 stars if it were in the US (where I am from). Unfortunately, we have very few cafes like this in America, so this place gets bonus points for me for being welcoming and existing.

Name: Suzu Cafe Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−6−5 銀座トレシャスビル 6F
Author: Jason C
Review:
Great service and good food. Food was much better than I was expecting, loved their lemon honey soda.

If you do not speak Japanese, be prepared to use Google translate on the menus and communicating with the wait staff. The wait staff were very patient with us.

Name: Suzu Cafe Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−6−5 銀座トレシャスビル 6F
Author: 日本人最愛-SOGO百貨商圈捷運店面
Review: Amazing cafe shop in Ginza office building.lunch set so delicious and healthy vegetables!The desserts Putin so sweet and cute all is my favorite one!2024/5/21

Name: Suzu Cafe Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−6−5 銀座トレシャスビル 6F
Author: Kulthirath Pakawachkrilers
Review:
Super blueberry cheesecake, very tasty, I love all the ingredients and combination. I had taste a custard, very good as well, suitable for me who don’t like that much so sweet. I have ordered a soy milk tea latte, also good. Highly recommended to visit. When I was there, quite not crowded but when during peak business hours, there are a Que to wait, please pick a good timing to visit :))

- Meant This Way

Name: Suzu Cafe Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 2 Chome−6−5 銀座トレシャスビル 6F
Author: Dany Khalife
Review:
This is like a hidden gem. The food was limited but great options and portions. Price was very reasonable and definitely good location to get a break from shopping.

The only things I noticed is that the table for two is a bit too small. It can be easy to run out of space with both having food and drink. The waitress was great but it seemed like she was alone for some time or the place is understaffed, it took us 10-15mins to place our order since getting the menus

Name: Fireking Cafe
Address: 1 Chome-30-8 Uehara, Shibuya City
Author: Mira M
Review: Pretty cool place! The food options are plenty and well made. The waiting time for the food was reasonable but our cocktails took like 20min which was disappointing. (It wasn’t full that day). But the drinks were tasty! The atmosphere is nice, definitely unique, lock key, good for date night. I definitely enjoyed it.

Name: Fireking Cafe
Address: 1 Chome-30-8 Uehara, Shibuya City
Author: S I
Review: Atmosphere is cool, the younger servers looked like they didn’t want to be here, food was mid at best - very under seasoned and lacking in flavor. Come for the cafe and desserts but don’t expect to be satisfied by the meal.

Name: Fireking Cafe
Address: 1 Chome-30-8 Uehara, Shibuya City
Author: Zachary Hubbell
Review:
Cool cafe during the day and bar at night. There are so many unique cafes in this area, but this one stands out. Unlike so many Tokyo cafes, Fireking is spacious and one doesnt feel cramped. Other than having plenty of space, the cafe is well decorated with modern art and has very friendly service. Free WiFi allows for getting some work done!

Try the lime soda! Recommended!

Name: Fireking Cafe
Address: 1 Chome-30-8 Uehara, Shibuya City
Author: Jean-François Rochon
Review: The place is swag! Really nice decor (don't miss checking out the toilet as well if you go). Upscale place, but still reasonably priced. The lunch special set me back 1500 Yen, including soup and salad (minuscule) and the dessert option with a supplement of 200 Yen (the mango mousse was fresh, tasty and worth the extra). Service was friendly, smiley and fast. Great food, freshness and the Thai bias on the menu is interesting. It's a welcome change after a week in Tokyo.

Name: Fireking Cafe
Address: 1 Chome-30-8 Uehara, Shibuya City
Author: Dmitry Ivanov
Review:
A bit on the expensive side but everything was delicious. Service is good too.
It's quite popular, we went at Thursday 10pm and the place was full and we had to wait ~10 minutes. And because it's so popular it was very noisy inside, it was hard to speak with each other.

Name: Common Cafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−6−1 １F
Author: Nina V
Review: Very cute place with a room at the back. On a bustling start of Golden Week 2024 (Sunday) the back room and most of the front parlor was packed. We were given a 90-min window to complete our stay. Given the low 80* Fahrenheit Tokyo weather we were delighted to take a mid-afternoon iced coffee break with a small sweet treat.

Name: Common Cafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−6−1 １F
Author: Chandrika
Review:
The place is quite popular lunch spot owing to reasonable priced weekday lunch set and salad and drinks free bar. The salad buffet was good.
Drinks you can have soup, soft drinks and tea coffee unlimited.
For the mains however the options are around 7-8dishes. Had the chicken thigh slow roast dish which tasted average.

Name: Common Cafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−6−1 １F
Author: Cath L
Review: We had coffee (cappucino and mocha) and cake here and it was delicious. An English menu is available which was helpful and the staff were friendly.

Name: Common Cafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−6−1 １F
Author: T S
Review: Nice decoration inside, we can be relaxed

Name: Common Cafe
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 1 Chome−6−1 １F
Author: Calvin Kwok
Review: I came here expecting a normal cafe experience but found out it was more like a restaurant sit down location. The food was average but the atmosphere was nice and had the feel of a restaurant to match the menu they had. They also offer lots of sake options.

Name: Bondi Cafe Hiroo
Address: 5 Chome-15-9 Minamiazabu, Minato City
Author: Jeremy Graue (shogungraue1990)
Review:
Picture this, you are tired, hot, and ready for a bite to eat in a cool place. You look up a cafe since you don’t really want to take up a seat and you’re just going to drink something before doing some light reading.

That’s when I saw a picture of the most amazing eggs Benedict ever. I’m hooked! Alright, so finding the place is a bit of a work of blind faith. It’s tucked into a tiny corner of what would otherwise be considered a dingy alleyway/parking lot.

The staff are amazingly kind and helpful, and despite my limited Japanese, we did indeed vibe. It’s a decently spacious place with surfer cabin vibes. Blankets everywhere to make it cozy and the 70s aesthetic really felt amazing.

The eggs Benedict, by the way, was as amazing as described. Paired with two strips of bacon over two large cuts of bread and a sauce that just begs to be sopped up. I cannot recommend this place enough. It’s just a quick walk from the station and a slightly less quick of a walk to the nearby Arisugawa-no-Miya Memorial Park.

Name: Bondi Cafe Hiroo
Address: 5 Chome-15-9 Minamiazabu, Minato City
Author: Noobysama
Review:
Loved it. The food is really tasty, nicely served. The vibe is great - and I bet it’s even cozier and more relaxing in summer, as they have an open terrace.
I see a lot pf people in the reviews are complaining about stuff’s attitude. Come on, they are sweet and chill, not chatty, so you can relax and just enjoy your day.
Loved the beer from Morocco and eggs benedict (they have several types of them).
Coffee was good also. And they have an acai bowl 💖
They don’t have a time limit in contrast to a lot of places setting a two hour limit nowadays, so you are free to hang out there as long as you like. Though there’s one thing to keep in mind - benches are wooden and sitting on them for a long time is a bit painful 🤭

Name: Bondi Cafe Hiroo
Address: 5 Chome-15-9 Minamiazabu, Minato City
Author: Sei Saito
Review:
Booked here for a birthday brunch and they kindly arranged the seating for a larger group of 10 (You can only hook up to 10 online, but they’re pretty flexible if you call them ahead of time!)
The birthday plate was pretty big as well (shared it with around 12 people, and I’m glad I did after all the food!)
Everyone was happy with the food’s taste, price, and portion. Moreover, it’s a perfect cosy, chill ambience you just want to spend time chatting with friends (plus, it’s dog friendly, which is a plus!) 🎈
The only con I’d say was the QR code for the menu since it wasn’t functioning very well, but they’ll take your order in person anyway!

Name: Bondi Cafe Hiroo
Address: 5 Chome-15-9 Minamiazabu, Minato City
Author: Clara BODIN- Official
Review:
Nice Australian theme cafe to hang out with a nice terrace. Free WIFI for those who want to work. You can come with your dog outside. 3 min walk from Hiroo station so very practical.

The food used to be better before so I was disappointed by the presentation and taste…. The service is much slower that before … I use fro love this place but it low down in quality.

I has a sword fish 🐠 and a coffee but rage was disappointing..¥2500 per person

Name: Bondi Cafe Hiroo
Address: 5 Chome-15-9 Minamiazabu, Minato City
Author: Ping Lim
Review:
Was craving for some good Aussie brunch and some googling leads to this place - I believe the name of the restaurant took inspiration from Bondi Beach in Sydney.

Took queue number around 11:45 and had to wait for over an hour with 8 groups ahead. Thankfully with the mobile queue system you don’t have to wait on the spot.

Ordered shrimp eggs benedict with salmon topping, sautéed salmon in mushrooms light cream soup, açai yogurt, and flat white. Food was just okay, nothing special and way overpriced for the quality and portion and it’s definitely not worth the long wait to get in. Plenty of better brunch options around, of similar if not lower price range. Service was also just so so.

Only perk imo is you get to meet many dogs and puppies here as it’s pet friendly :) wouldn’t recommend if you are a foodie.

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Daikanyama
Address: Daikanyama Twelve, 1F, 10-10 Daikanyamacho, Shibuya City
Author: Henry Kleppel
Review: Food was delicious and healthy. Man behind counter was incredibly friendly and conversational. Amazing place to have great food and relax with some CBD. Highly recommend and will come again!!

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Daikanyama
Address: Daikanyama Twelve, 1F, 10-10 Daikanyamacho, Shibuya City
Author: Eric S.
Review: On a quiet side street in Harajuku, you can telax with CBD infused foods and drinks.

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Daikanyama
Address: Daikanyama Twelve, 1F, 10-10 Daikanyamacho, Shibuya City
Author: Renana Banana
Review: Really great food and extremely friendly staff member. I had the matcha latte with yuzu and lasagne.

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Daikanyama
Address: Daikanyama Twelve, 1F, 10-10 Daikanyamacho, Shibuya City
Author: I U
Review:
Great Place.
Nice location.
Great coffee.

Name: HealthyTOKYO CBD Shop & Cafe Daikanyama
Address: Daikanyama Twelve, 1F, 10-10 Daikanyamacho, Shibuya City
Author: えむDee
Review: Cafe with great CBD products and vegan food! Has outdoor seats and staff are kind.

Name: Whistle Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishiwaseda, 2 Chome−10−18 パティオ西早稲田 1F
Author: Anna Mazzi
Review:
I started a 言語交換 with a friend here today. This place is quite cozy, and apparently not too crowded at lunchtime. We could talk together for a couple of hours without being worried about people there being too loud or stuff like that.

Actually I wanted to come here for a long while as I always walk past it when I go to university but I only got a chance to visit it today, one year after I made up my mind and I decided I wanted to try it.

I got the omurice, which is alright. But I think their strong point may be the pasta options, but I also saw quite some delicious looking desserts on their menu that I may try next time.

Only downside is that they only accept cash payment.

Name: Whistle Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishiwaseda, 2 Chome−10−18 パティオ西早稲田 1F
Author: Michele T
Review: Delicious karage plate and giant coffee! Drink set is +100 yen, super filling and great deal. The cafe has a chill vibe but not super quiet. Single bar seating at the window, a few tables and couch seating available. Great spot for studying if you like some sort of background noise. The menu has some great options but not overly packed with stuff. The waffles look amazing and I want to try those next time I come. Staff was super nice and accommodating.  New favorite cafe around Waseda and Baba.

Name: Whistle Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishiwaseda, 2 Chome−10−18 パティオ西早稲田 1F
Author: Eri Laura Camila Akamine Forero
Review: It's a great place!! The atmosphere was great, the music too, it wasn't too crowded at lunchtime and we had delicious fried chicken, the price is great for the portions and the staff is super friendly and kind. Definitely coming back!! It's worth it to come and study or have lunch too

Name: Whistle Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishiwaseda, 2 Chome−10−18 パティオ西早稲田 1F
Author: Yingying Henry
Review:
Friendly and welcoming cafe with attentive service. Music and kitchen noise level is pretty high so this place might not be very suitable for studying/ working unless you are trying to train your concentration skills.
There are no explicit signs prohibiting studying/working/ use of laptops though.

There is a variety seating types available; regular tables, couches with coffee tables and individual counter seats.

Food is pretty reasonably priced for the portion. My apple cinnamon waffles with caramel sauce had a creamy scoop of vanilla ice cream, but the waffles tasted similar to the make-it-yourself stuff at 3-star hotels’ breakfast.

They are currently running a “Natsume Collaboration” but sadly I don’t know who that is…. Natsume sets come with a souvenir you draw out of a box. Some customers got pretty excited about what they got, so this seems like a fun sort of place.

Name: Whistle Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Nishiwaseda, 2 Chome−10−18 パティオ西早稲田 1F
Author: Angel Li
Review: Satisfying meal and super nice and calming atmosphere. Usually cafes in Japan are more suited for dining instead of working/studying, but this place actually isn’t bad at all if you feel like staying for a bit longer and working on something.

Name: CAFÉ VELOCE
Address: タイムズステーション池袋, １Ｆ, 4 Chome-25-9 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Tyler McKinnis
Review: I come here regularly to study as it's pretty close by and has a lot of seats available, so I don't feel pressured to order a drink every hour. Pretty cheap price, chain store, and can get a bit busy during weekends but it's fine. I usually stay for 3 or so hours as it has charging ports at the seats where there's a wall.

Name: CAFÉ VELOCE
Address: タイムズステーション池袋, １Ｆ, 4 Chome-25-9 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: FishingTime Letsgo
Review: Stopped in this nice little cafe while walking the streets near Ikebekuro Station, I pointed to what drink I wanted and within a few minutes - received a nice latte, flavor was good, so I found a place to sit to enjoy my cafe latte.  I think this is a chain store but still worth a visit while in Japan.

Name: CAFÉ VELOCE
Address: タイムズステーション池袋, １Ｆ, 4 Chome-25-9 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Acacia Baldie
Review: Not bad coffee, lots of seats

Name: CAFÉ VELOCE
Address: タイムズステーション池袋, １Ｆ, 4 Chome-25-9 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Ryan B
Review: Great cafe for a small sandwich with coffee!

Name: CAFÉ VELOCE
Address: タイムズステーション池袋, １Ｆ, 4 Chome-25-9 Higashiikebukuro, Toshima City
Author: Frede Wittenkamp
Review: Nice place, friendly staff, good deserts.

Name: Teahouse Chainkan
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiningyocho, 1 Chome−10−6 日本橋ＳＤビル １階
Author: Zachary T Zhu
Review: Possibly some of the best tea that I've ever had! I got the oolong tea, which upon drinking I could immediately tell had been roasted and this depth of flavor that you so rarely get from tea. It had a slightly sweet, nutty flavor that I enjoy in my tea. Super nice staff that helped with the refill of the teapot. Quiet, cozy little spot and a super enjoyable experience to boot! You can definitely tell that they take their tea seriously, as it was the focus of the menu instead of being a side beverage.

Name: Teahouse Chainkan
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiningyocho, 1 Chome−10−6 日本橋ＳＤビル １階
Author: Carey Kelley
Review: Great tea, and lite lunch, service outstanding and a lot of patience explaining details

Name: Teahouse Chainkan
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiningyocho, 1 Chome−10−6 日本橋ＳＤビル １階
Author: G D
Review:
Cozy tea room. Very relaxing, and the staff are very nice. Good selection of teas.

It's a little pricey, but a great place to take a friend and just chill out over many cups of tea.
