Name: Teahouse Chainkan
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiningyocho, 1 Chome−10−6 日本橋ＳＤビル １階
Author: 律
Review:
It was a wonderful shop where you could relax and enjoy delicious Chinese tea.
The staff at the shop were very courteous, and I asked for the first tea ceremony, and it was interesting to see how they brewed the tea.
My only regret was that it was a busy time and the store was noisy, so it was difficult to hear the explanation.

Name: Teahouse Chainkan
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashiningyocho, 1 Chome−10−6 日本橋ＳＤビル １階
Author: よみ“Yomi”
Review:
You can enjoy authentic Kungfu tea at a reasonable price.

I wanted to have some tea after my meal, so I wandered in and was able to enjoy some delicious tea.
In addition to Chinese tea, you can also enjoy black tea and green tea.
Next time I would like to enjoy it with a meal.

It's a shop you'll want to stop by again when you're in the area.
Coffee is good, but it reaffirms that tea is also good.

Name: Afternoon Tea TEAROOM
Address: Toshima City, Nishiikebukuro, 1 Chome−1−25 東武百貨店池袋店 3階
Author: MASAKI
Review: Beautiful & comfortable cafe restaurant in Tobu department store !

Name: Afternoon Tea TEAROOM
Address: Toshima City, Nishiikebukuro, 1 Chome−1−25 東武百貨店池袋店 3階
Author: o a
Review:
I visited before 6pm on a weekday.
I got in without lining up.
Ordered the high tea set after 5pm.
A set of main food, drink, and half-sized dessert (or salad or soup).
I had scallops and scallops peperoncino, decaf iced tea, and shortcake. The price was 2,030 yen including tax, but I was very satisfied with the dessert.
The food and desserts change with the seasons, so I'd like to go again.

Name: Afternoon Tea TEAROOM
Address: Toshima City, Nishiikebukuro, 1 Chome−1−25 東武百貨店池袋店 3階
Author: min dada
Review:
I wanted to eat delicious tea and cake, so I went to afternoon tea.
The fresh cream on top of the cakes and scones was unsweetened and not too sweet, so it was very delicious.
The lemon cream decorated around the cheesecake was so delicious!
There were many different kinds of pot tea, and it was a novel tea with fruit that reminded me of a tropical country.
The inside of the store is calm and I can't help but be drawn to the cute products sold at the entrance.

Name: Afternoon Tea TEAROOM
Address: Toshima City, Nishiikebukuro, 1 Chome−1−25 東武百貨店池袋店 3階
Author: S R
Review:
It's very crowded on Saturdays and Sundays, but there are a lot of seats, so I feel like the turnover rate is faster than other restaurants. It can get a bit crowded due to the large number of people, so I recommend going during the day on weekdays or at night.

I often eat the standard tomato pasta because it always has a consistent taste, but I also look forward to the seasonal menu.

The tea pot holds 2 or 3 teacups, so you can fully enjoy your tea until you've finished your meal and dessert, and the tea comes out without tea leaves, so it doesn't taste bitter.

The store staff are also very courteous.

*Photo 2024 Spring Seasonal Menu

Name: Afternoon Tea TEAROOM
Address: Toshima City, Nishiikebukuro, 1 Chome−1−25 東武百貨店池袋店 3階
Author: Ken
Review:
It's a cafe with delicious food. Enjoy the menu, which changes seasonally.
There's a line on Saturdays and Sundays, but it's still worth going.

Name: THE TEA ROOMS 日本橋三越店
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashimuromachi, 1 Chome−4−1 Ｂ1
Author: que será, será
Review:
I visited on a Sunday before 11am. There always seemed to be one or two empty seats until around 11:30.

I ordered cream tea and carrot cake for eat-in and it cost just under 3,000 yen. I had lunch after this, but I ordered too much by myself💦

The carrot cake also has a sweet frosting. And the first taste. The texture is similar to pumpkin pudding. The pickled raisins are hardened at the bottom, and the walnuts are also large.

The clotted cream of the scone has a rich, buttery consistency. The scone itself has a strong buttery aroma, and is crispy on the outside and moist and delicious on the inside.

The Earl Gray tasted bitter at the end, but when they brought it to me, they offered to add hot water to it. It was about 3 cups worth even without adding hot water.

It was very comfortable and easy to spend time.

Name: THE TEA ROOMS 日本橋三越店
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashimuromachi, 1 Chome−4−1 Ｂ1
Author: dt k
Review:
I wanted to eat authentic scones, so I entered the store around 2pm on a weekday. There were several groups waiting to get in without a reservation.
I ordered a cream tea set that included two scones and a pot of tea.
The combination of the warm scones with a crunchy and fragrant filling, rich clotted cream, and strawberry jam was delicious and filling. The smell of butter when it was delivered whetted my appetite.
I ordered Earl Gray tea, and the pot is enough for 3 cups, and they will add hot water if you ask.
It was a great satisfaction.
I would like to try the afternoon tea next time♪

Name: THE TEA ROOMS 日本橋三越店
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashimuromachi, 1 Chome−4−1 Ｂ1
Author: k.はるみ
Review:
[Cream tea] is served, and the clotted cream is authentic.
When I attended a special tour of the Mitsukoshi Theater, there were so many people lining up before and after the event that I gave up on eating inside.

I bought scones and clotted cream for takeout. *4 scones...I purchased and paid for one additional scone, but unfortunately it seems that the staff forgot to include the extra scone. I guess he was busy...

The scones had a rich aroma and taste, probably because they were made with good ingredients. The clotted cream was also very delicious.

I wasn't able to eat it inside the store, but I thought it would be a good idea to enjoy it casually with my family.

Name: THE TEA ROOMS 日本橋三越店
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashimuromachi, 1 Chome−4−1 Ｂ1
Author: 安本明美
Review:
I didn't have much time before departure, so my lunch plans were more like brunch.
I added some clotted cream and it was really nice. The scones and tea6 were perfect and delicious.
However, the leaves (herb?) on top of the fruit were wilting, and I could hear the staff talking privately (like your child's school), which seemed to ruin the atmosphere of the store. It was a bit disappointing because it was delicious.

Name: THE TEA ROOMS 日本橋三越店
Address: Chuo City, Nihonbashimuromachi, 1 Chome−4−1 Ｂ1
Author: TAKE O.
Review:
A coffee shop located in the food district on the basement floor of the Nihonbashi Mitsukoshi Main Building where you can enjoy homemade scones and cakes made with fermented butter.

This time I ordered a simple ``Strawberry shortcake with drink (2090 yen)''.

The combination of moist sponge cake, mildly sweet cream that melts in your mouth, and sweet and sour strawberries was simple but exquisite.

It's located in the basement of Nihonbashi Mitsukoshi, so it's quite crowded during the holidays, and you'll have to wait in line, but if you have time, this is the cafe you'll want to go to.

Name: Tea Lounge The Garden
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 5 Chome−11−16 国際文化会館
Author: Chanuthi Rajapaksha
Review: Stunning atmosphere.. very nice for a slow breakfast or lunch .. reservation is always recommended.

Name: Tea Lounge The Garden
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 5 Chome−11−16 国際文化会館
Author: Haruto Kanamori
Review: This building was designed by the great talents of Japanese modernism architecture. Great foods and drinks, nice staffs, and amazing space.

Name: Tea Lounge The Garden
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 5 Chome−11−16 国際文化会館
Author: V
Review: Very pleasant place to go and relax with a cup of tea. Classy ambience, great view of a garden.

Name: Tea Lounge The Garden
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 5 Chome−11−16 国際文化会館
Author: dreamer J
Review: nice

Name: Tea Lounge The Garden
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 5 Chome−11−16 国際文化会館
Author: KEI YOSHIMURA
Review:
6 minutes walk from Azabujuban station!

I visited [Tea Lounge The Garden].

This lounge is located inside the International House of Japan, which was built as a place for international conferences and cultural exchange.

It can be used all day, from buffet style breakfast to lunch, dinner and tea time.

The International House of Japan itself is a members-only facility, but non-members can also enjoy the Tea Lounge The Garden and restaurant SAKURA.

In 1955, the vast site of 3,000 tsubo, which was originally a feudal lord's residence and then managed by the Mitsubishi Zaibatsu, was transformed into the current building by a joint design by three masters of Japanese architecture: Kunio Maekawa, Junzo Sakakura, and Junzo Yoshimura. The old building was expanded into a new building in 1976, designed by Kunio Maekawa.

It has been certified as a registered tangible cultural property.

The building overlooks the magnificent garden, which has been designated as a scenic spot in Minato City, and allows you to spend an elegant moment forgetting your daily life.

The location is Torii-zaka, a 5-minute walk from Azabu-Juban Station.

It is also accessible from Roppongi Station in less than 10 minutes.

The restaurant has 68 seats including table seats and terrace seats.

All of the dishes served are said to be prepared by the chefs of the Royal Park Hotel, so you can enjoy high-quality meals in a stunning location.

This time at lunch

"Seafood course"
¥5,580

I asked for it.

Below are the contents and impressions received on this day.

★Smoked salmon salad

★Today's soup (red pepper)

★White fish poire and head shrimp salad with sour cream and herbs

★Rice

The menu is supervised by a famous hotel chef, so the menu is classic and classic.

I really liked the taste of not only the main white fish, which was brought together by the sour cream, but also the red peppers that were served with today's soup!

Overall, the hospitality was great and it was a great lunch!

I would definitely like to visit again.

Thank you for the meal!

Name: Monmouth Tea
Address: 1 Chome-21-2 Sendagaya, Shibuya City
Author: Monica
Review:
Delicious sweetened black milk tea. The tea is strong in flavor and enriched with milk and sugar. For someone who never adds sugar to my coffee or tea, it’s definitely on the sweet side. But it’s not as sweet as sugary Starbucks drink or soda, so it was somewhat tolerable. You can’t make any changes or modifications to the drink. Since this is a black milk tea specialty store, the menu only consists of black milk tea (cold, hot, with whip cream, without whip cream…).

I wish they had a less sweet option. If they reduced the sugar a bit, it’d be perfect.

Service is very fast, you’ll be in and out in minutes with your drink.

Name: Monmouth Tea
Address: 1 Chome-21-2 Sendagaya, Shibuya City
Author: Edía Paff
Review: You can have milktea with ice cream here! I really like it. It's a sweet treat. The ice cream floats on a layer of whipped cream, so you can either mix it all together, or deink the tea as is and have the ice cream later on :3c

Name: Monmouth Tea
Address: 1 Chome-21-2 Sendagaya, Shibuya City
Author: Agnes S
Review: Legit milk tea. Nicely extracted tea + good quality milk. Not too sweet

Name: Monmouth Tea
Address: 1 Chome-21-2 Sendagaya, Shibuya City
Author: Kevin Kwok
Review: Get the hot milk tea with cream. There's a few benches outside. But if it's raining there's not really anywhere to sit

Name: Monmouth Tea
Address: 1 Chome-21-2 Sendagaya, Shibuya City
Author: Alyssa Bender
Review:
I am ADDICTED to Monmouth’s tea - in my past two visits I got two drinks per visit 😅 I appreciate that the taste of the tea is strong and is not overpowered by the milk or sugar, it reminds me so much of HK milk tea.

the owner and staff are very friendly ☺️

Name: Aoyama Tea Factory
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１８−6 望月ビル １階
Author: treeleaf64
Review:
I visited from America. This is the best quality tea from Sri Lanka and is well priced. I recommend drinking the tea without milk.
The owner is very friendly and gave me recommendations of many other tea places to visit.
Usually, there are other people to talk to. It’s a small shop. Good music selection.

Name: Aoyama Tea Factory
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１８−6 望月ビル １階
Author: Brian Chien
Review: This is a really nice tea place. The owner is friendly and talkative.

Name: Aoyama Tea Factory
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１８−6 望月ビル １階
Author: MARU 3 (まる3)
Review:
Aoyama Tea Factory in Jimbocho.
A specialty store for Ceylon tea. Not only is the tea delicious, but it goes extremely well with American waffles.
I was concerned about the loud voices of the regulars, but it doesn't seem like a place where you can spend your time in peace, so that's it.

Name: Aoyama Tea Factory
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１８−6 望月ビル １階
Author: る
Review:
I visited around lunchtime on a weekday. It was just the female clerk and I inside the not-so-large store, and we were able to spend a relaxing time while the zabadak music played. .

You can enjoy delicious tea and hot sandwiches for 1,100 yen. I had the beef stroganoff sandwich.
You can also order hot water for your tea, and best of all, it's cheap, as everyone said! !
Depending on the time of day, it was a wonderful shop where you might want to stay longer and get a refill of the pot.

Name: Aoyama Tea Factory
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１８−6 望月ビル １階
Author: kiwi0359
Review:
A tea shop that was on my bucket list
I went to Kanda to renew my license on a weekday, so now is definitely the time! I thought and charged

It was a really delicious milk tea!
I was full after eating tandan noodles for lunch, but I ended up ordering waffles from a tea shop like this (I was so full on the way home).

Probably because it was a weekday, there were a lot of other customers who were like the same type of people who were going to rush in.

Name: Sakurai Japanese Tea Experience
Address: スパイラルビル 5F, 5 Chome-6-23 Minamiaoyama, Minato City
Author: Roxy Thomas
Review: My husband and I went in today and were able to get sat without a reservation. This was on a Tuesday at noon, and there was a steady stream of customers. This was a highlight of our trip as we appreciated the care their team put into each part of the tasting. It was incredibly fascinating watching the tea maker prepare the water and teas, and the food/tasters paired nicely with the drinks. We found the experience to be extremely calming and sedating, with a sprinkle of ASMR. We highly recommend it for those that want to open up their palate to the regional teas Japan has to offer. PS their cashew dessert is insanely good.

Name: Sakurai Japanese Tea Experience
Address: スパイラルビル 5F, 5 Chome-6-23 Minamiaoyama, Minato City
Author: Max Bell
Review: Even though it was a little pricey, it was so good! The service and attention to detail was unparalleled. Me and my mother did two different tasting; I did a traditional set and my mom did the seasonal cold teas. The experience and experimentally left us talking about the great tea we had for the next week! Truly a one of a kind experience I cannot recommend enough. I think the more expensive tasting menus have more value here as they employ more experimental methods. Truly great! The Hojicha and Gyokuro were my favorites, and my moms were the Gyokuro over ice and the Kyobancha and Corn-foam

Name: Sakurai Japanese Tea Experience
Address: スパイラルビル 5F, 5 Chome-6-23 Minamiaoyama, Minato City
Author: Mischief Adler
Review: My husband and I were looking for something to do and decided to book this last minute. We were not expecting much; however, we were pleasantly surprised! The tasting experience was wonderful, with the tea and cocktails tasting quite good and unique. The tea master's were great at explaining everything in English as they did their preparations and took great care with their attention to detail. It was really interesting what them roast the tea leaves in front of you, and the candied snacks that accompanied the drinks were lovely! Definitely check it out!

Name: Sakurai Japanese Tea Experience
Address: スパイラルビル 5F, 5 Chome-6-23 Minamiaoyama, Minato City
Author: Vocaloid Anna
Review:
Ok, here’s my honest opinion:
A very cool experience but borderline pretentious. Now hear me out.

The way they prepare the tea was with very careful thought and timing. I can understand some elements from how they handle the cups to pouring, pulling inspiration from the Japanese tea ceremony. You feel they've done this a thousand times, it’s like their movements follow a dance/conducting an orchestra. You get to learn the different leaves and the subtle nuances between each one as they present it to you with leaves subdivided in each platter.

And… thats about it. The food/sweets was very mediocre and each time they pour you a cup, you're left wondering at the direction of if theres sweets coming out to complement the tea.. or drink while they simultaneously serve the other guests.

Afterwards, it felt like everybody got up and left quiet compliments as if convincing themselves their 1.5 hr experience was worth it. Was it because the tea taste was not to their liking..?

I ordered the most expensive on the menu to fully give my opinion. At 6,230 yen (~$40) you get about 3 small cups of tea, a half pinky nail size of sweet chestnut, a small rectangular size bento the size of your hand (rice, 3-4 pieces of sweet potato?, tiny grilled fish, and two other veggies), ending off your meal with s selection of sweet. I chose kashiwa mochi. Overall, I think for $40 with the experience was not so bad.

I wanna warn that the tea may not be for everyone. It may be too intense for some, being that it has this slight bitter but earthiness taste. They strain the tea leaves so you get the maximum taste from the tea leaves. I'm assuming thats why some guests made nervous comments: ah, I'm so full of tea… that was interesting.. Or even better, the gentlemen beside me looked like they were struggling to finish their tea sake.

I also noted that during my experience they didn’t let us record any videos, only take pics. But I see a few videos uploaded here..?

Since I was so satisfied and amused after the experienced, I wanted to support the shop further by buying a very expensive medium size tea scoop. I saw at their front and again when they used it during the tea experience. Excited,  purchased it without a second thought about the price tag thinking, this is a great buy since the yen conversion is so low! WRONG. Unfortunately they hike up the price when I checked the online store for the original merchant (kaikado.jp). The website sells for 20,200 yen (As of today about $131.99). Ok, surely there’s shipping that causes the inflation..? Nope total was $200.60 after shipping. In the store was priced at 38,500 yen (About $249.27 when I purchased 1 week ago, today would have been $246.62).

Buyers beware! Anyway, I recommend if there’s anything interesting in store you’d like to purchase, they can be found elsewhere for cheaper.

Come for the tourist trap experience. Stay for the different tea tastings.

Name: Sakurai Japanese Tea Experience
Address: スパイラルビル 5F, 5 Chome-6-23 Minamiaoyama, Minato City
Author: John Kenney
Review: I showed up without a reservation and had to come back 90 minutes later. The experience was worth it. each tea was unique and the way they prepared it was entertaining to watch. It was especially cool seeing what other people were getting. The tea makers were really knowledgeable and were able to explain how each tea was different in English. I will definitely come back during my next visit to Tokyo.

Name: BRITANNIA TEA
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１
Author: 吉田栗栖
Review: Looks pretty fancy at the entrance, but the price and quality of the menu is affordable and totally worth it! The mood upstairs is completely different too! Very chill, seem to have theme of outdoor picnic especially if you order the scone set menu because it comes with a cute little paper box that opens up into a picnic mat looking thing. The scones are delicious and the teas are delightful as well. You can check out their teabags too, they have several interesting flavours. This cafe has solo workspaces on the 3rd and 4th floor, with wifi, plugs and even a copy machine. Recommended for people who wants a quiet and chill place to study and work, while enjoying yummy stuff.

Name: BRITANNIA TEA
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１
Author: ゆり
Review:
I entered the store looking for a place to kill time and read without waiting in line.
It's on the second floor with only stairs, hard chairs, and the interior is clean, but it's quite lively with many groups of young women.
The tea served is in a take-out container, so it is bland.
After ordering and paying, wait at your seat.
You can take it to the second floor.

The prices are reasonable considering the fast food atmosphere.
Due to the location, there are few non-smoking cafes.
Next time, I'm thinking of splitting the cost of the space with drinks only.
Wi-Fi and power outlets available.
The taste of the tea is okay. The scent is good.
Scones are normal.

If you are traveling with two or more people, it may be difficult to find an empty seat.

Name: BRITANNIA TEA
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１
Author: 森裕次（ピッチ）
Review:
The atmosphere on the 1st floor and the 2nd floor is completely different~☆
The first floor is a sophisticated white-based space, and the second floor features a tree that is integrated with the lighting, and the walls are light blue, creating a different atmosphere from the first floor!

What makes me happy is that there is a tea stand on the table and a place to put used tea bags!
If you order it with ice cream, water drops will fall, but with this stand you don't have to worry about it falling.

There is also an outlet, so you can work perfectly ✨

Britney Hamada's signature was written on the wall along with a cute illustration☆

Darjeeling (ice cream) is quite easy to drink.
I would like to use it again ☆

Name: BRITANNIA TEA
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１
Author: Charlie
Review:
Delicious tea (Iced Earl Gray)
The cake is normal (lemon pound)
Beautiful refreshing light blue walls

Sales are on the 1st floor and eating and drinking is on the 2nd floor, but the stairs are steep and difficult for the elderly (there is no elevator, and the 3rd floor seems to be a co-working space).

Name: BRITANNIA TEA
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−１
Author: de ad
Review:
【postscript】
From around summer, it seems that instead of being closed irregularly, it has become a fixed holiday on Mondays and Tuesdays.
(Tuesdays are irregular holidays, but they are almost always closed)
It's a shame because it was the only place available when the nearby cafe was closed or full.

−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−−
This cafe is perfect for drinking tea, eating sweets, and taking a quick break.
However, the taste is really delicious! That's not to say, just enough to fill your stomach. By the way, even if you eat in, tea utensils are not available.
If you want to drink tea in a sturdy tea set, eat sweets, or have afternoon tea, it might be better to go to a specialty store nearby.
In addition, the 3rd and 4th floors are co-working spaces, but when groups of customers come to the open space on the 2nd floor and it gets noisy, you can hear the sound of laughter and chairs being pulled, and when you are on the 4th floor, you can hear the noise from the 2nd floor. You can also hear people on the third floor typing on their PCs and making phone calls (remote conferences?), so it's generally noisy no matter where you are. Are you taking advantage of coworking spaces? I think...
If you want to concentrate or drink in complete silence, you may want to spend the money and rent a special box.
Also, personally, I wish the 2nd to 4th floors would be built with the cleanliness and royal interior of the ordering space on the 1st floor.

One thing to note in particular is that the lights in the restrooms are powered by a motion sensor and will automatically turn off after about 30 seconds. You will have to relieve yourself in the dark for a while. In the first place, I can't find the switch anywhere, so I have to fight with this sensor to relieve myself, and in terms of hygiene, it's like the toilet at a nearby karaoke bar, so I don't really want to use it. .

Name: Kenyan Shibuya
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−14−8 南部ビル 1F
Author: Luk Cherry
Review: Best milk tea ever🤍 strong and aromatic black tea, with a touch of milk. The drink is rich and smooth. The cake was good as well. Staff are friendly and polite. Will definitely come back again🤍

Name: Kenyan Shibuya
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−14−8 南部ビル 1F
Author: Eric Cheng
Review: Was looking for a place to sit for a while and found this place, there is a wild range of tea selection from japan and oversea, which is good for tourist. The black tea is strong in flavor which is correctly brewed. The milk tea is superb as it copies from English style and allow you to add milk and sugar your way, avoiding the sugary water style Japanese milk tea. However the cake itself is too much cream, although the cream is light but yet firm.

Name: Kenyan Shibuya
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−14−8 南部ビル 1F
Author: ARBN
Review:
Essentially, the place has nothing to do with Kenya (the country). But the staff said that he thinks that a long time ago, the owner had a good friend from Kenya and naming the restaurant had something to do with that friend.

1,200 yen for the lunch set comprising of a main dish, soup, salad, and drink. Price is reasonable considering the area. Quantity was good and quality was ok.
The アイミティー (iced milk tea) was delicious - strong black tea flavor with semi-sweet milk; reminds me of the local milk tea (not the boba kind) in countries HK and Thailand!
Unfortunately, I didn’t have space for a dessert after my lunch, and the desserts were not available for take-out.

Name: Kenyan Shibuya
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−14−8 南部ビル 1F
Author: NA FA
Review:
Milk tea is a specialty! We had ice milk tea, hot milk tea, and weiner milk tea (has cream on top)
The former two has a strong sunny taste of black tea.
The latter one has more of a milky flavour, we all enjoyed them but I think hot milk tea (500 yen) is the best!

We also had blueberry cheese cake and tea jelly, both very delicious, would definitely recommend the tea jelly!

Name: Kenyan Shibuya
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−14−8 南部ビル 1F
Author: Dean Chen
Review:
Love here and it’s not my first time to visit here. Their milk tea is the best one I ever had in Tokyo. BTW, they called hot milk tea as Chi-mi-tea, but in fact, it has nothing to do with chi tea.
Their apple pie is also amazing. It wrap a whole apple in pie crust, and come with vanilla ice cream. When I was first time to visit there, it was the first time to try this kind of apple pie and it really surprised me! (Although I took banana cake this time)
Of course, their other cakes are also great. if you don’t like apple pie, I will suggest banana cake and cheese cake. They are also quite good taste to me.
In addition, they also serve regular meals, seems quite a lot of client come here for their meals, too.

Name: ZUU&HEIN Myanmar Tea House
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−23−5 2F
Author: 心春
Review:
This was the first time I ate Myanmar food. I had a date with my Burmese boyfriend and he brought me here.
Everything here was amazing including the food and service.
The staff is really cute, omg you won’t know how much I love the “လဖက်ရည်” - lahpet yay. The fish soup noodles was awesome too (a little sour, spicy and fatty), and my Burmese boyfriend loves the lahpet choke here (beans salad with green tea leaves???), it had a very attractive taste, quite suitable to eat with rice. The music was really good. I love here and would comeback here soon with my boyfriend ^^

Name: ZUU&HEIN Myanmar Tea House
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−23−5 2F
Author: Leah RTK
Review: Was here 9 months ago and this time around we ate here x3 times. I must say the food we had this time was very good and the price is cheap compared.

Name: ZUU&HEIN Myanmar Tea House
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−23−5 2F
Author: Raf Parker
Review: My grandpa is Burmese and I went here to send him photos. The samosa salad was so tasty! Definitely recommend! ❤️

Name: ZUU&HEIN Myanmar Tea House
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−23−5 2F
Author: Snow
Review: I went there around 4 PM. The shop was chill, playing Burmese songs and the food was delicious!

Name: ZUU&HEIN Myanmar Tea House
Address: Toshima City, Higashiikebukuro, 1 Chome−23−5 2F
Author: Kaori Ri
Review:
Favourite Myanmar Tea Shop.
I recommend to have lunch around 2pm~ so that you can avoid crowded ppl.

Name: TEA HOUSE COCOCHI
Address: Shibuya City, Jingumae, 4 Chome−4−8 スリーエスビル 2F
Author: Alex Rinn
Review: Very friendly staff, nice and calm place and good tea!

Name: TEA HOUSE COCOCHI
Address: Shibuya City, Jingumae, 4 Chome−4−8 スリーエスビル 2F
Author: けんだま
Review:
The second floor is the cafe room.
I'll pay the bill first. Payment cannot be made in cash. Credit cards and transportation cards can be used.

🌸

🍨Glass dessert beni

It is 1,500 yen including tax.

A glass dessert where you can enjoy the gorgeous sweetness of strawberries, the aroma of pistachios, and the sourness of blueberry framboise jelly.

It's a cute visual♪
The harmony of the pistachio cream, the sourness of the blueberries, and the various flavors is so delicious❣️

Name: TEA HOUSE COCOCHI
Address: Shibuya City, Jingumae, 4 Chome−4−8 スリーエスビル 2F
Author: Toshiki Morimoto
Review: Enjoy tea at TEA HOUSE, located on the 2nd floor of the flagship store of the skin care brand COCOCHI. You can enjoy delicious seasonal tea. This time, since it was early autumn, I had a tea called "Koyo". I was able to relax in a comfortable space where I could relax!

Name: TEA HOUSE COCOCHI
Address: Shibuya City, Jingumae, 4 Chome−4−8 スリーエスビル 2F
Author: Toshiki Morimoto
Review:
A very comfortable cafe.
A light woody scent.
The menu of teas and sweets that change seasonally all look delicious, and this time I chose the cocochi tea. The staff is very polite and it's a hidden gem!

Name: JURI'S TEA ROOM
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome, １４−４ ２Ｆ エタニティ
Author: M Hiroo
Review: Excellent British-style tea room in Azabu Juban. Scones and cakes are delicious. Their tea is also excellent. If you want authentic afternoon tea or cream tea then this is the place.

Name: JURI'S TEA ROOM
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome, １４−４ ２Ｆ エタニティ
Author: Olivia Marlin
Review: There were plenty of seats open and only two tables with a "reserved" sign on them and all their counter seats open. It seems like they have no interest in actually doing business.

Name: JURI'S TEA ROOM
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome, １４−４ ２Ｆ エタニティ
Author: Saleem Butt
Review: Very authentic

Name: JURI'S TEA ROOM
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome, １４−４ ２Ｆ エタニティ
Author: Thomas Neil James Shadwell
Review: I walked in when they were closed and Juri gave me free carrot cake! I was amazed to learn they came all the way from the Cotswolds to set up here! I will be back :)

Name: JURI'S TEA ROOM
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome, １４−４ ２Ｆ エタニティ
Author: n. n.
Review:
My daughter and I visited a tea room that we had always wanted to visit.
I was impressed by how delicious the scones and sandwiches were.
I don't usually eat sweets, but this one had just the right amount of sweetness.
We also had hot spring water and enjoyed a relaxing cup of tea.
It's a very friendly shop, and I'd definitely like to visit again.
I would like to try carrot cake next time.

Name: Nakajima Tea House
Address: Chuo City, 中央区Hamarikyuteien, １−1−1 浜離宮恩賜庭園
Author: Teckle Channel
Review:
There's a choice of matcha tea with either a Japanese sweet or a pancake for 1000 yen.
I don't like matcha tea but went for the experience, and it really is a lovely little place to stop for a rest while enjoying the park.
The sweet was a seasonal chrysanthemum and delicious, and the friendliness of the staff and ambience in there made it a lovely  break.

Name: Nakajima Tea House
Address: Chuo City, 中央区Hamarikyuteien, １−1−1 浜離宮恩賜庭園
Author: Woolnacht
Review:
Tasty traditional matcha, very good, but not so strong.
Too sweet, but cute dessert.
A lot of wood around, beautiful views.

Name: Nakajima Tea House
Address: Chuo City, 中央区Hamarikyuteien, １−1−1 浜離宮恩賜庭園
Author: Brian Arcillas
Review:
Excellent view to enjoy your tea and wagashi with.

Came on a Monday afternoon and the wagashi had sold out soon after but they also have packaged red bean and brown sugar jelly to offer as well.

Name: Nakajima Tea House
Address: Chuo City, 中央区Hamarikyuteien, １−1−1 浜離宮恩賜庭園
Author: CJ Phillips
Review:
Perfect place to drop in for a cup of matcha and sweet while strolling the gardens.

Very crowded during my visit (lots of tourists) which distracted somewhat from being able to enjoy the tea and view of the gardens (perhaps they could limit numbers inside or add spaces to the outside deck).

Pro-tip for a first timer to a Japanese tea room - don’t be like the other tourists in the line and ask the following questions which I actually heard during my visit:

1. Can I have something other than tea?
2. Do you have coffee?

Fun fact - the answer to each is ‘No, as it’s a tea house’.

Name: Nakajima Tea House
Address: Chuo City, 中央区Hamarikyuteien, １−1−1 浜離宮恩賜庭園
Author: Con Daf
Review: Great atmosphere, recently renovated and nice manta tea. Highly recommended, not much crowded. It’s inside a really nice park and the view is astonished!

Name: KIMONO TEA CEREMONY MAIKOYA TOKYO
Address: 1 Chome-12-2 Asakusa, Taito City
Author: Ron Gabriel
Review:
This was a last minute inclusion in our itinerary, our daughters loved getting to choose their kimonos. The MAIKOYA Tea Ceremony in Tokyo offers a deeply authentic and culturally rich experience that showcases one of Japan’s oldest traditions.

The ceremony was led by experienced host, who was skilled in the art of tea preparation but also patient in explaining the meaning behind each step. The hosts and visitors are dressed in traditional kimonos, adding to the authenticity of the experience.

The session begins with an introduction to the history and principles of the tea ceremony, followed by a hands-on demonstration. Participants are given the opportunity to prepare their own matcha (green tea).

We also appreciated the opportunity to wear traditional Japanese attire during the ceremony, which can be arranged at MAIKOYA. This further enhances the cultural immersion and provides a memorable photo opportunity.

Overall, the MAIKOYA Tea Ceremony in Tokyo is highly recommended for anyone seeking a genuine Japanese cultural experience. Whether you’re a solo traveler or part of a group, the combination of history, beauty, and hands-on participation makes it an unforgettable highlight of any trip to Tokyo.

Name: KIMONO TEA CEREMONY MAIKOYA TOKYO
Address: 1 Chome-12-2 Asakusa, Taito City
Author: Anisa Jamaleddin
Review: My group and I had a beautiful experience at this location. We opted to do the tea ceremony and kimono dressing. There was a wide variety of clothes for the ladies and the staff members who dressed us allowed us to choose our kimono’s/accessories with a wide range to choose from for all sizes. The staff members took care in their work and paid attention to detail to ensure we were comfortable and dressed to perfection. I do want to note that there wasn’t as wide of a variety in clothes for the gentlemen. The tea ceremony was educational and the staff member who run the class was very good at explaining and made fun jokes to make us all comfortable. There were plenty of photo opportunities within the building. 10/10 experience.

Name: KIMONO TEA CEREMONY MAIKOYA TOKYO
Address: 1 Chome-12-2 Asakusa, Taito City
Author: Tiffany W
Review: An unforgettable experience! We enjoyed learning more about the process of the tea ceremony while wearing beautiful kimonos. You get to rent the kimonos to take pictures around Asakusa as well 😄 I highly recommend booking this with your friends!

Name: KIMONO TEA CEREMONY MAIKOYA TOKYO
Address: 1 Chome-12-2 Asakusa, Taito City
Author: Elizabeth Plewis
Review:
I am so glad that I did this. It has made our holiday. My son and I wore traditional dress. The people were all lovely .The ceremony was interesting and serene. We weren't rushed. It was not expensive at all. We got some lovely pictures in their rooftop garden . I would highly recommend this experience and would like to thank
the lovely ladies who  made us feel so welcome .

Name: KIMONO TEA CEREMONY MAIKOYA TOKYO
Address: 1 Chome-12-2 Asakusa, Taito City
Author: Emily Phan
Review: I felt so beautiful in the kimono and we walked around to the temple to pray and take pictures! The tea ceremony was beautiful and insightful! 10/10 would come every time i visit tokyo :) very nice loving ladies who dress you and do your hair. Loving womanhood 💕

Name: Afternoon Tea Tea Room
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 7 Chome−1−1 アトレ上野 1階
Author: lemon
Review: very good place not only  at this place or ueno but other places too but limited menu  of food cause the main is still TEA (usally if you order its better to have set menu )

Name: Afternoon Tea Tea Room
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 7 Chome−1−1 アトレ上野 1階
Author: Ninja Kevin
Review: Fantastic happy staff made feel so welcome and speak English too. Beautiful store but it's under a train line which can be noisy. Amazing desserts and tea!

Name: Afternoon Tea Tea Room
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 7 Chome−1−1 アトレ上野 1階
Author: Emily
Review: The parfait and the tea were good but we ended up waiting 40 minutes with no indication of how long we’d have to wait. Not sure that it’s worth it honestly but if it’s empty and doesn’t have a wait then it’s a nice little spot.

Name: Afternoon Tea Tea Room
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 7 Chome−1−1 アトレ上野 1階
Author: Peihsi Lin
Review: Good access inside Ueno Station. Both meals and desserts are amazing! It is a good place to enjoy lunch or tea time and the service is also wonderful. After having meal, you can also do some shopping on home fashion goods or accessories.

Name: Afternoon Tea Tea Room
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 7 Chome−1−1 アトレ上野 1階
Author: Deana Miller
Review: Fairly busy tea room with a cute shop inside. I came in during the Strawberry Festival month and they offered a lot of strawberry desserts as well as a strawberry tea. The desserts were good; sweet but not sugary, while the tea was too sweet for my liking.

Name: Muan (Tea house)
Address: 1 Chome-1-76 Shirokanedai, Minato City
Author: Blue Nyaong
Review:
English possible. #Happoen I've wanted to visit for a long time.
Tea room in Happoen, Muan.
Both English and Japanese were available.
It was 1,100 yen per person for matcha + wagashi + Japanese candy, or 3,300 yen per person if they made the tea in front of you. I paid 1,100 yen. The service was very good...
One person 1100円で抹茶プラス和菓子。サービス最菓子。サービス最高でした。current timeでは土日月しかやってないです。
The atmosphere was very nice. The tea tasted good too. Even if you don't like matcha, I recommend going if you like the Japanese feel. If you are visiting Tokyo, this place is a must go! I liked their service as well.

Name: Muan (Tea house)
Address: 1 Chome-1-76 Shirokanedai, Minato City
Author: Ha Pilu
Review: Priority will be given to those involved in the wedding ceremony, but the general public can also tour the garden. I stopped by on my way home from Meiji Gakuin University on Sunday, and there were a lot of people from overseas visiting the park. The behavior of the staff here is amazing and I am even impressed.

Name: Muan (Tea house)
Address: 1 Chome-1-76 Shirokanedai, Minato City
Author: Haruko Ishikawa
Review: After admiring the gardens and rare bonsai at Happo-en, you can relax as you enjoy Japanese sweets and matcha tea in the small and quiet tea room. Having said that, I go there every time I have guests from overseas come over.

Name: Muan (Tea house)
Address: 1 Chome-1-76 Shirokanedai, Minato City
Author: Minh Dũng
Review: Beauty scene !!!

Name: Muan (Tea house)
Address: 1 Chome-1-76 Shirokanedai, Minato City
Author: hashima
Review: Happoen has two tea rooms. There is also a tea ceremony experience.

Name: Tea house
Address: 1-1 Hamarikyuteien, Chuo City
Author: T K
Review: You can enjoy traditional Japanese matcha served with wagashi (Japanese sweets). The scenic view also allows you to fully experience the essence of Japanese culture.

Name: Tea house
Address: 1-1 Hamarikyuteien, Chuo City
Author: Take
Review: I got the matcha set (850 yen). Matcha and Kintsuba while looking out at the garden were delicious.

Name: Tea house
Address: 1-1 Hamarikyuteien, Chuo City
Author: ミソカツの模型
Review: It might be nice to be able to eat and drink while looking at the garden, but I wish the matcha was added more carefully...but I guess it's not for Japanese people, so I can't help it.

Name: Tea house
Address: 1-1 Hamarikyuteien, Chuo City
Author: Anna uit de betuwe
Review:
Rest moment in centuries-old garden.
Shoes off and tea time with very nice ladies.

Name: Tea house
Address: 1-1 Hamarikyuteien, Chuo City
Author: T Saka

Name: Kanda Dessert
Address: 2 Chome-11-20 Kajicho, Chiyoda City
Author: Olof Olsson
Review:
Great place to stop by and have a chat with the friendly owner and regulars.
Excellent service!

Name: Kanda Dessert
Address: 2 Chome-11-20 Kajicho, Chiyoda City
Author: Hazel KURI-KURI
Review:
3 minutes walk along the railroad tracks from the north exit of Kanda Station. A small cafe that is wedged in between the rows of restaurants under the guardrail. There are only four counter seats with no depth inside the store.
It's a small place, so you can give up space with your neighbor and just talk. The master also joined in the conversation, and everyone present had a peaceful chat. The tea is very fragrant and delicious. We especially recommend the gorgeous tea soder blend, which is served at the banquet for Nobel Prize winners @Stockholm.

Although the physical space is small, this is a tea specialty store that somehow makes you feel warm. A mysterious cafe where you can feel the atmosphere of downtown Edo and feel like the story is about to begin.

Name: Kanda Dessert
Address: 2 Chome-11-20 Kajicho, Chiyoda City
Author: 不思議なつんでれ
Review:
I drank 3 types. The chai was exceptionally delicious. It's certainly different from the chai you get at Indian restaurants, and its sweetness, aroma, and taste make it easy to drink. If you don't like the taste of other countries, I recommend you try this at least once. Seriously, it tastes better than any chai I've ever had.

It's easy to drink other teas, but the set is too cheap, so I'm sure we're living in this modern age, and various prices are already going up due to rising prices, so I thought we'd have to raise the price, so I was wrong. I think the price will go up soon, so hurry up. As expected, the current price is too low. There are only four chairs inside the store, so it's a very small store. You'd better think it's quite narrow. It's just a strangely comfortable space. The manager and owner are nice people, so it's easy to talk to them.

Jam on ice cream is also highly recommended. You can drink really top-level, delicious tea here, but the store is small, but if you're close to a good store, you'll definitely want to try it. It seems like 22 years have passed since I moved to Kanda.

Name: Kanda Dessert
Address: 2 Chome-11-20 Kajicho, Chiyoda City
Author: Tokyo colobockle
Review:
A store specializing in tea not only from Northern Europe.

I've been curious about this store for a while now, and I've been to the store several times, but it was always crowded so it was hard to get in.

However, there was no guest this time.

When I opened the door, the elderly shop owner greeted me with a smile.

It's a Scandinavian tea shop, but I don't know much about Scandinavian tea. It's a genre I've never really dealt with.
After listening to a brief explanation, I felt that the owner must be a very passionate tea expert...

So, for the first time, I received the recommended Seder blend.

The taste is refreshing, and the aroma that enters the nose is gorgeous and classy. It kind of feels like Europe, but I don't really understand it yet...
However, there is no doubt that it is a delicious tea.

I received a bite of jam as a service.
This is my first time having tea with jam.
Flower-scented jam and black tea...a wonderful pairing.

When I suddenly looked at the menu on the blackboard,
I discovered the words “Lapsan Souchong”!

This tea was sold at the JAZZ cafe where I worked part-time when I was a student. It's nostalgic, and I haven't seen the characters since then. I'm sure it was Chinese tea, so I guess it wasn't just Scandinavian black tea.

The owner was pleased with the response to Lapsang Souchong, and told me that it was his favorite tea.

This shop offers a blend of Lapsang Souchong and tea called Kee Man.
Of course I'm curious about that, but I'm also curious about spice blends.

I'm confused about which one to choose next.

Name: Kanda Dessert
Address: 2 Chome-11-20 Kajicho, Chiyoda City
Author: たなべあゆみ
Review:
First timers can sample their favorite tea for free!

I ordered the seder blend that I was interested in, and when I finished drinking it, I was able to add some hot water to the same pot, so I could drink a lot.

The rose jam and the Scandinavian green tea and seder blend financier I ordered were also very delicious!

I had a lot of fun talking with the store manager about tea, and the store was busy with regulars, so I had a lot of fun.

I want to stop by again if I'm nearby!

Name: Rakuu-tei Tea House
Address: 11 Naitomachi, Shinjuku City
Author: Amanda Priest
Review:
Absolutely delightful tea house with onigiri and mochi in Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden. The onigiri range from 350-550 yen as of September 2024, and they were all fabulous! the white peach mochi was so good!! We got hojicha, sencha, and genmaicha iced teas - none were sweetened (as it should be), and the genmaicha was our favorite, strong depth of flavor.

NOTE you will need to pay to enter the garden to eat here. Well worth it in my opinion, provided you have time to enjoy the gardens before/after your meal.

Name: Rakuu-tei Tea House
Address: 11 Naitomachi, Shinjuku City
Author: Mrs. Mapo
Review:
Beautiful tea rest spot.
The capacity is 6 seats inside.
There are more seats outside - ask for takeout if the inside is full

Name: Rakuu-tei Tea House
Address: 11 Naitomachi, Shinjuku City
Author: laura liisa Lemetsar
Review: Located in a beautiful park. The park has an entrance fee of ¥500, after entering you can make your way to the tea house. Very nice tea, mochis and onigiris. Definitely worth a visit while walking around the traditional gardens.

Name: Rakuu-tei Tea House
Address: 11 Naitomachi, Shinjuku City
Author: Lidor Bracha
Review:
Soooo good!!
Price was ok.
It located in an amazing spot in Geyon park.
Don’t skip the onigiri - it was perfectly made! The white peach and fig mochi was so good!

Name: Rakuu-tei Tea House
Address: 11 Naitomachi, Shinjuku City
Author: Joseph Raczynski
Review: Lovely little respite from the heat in this traditional tea house. You'll need to remove your shoes, so wear socks. You'll either sit on the floor... There's two tables or at a short table in low chairs.

Name: Takizme
Address: 1 Chome-28-7 Higashishinagawa, Shinagawa City
Author: Eden Elhamias
Review:
The experience was one of a kind! I went there with my friend as a birthday gift and we have an amazing time. Out host was kind, polite and easy to understand and chat with. The place was very aesthetic, on a quiet neighbourhood in Tokyo. It was the best experience to unwind from the busy city sightseeing. The traditional tea and snacks was  unique and delicious, followed by explanations and meditation.

We had an amazing, relaxing and delicious time!

Name: Takizme
Address: 1 Chome-28-7 Higashishinagawa, Shinagawa City
Author: vincedelicious
Review:
If you're after a guided tea ceremony experience but don't necessarily want all the stiff rigidity with dress and stressing over unfamiliar customs, Takizme is perfect.

We had a lovely session where we could interact with the host in English. He guided us and through the experience and spoke very warmly of the tea we were having and the accompaniments which he crafted himself.

It's hard for me to truly describe how much I appreciated this form of tea ceremony: It's something I knew I would be recommending to all who come to Tokyo because of how easy it was to simply enjoy the pleasures of extremely thoughtful & well brewed tea - and to soak in the atmosphere of a calming & meditative session.

So it's not only for those who want/need an English guide or who are worried about not understanding the process - the tea & accompaniments themselves are extremely delicious and the setting is immaculate.

Name: Takizme
Address: 1 Chome-28-7 Higashishinagawa, Shinagawa City
Author: Anne-Hélène Monsimier
Review: Our experience at Takizme Tea Ceremony was exceptional! From the moment we entered, we were transported to a world of tranquility and serenity. The teas and snacks were perfectly paired and exquisite. The minimalist decor and traditional ambiance, with beautiful ceramics and furnishings, added to the peacefulness of the space. The attention to detail was remarkable and it is evident that every detail has been carefully considered by the team, resulting in a truly unforgettable experience. Our time at Takizme was not just a tea ceremony, but an invitation to fully immerse ourselves in the present moment. We left feeling enchanted by this tea ceremony and reminded of the importance of slowing down and being mindful in our busy lives. Highly recommend for anyone seeking a moment of tranquility and special Japanese experience in the heart of Tokyo!!

Name: Takizme
Address: 1 Chome-28-7 Higashishinagawa, Shinagawa City
Author: Lian Luo
Review: Highly recommended place for a Japanese tea experience! The atmosphere is great, with a lovely blend of modernity and traditional tea culture. Beautifully made Japanese confectionery is served and has an amazing, rich taste. From the decor to the tea cups used, everything is thoughtfully planned and prepared. A much needed Zen experience amidst the crazy heat and the usual hustle bustle of Tokyo. Felt so peaceful and grounded afterwards :)

Name: Takizme
Address: 1 Chome-28-7 Higashishinagawa, Shinagawa City
Author: Joyce Wu
Review:
I attended the tea tasting experience in September 2024 with a group of four, and it exceeded all my expectations. Despite the host having just returned from a trip and feeling a bit exhausted, he still managed to provide us with a wonderful and engaging experience.

Initially, I was hesitant because the location is a bit out of the way in Shinagawa, about a 25-minute Uber ride from central Tokyo. However, it was absolutely worth the trip! The experience is intentionally designed to be less traditional, so there's no need to worry about formal attire or rigid customs. The host explained that his goal was to create a space where guests could relax, enjoy, and learn about tea in a laid-back atmosphere.

Throughout the ceremony, we were encouraged to ask any questions that came to mind, and the host was happy to share his knowledge on everything from the ideal brewing temperatures to the history of Japanese tea customs, as well as insights into his personal journey and decision to start his own business. The room was also perfectly air-conditioned, which was a blessing on a hot and humid summer day, and the host kindly adjusted the temperature until we were all comfortable. The ceremony began with a calming mindfulness and meditation session, setting the tone for the rest of the experience.

We opted for the three-tea tasting with dessert pairing, and each tea came with its own special cup, carefully sourced from local ceramics shops. The experience was thoughtfully curated, encouraging us to appreciate the aroma of the dried tea leaves before each tasting.

Cold Brew Tamaryokucha Green Tea, paired with a Japanese-style jelly with peach
This was my personal favorite! The cold tea and refreshing jelly were the perfect match on a hot summer day. The jelly was light but packed with flavor, with a wonderfully delicate texture.

First Flush Genmaicha Brown Rice Tea, paired with a chocolate chip cookie with Japanese spices
This pairing had a more toasty, warm flavor compared to the first. The cookie reminded me of holiday treats, with notes of cardamom, cinnamon, and ginger—an unexpected but delightful combination with the tea.

Hojicha (Roasted Green Tea), paired with a fig and almond sandwich cookie
This pairing was deliciously balanced. The roasted flavor of the tea complemented the soft cookie, with the figs and almonds adding texture and depth. On a cooler day, this might have been my top choice, but it was hard to beat the cold pairings in the summer heat.

All of the desserts were made fresh in-house, and it was clear that a lot of love and care went into every detail. At the end of the ceremony, the host asked for feedback and thoughtfully considered our suggestions, which is probably why everything felt so perfected.

I highly recommend this experience to anyone visiting Japan. The host is very foreigner-friendly, fluent in English and French (and possibly more), and loves meeting people from all over the world. If you’re looking for a unique, relaxing, and educational experience, this tea tasting is a must!

Name: Sakura River Teahouse
Address: 6 Chome-19-2 Shinbashi, Minato City
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Chanoha
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 3 Chome−6−1 松屋銀座 B1F
Author: K L
Review:
Best tea house ever. Tranquil atmosphere. Its so different from the business of the shopping mall. Calm and relaxing atmosphere.
Only about 7 seatings. So you might have to wait depending on the people.
Staff is so nice. Some can speak English.
Matcha taste amazing. Sweet also taste great as well.
Highly highly recommend this tea house.
They also sell tea right outside of the tea house. The tea store is outside.
Will come back again just to relive the special moment.

Name: Chanoha
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 3 Chome−6−1 松屋銀座 B1F
Author: Wong Teck Jung
Review:
A rest stop before continuing my exploration of the greater Tokyo area. I initially tried the matcha tea and dessert set. The matcha had a wonderfully refined taste, and the seasonal dessert had a delicate touch of sweetness. It was a truly serene moment, with the sound of flowing water amidst the bustling Ginza district. The set concluded with sakura tea, which had a unique flavor reminiscent of a lightly savory plum. It was incredibly soothing to the throat.

In addition to the matcha and sakura tea, I also added Gyokuro tea to my experience. Its initial taste was difficult to describe, but after a while, I began to notice its distinct umami sweetness. It was a wonderful addition that completed my tea journey.

Observing the tea brewer meticulously preparing my teas added to the overall tranquility of the moment, further enhancing the experience.

Name: Chanoha
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 3 Chome−6−1 松屋銀座 B1F
Author: Nitin Nazeer
Review: A great place for great tea! I came later in the day with my where they offered a matcha or sencha, along with a seasonal dessert for just around $5 USD. They give great recommendations and the staff clearly take great pride in their work - highly recommend!

Name: Chanoha
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 3 Chome−6−1 松屋銀座 B1F
Author: Ozi James
Review: Amazing atmosphere and service. Nice waterfall, staff were really nice. Just a 4 star as I thought they would serve shaved ice but they only have one item on the menu depending on the day.

Name: Chanoha
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 3 Chome−6−1 松屋銀座 B1F
Author: Dima Balandin
Review: It’s a shop of Japanese tea,  and a cafe.  Many types of green tea, helpful stuff, reasonable price. They offer testing, what helps to choose if you are not familiar with this types of tea (after testing I bought strong Kumakiri, it  had intensive but very pleasant aroma and taste). You can also buy a cup of green tea “to go” or savor some green tea in a calm and fancy small cafe.

Name: 夢華居 Mukai
Address: Fukase, Bldg 2F, 1 Chome-7-11 Azabujuban, Minato City
Author: S R
Review: Used during afternoon tea time, the space is filled with plant decorations and the elegant furniture gives it an overall stylish feel. The tea utensils are also unique and are like works of art. My friends and I were relaxing in an extraordinary space, forgetting the feeling of the city.

Name: Alpaca cafe
Address: Toshima City, Ikebukuro, 2 Chome−23−1 中西ビル 1F
Author: Abby Brewer
Review: Cutest & tastiest tapioca shop in Ikebukuro area! Perfect place to grab a drink on a hot or cold day while exploring the city. The shop owner is very kind!

Name: Alpaca cafe
Address: Toshima City, Ikebukuro, 2 Chome−23−1 中西ビル 1F
Author: Nora Ti
Review: My all time favorite boba shop! The owner is super kind and uses fresh ingredients and fruits for their teas. I once had a whole lemon inside my boba. Would go there again every single day.

Name: Alpaca cafe
Address: Toshima City, Ikebukuro, 2 Chome−23−1 中西ビル 1F
Author: Jeff C.
Review: Great tea! Love the theme. No seating area, but the attendant was nice enough to let us sit on the side stools to enjoy our drinks.

Name: Alpaca cafe
Address: Toshima City, Ikebukuro, 2 Chome−23−1 中西ビル 1F
Author: Lanette Sok
Review: The tea was super delicious and made perfectly! Very nice and clean shop. Also super cute!! 💖

Name: Alpaca cafe
Address: Toshima City, Ikebukuro, 2 Chome−23−1 中西ビル 1F
Author: Kyle
Review: Best tapioca tea Iv had!! So tasty and the serving is so big! Great value! The staff was super friendly and helpful!

Name: Sui an
Address: 2 Chome-20-12 Shirokanedai, Minato City
Author: 久人郡

Name: Tea House Happa
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Honcho, 2 Chome−33−2 吉祥寺プティット村
Author: Suzana Hyakutake
Review:
Very nice atmosphere and cool architecture.
The French Toast, ice milk tea and tropical flower tea were delicious.
The basil chicken sandwich was awful. The chicken was so hard, dry and tasted old. There were no basil at all and the bread tasted like the cheapest one you can find on the market. The worst chicken sandwich I've eaten in my whole life.
The sides were good though.

Name: Tea House Happa
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Honcho, 2 Chome−33−2 吉祥寺プティット村
Author: Andra George
Review: It is a TEA house. I wouldn't waste time on lunch options, especially the chicken sandwich. Another reviewer mentioned it was dry and tasteless, and I second that. The chamomile cheesecake was light and fluffy, and goes well with the tea. A little crowded on Monday 12:30pm, and service was slow. It was ~25-30 minutes to recieve everything, and it was a LOT of plates for our small 2-person table. I would have liked staggered servicing.

Name: Tea House Happa
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Honcho, 2 Chome−33−2 吉祥寺プティット村
Author: Kristi J
Review:
Dined in here :) super nice interior and cute place to take nice photos.

Serving is a little slow but foods are good!

Recommended for the experience

Name: Tea House Happa
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Honcho, 2 Chome−33−2 吉祥寺プティット村
Author: J M
Review: Such a wonderful little spot! We couldn't get a reservation, so we tried walking in and the lovely staff squeezed us in for a quick tea time before the next table arrived. The atmosphere felt magical, and the prices were very reasonable for such a unique café. Kind service & the food arrived within a reasonable time. Highly recommended!

Name: Tea House Happa
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Honcho, 2 Chome−33−2 吉祥寺プティット村
Author: Perry Merrity
Review: We actually enjoyed the tea and sangria we had. It was nice to stop, sip, and enjoy a break after spending a few hours walking around Kichijoji.

Name: 表千家 茶道教室 清宮 - Tea Ceremony Lessons
Address: 18-15 Higashikishicho, Urawa Ward, Saitama
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Coffee tea house SHŪ Premium Shibuya Station
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−3 共同ビル 1 ・ 2F
Author: Han Yu Tan
Review:
I tried some of the desserts here and they’re pretty good, though quite pricey.

The apple pie is good, it’s not overly sweet, and there’s some cold ice cream to pair with the warm pie. The strawberry shortcake is pretty good, quite sweet. The roll cake is decent as well.

Name: Coffee tea house SHŪ Premium Shibuya Station
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−3 共同ビル 1 ・ 2F
Author: Frodo821
Review: A (Japanese) traditional cafe with decent but relaxed atmosphere, that offer us neat drinks and desserts at reasonable price. Recommended.

Name: Coffee tea house SHŪ Premium Shibuya Station
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−3 共同ビル 1 ・ 2F
Author: AJ M
Review:
The fruit desserts tasted great, but seriously how long does it take to bring out already made and ready to be served cakes? It also took a while to flag down staff to take our order.
Service was atrocious. Discriminating and snobbish staff, especially the glasses wearing one called Nakayama. You should be ashamed to work in a posh looking restaurant.

Name: Coffee tea house SHŪ Premium Shibuya Station
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−3 共同ビル 1 ・ 2F
Author: Samson Ong
Review: Coffee is average (siphon), but their dessert is decent. Come here for the atmosphere and/or their dessert

Name: Coffee tea house SHŪ Premium Shibuya Station
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−3 共同ビル 1 ・ 2F
Author: Jason Hiak
Review:
Quiet place in the middle of busy Shibuya. Was expecting a normal restaurant as it wasn’t crowded. Had a pleasant surprise with the coffee standard and the Christmas cake. Even the decorative chocolate taste of good quality.

Pretty expensive for a cake and a coffee.

Name: Aoyama Flower Market Tea House Kichijoji
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Minamicho, 1 Chome−1−24 アトレ吉祥寺 B1
Author: Jing Yao
Review: I will highly recommend this cafe to any cafe connoisseur. Food's amazing ! Plating is out of the world, totally down to the details with so much effort and thought put into it; 10/10 for aesthetics. Ambience is on the point as well. Staff service is excellent. Keep up the good work ! (:

Name: Aoyama Flower Market Tea House Kichijoji
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Minamicho, 1 Chome−1−24 アトレ吉祥寺 B1
Author: O.H G.
Review:
Relaxing!!! This beautiful green house is filled with flowers. The place truly delights your senses.

👅Taste: Fresh salads, sanwiches, tea or coffee
👃Smell: Floral, depending on the flowers they choose weekly
👁️Sight: The walls and table sides are covered in greens and flowers, calm and soothing.
🖐️Touch: You can touch the flowers but be gentle with them.
👂Hearing: Not too quiet or noisy, you can have tea witb your friends or have a meeting at this place.

Name: Aoyama Flower Market Tea House Kichijoji
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Minamicho, 1 Chome−1−24 アトレ吉祥寺 B1
Author: Miffafia
Review:
It's a little difficult to find but once you head down to B1 you can easily spot it!
Very sophisticated and relaxing cafe! The interior is decked with flowers and the staff were friendly and attentive.
I highly recommend their french toast, I could eat a million of them idk how they get it sooo perfect! Budget around ￥4000 for A special tea (800￥), lunch(1000-1300￥) and dessert (around 1000￥). I tried the special Autumn dessert and it was so delicious, topped with various fruits and nuts, flaky and buttery without being too sweet.  Also served with ice cream (not vanilla)

Name: Aoyama Flower Market Tea House Kichijoji
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Minamicho, 1 Chome−1−24 アトレ吉祥寺 B1
Author: Perry Merrity
Review:
Beautiful atmosphere and thoughtful dishes. Coming from America, we're used to salt and sugar overpowering natural flavors, but here you get true floral and vegetable flavors in the dishes that make you appreciate things as God intended 😋

Also the hospitality of this location matches the one near Omotesando in Shibuya. Thankful they kindly welcomed us foreign guests!

Name: Aoyama Flower Market Tea House Kichijoji
Address: Musashino, Kichijoji Minamicho, 1 Chome−1−24 アトレ吉祥寺 B1
Author: Dennis Falt
Review: Tea House in Kichijoji is on the B-1 floor of the Atre mall (the same building as the train station). The atmosphere is quite unique, with plants and flowers all around, giving you the feeling of being in an outside garden. The staff is very friendly. They offer sandwiches, salads, bread and cheese, and desserts. Their drinks are quite delicious, and, of course, they offer various teas. For a pleasant, relaxing break in your day, give it a try.

Name: 紅楼夢茶坊 東京支店
Address: Bunkyo City, Mukogaoka, 1 Chome−12−4 ミセレニ白山 1階
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Zuu ＆ Hein Myanmar Tea House
Address: Shinjuku City, Takadanobaba, 3 Chome−12−6 エスパス日拓高田馬場２
Author: May Thazinhlaing
Review: Don’t feel shy to call the staff they will explain you everything

Name: Zuu ＆ Hein Myanmar Tea House
Address: Shinjuku City, Takadanobaba, 3 Chome−12−6 エスパス日拓高田馬場２
Author: Eimoh ThwelKhaing
Review: Yummy authentic Burmese food. Milk tea is a must order.

Name: Zuu ＆ Hein Myanmar Tea House
Address: Shinjuku City, Takadanobaba, 3 Chome−12−6 エスパス日拓高田馬場２
Author: Mari Costa
Review:
A newly opened Myanmar tea house about a 3-minute walk from Takadanobaba Station.

It was pretty busy when we went on a weeknight ~5pm and it took a while for dishes to come out. At least it’s fresh?

But, I’m sure they’ll find their groove and learn how to handle busy hours.

The staff is very nice, though! They provide a pot of hot tea (with refills) when you first sit down.

I was a little bit disappointed with the menu. It was limited to small “plates” but everything we tried was good: the sugar paratha, the udon dish (I forget the name!), the Myanmar milk tea, the sumosas, and the meat dumplings.

It is 3 floors. First is counter seats, second and third are table seats. It got pretty loud because of the background music (which was fantastic) and the customers, but it felt nice to be able to come to a restaurant and worry about making noise if you’re with a kid. Hahah

I would come back here again if I’m craving Myanmar snacks and tea. I hope they serve more Myanmar food in the future. :)

Name: Zuu ＆ Hein Myanmar Tea House
Address: Shinjuku City, Takadanobaba, 3 Chome−12−6 エスパス日拓高田馬場２
Author: Thazin Phyo
Review: Can have delicious Myanmar food. They offer a pot of Myanmar traditional green tea as free.

Name: Zuu ＆ Hein Myanmar Tea House
Address: Shinjuku City, Takadanobaba, 3 Chome−12−6 エスパス日拓高田馬場２
Author: KYAW ZAYA
Review: You can try the real taste of myanmar foods. Clean & nice environment.

Name: Tea House Takano
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−3, 地下1階 寿ビルディング
Author: Satoshi Fukuda
Review: If you feel like having a decent cup of tea with a reasonable price in Tokyo, this is the place. While many cafes and tea houses in Tokyo serve scones with strange fake cream, the Takano’s clotted cream is quite acceptable even though the amount of the cream they serve is not enough. You can double the cream with ¥100. Their scones are not excellent, but I would say they are alright.

Name: Tea House Takano
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−3, 地下1階 寿ビルディング
Author: レトロ旅東京Retro Tabi Tokyo
Review:
Long-established tea room in Jinbocho.
Takano was the first imported tea speciality store in Tokyo. The place built his reputation since 1974 over a very large selection of high quality black tea from abroad.
The scones are quite good and served hot with jam and a small amount of clotted cream. You should order an extra serving of cream (+100 yen) if you like richer British style scones.
The staff is very polite and the atmosphere is very quiet and calm so you can easily relax while enjoying your tea.
Prices are more than reasonable for such a quality.

Name: Tea House Takano
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−3, 地下1階 寿ビルディング
Author: Milly M
Review:
They have fine selection of tea. Though I'm definitely on coffee side, once in a while it' s nice to drink some tea. We ordered nuts milk tea, spice milk tea and chocolate cake, green tea chiffon cake. The cream with the cakes was absolutely delicious.
They've got free wifi and charges, but I've seen it only in one place.
The staff is nice and it's not crowded.
You can come alone and just read a book, study or work.

Name: Tea House Takano
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−3, 地下1階 寿ビルディング
Author: Ivy oiyi Leung
Review:
Had afternoon at this basement cafe. The high ceiling, wooden table tops and plants add an earthiness touch to this tea house.. but all the pink stained cushions seats look dirty and makes me feel uncomfortable sitting on it.

I ordered a fresh orange juice plus cheese toast. The cheese toast looks great and has cucumber slices, egg salad and a thin slice of cheese inside. Bread taste fresh, crunchy and its well balanced. Sadly the FRESH orange juice taste like they mixed it with ice sugar water.. flavor and color were very light and it was unnaturally sweet.

Guess it will be a place definitely for tea instead of other drinks.

Name: Tea House Takano
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Jinbocho, 1 Chome−3, 地下1階 寿ビルディング
Author: Daniel Langer
Review: A relaxed atmosphere and a good service. They have a fine selection of black teas and know how to brew them. The price is fair.

Name: LiLiBET
Address: 2 Chome-36-5 Ikejiri, Setagaya City
Author: Nishi E
Review:
Tastes good and quite room, so comfortable area for neighbors.
I brought my daughter for lunch.
Menu is not for kids because curry, sandwiches and salads.
I heard the curry is not spicy, but she didn’t choose it.
I chose the sandwich plate, it’s good taste.
The mustard has a sharp taste and marinade isn’t so sour, they’re good balance.
French fries as a side are good, I gave them to her, she liked them.
She ate a chocolate cake up with apple juice.
She said toppings as nuts were a little bitter and a piece of cake is sweet.
I didn’t try tea. This day it was hot, I chose iced coffee.
If I can, I’ll try other drinks.

Name: LiLiBET
Address: 2 Chome-36-5 Ikejiri, Setagaya City
Author: Vincent N
Review:
Very good atmosphere with nice staffs.
The salada plate is very good and the low carbs desserts makes you want to eat more.

Name: LiLiBET
Address: 2 Chome-36-5 Ikejiri, Setagaya City
Author: Patrick Garwood
Review: LiLiBET is a small British themed cafe about 10 minutes walk from Ikejiri Ohashi station. I really enjoyed the food although they didn't have many dishes to choose from so there isn't a lot of variety. They do offer a good selection of teas though. Overall, an interesting little cafe that's worth a visit.

Name: LiLiBET
Address: 2 Chome-36-5 Ikejiri, Setagaya City
Author: M R
Review: Love the salad! 🙂

Name: LiLiBET
Address: 2 Chome-36-5 Ikejiri, Setagaya City
Author: Nicolas Julliand
Review: Food is great, but the service is far from anything you can expect in a Japanese restaurant.

Name: Tohaku Chakan
Address: 13-9 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Wendy N
Review:
We had a really memorable time here.
From the ambience of the traditional architecture to the trees rustling in the wind, you truly can’t help but feel relaxed and at peace here.

It seems unassuming but the mango mascarpone kakigori is one not to miss, especially in this warm autumn. This was the best matcha I’ve had all trip also! I’m grateful for the alternative almond milk option, it’s very thoughtful. The menu has a decent range from sweets to savoury for anyone after something other than dessert.

I hope everyone who comes here has the luck of meeting Mayuko! The most friendly, wholesome interaction we’ve had. Our Japanese was lacking but it wasn’t a problem as Mayuko had excellent English. Thank you Mayuko for taking the time to entertain 2 foreigners 🥰

Name: Tohaku Chakan
Address: 13-9 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Florine Clowez
Review: First thing first : this traditional Japanese teahouse is INSIDE THE MUSEUM GARDENS, meaning you need a ticket to reach it. This is the perfect spot to relax quietly after touring the museum exhibits. Please be respectful if you take pictures and videos (no livestream, add your voiceover later). They speak lovely English and accept credit card.

Name: Tohaku Chakan
Address: 13-9 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Veronica Rohach
Review: The staff was so polite and explained e everything in detail. The teahouse feels like a traditional teahouse. We had the best shaved ice I've ever had!

Name: Tohaku Chakan
Address: 13-9 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Robert Cota
Review:
We visited the tokyo national museum early in the morning to beat the crowds. It was soo beautiful! This lovely 300+ year old tea house felt serene to dine at while enjoying the garden views 🍵 🌸
We Felt like we were in ancient Japan for a minute. They include some traditional cuisine as you sit out side on a small patio on Tatami Mats.

The tea house itself can be found outside in the main garden area behind/next to the lotus pond. The garden grounds are a cute stroll through with a historic replica village & sculptures scattered around.

The food itself was Not too expensive, the quality was great overall!
The experience is well worth a short stop if you get a chance while visiting the museum or ueno ✌

The Tokyo national museum was amazing to see an experience all the artwork, their kimonos were crafted so elegantly. Its great to learn a little more of Japan's rich history,  We collected our stamps & made a stamp postcard to take home in the main hall of the museum!

The "ueno de bueno" was a fun salsa event randomly outside in the Ueno park area as well.

There was so much to do in this one area, we honestly didn't have to leave the park all day

Name: Tohaku Chakan
Address: 13-9 Uenokoen, Taito City
Author: Motoki Jimmy Hattori
Review:
Whenever I think of Ueno, I picture a park bustling with tourists, navigating through crowds—not exactly my favorite place to be. Until now. I’ve discovered a hidden gem for occasional “quasi” retreats.

Tucked behind the National Museum, you’ll find a beautiful Japanese tea house by the pond. Both the exterior and interior exude authenticity, with wall-to-wall tatami mats.

Despite being full, the space felt serene and uncluttered. Personal spaces were respected, with ample distance between tables to ensure privacy.

As a tea house, they naturally offer a variety of matcha selections. Surprisingly, they also have sake, wine, and beer options.

We opted for the matcha and wagashi (Japanese confectionery) set for ¥2200. For newcomers, it’s worth asking the staff about matcha-drinking etiquette. You can simply gulp it from the bowl, or opt for a more ceremonial experience with specific procedures.

If you’re fortunate enough to get a window seat, consider yourself lucky—it offers the best view of the Japanese garden. Slowly sipping matcha while gazing out at the serene scenery—it’s a perfect Zen moment, truly therapeutic.

Name: Brew Tea Co. { ブリュー ティー カンパニー }
Address: Minato City, Minamiaoyama, 6 Chome−6−21 グロービル青山 3F
Author: S. A.
Review:
Lovely tea, great people!
Best tea combination and creation. Amazing kinds of speciality teas that change according to month and season. The community of tea lovers are picked by up and a nice postcard wall is there to showcase the community.

Keep up the great work ❤️the tea and staff!!

Name: Brew Tea Co. { ブリュー ティー カンパニー }
Address: Minato City, Minamiaoyama, 6 Chome−6−21 グロービル青山 3F
Author: Daniel K
Review: Cool little tea store. Cute store.

Name: Brew Tea Co. { ブリュー ティー カンパニー }
Address: Minato City, Minamiaoyama, 6 Chome−6−21 グロービル青山 3F
Author: Takétoshi NAKAMURA
Review:
Cost    ＄
Food   ★★★★★
Decor  ❤❤❤

Name: Brew Tea Co. { ブリュー ティー カンパニー }
Address: Minato City, Minamiaoyama, 6 Chome−6−21 グロービル青山 3F
Author: MIWA MITAMURA
Review: I learned about their existence when a scone shop in Kobe sold this tea, and I bought it at a department store event in Osaka. I came to the store for the first time when I was in Tokyo. It's a bit of a walk from the station, but it's fun just looking at the rows of teas in pop-up packaging. You can also take out at the store, but I took a break and cooled down. Now that I've moved to Tokyo, I'll be stopping by again when I'm out for a walk!

Name: Brew Tea Co. { ブリュー ティー カンパニー }
Address: Minato City, Minamiaoyama, 6 Chome−6−21 グロービル青山 3F
Author: YY
Review:
“We want you to take your time while we brew your tea.”
So you can make each one carefully.

It has a strong aroma, the tea leaves have been removed neatly, and the aftertaste is very refreshing.

Both were delicious 😉

Fruit punch 330 yen
Jasmine green 330 yen

It's a British company with only one store in the world, in Omotesando. This is a store that I hope will continue for a long time.

Name: Hanabusa Chaya
Address: 2 Chome-14-1 Uenosakuragi, Taito City
Author: Kirby Martino
Review: Great tea and sweets in a beautiful room, made by a very nice woman. I will definitely visit the next time I’m in Tokyo, highly reccomend

Name: Hanabusa Chaya
Address: 2 Chome-14-1 Uenosakuragi, Taito City
Author: CF
Review: At the end of this almost secret little alley we were able to find a super cute mini tea room, with very charming service of tea, juice and little Japanese pastries. Very refreshing after hours wandering around old Tokyo :)

Name: Goku Tea House
Address: 130 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama
Author: Jacqueline Axelsson
Review:
Truly a treasure to come upon. The cafe was right above the store. I ordered the 8 seasons and it was amazing.
The scene was romantic and cozy. The waitress was extremely sweet and spoke amazing English!!
I recommend to anyone who enjoys a good cup of tea.

Name: Goku Tea House
Address: 130 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama
Author: やすみたび
Review:
Chinese tea specialty store.
The first floor sells tea and pottery, and the second floor is a cafe where you can have tea and sweets.
Steamed meat buns are also on sale, so how about buying them as souvenirs?

Name: Goku Tea House
Address: 130 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama
Author: Çağıl Harmandar
Review: Great tea place, the waitress was so helpful at translating the japanese menu for us. We had the "8 treasures tea" and shared it two people with the snack set menu of kinokoman and raisins, mango and pineapple cake. I recommend any tea lover to check out this place.

Name: Goku Tea House
Address: 130 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama
Author: Spring Learning
Review:
Very friendly staff, great products and authenticity at it’s core. When I asked the difficult questions regarding pu-erh tea, and expert presented herself to ensure I wasn’t lead astray. I ended up leaving with near top quality pu-erh tea which I am thoroughly enjoying right now. Indeed a great tea place.

Oh yeah, and only a few meters from the beautiful location posted in these pics.

Name: Goku Tea House
Address: 130 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama
Author: Kuri
Review: This is an authentic Chinese tea house where you can buy variety of Chinese tea and some tea things. And also, there is a cafe on the second floor. Orange crepe and Chinese steamed bun are tasty!

Name: Tea House Rosemary
Address: 3 Chome-70-4 Kosugimachi, Nakahara Ward, Kawasaki
Author: Arustan
Review:
Great cozy and relaxing place. Great chill out atmosphere with delicious cake and tea and friendly owner, everything with a vegan/bio philosophy.

The cakes are baked by the owner herself, so you might find out that not all the cakes in the menu are always available.
