I feel like this one is better in terms of price.

Name: ナインティー赤羽店 NINETEA(九茶) Akabane
Address: 1 Chome-20-5 Akabaneminami, Kita City
Author: 橘rain
Review:
I saw the Costa Coffee sign and went in.

The main drinks are standard drinks such as tapioca milk tea and egg tarts. There was also an eat-in space.

Please note that electronic money took a while (sorry if it has been improved)

Name: Cha x Ten Boba
Address: Arakawa City, Nishinippori, 2 Chome−26−10 メゾン丸十 1Ｆ
Author: Jasmine Usui
Review:
Great bubble tea place.
I always order the standard milk tea. The normal sugar level is not very sweet so if you like sweetness I recommend adding more sugar.

Name: Cha x Ten Boba
Address: Arakawa City, Nishinippori, 2 Chome−26−10 メゾン丸十 1Ｆ
Author: Abibia Berri
Review: It is a little cozy place with good prizes, delicious bubble tea, many different options, and costumization. Their secret grass jelly recipe is amazing. They know a bit of english and accept card.

Name: Cha x Ten Boba
Address: Arakawa City, Nishinippori, 2 Chome−26−10 メゾン丸十 1Ｆ
Author: Trisha P
Review: Got the brown sugar bubble tea. Fast service and delicious! Great option when you’re in the Nippori area. :)

Name: Cha x Ten Boba
Address: Arakawa City, Nishinippori, 2 Chome−26−10 メゾン丸十 1Ｆ
Author: E T
Review: The bubble is really chewy, gret texture. Milk tea is also very nice.

Name: Cha x Ten Boba
Address: Arakawa City, Nishinippori, 2 Chome−26−10 メゾン丸十 1Ｆ
Author: Deb C
Review: One of the better milk teas in japan. At least it tasted original and not accustomed to japanese taste. They also sell some taiwan snacks like 卤蛋 and pineapple tarts!

Name: MoMo茶 台湾タピオカ專門店
Address: エバークリーン, 松永ビル 1F, 3 Chome-54番5号 Eifuku, Suginami City
Author: Juan Diego Romero
Review: I got the large brown sugar oolong milk tea for 600 yen, good price for a large! Clean and good vibe inside :)

Name: MoMo茶 台湾タピオカ專門店
Address: エバークリーン, 松永ビル 1F, 3 Chome-54番5号 Eifuku, Suginami City
Author: Ryo Ishikawa
Review: I was wandering around as a cafe refugee when I found our shop in the evening, and since the shop was empty, I ordered a 700ml tapioca milk tea (600 yen including tax, 500 ml is 500 yen) and soaked it up. The owner was warm and allowed me to stay for a long time, so I was able to complete my tasks a lot. I was happy. Thank you to the shopkeeper.

Name: MoMo茶 台湾タピオカ專門店
Address: エバークリーン, 松永ビル 1F, 3 Chome-54番5号 Eifuku, Suginami City
Author: Chika Mofu
Review:
I was disappointed that the review I had written disappeared before it was posted😮‍💨I will write again...

It is located in the Nishi-Eifuku shopping street.
I've been curious about this place for a while, but since I only go to Nishi-Eifuku to see the dentist, I was finally able to visit.
Actually, what I'm looking for is not tapioca, but fried chicken! Taiwanese fried chicken is big, crispy, and delicious!
Out of the 4 types of black pepper, Mala, cheese, and cilantro, today I chose black pepper and Mala.

Furthermore, surprise! ! !
Oh my God, Taiwanese 🇹🇼 noodle strings 🍜! ! ! ! ! !
happy! ! ! ! ! ! Because I love it ❤️

I'm happy to have unexpectedly come across noodle strings.
There are very few shops that serve noodle noodles.

I made it with spicy beef noodle strings.

It's amazing, you can take everything home.
excited!

This is a popular store where Uber keeps coming to pick up your order.
It seemed like the owner was busy with just one surgery!
I wanted to help you.

Wait 9 minutes! So I went to the coin parking lot across from the station to get my car, and 10 minutes later I was waiting in front of the store.

I wanted to eat some piping hot fried chicken, but since I was undergoing dental treatment, I wasn't allowed to eat it for 30 minutes...
45 minute drive home.
The delicious smell of fried chicken fills the car!

I ate it as soon as I got home, but
I forgot to take a photo of the noodle strings! ! !
The noodles may have gotten a little thicker because they probably absorbed the liquid, but the taste was delicious and the spices were perfect. It's my favorite! !

Fried chicken is also crispy! !
It's huge.
It's belly bread.

Take care to drink the tapioca later.

It was delicious anyway.
Taiwan 🇹🇼 I feel like Taiwan.

Next time I would like to eat slowly at a restaurant.
Let's go before dental treatment.

Thank you for your delicious meal.

Name: MoMo茶 台湾タピオカ專門店
Address: エバークリーン, 松永ビル 1F, 3 Chome-54番5号 Eifuku, Suginami City
Author: N Dna
Review:
I thought it was a trendy tapioca specialty shop, but there were various side menus.
The chicken in the photo is just delicious! !
If I'm nearby, I'd definitely like to eat there again.
You can't see the tables from outside, so it's a bit difficult to enter for the first time, but I was able to slowly choose my menu at the counter and eat quietly in the back.

Name: MoMo茶 台湾タピオカ專門店
Address: エバークリーン, 松永ビル 1F, 3 Chome-54番5号 Eifuku, Suginami City
Author: yuka yuka
Review: It's delicious! Lots of quantity. The very friendly staff will carefully explain the menu to you. The store is surprisingly deep and has plenty of seats. You can take it home or drink it inside.

Name: CoCo-Toka Fresh Tea & Juice (Takadanobaba)
Address: 3 Chome-4-19 Takadanobaba, Shinjuku City
Author: T S
Review: During summer time, I feel like drinking sour juice. Here is the best place which they offer passion fruits juice, so nice flavor.

Name: CoCo-Toka Fresh Tea & Juice (Takadanobaba)
Address: 3 Chome-4-19 Takadanobaba, Shinjuku City
Author: Jupiter Nguyễn
Review:
We got half price bubba which is quite cheap, but still the taste wasnt as good as I expected. Or may be I have different fetish :))
I tried the brown sugar latte here and still prefer the brown sugar soy milk of Pearl Lady :/

Name: CoCo-Toka Fresh Tea & Juice (Takadanobaba)
Address: 3 Chome-4-19 Takadanobaba, Shinjuku City
Author: Neslie
Review:
Fast service
Attentive staffs
Delicious Drinks

A recommended place to those who love to try out different milk tea flavors

Name: CoCo-Toka Fresh Tea & Juice (Takadanobaba)
Address: 3 Chome-4-19 Takadanobaba, Shinjuku City
Author: OISHI FOOD BY TASi
Review:
Very
good customer service

Name: CoCo-Toka Fresh Tea & Juice (Takadanobaba)
Address: 3 Chome-4-19 Takadanobaba, Shinjuku City
Author: joy jolie
Review: No seat. Staff was not professional ( always forgot what I told and asked me again and again the same thing even I spoke Japanese to them normally)

Name: 好茶 大門店
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−1−1 酒井ビル ２Ｆ
Author: Alton Cao
Review: Currently my favorite bubble tea in Tokyo! Very strong tea flavor and the 50% sugar level is the perfect sweetness for me (I usually order 微糖). The location is great and the staff were also nice. I hope they do well so the store continues to stay in business since most of the best bubble tea stores in Tokyo have closed 😮‍💨

Name: 好茶 大門店
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−1−1 酒井ビル ２Ｆ
Author: Alexander Wong (Shuvi)
Review:
Came at 9pm, closed for no reason. No signs. Hours on front clearly says close at 11pm.
If you are going to say you are open, you should be open.
Reading other reviews, not the first time this has happened.

Name: 好茶 大門店
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−1−1 酒井ビル ２Ｆ
Author: Helen Al-kazaz
Review: Great Bubble tea, tastes good and beautiful presentation and added bonus of seeing Tokyo Tower if you sit at the window. It's on the second floor. Give it a try.

Name: 好茶 大門店
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−1−1 酒井ビル ２Ｆ
Author: naemari
Review: Came at 6pm on a Monday, building was dark, ringed twice and no one opened. There was no sign it would be closed today, so I walked all the way for nothing. Really disappointed.

Name: 好茶 大門店
Address: Minato City, Shibadaimon, 2 Chome−1−1 酒井ビル ２Ｆ
Author: Michael Cao
Review: Two friends and I found this place after a long night of exploring Tokyo. Absolutely fantastic boba and great service. Would highly recommend to anyone looking for awesome boba in the area.

Name: Cha Xiang
Address: 1 Chome-20-8 Kamimeguro, Meguro City
Author: 吉田栗栖
Review: Was craving for tapioca so we stopped by despite the bad reviews. Turns out it was actually pretty yummy! You just need to know how to customise your drink properly. Also the bubbles were very chewy so we really liked that as well (if you don't like softer chewy bubbles maybe this is not for you). We had cheese foam tea (Jasmine & Four Season Spring Oolong) with tapioca, less sweetness and less ice. Also they have a row of benches across the shop to enjoy your drink 🥤

Name: Cha Xiang
Address: 1 Chome-20-8 Kamimeguro, Meguro City
Author: Alicia Kiedrowski
Review: Enjoying the taste and texture of tapioca is specific to a person’s own preference. Personally I loved this larger size more “expanded” tapioca - I am from Canada and many places at home have it like this. So don’t let other reviews here scare you away. 660¥ for the delicious drink I had was very worth the price and enjoyable. Also the clerk is kind, and put my straw in my cup for me in a very sanitary way, without touching it. I wish I were visiting longer to the area, I would come back! ☺️

Name: Cha Xiang
Address: 1 Chome-20-8 Kamimeguro, Meguro City
Author: Elias Picciotto
Review: I went at 7pm to get a boba. The place says it closes at 10pm. I arrived and the rude guy told me we’re closed, I told him that why is he telling me is closed if in google it says you close at 10pm, so he came to the idea to say that everything is sold out. But he was very very rude and diss polite.

Name: Cha Xiang
Address: 1 Chome-20-8 Kamimeguro, Meguro City
Author: Cynthia L
Review: Disgusting mushy bubbles and SUPER sweet even though I ordered 0 sugar.  You have to make the bubbles fresh daily or they turn mushy.  These were bubbles stored in the fridge and reheated!!  Gross gross gross and overpriced!!!  DO NOT GO HERE

Name: Cha Xiang
Address: 1 Chome-20-8 Kamimeguro, Meguro City
Author: Jeffrey Frank
Review: Our only unfortunate food experience in Tokyo.  Boba was "stored" in a rice cooker?  Taste was off.

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: Shibuya City, Sendagaya, 5 Chome−24−55 NEWoMan 新宿 ２Ｆ
Author: Brandon Wu
Review: Good spot for specialty coffee in Shinjuku Station. The filter selection is good value. Good breakfast, waffles there is really nice.

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: Shibuya City, Sendagaya, 5 Chome−24−55 NEWoMan 新宿 ２Ｆ
Author: Lee Vivian
Review: we made a detour here due to IG post pop-up. we ordered two pour-over. street level and 1950. both are well and smooth flavored. not typical deep roasted coffee in japan. the mikan flavor is dominant with street level. nice coffee. seats were a bit crowded. this spot is located at the station building. very busy outside and inside the cafe. cannot sit for too long.

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: Shibuya City, Sendagaya, 5 Chome−24−55 NEWoMan 新宿 ２Ｆ
Author: George Curry
Review: For me enjoying a great cup of coffee involves a number of things. The coffee, the atmosphere and if in a sit in cafe, the service. At this place I felt the service was spot on. Informative about the coffee and memorably friendly which is why I'm giving a 5. However, the atmosphere did feel somewhat claustrophobic but I must take in consideration that it is located at a hectic train station! As for the coffee itself, solid.  The Ethiopian cold brew in particular was brewed perfectly!

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: Shibuya City, Sendagaya, 5 Chome−24−55 NEWoMan 新宿 ２Ｆ
Author: Ming-Uei Hung
Review:
It’s an amazing coffee place in Shinjuku station (outside the gate). The location was nice and convenient.
My wife and I came here for breakfast. We ordered a plain waffle, a grilled cheese, and two cups of latte.
The lattes were great. They already looked beautiful before we tasted them. The flavor was not too bitter and not too sour. The hint of sweetness brought by the milk and foam balanced the whole cup of the latter to the perfect point.
The grilled cheese was extraordinarily delicious. It was probably the best grilled cheese I’ve ever had. First, they are freshly made, and the presentation was good. When you gave a bite, the bread was so crunchy. I assumed that there was a layer of sugar coated on the surface, and it matched perfectly well with the cheese. The sweetness was just right on the spot. The melted cheese and the sweet bread mixed together and elevated the whole grilled cheese to a new level. I highly recommend everyone to try it! (It also came with pickles!)

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: Shibuya City, Sendagaya, 5 Chome−24−55 NEWoMan 新宿 ２Ｆ
Author: Vinesh Chathurr
Review:
It is on level 2 of the mall. It’s more towards the far side from the station. Little hard to find. Decent amount of seating available.
Coffee is average (as per Australian standards). Service is fast and the staff are super friendly.

Name: Streamer Coffee Company Akasaka
Address: 3 Chome-6-14 Akasaka, Minato City
Author: Alex Emexezidis
Review:
Good environment to have a coffee in at Akasaka. The coffee (espresso) is more on the darker roast side, which isn't exactly my taste, although it makes the latte art look good (more contrast).

The environment is pretty calm inside, although it was full when I visited - but keep in mind that everybody was stuck to a computer or phone screen. So, it's also probably a place where people like to work from.

The price was also a bit on the higher side.

Name: Streamer Coffee Company Akasaka
Address: 3 Chome-6-14 Akasaka, Minato City
Author: Peter
Review: Wow so much to fit in this review. The coconut island latte is a perfect if you like your coffee on the stronger side. I got the chicken sandwich but it’s missing something to make it more than just an average sandwich. The chocolate cinnamon roll was amazing I’ve personally never tried anything like it. The down side to eating in the shop was there was a man picking at his athletes foot the entire time there. Idk if the employees saw and batted an eye. But he did leave his cheese (dry feet crumbs) on the seat for others to take.

Name: Streamer Coffee Company Akasaka
Address: 3 Chome-6-14 Akasaka, Minato City
Author: Bianca Ritorto
Review:
Coffee is amazing! Chocolate donut was good, and the ham and cheese sandwich also very good but can get same quality at 7 eleven too. The lemon loaf we tried one day was not very good.
Would come here for the coffee maybe skip the food!

Name: Streamer Coffee Company Akasaka
Address: 3 Chome-6-14 Akasaka, Minato City
Author: kazue
Review: the tiramisu shaved ice i had was very tasty, it wasn't too sweet which i really liked. it's a nice and quiet spot to relax at. the prices were a bit higher than you would expect from a cafe in tokyo but i personally think it was worth it, i would come back for sure!

Name: Streamer Coffee Company Akasaka
Address: 3 Chome-6-14 Akasaka, Minato City
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: I’ve been to several Streamer Coffee shops throughout Tokyo and they have always had a solid cup of coffee. I really think the lattes are very good here, so recommend you trying any variety they may be serving at the time. The service is always fast and friendly, and the atmosphere is consistently laidback. A good option when you’re roaming around and want a nice coffee break.

Name: OBSCURA COFFEE ROASTERS
Address: B1F, 2 Chome-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya City
Author: E Meerbach
Review:
One of the top 10 lattes I’ve ever had in my life!
They have incredibly flavorful espresso that was perfectly balanced with their choice of oat milk. Even after the ice melted, the drink was still perfect and flavorful, which is a big marker of a great latte for me.

the person at the counter was very kind, despite my not being able to speak Japanese! They had a ton of coffee that, if the espresso is any indication, is all probably very good as well. The shop is open until later than most cafes, and they also offered decaf espresso, which are both much appreciated!

For anyone confused about the location, it’s located in the food hall under Shibuya station!

Name: OBSCURA COFFEE ROASTERS
Address: B1F, 2 Chome-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya City
Author: Gino Araullo
Review:
I got lost trying to find Obscura the Obscure cafe, going here from Shibuya Sky (Tokyu Foodshow Extra)  thankfully after an extensive search I found out it was right OUTSIDE beside Tokyo Foodshow 2 (not inside the Foodshow).

Thankfully my search of Obscura yielded me a good selection of coffee I could take home to the Philippines. If you get lost here, it's outside beside Tokyu Foodshow 2 at the B1f level, you can ask how to get there from info kiosks. It's also pretty near the Hachiko entrance so it's enter the basement to go from there.

The staff were super friendly and accommodating despite some limited English speaking ability, and there's also a volume discount which you get by purchasing 2 or more packs of coffee.

Name: OBSCURA COFFEE ROASTERS
Address: B1F, 2 Chome-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya City
Author: Prawee “Mim” Nonthapun
Review:
I recommend going to shibuya scramble and then taking the 7a exit into b1 to find the coffee shop. Been lost like twice and that’s me being an idiot.

Really great shop with great coffee. Best latte I’ve ever had. Don’t get oat milk as it will dilute the amazing ness of coffee - go for normal milk and take lactaid if you have to.

I bought a ton of beans home. Super excited!!

Name: OBSCURA COFFEE ROASTERS
Address: B1F, 2 Chome-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya City
Author: Junyoung Park
Review:
“Great cafe right in front of the Tokyu food show”

Some people get lost on their way to the shop. Just look for the Tokyu food show and its on the outer side of the area.

Kind owner gives you  explanation in the menu. Even if she does not speak english I could feel her strong passion.

The coffee is brewed, not poured over or amercian style espresso shot diluted one. Great taste.

FYI no seats available.

Name: OBSCURA COFFEE ROASTERS
Address: B1F, 2 Chome-2-1 Dogenzaka, Shibuya City
Author: Luca Perrino
Review: Cannot say about other kind of coffee they sell but they have no knowledge about what is real espresso. Making a good Espresso is not only about to use high quality coffee beans (as I am sure they use) but is a complex mixture of techniques,  experience, materials, passion and much more. Probably they doesn't need to put efforts in trying to make a mediocre (yet expensive) espresso, better they stick on high quality drip or American coffees.

Name: THE ROASTERY BY NOZY COFFEE
Address: Reveur Jingumae, 5 Chome-17-13 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: Dalton Fox
Review: The coffee and service here was outstanding. The staff was extremely kind and patient even though we speak very little Japanese. The coffee itself was masterfully roasted and the NY Rings were some of the best we've had. The soft served ice cream was also a wonderful treat. The smell of fresh coffee actively roasting creates a sense of euphoria. We'll definitely visit again if we're ever walking through this neighborhood.

Name: THE ROASTERY BY NOZY COFFEE
Address: Reveur Jingumae, 5 Chome-17-13 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: Devildman
Review:
Tried the delicious Cup of Excellence 2022 winner, an Ecuadorian brew, as straight espresso.
Absolutely delicious.
The Staff were extremely knowledgeable, attentive and welcoming.
Ambience was lively, yet warm and fun.
Highly recommend when you are in Shibuya, Tokyo.

Name: THE ROASTERY BY NOZY COFFEE
Address: Reveur Jingumae, 5 Chome-17-13 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: Beth Carlin
Review: The espresso soft serve is my favorite in all of Tokyo. I love grabbing a cone and then hunkering down near the roaster in the back. It’s a cozy place with only windows at the front which I prefer for a dark cozy vibe. The free chilled water as you visit is also nice. When the weather is nice the outdoor area is also nice to people watch in.

Name: THE ROASTERY BY NOZY COFFEE
Address: Reveur Jingumae, 5 Chome-17-13 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: Barry
Review:
MAR 2024

Ordered a café latte using their award winning Platanares coffee beans and the espresso soft serve. The coffee was pretty strong and smooth with a sweet note, very good but not sure if it is much better than their other bean which cost half the price. The soft serve was creamy with a tasty coffee flavor but wished it was a bit stronger. Service was good and the staffs/baristas were polite.

Name: THE ROASTERY BY NOZY COFFEE
Address: Reveur Jingumae, 5 Chome-17-13 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: Thanh Hoang
Review: Very trendy coffee shop, the Costa Rica beans was just ok. Pricey. Coffee + ice cream ¥1550

Name: VAULT COFFEE
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 4 Chome−5−1 3階
Author: Pete Bhakins
Review: Hidden coffee shop in the small building Akihabara, the cinnamon toast is surprisingly crispy and smell good! The topping cream is great, it’s a cream! I knw what I mean, and wide selection of coffee beans for a drip cup! But expected abit lighter roast cup! Overall a good place to sit and chill in the area.

Name: VAULT COFFEE
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 4 Chome−5−1 3階
Author: Edman Hong
Review: The food and coffee are great, the staff is nice and they speak a some english so that helped us a lot. The location is cozy and homey with jazz playing in the background. Some of the best coffee I’ve had here!

Name: VAULT COFFEE
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 4 Chome−5−1 3階
Author: Drew Carroll
Review:
Good coffee, with a pretty large lineup of single origin options. They also offer a surprisingly large menu of toasts, pastas, and sweets. Along with other drinks like tea and ice cream floats.
Service was pretty slow given that we were one of three parties there at the time, but we weren't in a rush.
I particularly enjoyed the cheese toast with mushroom, the addition of lemon on top was surprisingly tasty!

Name: VAULT COFFEE
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 4 Chome−5−1 3階
Author: Lévannah Rauzada
Review:
The ambiance was very cozy and relaxing, but the most amazing part was the food!
We ordered a cheese toast, ogura toast, cafe au lait and a fizzy lemonade, and they were all extremely delicious!
I greatly recommend this cafe!

Name: VAULT COFFEE
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 4 Chome−5−1 3階
Author: Jack Jaegar
Review:
Great kissaten-style cafe tucked away in Akihabara. It has a huge counter where you can sit and watch the baristas prepare the massive toasts and coffee.

The cinnamon toast was fantastic; crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. I also had the King Arthur blend drip coffee which was great too. My friend had the almond cafe au lait made with soy milk - very very delicious!

Don’t be in a rush when you come though. There were only two staff working so it took a long time for the order to come. This was totally fine with me but something to keep in mind if your time is limited. The opening hours on Google are also not always accurate. It said 12pm when we left the house. Then it said 12:40 when we got to Akiba. It didn’t actually open until after 1pm. So don’t go early!

Name: TULLY'S COFFEE & TEA Lumine Yurakucho Shop
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 2 Chome−5−1 ルミネ有楽町 8F
Author: Sholes K
Review:
Very big Cafe
Can seat around 50 people I think.
Ordered the new chocolista which was good but not great.
Very crowded in holidays and weekends but can still get a seat.
They have lots of variety of ground coffee packs and also whole coffee beans
They have gift bags too, but is quite expensive like 6,000 yen
Overall a good cafe if you’re in that area!

Name: TULLY'S COFFEE & TEA Lumine Yurakucho Shop
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 2 Chome−5−1 ルミネ有楽町 8F
Author: Victor Irie
Review: Great place but crowded. I would recommend hot Caramel Houjicha Latte.

Name: TULLY'S COFFEE & TEA Lumine Yurakucho Shop
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 2 Chome−5−1 ルミネ有楽町 8F
Author: Denis Y.
Review: Great wide and quiet place, not many customers during lunch time. Many seats, all is convenient and clean. Extremely polite and friendly staff. Unfortunately not everything out of the Tully's menu is presented here, but everything is clean and fresh. Recommend!

Name: TULLY'S COFFEE & TEA Lumine Yurakucho Shop
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 2 Chome−5−1 ルミネ有楽町 8F
Author: s m
Review: tea good

Name: TULLY'S COFFEE & TEA Lumine Yurakucho Shop
Address: Chiyoda City, Yurakucho, 2 Chome−5−1 ルミネ有楽町 8F
Author: paru chan
Review:
This is one of nine concept stores called &TEA, which is a new type of tea business, and has opened mainly in major cities.
I stopped by to get a caramel latte without doing any research.
I didn't have any, so I took out a cafe latte.
The tea menu is amazing and the coffee menu is very limited, so if you're looking for coffee, it's best to go to a regular store.

The inside of this store had a very open feel, and although it was a weekday evening, it was almost full. It wasn't noisy, so I thought I could take it easy ◎ Next time I want to drink tea, I'll stop by lol

Name: Roasted COFFEE LABORATORY Shibuya Jinnnan store
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−6−3 フラッグ １Ｆ
Author: Celina Sophie
Review:
If you‘re here to eat delicious pancakes.. don‘t come. I had the worst pancakes ever there. Just based on the flavor of the vanilla ice cream and the lemon butter - delicious. But the pancakes and batter itself were hard, crumbly and extremely dry, like old bread -just look at my pictures.
However, the fish sandwich and the Matcha Latte Espresso were very very good! If you grab a sandwich and a coffee, this place is a good choice. If you want pancakes… do NOT come here. The pancakes would be good if they were made fresh but served like old crumbly cake - they are bad and extremely overpriced.

Name: Roasted COFFEE LABORATORY Shibuya Jinnnan store
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−6−3 フラッグ １Ｆ
Author: Christian Josef
Review: Coffee was great along with the food and service, atmosphere and vibe is very comfortable with melo music playing in the background. Great place to study or meet up with some friends!

Name: Roasted COFFEE LABORATORY Shibuya Jinnnan store
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−6−3 フラッグ １Ｆ
Author: Yelyzaveta Shevchenko
Review:
I really liked the atmosphere in this place, the nice staff, the modern approach.
But my pancakes were just burnt, I understand that the place is more focused on coffee, but if you put something on the menu, you need to bring it to perfection. There was no airiness or tenderness in these pancakes at all. The coffee was really incredible, but I really wanted to have a delicious breakfast, and unfortunately it didn't work out.🥲

Name: Roasted COFFEE LABORATORY Shibuya Jinnnan store
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−6−3 フラッグ １Ｆ
Author: Ariana Santacruz
Review: Cute little coffee shop :) I got the milk tea but it was way too milky that it just ended up tasting like milk 😅 the biscuit pancakes were good!! They were a little dry but maybe since they are biscuit pancakes that’s why but I was expecting a different texture. Staff was very nice and restaurant was clean.

Name: Roasted COFFEE LABORATORY Shibuya Jinnnan store
Address: Shibuya City, Jinnan, 1 Chome−6−3 フラッグ １Ｆ
Author: Sebastian V.
Review: Quite busy but expected on Sunday midday, their cold brew is pretty good, slightly louder than ideal so would not recommend for studying or working

Name: Oslo Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Kabukicho, 1 Chome−2−2, Shinjuku Subnade Building, 1 号
Author: Bryan Chan
Review:
The walnut pancake is memorable. The texture is soft.

Their pudding is also very nice.

The attendants are very friendly and diligent. The place is nice and clean.

Name: Oslo Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Kabukicho, 1 Chome−2−2, Shinjuku Subnade Building, 1 号
Author: すみえ墨絵
Review: The restaurant serves delicious banana caramel pancakes paired with excellent coffee. The flavors are outstanding, making for a delightful breakfast. However, the ambiance is a bit dark, which can make it hard to enjoy the meal fully. Overall, a great spot despite the lighting issue.

Name: Oslo Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Kabukicho, 1 Chome−2−2, Shinjuku Subnade Building, 1 号
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: Nice little cafe in the underground Subnade complex. It has Northern European roots, with desserts and a few pasta dishes. The drip coffee was good, as I opted for the iced version (had to since Tokyo is stupid hot in July and August. I tried the Ostkaka, which is a Swedish cheesecake, and it was different but tasty. Service was fast and friendly.

Name: Oslo Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Kabukicho, 1 Chome−2−2, Shinjuku Subnade Building, 1 号
Author: Gator of Wall Street
Review: We were looking for a place for coffee and stumbled upon this cafe. The cafe was full when we visited but we waited around 10-15 minutes and we were given a table. Ordered the coffee, apple pie and a pasta. The taste was great for all our orders and the prices were very reasonable.

Name: Oslo Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Kabukicho, 1 Chome−2−2, Shinjuku Subnade Building, 1 号
Author: Kamil Deen
Review: Excellent food, and the coffee is not bad. Service was super, and I love the decor and overall vibe. Well worth a lunch or coffee here

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: 5 Chome-16-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: Serene Andrade
Review: I’ve been to Verve a few times now, and it’s a solid coffee spot. The coffee is really good, though a bit on the pricey side. The location is nice, and there’s plenty of seating, which makes it a great place to hang out or get some work done. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in the area!

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: 5 Chome-16-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: Kaman Kwok
Review: A wonderful coffee shop that offers good coffee, wonderful ambiance and attentive service. The barista gives us an English menu before ordering and the latte is made fresh and timely. There are inside and outside seatings. It’s quite a breezy evening to enjoy coffee with the tables and chairs at store front.

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: 5 Chome-16-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: Yiannis Strouthos
Review: The coffee and food were pretty decent. The armosphere here is very cosey and homey while at the same time modern. The staff is nice and helpful. It's incredible how hard it was to find decaf coffee in Seoul and Tokyo,  but I finally did!!

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: 5 Chome-16-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: Nick Netto
Review: By food, I actually mean coffee.. it is fantastic! Much needed cuppa and place to get some work done. There's an offer of 200 yen off refills (up to 3 per day I believe), so this could come in handy if you're staying for a bit. Some cakes, pastries, and grilled sandwiches too. Overall, really good coffee in a nice environment.

Name: Verve Coffee Roasters
Address: 5 Chome-16-7 Roppongi, Minato City
Author: linh dang
Review: Situated at a very beautiful place in roppongi, had a very bright and modern vibe. One of the few cafe shops in Tokyo where they’ll take your name and say it out loud when it’s your turn. Carrot cake was so much better than i thought, a bit expensive though.

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-12-14 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Pedro Sá Moreira
Review: Lovely cafe. Atmosphere with jazz music and classical pieces, comfortable furniture, plug sockets and WiFi available all around. Staff is super friendly and efficient. Good coffee and sandwiches

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-12-14 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Pastel Ophelia
Review: Wonderful experience, its also so lovely to have a fully vegan option on the menu too 💛

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-12-14 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Andrew Lee
Review:
Coffee is good, but the shop is small.
Not enough space to seats during weekend.

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-12-14 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Fabrizio Bozzato
Review:
Some staff members appear to be robots.
I wanted to order. I gave up. I walked away.
Other Tully's Coffee shops are way more welcoming than this one.

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-12-14 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Donna Bentley
Review: Whilst in Shibuya I had struggled to find space to get away from the bustle and buzz. This place was just what was needed! Lovely spaceous surroundings, polite staff, chilled atoms and really nice coffee.

Name: Ralph's Coffee Shinjuku
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−2 ルミネ新宿店 ルミネ2 1F
Author: Mart MAK
Review: A Quick service and effective by the lady at the 13th of March 2023 at 7PM. Got my cutie tumbler for bringing home to Sydney Australia 🇦🇺- Arigato

Name: Ralph's Coffee Shinjuku
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−2 ルミネ新宿店 ルミネ2 1F
Author: Dimitar Stanimiroff
Review: Waste of time - they don’t actually serve coffee. They only sell Ralph’s merch.

Name: Ralph's Coffee Shinjuku
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−2 ルミネ新宿店 ルミネ2 1F
Author: Amélie Noir
Review: Nice gifts but name is deceptive no coffee is served there

Name: Ralph's Coffee Shinjuku
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−2 ルミネ新宿店 ルミネ2 1F
Author: Andrew Fisher
Review: Misleading they don't actually make coffee, just sell some mugs and beans for home

Name: Ralph's Coffee Shinjuku
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 3 Chome−38−2 ルミネ新宿店 ルミネ2 1F
Author: Zahraa Al Hiti
Review: The best

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Shinjuku Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 4 Chome−1−6 NEWoMan SHINJUKU 1F
Author: Rostono
Review:
Located on the first floor, have access from inside and outside (across the Victoria golf building).

They have a big and spacious place with a high chair. They also sell merchandise and beans.

Although its relatively more expensive compare to the similar coffe brands but they have a good long black. Almost every morning while I stayed at shinjuku, i have a breakfast and coffe in this place.

The staff also polite and good in english.

Recommended

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Shinjuku Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 4 Chome−1−6 NEWoMan SHINJUKU 1F
Author: Miesha
Review: Coffee and choc drinks are very nice but the place is way overpriced. We were lucky finding a seat for our family of 3. Range of pastries and cookies quite limited but drinks were top notch.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Shinjuku Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 4 Chome−1−6 NEWoMan SHINJUKU 1F
Author: Bryan D
Review: Hip coffee shop in Shinjuku serving up excellent coffee. It gets very busy, so you may struggle to get a seat.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Shinjuku Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 4 Chome−1−6 NEWoMan SHINJUKU 1F
Author: Nina V
Review: Spacious cafe that is light and bright. The food was not remarkable (3 stars). The drinks were okay (3-4 stars). Loved the contrast of this cafe to the dark Starbucks across the street. We had a 20+ year reunion and felt comfortable enough to have a private conversation for about 80 minutes.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee - Shinjuku Cafe
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 4 Chome−1−6 NEWoMan SHINJUKU 1F
Author: Bradley Jolly
Review:
While the service and atmosphere of the cafe was lovely, I couldn’t quite agree with the taste of the coffee. With so many other amazing cafes nearby, only go here if you really want to try Blue Bottle once.

That said, they have many exclusive coffee types and a wonderful range of supplies as well. Maybe I just got unlucky!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Shibuya
Address: 1 Chome-7-3 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Aleš Chromec
Review:
Amazing cafe. It's located within small park.

From the side of of building there is nice outside seating area which can be used even in the rain.

On the second floor there are places suitable for sitting in with laptop and also few sofas.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Shibuya
Address: 1 Chome-7-3 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Kayne (Kano La La)
Review:
From the coffee to the sweets, there's no room for doubt.
Architectural delight, blending right in the scene,
Outdoor seating 'mongst the trees, so serene.
Inside's a stunner, designed with such flair,
Upstairs meeting room, chill or connect, if you dare.
Blue Bottle Cafe, where style meets taste,
In Shibuya, it's the spot, ain't no time to waste.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Shibuya
Address: 1 Chome-7-3 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Eric Miller
Review:
Ample space for early morning caffeine-hounds. Tucked away in a semi-convenient location in Shibuya, Blue Bottle does it again with another exceptionally well thought out space-within-a-space. Counter and outdoor seating on the ground floor, lounge seating on the second. Though not as large as the Daikanyama location, it is larger than the Aoyama location.

A trend has seemingly emerged with Blue Bottle offering unique menu items at different locations. Here they offer a new twist on their hand crafted sweet or savory waffles with the addition of “Cinnamon Butter.” Sprinkled with finely chopped chocolate bits, the cinnamon infused air-whipped butter brings a new level of sweetness and texture to their mainstay breakfast treat offering.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Shibuya
Address: 1 Chome-7-3 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Tanisha Hippolyte
Review: The staff here are so friendly and helpful. We had the grilled cheese with tomato soup and the Waffle with cream. My grilled cheese was so warm and comforting. The waffle wasn’t too sweet and presented really nicely. The cafe had a mellow atmosphere with nice lighting.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Shibuya
Address: 1 Chome-7-3 Jinnan, Shibuya City
Author: Hang Man Helen Fung
Review: It's a hot day but worth going. It's a nice spacious environment and very relaxing, The single origin coffee and the limited cheesecake are good (not a NY cheesecake style)! 😋😋 Would definitely come back again!

Name: Streamer Coffee Company
Address: 1 Chome-20-28 Shibuya, Shibuya City
Author: Agista Jung
Review:
Discovered this place through a content creator and one of my followed barista usually comes to this place.

This place is popular for its beautiful latte art. But when I got here the place is too crowded so I decided to do a take away order. I had the simple latte. The coffee is smooth and creamy. I love Japanese coffee that is not too strong for me so I enjoy it the most. I would say that the coffee itself can be taken lightly by my stomach. And the texture of the coffee itself is smooth unlike coffee I had in Indonesia.

The staff are friendly and they accept cashless payment. A good place to go for a good coffee instead.

Name: Streamer Coffee Company
Address: 1 Chome-20-28 Shibuya, Shibuya City
Author: JH
Review: Love the vibe here, the crew was also very charismatic-chill. Had the revolver latte which is a double shot and it was superb.

Name: Streamer Coffee Company
Address: 1 Chome-20-28 Shibuya, Shibuya City
Author: Welly Lim
Review: I dont review much on cafe normally, but i kind of think this one has a potential. The latte and matcha coffee we ordered....average (latte may below average if you are a stronger coffee drinker...it tastes milk...remind me of starbucks) But, atmosphere wise, its great place to chill and meet ur friends. Hope next time i will have better comments on the coffee :)

Name: Streamer Coffee Company
Address: 1 Chome-20-28 Shibuya, Shibuya City
Author: Pongsathorn Surapab
Review: Great, but sorry. Overrated. The oreo cheesecake is TOO MELTED. Coffee is probably dark roasted. The staff didn’t tell anything about the coffee. I order large Iced Americano and Oreo Cheesecake cost ¥1,550. Much more price than quality

Name: Streamer Coffee Company
Address: 1 Chome-20-28 Shibuya, Shibuya City
Author: Sadaphong Kitiphankul
Review: I had a delightful experience at Steamer Coffee Roaster, conveniently located near the station. The warm and cozy atmosphere, coupled with the best-tasting coffee, made my visit memorable. The place was not too crowded, and the staff exhibited great hospitality. They spoke English fluently and were very friendly. Highly recommend!

Name: Yanaka coffee and tea
Address: 2 Chome-33-6 Kitazawa, Setagaya City
Author: achinoam rikin
Review:
Rude and unwelcoming. We were trying to order and sit they wouldn’t let us and not only did they kick us out, they made us buy their bad coffee for using the toilet, before they kicked us out. Avoid going!
The worst experience we had in Tokyo. There are so many nice places and welcoming people don’t bother wasting your time in this lame place

Name: Yanaka coffee and tea
Address: 2 Chome-33-6 Kitazawa, Setagaya City
Author: MeT
Review: Fantastic hideaway cafe that does excellent coffee. Both indoor n el fresco seating. Besides great coffee n teas, they also retail beans. Quite a wide variety for brewing in or take away. Yanaka’s is located in a side lane on a corner, ambience there is quiet relaxed and contemplative- perfect after the hustle n bustle of trendy Shimokitazawa! Prices are very reasonable and it’s a wonderful cafe to kick back your heels and have a cuppa or two of your favourite Java. Always make it a point to visit whenever we’re in that part of the city. Highly recommended 🎖🎖🎖🎖🎖

Name: Yanaka coffee and tea
Address: 2 Chome-33-6 Kitazawa, Setagaya City
Author: Divyanshu Solanki
Review: Their coffee tastes really great !

Name: Yanaka coffee and tea
Address: 2 Chome-33-6 Kitazawa, Setagaya City
Author: Moshe Ashkenazi
Review: It looks like amazing place, but the attend didn't want to serve a coffee without milk/cream, which I can not have.

Name: Yanaka coffee and tea
Address: 2 Chome-33-6 Kitazawa, Setagaya City
Author: Leo Smith
Review: banging coffee hot ppl

Name: Torahebi Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 15−1 アウトサイド 渋谷PARCO 3F
Author: Dennis
Review:
Great coffee experience!

I tried the Sake blend. Very unique flavour and notes. They also offered a tasting sample of the banana blend and old aged blend which also has their own unique flavours.

¥1,000 for take away, ¥1,500 for dine in. Although pricey it's definitely worth it if you're a coffee lover.

I ended up buying the Sake blend drip bags to take home with me.

Name: Torahebi Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 15−1 アウトサイド 渋谷PARCO 3F
Author: Sabreen Abdullah
Review:
The interior design drew me in while we were  exploring Shibuya. Little did I know that we were about to try the best coffee in shibuya (and the best coffee we had during our entire trip to Tokyo).

As someone from Seattle, coffee is a big part of our culture and this place did not disappoint.

I recommend coming here multiple times and getting beans here as a souvenir. It was a lovely experience!

Have you tried this place?

Name: Torahebi Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 15−1 アウトサイド 渋谷PARCO 3F
Author: Andy Cha
Review:
This store's coffee is crazy.

The owner has a scientist background and he selected only certain beans with special characteristics to use.

🍓 Aged Blend smells and tastes like strawberry.

🍌 Special Blend smells and tastes like banana.

🍓 Iced Cafe Late using the aged blend tastes like strawberry milk.

I highly recommend trying these coffees. Even though the prices are expensive, it really is worth it to try them.

Name: Torahebi Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 15−1 アウトサイド 渋谷PARCO 3F
Author: L T
Review: It is located on the 3rd floor of Shibuya Parco (you can use an escalator outside at the front of the building), not in the Chaos Kitchen as it shows on Google map. The coffee was great. The only downside is the limited dine-in space.

Name: Torahebi Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Udagawacho, 15−1 アウトサイド 渋谷PARCO 3F
Author: Philipp Günther
Review:
Really nice Coffee with very friendly people.
I tried a Cafe latte and a Handdrip. It was an amazing experience. Never try such an fruity Coffee before. I can totally recommend it!

Name: STREAMER COFFEE COMPANY AZABU-JUBAN
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 6 Chome−11−16 中銀六本木マンシオン
Author: E E
Review: My goto latte spot in Tokyo! Their lattes never disappoint me! This location has a different vibe from other stores. The barista was really sweet and friendly too! Will be back again!

Name: STREAMER COFFEE COMPANY AZABU-JUBAN
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 6 Chome−11−16 中銀六本木マンシオン
Author: Ramon Gino Jr. Chan
Review: Coffee and pastry are good and the price is reasonable considering its location. Staff are all friendly, but one staff is particularly outstanding. I wasn’t able to get his name but I overheard that he’s a student from Hawaii. He’s very helpful especially when giving tips to foreign tourists who visit the cafe.

Name: STREAMER COFFEE COMPANY AZABU-JUBAN
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 6 Chome−11−16 中銀六本木マンシオン
Author: Yuji Parker
Review:
Great spot to grab a coffee and get a little work done or meet with a friend.

The coffee was good. They said they use 3x the amount of coffee beans for best flavor profile but I found my mind drifting to thoughts of sourcing and sustainability. Do they “use” them or pass them on to less discriminating cafes? 😅

Anyway, the staff was really friendly and the ambiance is cool - a juxtaposition of old and new, Western and Japanese. Imagine drinking coffee in an old tea house while listening to the latest playlist from LA.

Streamers was the perfect, cozy spot on a cold and rainy Sunday morning.

Name: STREAMER COFFEE COMPANY AZABU-JUBAN
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 6 Chome−11−16 中銀六本木マンシオン
Author: FoodW4nd3r3r
Review: This place served real coffee with good vibe. The only down side was not much variety on pastries but overall it was excellent!

Name: STREAMER COFFEE COMPANY AZABU-JUBAN
Address: Minato City, Roppongi, 6 Chome−11−16 中銀六本木マンシオン
Author: Andrea Grabowski
Review: Streamer Azabu Juban is a must see and do! The peeps here are friendly and the coffee is outstanding no matter if your jam is a Revolver Latte 😋 or an Espresso Gin & Tonic!  You can sit and sip, enjoy the music and local vibe or take your coffee to go if you’re on  an adventure! I highly recommend checking this spot out!

Name: Ginza Coffee - Ginza Sukiyabashi
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 4 Chome−2−14 DK銀座ビル 2F
Author: Bume Thongsri
Review: The atmosphere looks like a dating spot to me. The coffee is great with good aroma. I also order pasta and toast. They taste great! The service is up to Japan standards, nice hospitality.

Name: Ginza Coffee - Ginza Sukiyabashi
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 4 Chome−2−14 DK銀座ビル 2F
Author: Kalen Tang
Review: Finished shopping at 9pm on a Tuesday night and passed by this coffee house, so I decided to have a dinner there.  Nice environment, not noisy, not crowded, clean, no need to wait for tables.  The staff who served me was super nice.   Food was not bad, meeting my expectations.  Overall an above par experience.

Name: Ginza Coffee - Ginza Sukiyabashi
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 4 Chome−2−14 DK銀座ビル 2F
Author: lynn
Review: such a hidden gem for a late night cafe, walked in around at 9.40 pm and they were so accomodating to serve us late - cannot really comment on the food but for a late night dessert it was lovely just to hangout here and relax - would come back if i stay in ginza again

Name: Ginza Coffee - Ginza Sukiyabashi
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 4 Chome−2−14 DK銀座ビル 2F
Author: Rita
Review: We came here for coffee to take a rest in between shopping. It’s Sunday so need to wait about 30 min. We try their afternoon set - choose french toast and pancake.  Food and coffee is ok but no surprise.  Seating is quite comfy though, a place good for hang out.

Name: Ginza Coffee - Ginza Sukiyabashi
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 4 Chome−2−14 DK銀座ビル 2F
Author: Nic Uyliapco
Review:
Just searched Coffee shops nearby and I didn’t want the usual mainstream coffee shops like StarB… or the likes.
Great atmosphere
Selection is decent
Kids can have Pancakes and Shakes

Name: Tully’s Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Shibuya, 2 Chome−24−12 スクランブルスクエア 9階
Author: daddybear
Review: Flat white is nice although I ordered latte. It is pity using paper cup rather than a mug .

Name: Tully’s Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Shibuya, 2 Chome−24−12 スクランブルスクエア 9階
Author: Manjit Grewal
Review:
Always loved my time at Tully’s Cafe.
A cup of coffee and Choco donut are enough to recharge your battery and keep you going.

Visited on 14 March 2021 ❤️✨🌟
Stay safe and happy everyone.
-love happy-

Name: Tully’s Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Shibuya, 2 Chome−24−12 スクランブルスクエア 9階
Author: Argel Cruz
Review: Massive seating capacity, built for relaxation so turnover is pretty slow. Had Macha Latte and it was good.

Name: Tully’s Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Shibuya, 2 Chome−24−12 スクランブルスクエア 9階
Author: Sharon Stone
Review: They only have pre-sweetened matcha which tastes more like sugar. Little sad after reading the reviews saying it is decent, but it's just the cheap sugared stuff. Service was normal and the place looked packed.

Name: Tully’s Coffee
Address: Shibuya City, Shibuya, 2 Chome−24−12 スクランブルスクエア 9階
Author: Nicolette Foong
Review: There’s a nice place to hang around (Tully’s Coffee Commu Square) that’s right next to Tully’s Coffee. I got the hot Matcha Latte but it was quite disappointing. It has a weird texture and tastes diluted. :/

Name: Brewing Room
Address: 4 Chome-16-7 Setagaya, Setagaya City
Author: Wes Thorpe
Review: As soon as I walked in I knew that this was where I wanted to be. Dim (it was evening), warm, comfortable, funky atmosphere and Bill Evans playing on the stereo. Saw sandwiches on the menu. Was skeptical at first - I have high standards for sandwiches. One bite of the chicken saute sandwich and I was beyond happy. The coffee was good, the cake was fine, but that sandwich! Will be back.

Name: Brewing Room
Address: 4 Chome-16-7 Setagaya, Setagaya City
Author: Marc Sardin
Review: Absolutely my favourite place for breakfast in Tokyo. Homemade bread, delicious sandwiches and coffee, lovely people and great selection of music (not that mainstream stuff)

Name: Brewing Room
Address: 4 Chome-16-7 Setagaya, Setagaya City
Author: YC Kew
Review: We had hand drip coffee and dessert. The dessert was great! Plenty of coffee beans selection, seems like the owner roasts the bean himself.

Name: Brewing Room
Address: 4 Chome-16-7 Setagaya, Setagaya City
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: Very laidback vibe with great music and great staff. The seats are comfortable and the pace is slow, just how I like to drink my coffee. Several beans to choose from depending on your tastes. Didn’t eat anything, but there are some options on the menu. Cool spot...recommend checking it out.

Name: Brewing Room
Address: 4 Chome-16-7 Setagaya, Setagaya City
Author: Majbritt Takkebos
Review: Amazing atmosphere and lovely host with the best coffee!!

Name: The Coffee Counter Tokyo (カフェ)
Address: 東神田イチオクビル 1階, 1 Chome-17-5 Higashikanda, Chiyoda City
Author: Павел Наумов
Review:
We’ve been there a couple of times during our Tokyo trip and we definitely will visit this place again. To be honest, we have never seen local people in there, so I guess this coffee shop is more like a western style coffee counter 😅
However, they do serve good classic coffee and great panini. Croissants are also very good. It’s a great place for a quick western style type of breakfast.

Name: The Coffee Counter Tokyo (カフェ)
Address: 東神田イチオクビル 1階, 1 Chome-17-5 Higashikanda, Chiyoda City
Author: KCJ K
Review:
It share the location with other shops. So it does smells like curry once entered the shop.
But, Amazing banana bread and flat white.
She made the banana bread in the shop, very moist and delicious. Flat white was well balance, great espresso flavour. Recommend.

Name: The Coffee Counter Tokyo (カフェ)
Address: 東神田イチオクビル 1階, 1 Chome-17-5 Higashikanda, Chiyoda City
Author: Fatima MF
Review:
The coffee and the matcha are really nice! Also is one of the few places that have oat milk in Tokyo! We had a plain croissant, banana bread and a cookie. They all tasted amazing, however the cookie was a bit too sweet for my taste. The place is shared with some other establishments. The girl was super nice and helpful!
For people who are celiac, they have gluten free cookies !!

Name: The Coffee Counter Tokyo (カフェ)
Address: 東神田イチオクビル 1階, 1 Chome-17-5 Higashikanda, Chiyoda City
Author: Karo W
Review: Delicious matcha latte with oat milk, not sweetened and not bitter, just perfect! Oishī 😻 we went there three times everytime we have been to Tokyo so far. They also have gluten-free cookies — try the matcha ones! The brownie (not gluten-free) is a treat, too 😻 The staff is lovely, helpful and speaks English. Arigato gozaimasu 💙

Name: The Coffee Counter Tokyo (カフェ)
Address: 東神田イチオクビル 1階, 1 Chome-17-5 Higashikanda, Chiyoda City
Author: Matthew Fale
Review: Friendly staff and delicious food. Ordered the almond croissant, ham and cheese panini, chicken pesto panini, iced latte and iced chai. $26 usd breakfast for  two.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 1 Chome−17−6 アトレ秋葉原１ 一階
Author: James Mielke
Review: Nice spot for a quick coffee, or in my case an iced matcha, especially since I couldn’t find their more well-known counterpart in the station. Lots of places to sit, although this is at Akihabara Station so it may not be the case during rush hour.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 1 Chome−17−6 アトレ秋葉原１ 一階
Author: Zwe Kyaw Zwa
Review: Loved the quality and flavour. Decent price. Best barista coffee in Akihabara. Make sure to try the Honey Latte. 🍯

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 1 Chome−17−6 アトレ秋葉原１ 一階
Author: Terence Lim
Review: Delicious and flavourful latte, warm and cosy place to get a drink and chill. Staff were friendly and provided menu. A place to get a quick coffe break, price point a little on the high side ard 650 yen

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 1 Chome−17−6 アトレ秋葉原１ 一階
Author: yusraa .z
Review: Was looking for a cafe to stop by before heading to the airport. Sarutahiko is right outside one of Akihabara station’s gantry which is super convenient. Their honey latte and oat milk latte is a must try. Highly recommend their milk france bread and croissant with izuni butter too!

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee
Address: Chiyoda City, Sotokanda, 1 Chome−17−6 アトレ秋葉原１ 一階
Author: Ellen Brigitta
Review: I ordered the coffee latte with a hint of grape but i think the coffee is too sweet and instead of grape's flavor, i tasted it like banana. Maybe the regular coffee is better

Name: Cat Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-6-9 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: T Nicks
Review: Very nice, clean and friendly

Name: Cat Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-6-9 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: Mark Grixti
Review: Cat

Name: Cat Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-6-9 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: Ali Grixti
Review: Cat

Name: Cat Coffee
Address: 1 Chome-6-9 Jingumae, Shibuya City
Author: E S

Name: REC COFFEE Suidobashi
Address: 3 Chome-10-1 Misakicho, Chiyoda City
Author: Sam V
Review: Highly recommend this cafe if you’re after excellent coffee and food. They’re clearly passionate about coffee and it really shows as it’s really exceptional! The cafe overlooks the river and has a great atmosphere, with friendly and welcoming staff. Definitely coming back.

Name: REC COFFEE Suidobashi
Address: 3 Chome-10-1 Misakicho, Chiyoda City
Author: Barry
Review:
MAR 2024

Ordered a café latte and a matcha gateau chocolate which comes with a scoop of vanilla ice cream. The café latte has a balanced light roast flavor without tasting milky. The gateau chocolate was rich and fudgy with a pretty good matcha flavor; and the vanilla ice cream pairs well to balance the sweetness. The barista was friendly and polite.

Name: REC COFFEE Suidobashi
Address: 3 Chome-10-1 Misakicho, Chiyoda City
Author: milky doll
Review: lovely friendly barista and service. Staff spoke English. good iced americano and their buttered toast was thick and fluffy soft. I bought 2 bags of their coffee beans. from the time of roasting, it is a 3 months shelf recommendations. since I bought coffee beans, you get a free latte or americano but since I got decaffeinated (which costs more for decaf), I paid for my drink. kudos for free coffee with bean purchase though. Relaxing cafe by the water and so close to train station.

Name: REC COFFEE Suidobashi
Address: 3 Chome-10-1 Misakicho, Chiyoda City
Author: Mary Bello Ruiz
Review:
Small cafeteria overlooking the river.
The ground coffee is delicious and the toast they prepare are super fluffy and juicy. It was a great decision to have breakfast here.
Besides going for breakfast you can also buy coffee beans if you wish. They have a shelf with all the products.
The staff is young and well qualified.
Very affordable prices.

Name: REC COFFEE Suidobashi
Address: 3 Chome-10-1 Misakicho, Chiyoda City
Author: Dan P
Review:
Coffee and smiles and stay for a whiles…

This is my type of coffee shop. Artisanal and full of passionate friendly people, without the pretentiousness.

I’ve been here a couple of times this year (May and Nov), and was impressed with the coffee both times. They have an awesome selection of single origin, and the staff are very well educated on the flavour profiles, styles, roasts and aftertastes!

I was pleasantly surprised on this latest visit to be greeted by a charming young lady with perfect English (it’s better than mine!), and her positive energy was infectious and infused me with a spring in my step. She helped me select two coffees throughout my stay - and gave me some interesting backstory on each.

The inside of the shop is cosy and warm, capturing a lot of natural light. You can grab a seat on a communal bar table overlooking an outside public seating area and garden and watch people cross the bridge; or seat at the tables overlooking the water.

If you have a spot of work to get through or just want a nice casual place to stop with a friend whilst exploring the surrounding area, don’t miss this place!

Name: Saza Coffee KITTE Marunouchi
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 2 Chome−7−2 ＪＰタワーＫＩＴＴＥ
Author: Ivy Wu
Review: A very (probably the only) serious coffee shop in Marunouchi. Very expensive coffee by Tokyo standard (JPY1,200 for the geisha coffee I ordered, but it came with a free Kenya sample). Also serves siphon coffee. You may have to queue to dine in. Only counter seats so would be hard if you come in group of 4 or more.

Name: Saza Coffee KITTE Marunouchi
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 2 Chome−7−2 ＪＰタワーＫＩＴＴＥ
Author: Danny Seow
Review: Nice coffee with wonderful services. You can buy coffee bean at the cafe too.

Name: Saza Coffee KITTE Marunouchi
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 2 Chome−7−2 ＪＰタワーＫＩＴＴＥ
Author: The Cat
Review:
Inside surrounding glass buildings, this cafe standout with its decor, comforting coffee. They have all the coffee tools to cater to every coffee lover's fancy.
Not much options for snacks or desserts but the chocolate bar is heavenly!! Well balanced chocolate with a tinge of orange. Must visit if you are nearby!

Name: Saza Coffee KITTE Marunouchi
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 2 Chome−7−2 ＪＰタワーＫＩＴＴＥ
Author: Steg
Review: Very good. Nice service, delicious coffee, kind staff. Very mild and relaxing atmosphere )) highly recommend. Take a walk around Emperor Palace and settle down for a cup on flavorous coffee and share memories.

Name: Saza Coffee KITTE Marunouchi
Address: Chiyoda City, Marunouchi, 2 Chome−7−2 ＪＰタワーＫＩＴＴＥ
Author: APC SSP
Review: June 16.2023 @2:30 p.m. I arrived at the cafe and there was no seat available. It was only high chairs and counter bar with 2 rows narrow in it. Too bad coffees looks good for this area. I ended up find another café in Ginza to enjoy my coffee of the day.

Name: THE SMOKIST COFFEE 東新宿店
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 6 Chome−29−11 イーストクロスタワー1・2F
Author: Takuki
Review:
Nice place with good environment and reasonable price of coffee and food.
And almost all the seats have power socket, a good place for studying or reading books.
The second floor is smoking area.

Name: THE SMOKIST COFFEE 東新宿店
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 6 Chome−29−11 イーストクロスタワー1・2F
Author: ridho ardhi syaiful
Review: Just ordinary coffee shop. It allows to smoke at second floor.

Name: THE SMOKIST COFFEE 東新宿店
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 6 Chome−29−11 イーストクロスタワー1・2F
Author: Nebula
Review: Wonderful spot for tourists who like to smoke, this lovely café became a part of my morning routine staying in Shinjuku. Can get a bit busy at times but is always quiet and relaxing. Highly recommend for smoking tourists.

Name: THE SMOKIST COFFEE 東新宿店
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 6 Chome−29−11 イーストクロスタワー1・2F
Author: Travel And Dine
Review: Nice service. Bit of a view over the crossing. Stating the obvious but don't go on the 2nd floor if you don't want to be around smokers.

Name: THE SMOKIST COFFEE 東新宿店
Address: Shinjuku City, Shinjuku, 6 Chome−29−11 イーストクロスタワー1・2F
Author: Kanai Pathak
Review: Free WiFi and charging at every table

Name: Crossing Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−1, Ginza Place, 2F
Author: Lea Meyer
Review: Very nice coffee for Nissan car lovers! The drinks taste good and look great with the picture printed on them!!! You can also admire beautiful cars there and even sit in one if you dare to ask:))

Name: Crossing Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−1, Ginza Place, 2F
Author: E D
Review: Average and affordable coffee attached to Nissan show room. The cafe is located 2nd floor of Nissan crossing building in Ginza, Tokyo.

Name: Crossing Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−1, Ginza Place, 2F
Author: William To
Review: A semi hidden coffee shop in the Nissan Crossing building. You can choose a Macchi-art on your drinks which include the fleet of Nissan cars, or you can have the staff take a picture of you on their iPad and put that instead. My wife chose the Sakura White Chocolate latte while I went for the Matcha Latte. Both tasted great and gave us the boost we needed to continuing exploring Ginza.

Name: Crossing Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−1, Ginza Place, 2F
Author: Monstar 80 (Land80x)
Review: Went looking for the cafe and was surprised to see the gallery of the new gtr and my fair ladies.  Coffee was great the picture they put on the coffee was awesome.  The staff were super nice and some spoke to English which was nice since I looked like a lost puppy walking around.

Name: Crossing Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−1, Ginza Place, 2F
Author: Amy K
Review:
If you come to visit Tokyo don’t miss here. The coffee art was so cool also yummy.
You older the coffee then stuff will take picture of you or whatever you like to make the coffee art. The cold coffee will keep the art longer so recommend!

Name: GESHARY COFFEE HIBIYA
Address: Yūrakuchō Tōhō Bldg., 1 Chome-6-3 Yurakucho, Chiyoda City
Author: Kyle Wong
Review: Very elegant atmosphere. They have multiple floors of seating, with lots of electrical outlets! We got the geisha lattes and a mango cake.  Very tasty. I also enjoyed the extras like the coffee jelly and cold brew tasting. It is pricey.

Name: GESHARY COFFEE HIBIYA
Address: Yūrakuchō Tōhō Bldg., 1 Chome-6-3 Yurakucho, Chiyoda City
Author: John de Souza
Review: Amazing place. They are very serious about making delicious coffee. Trust their recommendations, they know what they are talking about. They also have sumptuous desserts, that are not too sweet, to complement the coffee. They have wonderful seating areas on multiple floors with amazing views through large windows. They also have good WiFi. Enjoy

Name: GESHARY COFFEE HIBIYA
Address: Yūrakuchō Tōhō Bldg., 1 Chome-6-3 Yurakucho, Chiyoda City
Author: P & L McC
Review: A splurge coffee and pastry experience, ours cost us over 4K in yen. Coffee was dark and smooth, while the geisha coffee was more like a frappe and sweet, with coffee jelly (would make a good stand-alone treat). The chestnut pastry and lemon tart were on point, the chestnut topping was creamy and flavorful, better than the one I had in Paris

Name: GESHARY COFFEE HIBIYA
Address: Yūrakuchō Tōhō Bldg., 1 Chome-6-3 Yurakucho, Chiyoda City
Author: Ribeye
Review:
Great place to taste different varieties of specialty geisha coffee. Noticeable aroma of floral notes, such as jasmine, chocolate and honey.
Some of the beans were fermented and even had notes of wine.
Overall an excellent place to chill and even get work done as they have multiple floors of seating area and is nice and quiet on weekdays.
The coffee isn't cheap for sure, but it's very reasonably priced for speciality geisha beans.

Name: GESHARY COFFEE HIBIYA
Address: Yūrakuchō Tōhō Bldg., 1 Chome-6-3 Yurakucho, Chiyoda City
Author: Nikol Hristova
Review: Very calm and pretty atmosphere. The staff was great and knew English. The coffee barely had sugar, which I loved, and their cheesecake is very good and creamy. You take the elevator to the sitting area, which is very large, and they bring your order to your table.

Name: Cafe de l'ambre
Address: 8 Chome-10-15 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: John Nguyen
Review: This is a very cute and intimate coffee shop where there is a massive selection. The waitress felt a bit antsy to get people to order quickly but was able to point out popular choices. I picked the no. 7 which was very sweet but that was what I was looking for. It was delicious!

Name: Cafe de l'ambre
Address: 8 Chome-10-15 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: S So
Review: Coffee ONLY shop, non smoking and cash only.  offers both blended coffee and choices of single origin all on pour over. They indicated they use their own custom made pour-over gear.  Standard dose starts with 18g beans and three cup sizes, we chose medium 70cc which is quite intensive for pour over but we felt it should have been the new standard.  Double shots use 28g dose with double of volume.  The single origin coffee we ordered seemed to be medium to medium dark roasted, the Kenya maintained a high level of acidity while having hints of roasty and chocolate bitterness.  The waitress spoke fluent English and offered recommendations based on our requirements.  Staff were friendly and knowledgeable.  Recommended for specialty coffee lovers.

Name: Cafe de l'ambre
Address: 8 Chome-10-15 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Han Low
Review:
“Would rate it 0 ⭐️ if the app allowed”

The coffee and coffee jelly are ridiculously priced, which by itself is fine, but not when it is tasteless and fragrant-less.

The biggest 👎🏻 was the poor service and disgusting attitude from the servers and workers. We were constantly given side eye merely because we requested for a corner table (which was empty) to assist my dad with moving around. Instead, we were given a table in the middle and constantly had to get up to allow others to move through.

Name: Cafe de l'ambre
Address: 8 Chome-10-15 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Bonnie
Review: Delightful little nook, arrived at opening but already getting quite busy when we left (and we didn't stay that long!). Easily the most amazing coffee I have had in my life - asked for 'osusume' and ended up trying the 'Queen Amber'. Smooth and strong coffee with a layer of sweet milk on top (not mixed), giving a delightful dual flavour with each sip. If you love coffee, it's an amazing experience you won't regret! English and Korean menus also available. Note: no food, coffee only!

Name: Cafe de l'ambre
Address: 8 Chome-10-15 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Denis Roschlau
Review:
Perfect hand drip coffee paired with kind and friendly service.
It was very hot outside and they forced me to drink more water, which I appreciated.
It tried the Guatemala beans and the coffee was aromatic, smooth and of course delicious.
I will be back to try more!

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Shinjuku 2-chome
Address: 2 Chome-5-9 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Debi Slinger
Review:
I keep coming back And that’s because of the service, the food and the warm atmosphere. Today was pancakes with a mocha latte. Meal was a good size and at around 1130Yen not too bad value for money. Staff were very sweet and patient with me as a I chose my order. They were doing a brisk trade with locals and internationals and certainly giving the McDonalds on the other corner a run for its money.
Plenty of nice coffee and other treats to buy to take with you as well.
Wheelchair accessible and kid friendly.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Shinjuku 2-chome
Address: 2 Chome-5-9 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Lea L
Review: The service was excellent. The food wasn't satisfying sadly. We came here in the morning for breakfast, so there aren't many people here. There are seats on the second floor where you can people watch.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Shinjuku 2-chome
Address: 2 Chome-5-9 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Levi Flaman
Review: Food was okay; got some kind of spaghetti dish but although it tasted fine, it wasn't particularly filling. Tried to order an iced coffee with a scoop of T's Ice inside similar to an article I read on SoraNews24 a while back, but either I wasn't ordering correctly or the cashier misunderstood me because I ended up getting the ice cream scoop on the side which I didn't want.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Shinjuku 2-chome
Address: 2 Chome-5-9 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Elaine Farore-Love
Review: Equivalent to Starbucks. Great gourmet coffee and matcha drinks with large indoor and outdoor spaces to sit down, walking distance to the train station near Shinjoko Garden (cherry blossom)

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Shinjuku 2-chome
Address: 2 Chome-5-9 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Regina Falconer
Review: Great coffee and I'm not a coffee drinker and fresh breakfast sandwich...

Name: Live Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Tsukiji, 3 Chome−5−13 北村ビル 1F
Author: JUHYUN PARK
Review:
Really good tasty and what a great baristas in here! They are very kind and sweet to us.
And no.1 blue mountain coffee was also great♥️♥️♥️

Name: Live Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Tsukiji, 3 Chome−5−13 北村ビル 1F
Author: Sinan Mandaci
Review:
I stop here to drink coffee every time after my annual health check-up in nearby St.Luke Hospital. It is cozy and lovely place just to sit down and enjoy the coffee from variety of beans.

You get free coupon/discount if you buy beans though surprisingly reasonably priced shop (~250¥) if you are here to drink coffee only.

Name: Live Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Tsukiji, 3 Chome−5−13 北村ビル 1F
Author: matthew fong
Review: Stopped by on the way to the markets, people queuing is always a good sign. I had the mocha and a cupcake, nice and hot and very friendly staff. Definitely pop by people

Name: Live Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Tsukiji, 3 Chome−5−13 北村ビル 1F
Author: Matthew Stovall
Review: Spending my morning in Ginza/Tsukiji area and needed a coffee. Sadly, thought we might have to settle for a Starbucks/Doutor/Cafe de Crie when we stumbled upon this gem of a coffee roastery. To begin, the tiny little shop consisting of five tables, smells incredible. Second, there was a stream of locals grabbing coffees to go, always a good sign. Third, the four women working behind the counter were super helpful, kind, and friendly—when we arrived, all of the tables were filled so one of the women opened up some counter space for us and once a table opened, she ushered us to it almost immediately. Fourth, the coffee was inexpensive—¥300 for an iced coffee. And fifth, it was delicious. Well done Live Coffee!!

Name: Live Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Tsukiji, 3 Chome−5−13 北村ビル 1F
Author: Hana
Review:
This is a hidden gem in Tsukiji.
Loved the coffee, service, and the atmosphere.  Price range is 250-500 yen for a cup of coffee. hugely recommend!

Name: Kanno Coffee Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−9 ＢＩＮＯ ２Ｆ
Author: Daisy Tsai
Review: If you're looking for a relaxing spot to unwind after a day of shopping, I highly recommend this cozy restaurant. The food is delicious, with a menu offering a variety of flavorful dishes that cater to different tastes. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, perfect for a quiet meal or a casual gathering with friends. The staff is exceptionally friendly and attentive, making sure you feel right at home. Whether you're seeking a peaceful break or a place to enjoy a great meal, this restaurant is the ideal choice for a pleasant dining experience.

Name: Kanno Coffee Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−9 ＢＩＮＯ ２Ｆ
Author: Ja /Ja
Review: Nice ambiance, delicious food. There is a cake & coffee/tea set with a selection of cakes to choose from. We had a baked cheesecake and chocolate tarte. Very light and delicious. The tomato mozzarella pasta is awesome!

Name: Kanno Coffee Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−9 ＢＩＮＯ ２Ｆ
Author: Tony Yeung
Review: Nice quiet corner on 2nd floor in Ginza area.  Gd place to take a rest in midst of shopping where most street floor shops are full.  Coffee n cakes are gd but not excellent but the coffee presentation is kind of special .

Name: Kanno Coffee Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−9 ＢＩＮＯ ２Ｆ
Author: CR
Review: All drinks (coffee, orange juice, tea) are at least ¥1000. French toast is under ¥900 and very delicious. Comes with a side salad you can eat in one bite. French toast is gooey throughout . Came on a Tuesday at 6:00 and it was empty

Name: Kanno Coffee Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−8−9 ＢＩＮＯ ２Ｆ
Author: Edwin Mok
Review:
To continue our dinning experience the first 24 hours arrived from New York, we randomly picked a cafe for afternoon tea.

Ordered two desserts. Encore with another round of coffee. Photos - self explanatory.

We were seated in a booth seat by the window. Pretty nice environment and services were decent. 😋😋

Name: Robert’s Coffee Azabujuban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome−2−5, Frencia Azabu Juban South, １階
Author: Pang Thunyalak
Review:
I found this place by chance while walking around Azabujuban and was intrigued by their gelato selection. They have 7 flavors to choose from. I ordered the raspberry and blueberry yogurt gelato, and it was great. However, the price seemed a bit high for the small cup size.

They also offer drinks and food for dinner, which you can order through a machine. Unfortunately, you can’t order ice cream through the machine. The shop is quite spacious, with both indoor and outdoor dining options.

If you’re looking for a place to relax, I’d recommend stopping by here.

Name: Robert’s Coffee Azabujuban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome−2−5, Frencia Azabu Juban South, １階
Author: Matthew Stovall
Review: A nice sized coffee shop located in an absolutely fabulous location. The cinnamon gelato is other worldly; the chai tea latte is weak in flavor, too milky; the affogato was serviceable.

Name: Robert’s Coffee Azabujuban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome−2−5, Frencia Azabu Juban South, １階
Author: Kelly in Japan
Review: Stopped here for a sandwich and a drink. It was alright but not amazing. The service was reasonable and it was so hard to find a seat indoors. Unfortunately a busy place like this only has 1 toilet, so it took some time to get in. Maybe other locations are better but I don’t know this was my first time visiting a Roberts coffee. Visit if you like and formulate your own opinion.

Name: Robert’s Coffee Azabujuban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome−2−5, Frencia Azabu Juban South, １階
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: A nice place to grab a coffee and hangout out or do some work. Plenty of indoor seating with some outdoor options as well. A should drink selection, but I just opted for the drip coffee. It was good, but make a little weak for my taste. Friendly staff and reasonable prices make this a nice option in the area.

Name: Robert’s Coffee Azabujuban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 2 Chome−2−5, Frencia Azabu Juban South, １階
Author: Tomoko A.
Review:
Ordered a Robert Mocha at a cafe I wanted to visit. I was lucky that there was a seat available.
The mocha looks impressive and when you put your mouth on it, you get a leaky mustache. Enjoy it!

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Azabu-Juban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 1 Chome−11−14 ＳＢＳビル
Author: Hank Chang
Review: Quick and efficient Starbucks, two levels (seating upstairs), and great sakura donuts!

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Azabu-Juban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 1 Chome−11−14 ＳＢＳビル
Author: Jason Chuei
Review: Cool branch on the Main Street of Azabujuban, spanning three floors with a small outdoor garden and seating area on the third floor.

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Azabu-Juban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 1 Chome−11−14 ＳＢＳビル
Author: Gabe Garriga
Review: Quite honestly in the states I’m not a big Starbucks fan but here it’s way better. The coffee is not bitter. The latte is very smooth and creamy. I wish my Starbucks At home was like here. Very good place for Ordering in English too.

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Azabu-Juban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 1 Chome−11−14 ＳＢＳビル
Author: Brent
Review: Starbucks food is pretty average, nothing amazing. They do have seasonal things that are worth trying once. They also have pretty average coffee, there’s better coffee options less than 1 minute walk from here. This starbucks though, has 3 floors for meeting up with friends, studying, reading a book, or just lounging. I think this is the main reason to come to starbucks. It can get very busy 3-6 and there will be no where to sit.

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Azabu-Juban
Address: Minato City, Azabujuban, 1 Chome−11−14 ＳＢＳビル
Author: Sun Shakur
Review: It's a three story building so easier to find a table. They keep the place nice and clean as any other starbucks in Japan. I like how they have a bench you can sit since you don't see any place to sit outside in Japan. The tables are pretty close to each other and it's so quiet that I felt like I had to stay quite too. lol

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee atré Ebisu Westside Store
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 1 Chome−6−1 アトレ恵比寿 西館 １階
Author: Jason Chuei
Review: One of three Sarutahiko stores in Ebisu, this one located in the west building of Atre. Open setting adjacent to the mall traffic. French accordion music playing overhead. Matcha latte was pretty good.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee atré Ebisu Westside Store
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 1 Chome−6−1 アトレ恵比寿 西館 １階
Author: Karen On
Review: Had a very nice experience here. Their caffe latte is very delicious! The staff taking my order spoke really fluent English so it was easy for me to order. There's a dine-in area beside the counter, but the nice thing is that there are many benches in front of the store, so if you cannot find a seat inside you can sit outside on the benches directly facing the store. When I finished my drink, a kind staff member asked me if I enjoyed my drink. I had a really nice experience here and have been back here 3 times since my first visit, introducing it to new people each time.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee atré Ebisu Westside Store
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 1 Chome−6−1 アトレ恵比寿 西館 １階
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: I came across the other Sarutahiko coffee shop in Ebisu a year or so ago and really enjoyed the flavor, so thought I’d try here too. You’re still getting the same delicious cup here with fast and friendly service. A few seats to sit and enjoy your drink. Located right behind Shake Shack. A nice pit stop while in the area.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee atré Ebisu Westside Store
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 1 Chome−6−1 アトレ恵比寿 西館 １階
Author: Omri R
Review: Sarutahiko’s premium coffee take (similar to Starbucks Reserve). This shop is really nicely designed and the staff is very nice and polite (they really go the extra mile in being serviceable). The coffee is good and is about 400-500 yen per cup which is higher than the regular Sarutahiko coffee branches. I liked the furniture design in this shop and the the nice colors and lighting. The choice of foods is quite limited, but pretty high quality. Portions are small in a similar vein to Blue Bottle and other higher-end shops.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee atré Ebisu Westside Store
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisuminami, 1 Chome−6−1 アトレ恵比寿 西館 １階
Author: Edmund Yip
