Review:
Great coffee, friendly staff and superb breakfast!

Excellent hand-dripped coffees and espresso drinks!

You must try the eggslut here! It is a satiating mix of steamed egg and mashed potato, eaten with crispy toast! And the most beautiful takeaway coffee cups in the world! Look out also for coffee beans and attractive merchandise. They open early and it's just beside Ebisu station!

Name: MIA MIA TOKYO
Address: 4 Chome-10-1 Nagasaki, Toshima City
Author: Thomas
Review:
The service is top notch and the environment of the neighborhood (and this shop) is the main selling point for me.

I think the layout and the menu are slightly chaotic, and I wish the beans they sold were a little more affordable and listed the roasting date instead of "best by" date, but otherwise the small snack and coffee I had were both solid.  I guess sandwiches and other things come around lunch time, but I was there early.

Extra thanks for proving parking space for my bike 🏍️

Name: MIA MIA TOKYO
Address: 4 Chome-10-1 Nagasaki, Toshima City
Author: James
Review: This cafe has a selection of many Australian coffee beans and the friendliest vibe that I've encountered in Tokyo. Coffee lovers hang out here. They even have Vegemite.

Name: MIA MIA TOKYO
Address: 4 Chome-10-1 Nagasaki, Toshima City
Author: FT
Review:
Probably the best cafe in Tokyo that I’ve been to. The coffee, atmosphere, people, music and location. I feel so at home here.

Beans are from Padre Coffee (Australia) and my favorite is their latte paired with the cardamom bun.

I love that it’s a warm and cozy spot where you can end staying for hours talking to the owner, who is Australian, baristas and new folks. Mia Mia in the evening is very different, it turns into a bar and has really fun vibes.

Unlike the spots you see in central Tokyo, this place encourages you to connect with other customers, which is amazing and we need more spots like Mia Mia.

Name: MIA MIA TOKYO
Address: 4 Chome-10-1 Nagasaki, Toshima City
Author: ari salomon
Review:
Third wave is the best wave. Learn more by chatting with the gregarious Australian owner of this charming cafe who was also a coffee writer for years. He is connected to coffee culture in tokyo in a deep way and has a line of coffee-themed handkerchiefs and other items. They are available in the cafe and the full line is available online.

Such a friendly crew at MIA MIA. No AC but I didn’t even notice so come sit and enjoy.

Name: MIA MIA TOKYO
Address: 4 Chome-10-1 Nagasaki, Toshima City
Author: Lex Miller
Review:
The good folk at Mia Mia obviously value community, character and ambience. There's an air of fashionable Showa vintage about the place - It attracts people who have a good sense of art and design. There is an associated gallery just around the block.

It's welcoming - The kind of place where you are more than likely to find yourself in a conversation. Mia Mia makes an effort to reach out to neighbouring residents and businesses, strengthening the local community, a bit like the sort of friendly close-knit neighbourhood you might see in one of those heart-warming Japanese TV dramas.

My first visit was for a wonderful soul-soothing music performance, but I'm looking forward to returning to try their coffee, perhaps with one of their cardamom rolls, or an ANZAC biscuit.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Ali Chua
Review:
Grabbed a coffee here, it's consistent across branches in other countries. Would prefer if they offered skimmed milk.

It's decently priced and there was too long a queue. Saw the staff making the drip coffee and he is very conscientious about it.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Skye Cai
Review:
Tried their cakes which is quite disappointing.
The cheese cake doesn’t taste made with good milk and cheese. That it doesn’t melt in the mouth or has heavy cheese flavours.
The brownie is not tasty too. Super hard and not soft.
The better one is the coconut pound cake that has strong coconut taste.
In overall not much services but good atmosphere. Merchandises are expensive.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Rakhmadi Kusumo
Review:
Visiting Blue Bottle Coffee at the bustling B2 Ginza Six Mall can be a challenging experience due to its popularity. The cafe was extremely packed during my visit, resulting in long waits for both seating and ordering. The crowd, characteristic of such a renowned spot, unfortunately detracts from the usually serene coffee experience one might expect from Blue Bottle.

I ordered a cappuccino and an iced latte. While both drinks were up to the high standards Blue Bottle is known for, with the right balance of flavors and expertly brewed coffee, the overall experience was overshadowed by the excessive wait times and the chaotic environment. Additionally, a significant error occurred during my transaction, as I was charged twice for my order, further complicating my visit.

Given the stress and inconvenience of navigating the crowded space and dealing with billing errors, I would recommend seeking out a quieter, less crowded venue for those looking to enjoy their coffee in peace. While Blue Bottle Coffee offers quality beverages, the Ginza Six location might not be the best choice for those who prefer a more relaxed setting to truly savor their coffee.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Hi Goat Oh Deer迷鹿与高羊
Review:
Blue bottle is like the better version of Starbucks franchise.

Nola Float (736Y with tax) smooth coffee, Coffee not sweet, but the ice cream is sweet. Not bad.

The place is pretty crowded and noisy though.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee Ginza Cafe
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 6 Chome−10−1 GINZA SIX B2F
Author: Eileen Neo (Sgsohotleh)
Review: Not as iconic compared to the rest of the Blue Bottle Coffee Cafe! But the interior of the design is still very beautiful and minimalistic. Coffee wise, on the slightly pricey side. I guess due to branding. They also carry their own merchandise too, if you are a fan of blue bottle. In the outlet, we tried the basque cheesecake! It’s so so good! Ended up, I thought the cheesecake was a better choice compared to the coffee. Sadly, they don’t allow takeaway for the cheesecake. Definitely a must visit, given its popularity. 💯

Name: Bless Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 2 Chome−5−17-1F 京橋SKビル 1・2F
Author: Teo Zilin
Review:
Came here to try out the food since it was recommended, however was left utterly disappointed when the guy at the counter was playing with his phone instead of attending to the counter. When we finally get to order, he looked grumpy when we interrupted whatever he is typing on his phone. (He looked like he does not want to be there lol)

Only positive thing i would like to comment is the lady who came to serve us the sandwich.  Food is ok but could be better :)

Name: Bless Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 2 Chome−5−17-1F 京橋SKビル 1・2F
Author: Jim Smith
Review: Great little coffee shop large selection of pastries snacks etc.. some snacks are meal sized just popped but would go again

Name: Bless Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 2 Chome−5−17-1F 京橋SKビル 1・2F
Author: Chandrika
Review: Location wise its a nice place to sit and eat and look around. The coffee was extremely average and felt a bit milky. The sandwich BLT was quite expensive but not that amazing at all. The bread toast became soggy!

Name: Bless Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 2 Chome−5−17-1F 京橋SKビル 1・2F
Author: Ali C
Review: Quaint spot.. love the name. Kind staff. Very clean. Quiet. Good coffee and free wifi. I had the maple latte and tried the granola cookie and lemon pund cake.. nice for people watching also

Name: Bless Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 2 Chome−5−17-1F 京橋SKビル 1・2F
Author: Christian Bayerle
Review: Good real cappucinos on ground level, which is difficult to find in Ginza.

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 3 Chome−1−1 東京スクエアガーデン B1F
Author: Helio Ciffoni
Review: This place used to be an European coffee house, with no attraction as cafeteria. Now, as a Tully’s, it is always full, with neighboring office workers come not only to sip a café on Tully’s style but to work or get together.

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 3 Chome−1−1 東京スクエアガーデン B1F
Author: Newberry Ishi
Review:
You can sit out side that a bit relaxation environment.
and off course Kind staff.

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 3 Chome−1−1 東京スクエアガーデン B1F
Author: Lidia Alejandra Romero
Review:
Let me tell you….
We landed here by chance, after having breakfast at a very disappointing place we decided to have our espresso here and it was the best coffee!!! I would comeback but just for the espresso because they have nothing to offer gluten free.

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 3 Chome−1−1 東京スクエアガーデン B1F
Author: Lana Clara
Review:
Three times I said "soy milk", but I still got a "soy latte" for pay🤷‍♀️
Too loooooong service at all

Name: Tully's Coffee
Address: Chuo City, Kyobashi, 3 Chome−1−1 東京スクエアガーデン B1F
Author: Willian Santos
Review: very pleasant, good location, excellent service

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Ginza Exitmelsa
Address: 5 Chome-7-10 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Lea L
Review: Even though it was crowded in the morning, we managed to get a seat. Looking through the variety of cooked food, they do have a lot more options than what is offered in Singapore. I see more options for vegetarians. Staff is able to understand English so you can order in English. Tried out their offerings that Singapofe doesn't have, and I love it. Great place to have breakfast in Ginza as many places nearby only open at 11AM.

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Ginza Exitmelsa
Address: 5 Chome-7-10 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Andy O.Y
Review: Coffee was ok but table was very dirty, there were food bits, and sticky traces, very disgusting

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Ginza Exitmelsa
Address: 5 Chome-7-10 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Sake Sommelier
Review: Very crowded on the day i went, the trash is overflowing and the tables are not wiped and left as is after customers left.  Very busy i guess… but coffee is the same.

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Ginza Exitmelsa
Address: 5 Chome-7-10 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Boss
Review:
What a great Starbucks, huge, clean and hidden.
Definitely worth coming to relax or work.
Came at 2pm on a weekday and was able to find a space to sit and talk in private.
Even though it's in the basement it didn't feel as it was.
Recommended

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Ginza Exitmelsa
Address: 5 Chome-7-10 Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Chong Peng How
Review: A reprieve from the shopping and crowd. Ample seating but can be crowded

Name: Scoop Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Okubo, 1 Chome−10−15 2階
Author: Sofie
Review: My friend and I visited this cafe at midday on a weekday. It got busy very quickly at around 1:00pm. I highly recommend the watermelon shaved ice. It was delicious! I also really liked the coffee, which had a strong roasted flavour. Service was not great, but I think that’s pretty normal for cafes in Shin-Okubo.

Name: Scoop Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Okubo, 1 Chome−10−15 2階
Author: Allison K
Review:
The cafe is really spacious and perfect for spending the whole afternoon chilling here.
Kind of wish the coffee was served in mug instead of paper cup though.
For some reason, all of the customers are female and some of them asked to take picture with the staff(maybe he’s famous or something?)

Name: Scoop Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Okubo, 1 Chome−10−15 2階
Author: Muri Muri
Review:
On a nice sunny day, get the Einspanner (iced) coffee!! Sooo good 😋 Loved the Orea Banana pudding too.
It's not where Google shows yous, it's next to a grocery shop and you need to go upstairs but worth 😌👌

Name: Scoop Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Okubo, 1 Chome−10−15 2階
Author: Half Rats Studio
Review: A spacious, atmospheric cafe with a nice range of options on the menu.  I was excited to try the white mocha but the einspanner also looked great,  as did the seasonal drink menu, which I didn't see until after I had already ordered. Wifi is available and payment seemed to be only with cash. They had a small terrace section which seems nice to try when it gets warmer. Prices are comparable to other cafes in the area  (around 600/700 yen).

Name: Scoop Coffee
Address: Shinjuku City, Okubo, 1 Chome−10−15 2階
Author: fu
Review:
・Black Cherry Berry Tea ¥650
・Affgato(Matcha) ¥650

2024/04/08 17:00

Name: Alpha Beta Coffee Club
Address: Meguro City, Jiyugaoka, 2 Chome−10−4 ミルシェ自由が丘 3F
Author: Aris
Review:
We had two soy lattes and one of the desserts, all of which were well made. I would have liked a bit more coffee for the price, though.

The café was calm and peaceful, with music to suit its energy.

Service was very friendly and competent.

Overall, a positive experience. I'll return.

Name: Alpha Beta Coffee Club
Address: Meguro City, Jiyugaoka, 2 Chome−10−4 ミルシェ自由が丘 3F
Author: Perry Merrity
Review:
This place is a vibe. Very modern feel with wood and concrete accents. There's also a large patio you can sit on.

I ordered a lemon cold brew and found it really satisfying to sip on a slightly warm day out on the patio. Normally I would order black coffee when I try a new shop, but the cold brew was enticing and it didn't disappoint.

To find the shop, take the elevator to 3F and it'll be on your left.

Name: Alpha Beta Coffee Club
Address: Meguro City, Jiyugaoka, 2 Chome−10−4 ミルシェ自由が丘 3F
Author: Leander Blaich
Review: Nice place where lots of people do work and drinks taste good. I personally recommend the iced Matcha and dirt chai latte.

Name: Alpha Beta Coffee Club
Address: Meguro City, Jiyugaoka, 2 Chome−10−4 ミルシェ自由が丘 3F
Author: Yuko
Review: It’s a convenient cafe near the Jiyugaoka station. It’s relatively less crowded maybe because this cafe is located on the third floor of a building and you have a little bit of difficulty getting there. The vibe is so good that you can relax and enjoy their food and coffee. On weekends at noon, many customers come and then the service gets laggy, so I recommend you to visit here in the morning or evening.

Name: Alpha Beta Coffee Club
Address: Meguro City, Jiyugaoka, 2 Chome−10−4 ミルシェ自由が丘 3F
Author: Sascha Gloede
Review:
I recently had the pleasure of visiting the Alpha Beta Coffee Club, a delightful coffee place situated in the 3rd place. The moment I stepped in, I was greeted by a cozy and inviting atmosphere, with both indoor and outdoor seating options.

The highlight of my experience was undoubtedly the rich and smooth taste of their coffee. Each sip was a heavenly treat, leaving me craving for more. Whether you're a fan of espresso, Latte, Iced Latte, or a simple black coffee, their menu offers a wide variety of options to suit every palate.

What truly set this place apart, however, was the staff. The team at Alpha Beta Coffee Club was not only knowledgeable and passionate about their craft but also incredibly warm and friendly.

Whether you're looking for a quick morning pick-me-up or a relaxing spot to unwind with friends, Alpha Beta Coffee Club is the place to be. With its fantastic coffee, comfortable ambiance, and lovely staff, it's a coffee lover's paradise. I highly recommend paying them a visit—I guarantee you won't be disappointed.

Name: Walkabout Coffee
Address: Chiyoda City, Kanda Nishikicho, 2 Chome−5−25 名古路ビル新館
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Poppins Coffee and Tea
Address: 4 Chome-27-17 Koenjiminami, Suginami City
Author: Bliss
Review:
Stumbled upon this lovely cafe on my trip to Tokyo last month and I was so pleasantly surprised ! Firstly, the exterior and interior are both beautiful, the cafe has such a calm, vintage feeling with polished wood insides and a long bar that makes for the perfect environment to chat with customers and staff and bring joy to your day. The menu is perfectly simple, and everything we had was delicious. I recommend the buttered toast, egg and salad, all so tasty and flavourful but not overcomplicated. The food was so affordable too ! The highlight is the beautiful vintage teacup collection and the friendliness of the couple running the cafe who very kindly and patiently took my parents’ order who aren’t able to speak Japanese like me. I would absolutely recommend a stop here for breakfast, lunch or a snack at any time ! I can’t wait to visit again on my next trip… ありがとう ございました :)

(Excuse the photo of the food, it was presented very neatly, we just managed to make a mess)

Name: Poppins Coffee and Tea
Address: 4 Chome-27-17 Koenjiminami, Suginami City
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: A great place to grab a cup of coffee. The rare cheesecake was pretty solid as well. The man and woman running the place were super nice, and the prices were reasonable. It definitely has an old school feel, which is a good thing here. Fairly smoky inside, but it could have been because I was there at night.

Name: Poppins Coffee and Tea
Address: 4 Chome-27-17 Koenjiminami, Suginami City
Author: Paul Carles
Review: The perfect breakfast spot does exist. It's called Poppins Coffee & Tea, in Koenji, Tokyo. Absolutely delightful ! I will be back tomorrow.

Name: Poppins Coffee and Tea
Address: 4 Chome-27-17 Koenjiminami, Suginami City
Author: ronin
Review: This place is such a cute small coffee shop. They have a very nice menu sample, not too big, not too small. the owner of the shop is so sweet as well as the vibe of the place just feels so homie.

Name: Poppins Coffee and Tea
Address: 4 Chome-27-17 Koenjiminami, Suginami City
Author: Dominika
Review: Very cute place, nice tea and very good cinnamon toast. Amazing athmosphere. :)

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Prime Five Tokyu Plaza Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−2−1 東急プラザ銀座 B1階Ａ区画
Author: moza alnaimi
Review:
Very beautiful place to drink your tea
Clean
Very friendly staff
The food is great
The atmosphere: can’t ask for more

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Prime Five Tokyu Plaza Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−2−1 東急プラザ銀座 B1階Ａ区画
Author: Nik Supatravanij
Review: kicked us out of the store due to their one person one drink policy where to sit down every person must make a purchase even though the store completely empty. terrible policy and customer service.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Prime Five Tokyu Plaza Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−2−1 東急プラザ銀座 B1階Ａ区画
Author: Adri Cal
Review: Really nice place to sit down and enjoy a coffee.  Establishment has an area to sit down and charge your phone .  Quiet place  to rest for a little bit .

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Prime Five Tokyu Plaza Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−2−1 東急プラザ銀座 B1階Ａ区画
Author: Fabio Pizzuto
Review: Beautiful Cafe of the Tully's chain. However, this shop's prices are out of control. 500 yen (about $5) for a normal cup of  espresso is ridiculous. Avoid at all cost.... A generous 1 star.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Prime Five Tokyu Plaza Ginza
Address: Chuo City, Ginza, 5 Chome−2−1 東急プラザ銀座 B1階Ａ区画
Author: Samuel Leung
Review: The service was polite and good. However, instead of the original tea that was ordered, they mistakenly served hot coffee because of different languages. Fortunately, the cashier was very polite and helpful in promptly rectifying the situation by providing a refund, correcting the order, and offering a replacement beverage.

Name: Coffee & Salon
Address: 銀座白亜ビル Ｂ１Ｆ, 6F, Japan, 3-chōme Ginza, Chuo City
Author: Teason Miao
Review:
Got myself a delicious chocolate.

The chocolate milk was delicious.

Name: Coffee & Salon
Address: 銀座白亜ビル Ｂ１Ｆ, 6F, Japan, 3-chōme Ginza, Chuo City
Author: 山羊めえ
Review:
Since the table is matte black, people's fingerprints remain on it very well. I borrowed a duster because I hadn't wiped the table clean, but they handed it to me and that was it.
Leave dirty, unwashed cups on the counter.
I ordered a latte. A glass was brought to me, but is this supposed to not be mixed?
There are no plates in the parfait. I have no idea where to put the herbs on top if the customer doesn't eat them.
I asked. How do you drink a latte? and
There is a straw, so if you need it, you can bring it and mix it.
I've always thought that MUJI was a representative company in Japan, but it's a shame. If this is the case, I don't think it would be a good idea to build a cafe.

Name: Yanaka Coffee
Address: Minato City, Nishishinbashi, 2 Chome−6−3, Echigoya Building, 1F
Author: Robert Blake
Review: A great place to pick up some freshly roasted coffee beans.  If you have 10 minutes, you can pick out your own beans and they will roast them for you.

Name: Yanaka Coffee
Address: Minato City, Nishishinbashi, 2 Chome−6−3, Echigoya Building, 1F
Author: Therese Wong
Review: I use an automatic espresso machine at home and the coffee has never tasted this good. Yanaka offers over 20 types of coffee beans that they roast for you on the spot. They even have 2 types of organic decaf beans, which I don't find often in Tokyo. They sell coffee drinks too but seating is limited. The clientele is mostly salary men. I usually just get the beans.

Name: Yanaka Coffee
Address: Minato City, Nishishinbashi, 2 Chome−6−3, Echigoya Building, 1F
Author: Shorya Gupta
Review: Great coffee and bean selection. They roast it fresh once purchased

Name: Yanaka Coffee
Address: Minato City, Nishishinbashi, 2 Chome−6−3, Echigoya Building, 1F
Author: FlyMe Away
Review: Cozy little cafe in a corner. Coffee is warm n gentle, even on empty stomach. Good coffee. Thanks.

Name: Yanaka Coffee
Address: Minato City, Nishishinbashi, 2 Chome−6−3, Echigoya Building, 1F
Author: CJ Whittaker
Review: Such a great coffee shop. Great staff and selection, great taste, good place to work. Will be back!

Name: Saza Coffee
Address: 2 Chome-17-14 Shinbashi, Minato City
Author: Rishad Rahman
Review: Nice enough coffee but their crepe cake slice was disappointing! The cold strangely separated cream and the hard, grainy crepe layers leaves much to be desired. It is also very sweet.

Name: Saza Coffee
Address: 2 Chome-17-14 Shinbashi, Minato City
Author: Wich
Review: Here on Holiday from Australia for 2 weeks struggling to find a great coffee...until I found this place..like WOWWW! The taste, strength and temperature of the Latte was spot on. Highly recommended if you need a good cuppa!

Name: Saza Coffee
Address: 2 Chome-17-14 Shinbashi, Minato City
Author: 吉田栗栖
Review: Great latte and coffee jelly for coffee lovers. They have a wide range of coffee menu, from affordable coffee blends to 3000yen geishas.  They also have a wide variety of coffee beans  available for purchase as well. It is right in front of the station exit so it's a great place to wait for friends.

Name: Saza Coffee
Address: 2 Chome-17-14 Shinbashi, Minato City
Author: Julian Toedter
Review:
Conveniently located coffee shop outside of JR's Shinbashi station. It's a bit small and cramped but they're quick on your orders, accept every payment option (e.g. pasmo/suica, credit card, R Pay/PayPay, etc.).

Coffee and matcha tea that we ordered were pretty decent. We enjoyed the layer cake as well!

If you're signed up for JRE points, you can get extra points at this store!

Name: Saza Coffee
Address: 2 Chome-17-14 Shinbashi, Minato City
Author: jay inka
Review:
During our recent visit to Tokyo, my companions and I had the pleasure of exploring various cafes in search of a truly exceptional cup of cappuccino. While we encountered numerous establishments, the majority fell short of our expectations. However, we were fortunate enough to stumble upon a hidden gem known as Saza Coffee, located in the charming district of Shinbashi.

Despite its modest size, Saza Coffee boasts an impressive lineup of dedicated baristas who possess an exceptional level of professionalism and talent. The moment we entered, we were greeted by their kind and cheerful staff, setting the stage for an unforgettable coffee experience.

Our expectations were not only met but exceeded when we tasted the cappuccinos prepared by the skilled baristas at Saza Coffee. The attention to detail and precision in crafting each cup was truly remarkable. From the perfectly balanced espresso base to the flawlessly textured milk, every sip offered a harmonious symphony of flavors that delighted our palates.

We were particularly impressed by the baristas' ability to consistently deliver a high-quality cappuccino, showcasing their mastery of the craft. Their passion for coffee was evident in every pour, and their dedication to their art was truly inspiring.

If you find yourself in Tokyo and share our love for a good cup of cappuccino, we wholeheartedly recommend paying a visit to Saza Coffee. Despite its small size, this unassuming establishment houses a team of talented professionals who have mastered the art of coffee-making. Prepare to indulge in the best cappuccino Tokyo has to offer, and allow the friendly atmosphere and exceptional service to enhance your overall experience. Saza Coffee in Shinbashi is undoubtedly a destination worth seeking out for coffee enthusiasts seeking perfection in every sip.

Name: AMAZING COFFEE
Address: Meguro City, Aobadai, 1 Chome−18−7 カスタリア中目黒 211 1F6区画
Author: ELSE
Review: I really loved the ambience of the shop. It's simple, quaint and rustic. I ordered the caramel latte and was pleasantly surprised that it wasn't too sweet. The MPV is the milk. It was creamy and delicious for a latte.

Name: AMAZING COFFEE
Address: Meguro City, Aobadai, 1 Chome−18−7 カスタリア中目黒 211 1F6区画
Author: Lera Lera
Review: Great coffee and service, and super cute vibe

Name: AMAZING COFFEE
Address: Meguro City, Aobadai, 1 Chome−18−7 カスタリア中目黒 211 1F6区画
Author: H Katharina
Review: Tried the really yummy Happy Latte, some sweet spots for a fully great treat. Queuing takes time here, too long for my taste. No cookies or cake or other is sold to go with the coffee.

Name: AMAZING COFFEE
Address: Meguro City, Aobadai, 1 Chome−18−7 カスタリア中目黒 211 1F6区画
Author: HECTOR NAVA
Review: We were there with friends some days ago. I tried glace as always which was so-so, can't say it was good. But for only coffee break its nice place.

Name: AMAZING COFFEE
Address: Meguro City, Aobadai, 1 Chome−18−7 カスタリア中目黒 211 1F6区画
Author: 官沛柔
Review: It’s a good place to have an amazing coffee 👍

Name: Murrma Coffee Stop
Address: 2 Chome-6-1 Honmachi, Shibuya City
Author: Xiro Ar (Alphonz)
Review:
A coffee stop where the polite and pleasant shopkeeper serves meticulously crafted fairly tasty coffee varieties.
A cup on your ordered beans is slightly more expensive than the fast “Today’s coffee” but worth a try to appreciate the value. Cash payment only.
You can have your favourite one inside or just outside the shop if you like though the space are limited.
A little pity that its opening days and hours are also somewhat limited maybe due to a solo-run store, like Mondays, Tuesdays and late afternoons are off.

Name: Murrma Coffee Stop
Address: 2 Chome-6-1 Honmachi, Shibuya City
Author: Justin Tan
Review: Very good coffee tucked away at the outskirts of Shinjuku area. This small café has a very quaint look and chill vibe to on the outside to just sit and people watch. Definitely worth checking out if you are in the area. White coffee was very creamy and nutty while the black was well balance, not too fruity not too floral.

Name: Murrma Coffee Stop
Address: 2 Chome-6-1 Honmachi, Shibuya City
Author: Jason Chuei
Review: Cool pocket cafe with chill vibes, a welcome reprieve from the busy main road outside. Wooden raw decor scattered with coffee beans and sacks. Chai latte made with Prana Chai, always good. Cash only.

Name: Murrma Coffee Stop
Address: 2 Chome-6-1 Honmachi, Shibuya City
Author: Peter Galante

Name: Murrma Coffee Stop
Address: 2 Chome-6-1 Honmachi, Shibuya City
Author: Claire Williamson
Review: A lovely coffee shop near Hatsudai station. A variety of beans roasted in-house (I had the Peru, it was a lovely tea-like light roast) and a small selection of pound cake and scones (also beer). Only four or so seats so to-go would also be an excellent option as you wander around the neighborhood.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee Shimokitazawa
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−24−5 シモキタフロント 1F
Author: Raluca Antuca Bordot
Review: Absolutely delicious. I loved the warm sandwich and the limited edition honey latte was one of the best I ever had. The staff was also friendly and spoke English. I highly recommend it, especially as a sweet break from thrift shopping in the area.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee Shimokitazawa
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−24−5 シモキタフロント 1F
Author: Foong Yee Loh
Review: Pretty good coffee here. You can’t miss this shop as it’s located right outside the train station. It has an interesting interior layout too. Had a hot latte and iced latte, extra shot. Both were good.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee Shimokitazawa
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−24−5 シモキタフロント 1F
Author: thomas sipes
Review: I seek out great espresso, specifically cortados or gibraltars, and beans wherever I travel (we're 3rd wave people). I've been to sarutahiko in Kyoto and was wildly impressed. In Shimo They are just as great  with  a delicate fruity and pineapple flavor to the beans. They have a San Remo machine, which makes fantastic espressos. We're not into lattes, and that seems to be the only choice on their menu. How to explain  a cortado  was a challenge and some Google translation. The staff are amazing, The barista seemed to know what she was doing. So in the end they served us a shot of  espresso and a small cup of cold milk. Granted it was 90 degrees outside...so they do understand Iced drinks too.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee Shimokitazawa
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−24−5 シモキタフロント 1F
Author: Ivan Michael Pung
Review: Very short walk from the rail station. Staff are friendly and they know their coffee. Make such an effort to brew my coffee, I’m really impress.

Name: Sarutahiko Coffee Shimokitazawa
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−24−5 シモキタフロント 1F
Author: Charis Wong
Review:
very nice staff. Will serve you with patience and ask you for coffee tasting. We tried Christmas Tiramisu Latte and Honey latte. Nice to have a drink and relax after shopping.
Very near to the 下北沢station東口

Name: Beck's Coffee Shop
Address: 5 Chome-27-8 Ueno, Taito City
Author: Danielle Roy
Review: We really liked Beck's Coffee Shop, so we visited a second time before leaving. The egg and ham sandwich with coffee was great. We enjoyed the self-service machine to place our order and read the menu in English. The tables are quite close together, but a good amount of seating.

Name: Beck's Coffee Shop
Address: 5 Chome-27-8 Ueno, Taito City
Author: Hang Man Helen Fung
Review:
I was having breakfast there and the toast set with the whipped cream was very delicious! 🤤
The location is just near the station!

Name: Beck's Coffee Shop
Address: 5 Chome-27-8 Ueno, Taito City
Author: Susan Tan
Review: Ordering is done via a self help machine, which has various languages to choose from. Certainly tourist friendly. However their coffee wasn't that great. Nice, quiet atmosphere though.

Name: Beck's Coffee Shop
Address: 5 Chome-27-8 Ueno, Taito City
Author: Little L (and the family)
Review: I found this coffee shop when I was exploring around okachimachi for a bite to eat and then I found this chain coffee shop nearby the station which was already a good thing for me since I can easily walk back to the station. I ordered an iced Americano there and tried it without any fuss. The coffee was very light which made it easy and relaxing for me to drink it.

Name: Beck's Coffee Shop
Address: 5 Chome-27-8 Ueno, Taito City
Author: Dee A
Review: Glad they have an English menu (ordering via touch screen)! Great and very quick service. The food was also good.  Perfect for a quick bite.   They also have outlets to charge your devices :)

Name: Coffee Shop Top
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−8 道玄坂センタービル 地下１階
Author: Mikel Carr (Reckless Thor)
Review: A tiny bit expensive and no wifi but decent food and interesting atmosphere away from the hustle and bustle. Smoke is heavy in the air. Make sure to drink water and don't stay too long.

Name: Coffee Shop Top
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−8 道玄坂センタービル 地下１階
Author: Hamish
Review:
Yes people smoke in there. But that’s just Japan, and it’s pretty obvious before or as soon as you walk in.

No they don’t do espresso. They do real coffee, and it’s very good.

Ignore the reviews from intolerant American clowns.

Name: Coffee Shop Top
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−8 道玄坂センタービル 地下１階
Author: Chung C.K.
Review:
Heavy cigarette smell in the shop. Nearly all customers here would smoke. Not recommend to girls.
Coffee is not bad though.

Name: Coffee Shop Top
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−8 道玄坂センタービル 地下１階
Author: Robert Orynich
Review: Do NOT go here unless you are an avid tobacco smoker. It smells like US bars in the 90s. Also, they don’t serve espresso.

Name: Coffee Shop Top
Address: Shibuya City, Dogenzaka, 2 Chome−29−8 道玄坂センタービル 地下１階
Author: Junichi Uozumi
Review:
A traditional style Japanese cafe.

Note: smoking allowed.

Name: Hoshino Coffee
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 2 Chome−12−1 セントラル２１ビル 2F
Author: Fon Rize
Review:
Cute and cosy café, nice and friendly staff but a little tired due to the amount of guests.

Drinks and desserts are 10/10, especially soufflé with anglaise sauce, it's just heavenly.

Name: Hoshino Coffee
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 2 Chome−12−1 セントラル２１ビル 2F
Author: Denisa-Stefania-Adriana Tudor
Review: It’s a good place to have breakfast, the fluffy strawberry pancakes were very nice. I’m not a fan of their coffee, as it was too bitter and roasted for my taste, but the iced tea was nice - strong and full of flavour. Also, they don’t have any vegan food options - they do have plain toast but I’m not sure if that’s vegan. Queuing time is small or nonexistent, as they have lots of locations around Tokyo.

Name: Hoshino Coffee
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 2 Chome−12−1 セントラル２１ビル 2F
Author: Francois Pradeau
Review: Very cozy place, particularly on a rainy day. Pancake was very good and so was the coffee! Easy to order, English menu available, fast service, nice jazz music. A restful stop from the city crowds.

Name: Hoshino Coffee
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 2 Chome−12−1 セントラル２１ビル 2F
Author: Scott Savage
Review: Old school timber panelled breakfast diner. Souffle pancakes and great french toast. Nice to offset the sugar with a side salad. Great service. Nice booths overlooking the intersection. Take the lift to the second floor.

Name: Hoshino Coffee
Address: Taito City, Ueno, 2 Chome−12−1 セントラル２１ビル 2F
Author: Dingo Ko
Review: Nice cup of coffee! Good and simple tradition Japan style curry and napoleon spaghetti just like in First Love drama. A nice place to refreshing yourself.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Tsukiji St. Luke Street
Address: 3 Chome-6-2 Tsukiji, Chuo City
Author: doris
Review: Excellent place to relax. The staff are friendly and professional. The place is clean. There are plenty of desks with charging stations for people to work at comfortably. Everyone there is quiet and respectful. High ceiling with tall windows all around providing good natural sunlight. Their free wifi there was strong and stable, and does not automatically disconnect you like some other option laces. If you’re looking for somewhere spacious and comfortable to read a book or work, this place is it.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Tsukiji St. Luke Street
Address: 3 Chome-6-2 Tsukiji, Chuo City
Author: Luis M.
Review: The coffee itself is not amazing, but the place is really good, especially to work. You have many plugs to charge your phone or connect your laptop, there’s free Wi-Fi and a huge window that brings good light and lets you check the street. There are many tables, so it’s not difficult to find a seat. They have plastic screens between seats as a measure against COVID-19, apart from sanitizer gel and employees wearing masks. Friendly staff.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Tsukiji St. Luke Street
Address: 3 Chome-6-2 Tsukiji, Chuo City
Author: Diana Peko
Review: Very spacious venue with lots of tables, free wifi, and (my favourite) a high ceiling and a beautiful window. There is a nice working space where you can charge your laptop. Coffee is more expensive than in other places, that is why I gave it 4 stars.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Tsukiji St. Luke Street
Address: 3 Chome-6-2 Tsukiji, Chuo City
Author: Hatsujoukikuma naster
Review:
Not for coffee Aficionados, the coffee is not great but the place is  spacious and nice.
Charging spots are available for free.

Name: Tully’s Coffee - Tsukiji St. Luke Street
Address: 3 Chome-6-2 Tsukiji, Chuo City
Author: Zachary Hubbell
Review: This is one of our favorite coffee shops in Chuo-ku. The cafe is large with high ceilings and the cafe offers free and very fast WiFi.

Name: Bookends Coffee Service
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−11−17 佐藤ビル １F
Author: Tom James
Review: Cute little coffee shop. Had an amazing caramel cream latte. Friendly staff and nice vibe.

Name: Bookends Coffee Service
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−11−17 佐藤ビル １F
Author: Ayoob Ilyas
Review: The shop is really small and has a nice vibe to it. It has old and unique posters, books and many things on display, along with the music and has a relaxed and retro atmosphere. I had the coffee jelly with ice cream (and coffee), which tasted pretty good. The prices are quite low as well, I haven't seen a cheaper cafe yet in Tokyo. The beans seemed to be roasted quite a bit.

Name: Bookends Coffee Service
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−11−17 佐藤ビル １F
Author: Hannah Goh
Review:
my flat white was great - nutty and robust, perfectly warm so i can drink immediately (i love it when baristas nail the temperature!)

the pastries were delicious and sitting outside made for a perfect afternoon of people-watching and reading. music was really good too.

Name: Bookends Coffee Service
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−11−17 佐藤ビル １F
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: Really enjoyed my experience here, from the decor to the service to the coffee. The staff was super friendly and helpful, and the iced coffee was brewed perfectly. The ambiance is very eclectic, with the Millennium Falcon even making an appearance. I will come back for sure. Great option in the area and close to the station.

Name: Bookends Coffee Service
Address: Setagaya City, Kitazawa, 2 Chome−11−17 佐藤ビル １F
Author: Andrew Lam
Review: We finished lunch in the area and needed a caffeine fix so we decided we would try this little coffee shop out. The decor theme is vintage-hipster kind of vibes and the inside is very well decorated, we loved sitting inside to have coffee. We came here in December and the shop had all  Christmas themed decorations and cups, very festive! They have very good selection of coffee and drinks with pictures on the menu posted on the door. The coffee was really good, smooth and there are some small baked goods like cakes that go well with the drinks. Overall would definitely return for coffee!

Name: COFFEE・KAN Asakusa store(珈琲館 浅草店)
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−6−1 味乃一番ビル
Author: Bryant Zeng
Review:
We stopped here for a busy morning around Asakusa.

The hotcakes were good. I've never had red beans as a pancake topping before, which was really tasty and interesting.

The only thing I'd have to point out was the wait times. It took a bit to get a table, to get a menu, then get our food. It was the morning so slightly understandable on wait times.

Name: COFFEE・KAN Asakusa store(珈琲館 浅草店)
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−6−1 味乃一番ビル
Author: Stefanie Molina
Review: Highly recommend this place! We came here for breakfast two days in a row. Their pancakes are so delicious and I enjoyed their coffee as well. We are back home now and the kids are still talking about the pancakes from Coffee Kan. Will visit again next time when we are in Tokyo.

Name: COFFEE・KAN Asakusa store(珈琲館 浅草店)
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−6−1 味乃一番ビル
Author: Jeff Mak
Review:
I've ordered 3 morning sets for me and my parents. I tried the pancakes and Blue Mountain Coffee.

For pancakes, it tastes good and likes eating a cake, can have a try for pancake lover.

For the blue mountain coffee, it's a joke as a scaled coffee shop. As a hand drip coffee lover, I can say this coffee (black) is full of the taste of astringency, really not like a good blue mountain coffee, I will give zero for the blue mountain, not worth to pay extra money to try it.

For the service, my father is disabled. When we arrived the coffee shop, I showed our number of person to a waitress but she has no reaction and at last skip us when they arranged the table to the queue.  Luckily the table before us left due to some reason. I am not saying their service is bad, they have some waiters very hardworking, I can only give an average score to their service.

Overall, it's still a good coffee shop if you would find a place to take some rest.

Name: COFFEE・KAN Asakusa store(珈琲館 浅草店)
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−6−1 味乃一番ビル
Author: Indra Isnato
Review: really like eating here, especially the dessert is very delicious. The location is also very comfortable and Instagrammable. Don't forget to take pictures, IG: @Indra_Isnato

Name: COFFEE・KAN Asakusa store(珈琲館 浅草店)
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−6−1 味乃一番ビル
Author: Teresa K.
Review: Cute place! Lots of wonderful brunch options and lots of variations of their sumibi (charcoal fire) coffee. I got the iced “sumibi cafe au lait harmony”, which was beautifully smooth, and their seasonal lemon mascarpone pancake set with choco coffee. There was ice cream in both the main dish and my choco coffee which was a surprise. The choco coffee was too sweet for me, as there was choco ice cream on top and choco syrup at the bottom. Service was lovely. They also sell bags of coffee and some other merchandise near the cash register. Cute, lovely place to have breakfast/brunch/lunch!

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Kaminarimon
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−18−2 雷門旅館
Author: Kyle Scouter
Review:
This Starbucks is busy, but the level of hospitality is outstanding.

When I visited, the barista was communicating in multiple languages—English, Chinese, Russian, Spanish, and Korean. It’s rare to see someone so talented.

If you want an experience beyond just coffee, this is the place to be.

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Kaminarimon
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−18−2 雷門旅館
Author: Ras
Review:
This Starbucks has all the views 😍

This is an incredibly busy Starbucks BUT the service was so smooth and it was very quick. It has such amazing views of the Kaminarimon Gate. Potentially one of the best Starbucks I’ve been to.

If you go onto the second floor, there is a balcony area where you can sit and relax and take in the beautiful view. 🤩

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Kaminarimon
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−18−2 雷門旅館
Author: Ornella B
Review: It's always a must in my opinion to go to an Starbucks at every country you visit. I thought Starbucks in Japan might be better than Starbucks in Korea but it wasn't the case? I think it was similar or even more alike to the US.. there are some items you can only catch on their season. For example in spring they have the Sakura chiffon, I've always wanted to try it. It was fluffy, not dry, and decent Sakura flavor. They didn't have their Sakura drinks anymore, so it was dissapointing. I ordered matcha and it was if not the same, super similar with overseas. Nevertheless, visit a Starbucks in every country you go to!

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Kaminarimon
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−18−2 雷門旅館
Author: Clarisse De Guzman
Review: 5 stars for the very friendly staff, and the convenient location in a high-tourist area. We went around 8:00AM so it wasn't busy at all and there weren't crowds around Kaminarimon yet. We tried some seasonal menu items like pumpkin and matcha mochi in October. The rest of the menu is pretty standard for a Starbucks.

Name: Starbucks Coffee - Kaminarimon
Address: Taito City, Asakusa, 1 Chome−18−2 雷門旅館
Author: April Kerslake
Review: Was nice to pop into Starbucks to get away from the huge crowds of Senso-ji. It wasn’t overly busy so we had a lovely view of senso-ji sitting on the balcony. They have soy milk and give you a ticket that says “soy milk” to hand to the barista when you pick up your drink so you know your drink is soy milk. They also had this plant based/vegan matcha roll which was delicious.

Name: DUCT COFFEE LAB
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisunishi, 1 Chome−33−18 コート代官山 1F
Author: Chris Henry
Review:
We love the puffins!! It’s like, a flat muffin? I had the blueberry and cream cheese and my daughter had the chocolate one. Both were super tasty and not too sweet. Also, not sure if it’s cause we came near the closing time, but the puffins were 50% off.

The cafe latte was quite decent and well made, but not my favorite. The espresso was a bit more bitter than sour. So if you like that, you’d probably love it.

Name: DUCT COFFEE LAB
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisunishi, 1 Chome−33−18 コート代官山 1F
Author: ruth tan
Review: lovely coffee shop we stumbled upon on the way to the river. came here with 6 people and everyone managed to find something they liked! the stand outs were the milk tea and gingerbread latte (may be seasonal). got both with oat milk and they tasted great :)

Name: DUCT COFFEE LAB
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisunishi, 1 Chome−33−18 コート代官山 1F
Author: Hichem Merabet
Review:
A good place if you want to work on your computer or just read a book.
The latte is delicious, with a good amount of coffee. I also tried those kind of cookies bread which was delicious !

It’s a bit expensive but at least you are sure to enjoy what you are eating or drinking.

You can even charge your phone and computer there.

I recommend

Check my travel in my Instagram : @im_xyer

Name: DUCT COFFEE LAB
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisunishi, 1 Chome−33−18 コート代官山 1F
Author: Therese Wong
Review: I was the first customer so I was able to grab the best seat at the table facing the street. Ordered an ice hand-drip coffee. I usually prefer a dark roast but it was ok. Will try the yummy-looking "puffins" next time.

Name: DUCT COFFEE LAB
Address: Shibuya City, Ebisunishi, 1 Chome−33−18 コート代官山 1F
Author: Justin Shircliff
Review: Quality spot to grab a coffee. The drip coffee was especially good, but the latte was on bar with some of the best I’ve tried. The space is cool with a nice vibe. The staff was friendly and helpful in picking what bean to try. I’ll add this to my list of regular coffee strips, so I think you should give it a try.

Name: Yamamoto Coffee
Address: 3 Chome-17-11 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Eric
Review: Nicest Coffee store in Tokyo for fresh beans like you want them. Great service and every little machine you want, they have it.

Name: Yamamoto Coffee
Address: 3 Chome-17-11 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Kevin Kwok
Review: This place has been here forever. Lots of great coffee equipment but don't forget to get some very affordable freshly roasted beans as well. 100g as low as ¥550. And they can grind them for free of you ask nicely.

Name: Yamamoto Coffee
Address: 3 Chome-17-11 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Cameron Pratt
Review: Really nice shop selling pretty much anything a coffee enthusiast might want. So many different brewers and ceramics available, it trully is a nice shop for me. I wanted to get something from Origami while traveling in Japan and they had a really beautiful red tasting cup from them that I really love. Shop owner is kind and friendly.

Name: Yamamoto Coffee
Address: 3 Chome-17-11 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Lauren Bowman
Review: Like others have said, this shop has a truly impressive array of coffee brewing equipment and accessories for most hand-drip systems. The clerks speak some English and are super friendly and helpful. Don't pass it by if you're a coffee lover visiting Shinjuku!

Name: Yamamoto Coffee
Address: 3 Chome-17-11 Shinjuku, Shinjuku City
Author: Megan Griffith
Review:
Great little coffee place, got to see them roast their own beans in store while I was going downstairs to look at their teas.

They have a lovely selection of loose leaf black teas downstairs, as well as herbal teas perfect for sleep and making your own brews at home. Very interesting small store with antique coffee drips, grinders, brewers, along with unroasted beans, and an assortment of pre roasted, unique beans. Very cool store! Love it! I always walk by when I can, even if I don’t plan to go inside so I can smell the store on my way to work ;)

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Afrika Lovers
Review:
Truth seemed to be a guarantee for great quality of food and beverages and amazing atmosphere. Where the latter still stands, the former is a bit shaky recently.

We ordered a free-range burger that was way too greasy, more than blend taste wise and somehow just not doing it.

The pastries (chocolate croissants) were better. The teas (lemon rooibos & earl grey) were good whereas the orange juice was not made from fresh oranges but concentrate.

Come on Truth you can do better. Almost a 2 stars for the food but we don’t want to be too harsh. Will be back soon to see if there are improvements…

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Milo
Review:
What a truly awesome experience! Walking by their little unassuming outside coffeebar, I asked for a seat and was absolutely astounded by what awaited inside! The setup is incredible, absolutely incredible. Awesome music, amazing fun staff, cool antiques, mechanical design, coffee roasting, pleasing aesthetic, delicious food and outstanding coffee. This place has it all!
Its been a minute since any shop has had such an effect on me. I went in with the intention of starting a book I had bought down the street but instead just stared in amazement at this wondrous place. There are so many neat things going on, apparently they also have fantastic breads and pastries but I won’t even get into that. My favorite was the rotating coffee bean stand and amazing server Siya ✊🏽💜
A tremendous thank you for this great coffee experience.

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Toni Henning
Review:
I thoroughly enjoyed the outing to Truth Coffee. Their coffee IS amazing. I had a Resurrection Latte, and my son had a flat white.
I really appreciate that they don't charge extra for milk substitutes. The food was extra yummy. The decor is unusual - everywhere you look - such interesting things. The menu is fun to read! I love the way the waiting staff dress, too. I have one little complaint. When we ordered, I asked if they have muffins. Our waiter said no and proceeded to indicate on the menu what I could choose from. Clearly, I was in the mood for something sweet or baked if I asked for a muffin, right? Only after our food arrived, I saw other patrons with cake. It would have been great if our waiter had told me that, while they don't have muffins, they have other baked goods. Luckily, the avo on toast was so delicious that I got over it. Haha! Next time I go, I will order from the bakery.

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Tarcisio Borges
Review: We absolutely loved Truth Coffee! The atmosphere is incredibly unique with its steampunk-inspired decor, making it feel like you’ve stepped into a different world. The coffee is truly exceptional—carefully roasted and served with great attention to detail. The staff is knowledgeable and friendly, guiding you through their impressive selection. It’s no wonder this spot has been recognized globally as one of the best coffee shops. Definitely a must-visit in Cape Town!

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Pete T (epetet)
Review: A funky atmosphere with fantastic coffee and unique food options.  More than just a touristy spot they have a great variety of coffees and bakery products as well as kitchen-made menu options.

Name: The Backyard Café
Address: 4 Sol Cohen Rd, Heathfield, Cape Town
Author: Barbara Newport
Review: Great venue and food, but it was cold, I suggest indoor heaters. No liquor license, my companion would have liked a glass of wine with her meal. Beautifully decorated and definitely recommended.

Name: The Backyard Café
Address: 4 Sol Cohen Rd, Heathfield, Cape Town
Author: Skye Doust
Review:
This out of the way Café is a very pleasant surprise. Lovely atmosphere inside, with a covered and uncovered area, full of greenery. Summer, our waiter, was delightful, friendly, and helpful.
The menu is broad enough to appeal to a wide variety of tastes, and the food itself is very well made and incredibly tasty.

Name: The Backyard Café
Address: 4 Sol Cohen Rd, Heathfield, Cape Town
Author: Liesl Jegels
Review: Beautiful setting, I want a backyard just like that one. Cosy and tranquil. Only thing water kept dripping on the guest head and into food from the outside overhead covering.  As people are over their hair🤣.. Excellent service, professional, just awesome. Thank you.

Name: The Backyard Café
Address: 4 Sol Cohen Rd, Heathfield, Cape Town
Author: Linda Henderson
Review: Backyard Cafe hosted our Wedding Anniversary Brunch on 8th June - what a wonderful event! Attentive and friendly staff, absolutely delicious food, cosy fire - it was a superb event.  We can highly recommend Backyard Cafe

Name: The Backyard Café
Address: 4 Sol Cohen Rd, Heathfield, Cape Town
Author: Sybz Solomons
Review: Had a woman's day breakfast on a very cold day. Lovely place, beautiful decor, and very reasonable pricing. A pleasant visit for us a rowdy crowd of ladies. Thank you!

Name: Caffe Neo
Address: 129 Beach Rd, Mouille Point, Cape Town
Author: Isabel Pieterse
Review: An absolutey lovely spot for a breakfast or luch - overlooking the well known lighthouse with ocean views. This is an unpretencious gem offering greek menu items at very reasonable prices. I had their cuppaccino - absolutely amazing, and for lunch had a Pita with chicken and my hubby had a mouth watering mousaka  - We both really enjoyed our meal - and the staff was so friendly and attentive with quick service.

Name: Caffe Neo
Address: 129 Beach Rd, Mouille Point, Cape Town
Author: khwezi mbuyisa
Review:
The food was great and the waitress that was assisting me was out of the world. She is kind and served me with a smile all the time.

Guys from Azurro are very nice and accommodating. They make you feel like a VIP
Thank you Muzi ( not sure if I have your name correct)
Thank you Nikita- keep on shining that bright smile

Name: Caffe Neo
Address: 129 Beach Rd, Mouille Point, Cape Town
Author: Alma Pretorius
Review:
Monday 19/08/2024
We stayed in La Splendida Hotel, literally 2 blocks away from Cafe Neo.  It was time for breakfast.  I couldn't find ANY menu online, so we went in blind.  I chose to wear an egg and spatula earrings.
Cafe Neo is Greek-based.  Hein ordered a breakfast sandwich - eggs, bacon on Turkish bread with sesame seeds.  I ordered a Spinakopita and the Greek breakfast (pita, taramasalata, lots of olives, fresh cucumber batons, baby tomatoes and feta cheese.

Sat on patio - overlooked the sea.  Glorious day!!!  After Ashley our waiter took our orders, another guy stopped by our table - asked if we ordered eggs - 'cause they kinda ran out of eggs, but .... maybe they can borrow my earring.  Hahahah!!!!!  For a few seconds I thought he was serious!!!

Food was absolutely stunning!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!  Will go again.

Name: Caffe Neo
Address: 129 Beach Rd, Mouille Point, Cape Town
Author: Jolande Scheepers
Review: Lovely place with beautiful views. Their croissants and coffee are really good.

Name: Caffe Neo
Address: 129 Beach Rd, Mouille Point, Cape Town
Author: Ryoji Yamada
Review: Well meaning place with delightful view.  Super slow service and my order was wrong despite repeating it several times.  Ordered a veggie omelette with cheddar cheese.  Received an omelette with a soupy mess of tomato, huge chunks of bell pepper and overcooked “caramelized” onions, no cheese.  Iced latte was not good, the espresso was poured over the ice watering it down and making it taste like instant coffee.  Smoothie was good, not the best but very fresh and decently tasty.  Rye toast was superb!  As were the fresh slices of avocado and cucumber.

Name: Starlings
Address: 94 Belvedere Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Barbara Newport
Review: Just had tea and hot chocolate with a friend. Relaxed atmosphere and lovely little outdoor tea garden. Staff very friendly.  Definitely recommended.

Name: Starlings
Address: 94 Belvedere Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Joshua Coetzee
Review:
Overall experience was slightly underwhelming. My issue in particular was around the fact that i was charged R88 (R80 + tip refer image of bill) for a coffee, probably the most expensive coffee in Cape Town… it was meant to be a triple cappuccino. Then, if the coffee was any good, maybe i could be ok with the price, but unfortunately it was mediocre at best.

When addressed with the manager on duty, it was apparent that the manager was not empowered to deal with situations like this.

This venue was very disappointing and sorry to do this, but certainly would not recommend it.

Name: Starlings
Address: 94 Belvedere Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Toni Cooke
Review: Very cosy. The outdoor area is very relaxing with a mixture of sun & shade streaming through the trees.  Indeed a charming place to meet for a cuppa & a treat. The salmon quiche and salad was amazing. Thanks to our server who was very friendly and so patient with us. I definitely recommend a visit.

Name: Starlings
Address: 94 Belvedere Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Jacqueline Short
Review: To the owner or manager of the Starlings coffee shop. I hope you don't mind me sending a review about the business. Well it is a very nice place and the customers come to have breakfast or lunch at the coffe shop and also the service from the waiter or waitress is very good. And it is a pleasure able place to visit as well.😃

Name: Starlings
Address: 94 Belvedere Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Ok-Geum Kwon
Review:
You can park at the entrance of the restaurant, but it's narrow and there's not enough space, so park by the side of the road. The food is delicious and plentiful. The coffee was good, too. The staff were kind. The bathroom was clean, too. There is also an outdoor table. All right, but there may be bird poo on the chair, so please take a good look.🤪
It's a good restaurant I want to visit again.
There is no play area for children.

Name: Saint James Cafe
Address: 43 Station Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: PN
Review:
Over priced little coffee shop in Observatory.
Had to wait for a table as they seem to be popular. Paid R80 for a simple sandwich.

Name: Saint James Cafe
Address: 43 Station Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Matthew Alberts
Review: This place is priced higher than it should be, service could be better (more rotational waiters). Pastries are always finished by 11am even before (so they could make more), food is a bit overpriced. Atmosphere is great for a new meeting place or working area.

Name: Saint James Cafe
Address: 43 Station Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Jess Vermeulen
Review: I absolutely adore this cafe. I go to get coffee at least twice a week with my friends. The staff are always super friendly and are quick when getting our orders ready. Perfect stop between lectures!

Name: Saint James Cafe
Address: 43 Station Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Moena Weiss
Review: Best coffee spot in obz!! Such good vibes and the most amazing staff! Definitely recommend!!

Name: Saint James Cafe
Address: 43 Station Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: E L
Review: Not impressed after hearing good things. Meal prep takes upwards of 40min and zero check in by staff while you wait. Staff literally just dump your coffee on your table while talking with eachother. I don't know if it's because they are tipped before giving the service or what. But especially the waitress had a very patronizing tone should you inquire on what's going on with your meal. Implying its the customers fault that they apparently have too little stove plates for their number of tables ( even if you arrived while it was pretty quiet. All in all, even though the portion size is good the service and seating is not the best.. coffee is good though.

Name: Café Frank
Address: 160 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Carole-Ann van der Feyst
Review: Very nice coffee.  I think sandwich was a bit too pricy, and not special.  I should have gotten lunch meal. A main and one side. That looked very tasty.

Name: Café Frank
Address: 160 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Chantal Naidoo
Review: The food was good, the setting lovely.  Need to try a lot more on the menu

Name: Café Frank
Address: 160 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Theo Giannelos
Review:
I am a regular at Cafe Frank and the food is of a consistently high standard.
Besides the regular items on the menu, it is the meals chalked up daily on a board featuring tasty soups, main meals, salad bowls that provide variety. I have never been disappointed with the quality of the food. I had a Malaysian dhal and Hungarian goulash soup - outstanding. The dhal was so good that I ordered an additional portion to take home!
The service is excellent and the atmosphere casual and easygoing.

Name: Café Frank
Address: 160 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Tatenda Chidora
Review:
Loved the restaurant
Great service
Offers delivery
Food was so fresh and have a great pastry section.

Name: Café Frank
Address: 160 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Dani Du Toit
Review:
If you are looking for fresh, delicious, healthy and exciting food then look no further than Cafe Frank.

I have been consistently wowed by how absolutely YUM every single thing is.

HIGHLY RECOMMEND IF I COULD GIVE 10 STARS I WOULD.

Name: The Blue Cafe
Address: 13 Brownlow Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Natalia Flores
Review: Beautiful place with amazing food. We had the tomato soup and beef lasagna. Also got carrot cake, butternut lasagna and bread to go. The carrot cake is one of the best I've tried. I highly recommend the place.

Name: The Blue Cafe
Address: 13 Brownlow Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Charel Olivier
Review:
Good coffee and food.
The chicken curry was not hot. The way I prefer it.
It needs a bit of flavourful though.
The lasagne was very tasty.

Name: The Blue Cafe
Address: 13 Brownlow Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Renee Richter
Review: Adorable neighborhood cafe and store. Lovely homey vibe. Incredible food and decor! Would come here again and again!

Name: The Blue Cafe
Address: 13 Brownlow Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Miroda Otto
Review: Stopped here on our mtb ride and had laugen bread rolls. The coffee, almond croissants and bread was amazing. Great atmosphere. Will definitely be back

Name: The Blue Cafe
Address: 13 Brownlow Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Colleen Mclintock
Review: Booked a table for 5 outside on the deck yesterday with Faith, I was assured it would all be in order but when we arrived the table was not ready, seems like there was a double booking. Since I was asked to write my name and contact dets on a random piece of paper I am not surprised. Jessica tried to help and apologised. We went down stairs to start with a really professional water Franklin, he took our order perfectly.  We ended up in our spot after about 30 minutes and enjoyed that experience much more. It looks like a very challenging space for the staff who work there. Thank you for all the effort, just a suggestion that a digital booking system would ensure people get the tables they book for. Such a popular spot 💕

Name: Beezle Bistro
Address: 86 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Caylee Lombard
Review:
Beezle is a hidden gem!
We were on our way home but stopped by for a quick drink order late at night. They were still open and so happy to help. They were so friendly and helpful, I would come back just for them!

The food was amazing, the drinks are good.

The atmosphere is phenomenal and so so cool.

Name: Beezle Bistro
Address: 86 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Maryke Badenhorst
Review: Delightfully friendly staff. Lovely interior. Great breakfast menu. Everything was delish. Highly recommended the Eggs Benedict with salmon and the Turkish eggs. Those who had the 3slice toasties struggled to finish it all!

Name: Beezle Bistro
Address: 86 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: G P
Review:
Good place for coffee and a quick bite, didn't get a chance to try out food but it surely looked appetizing.
I had a coffee and biscotti, one cannot go wrong with either of these choices in a good Café/bistro. Both were good.
Service was quick, décor resembles a rock band's rehearsal area and I loved it. Apart from Sonder's Café, I liked this place in this area. Will try the breakfast options next time.

If you are in the area do give them a visit.

Name: Beezle Bistro
Address: 86 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Andrew Draper
Review:
Wonderful family run bistro where you can find wholesome, tasty, innovative and passionate food that tastes like it’s been made by people who love people.
Highly recommended with chilled friendly staff and music and the three layered three cheese toasted sandwich was incredibly decadent and elevated, like everything on the menu, classic comfort food to the next level.

Name: Beezle Bistro
Address: 86 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Roy Lyle
Review: So our family of 8 went here on late Sunday afternoon and had a great meal. Everyone in ur clan is super fussy and yet everyone of us thought the meal and service was excellent. The buttermilk fried chicken is some of the best I have ever had and the burgers were super juicy and very tasty, again right up there with the best I have tasted. We even had to go on further and have cheesecake afterwards. I don't often give a place 5 stars but there you go we definitely had a 5 star meal.

Name: Empire Cafe
Address: 11 York Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Chantel Nienaber
Review: The food and wine are always perfect, we go there every time we have a tattoo done around the corner. The clam pasta was to die for.It is also great that the owner/chef is very hands on.

Name: Empire Cafe
Address: 11 York Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Louise van Biljon
Review: Chef/owner Clayton makes the best Spaghetti Vongole. We discovered this one afternoon when another chef was having lunch there and told us to have it. It is delicious and made with care. Very friendly service. Grab some of their crackers and cheese straws on your way out. Made with quality ingredients.

Name: Empire Cafe
Address: 11 York Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Elle Eastwood
Review: The brunch was so good however we waited about 45 minutes to get our juice and even longer for the food! It was a shame because we would love to come back but 60 minutes+ waiting time is a little too long for us! It felt a bit hectic inside, the staff were very busy and in fairness they were apologetic. The eggs Benedict with salmon was the best I’ve found in Muizenberg! We sat upstairs, the view was really nice as you can see the sea. I gave it a 4 for food due to a takeaway I had recently, it was the ham & cheese toastie, however it was too heavy, it tasted like it had been deep fried in lots of butter and there was a lot of cheese.

Name: Empire Cafe
Address: 11 York Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Hayley Lee
Review: Very very very hospitable staff and fresh seafood that made my day very enjoyable

Name: Empire Cafe
Address: 11 York Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Derek Less
Review: Great quaint place . Great atmosphere . Amazing lemon meringue and coffee . Great barista .

Name: Cafe 22 on Oxford
Address: Cnr Oxford &, 22 Cambridge Pl, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Charné Swart
Review: First time here and WOW!! Service, food, atmosphere - all was excellent! I didn't even get a pic of the food I gobbled it so quick. Our new favourite brekkie place for sure! We can't wait to come back for a lunch 👏

Name: Cafe 22 on Oxford
Address: Cnr Oxford &, 22 Cambridge Pl, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Sally Spark
Review: First time customers.  Everything about the restaurant was fantastic

Name: Cafe 22 on Oxford
Address: Cnr Oxford &, 22 Cambridge Pl, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Mieke Fabel
Review: This is a cute breakfast spot. It’s feels very local and low-key. The food isn’t amazing but it’s not bad. It’s exactly right, if that makes sense. The people are welcoming and it seems like there are very loyal customers.

Name: Cafe 22 on Oxford
Address: Cnr Oxford &, 22 Cambridge Pl, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Ashlin Darius Govindasamy
Review:
I just took a walk around Durbanville. Checked there was interesting vibes here. Walked past this joint instantly just decided to have lunch here.

Friendly staff. Delicious meal with large portions. Your guys are gonna make me fat.
Anyway if you are around town stop here and have a meal the price and service is immaculate. When I'm around Durbanville again next time will pay a visit!

Name: Cafe 22 on Oxford
Address: Cnr Oxford &, 22 Cambridge Pl, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Karin le roux
Review: What a warm and friendly restaurant, the food was very nice, the service was phenomenal, this is a definitely hidden gem. The coffee is amazing, it’s beautiful and very homely in this restaurant. It’s wheel chair friendly and the kids will love it too.

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Ghalib Bin Ghalib
Review:
Origin Coffee Roasting in Cape Town is a fantastic spot not only for exceptional **coffee** but also for a great **breakfast**. Their **espresso**, **pour-over**, and **milk-based** drinks are consistently excellent, showcasing a range of flavors that highlight the care they put into their roasting process. Plus, their variety of **single-origin** options is perfect for those who love exploring unique flavors from different regions.

I also had breakfast here, and the food was just as impressive. Whether you're in the mood for something hearty or light, their menu offers delicious options to pair perfectly with your coffee. The overall experience makes Origin Coffee a go-to for both coffee lovers and those looking for a satisfying meal to start the day right!

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Ammar Alaidarous
Review: Awesome service, great atmosphere,  fresh food and tasty coffee. Their Benedict got a twist of beetroot,  the pabka is out of this world 🌎,  and the Portuguese tart is so smooth and delicious. They kept asking us if we're happy 😊 we were extremely happy having our breakfast there, and thanks Anni for taking care of us..

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Katie Pugh
Review: Love this coffee shop, awesome single origin coffee to try. The staff recommended an off menu Peruvian bean that was superb using a chemex brew. They also have great breakfast options as well, my husband said the almond croissant is one of the best he’s had and I loved the omelette with the meatball and local fontina cheese. Highly recommend a visit here.

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Radesh N
Review: If i could explain it, I would say it has a cool industrial look and a New York City kinda feel to it. Very interesting menu and lots to choose from. Really good coffee ( but i forgot to take a pic ) and my wife enjoyed her tea. The muesli with fruits were very refreshing. We also enjoyed the eggs benedict and the chicken, avo & mayo bagel. Service is very quick. Would definitely recommend for breakfast/ brunch and for coffee lovers.

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Stevel Marc
Review: I’ve been here a few times. I love this café. It has a great atmosphere. The staff is friendly and eager to help, and the food is tasty.

Name: Four & Twenty Cafe
Address: 23 Wolfe St, Wynberg, Cape Town
Author: Mary Banks
Review: My friend andi had tea with scones, jam and cream. The scones were big and tasty, however the jam provided was barely enough.. We requested more. The cream was plentiful. There was also not enough hot water in the teapot...I requested more. This was my first visit. The staff are friendly and the menu extensive.

Name: Four & Twenty Cafe
Address: 23 Wolfe St, Wynberg, Cape Town
Author: Lauren Kruger
Review: A cosy brunch spot in the Southern suburbs. My colleagues took me out for spouls on my birthday. The rain just added to the appeal of the restaurant and we were very happy to huddle inside. The breakfast menu has such interesting twists on the classics that you are used to.

Name: Four & Twenty Cafe
Address: 23 Wolfe St, Wynberg, Cape Town
Author: James Sellars
Review: One of the best cafes in Cape Town that we've been to, multiple times. Their drinks, food and desserts are all next level and the staff are lovely

Name: Four & Twenty Cafe
Address: 23 Wolfe St, Wynberg, Cape Town
Author: Bianca Louw
Review: Lovely little gem. Their breakfast bowl was lovely, well worth the lactose response I had. Could do without being forgotten about because I seemed fine when I wanted to order some more stuff. But other than that, neat!

Name: Four & Twenty Cafe
Address: 23 Wolfe St, Wynberg, Cape Town
Author: Isabella Strydom
Review: Four & Twenty is a lovely, cosy cafe. Although it can get very busy, the food and coffee arrives quickly, and the staff are very friendly and provide great service. The meals are a little bit on the expensive side, but I highly recommend coming for an Eggs Benedict breakfast. Definitely will return soon!

Name: Lazari
Address: and, Cnr Upper Maynard, 221 Vredehoek Ave, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Less is More
Review:
If you’re looking for a great spot to have breakfast/brunch, that’s the place!

All staff are incredibly friendly, food is fresh and tasty, fast service.

Tips: try their Turkish eggs and apricot pie, they’re really really nice!

Name: Lazari
Address: and, Cnr Upper Maynard, 221 Vredehoek Ave, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Meg Hatton
Review: Such a stunning restaurant ! Perfect for a sunny or rainy day. Loved the atmosphere and all round, the food was amazing - added bonus was that the oats were gluten free and absolutely delicious 🤩 Would highly recommend !

Name: Lazari
Address: and, Cnr Upper Maynard, 221 Vredehoek Ave, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Noelene Curry
Review:
My goddaughter and I had lunch at Lazari yesterday.
The meals were delicious and the staff was welcoming and friendly.
I would definitely recommend a visit.

Name: Lazari
Address: and, Cnr Upper Maynard, 221 Vredehoek Ave, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Talisa Jane Du Bois
Review: 10/10 flat white! Rosetta beans, lovingly and intentionally prepared. Wholesome and delicious food. Kind, considerate, and a lot like home!

Name: Lazari
Address: and, Cnr Upper Maynard, 221 Vredehoek Ave, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: gary meyers
Review:
A great experience , friendly and excellent food ..best of the best and I have been around ...this is an outstanding eatery  , cafe and meeting place .
Coffee and cupcakes are to die for , the eggs Benedict is out of  " Africa "
Be there again soon ....

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Caitlyn Ganesh
Review: The tomato soup was delicious and perfect for the cold Cape Town weather. The restaurant was packed on this particular Saturday evening, expected for Observatory though, but it took around 20mins or so just for my drinks order, and at least I got a knee blanket to embrace the cold. Will definitely return on my next trip to Cape Town and hopefully it will be warmer🙌🏽

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Lyndsey Petro
Review:
Always considered this more of a café, but was pleasantly surprised by the variety and yumminess of the menu.

Great spot for meetings and 'working from home'.

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Jain Halder
Review: The food was heartwarmingly good. Their selection of drinks ranged from a wide variety. The place is a little crowded but very warm and cozy thanks to their fire place. Definitely recommend coming here but only if it's not crowded.

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ziggy Ezulu
Review:
This definitely makes the Grade in the Cosy Chic Cafe category.
Beautiful interior with picturesque bar and functional wood-burning fireplace in the corner.
Tables by the window with the outside view- the best for just chilling and watching life pass by.
Went there in the evening and the ambiance was perfect - relaxed, good muted music, the buzz of conversation.
Friendly staff and good service.
Can definitely recommend this 😉👍

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ntando Sithole
Review: A great place to work and eat delicious food. The food is also affordable, the atmosphere were just right and the staff was very friendly. For the students, I highly recommend it for study dates. It's close to the UCT health science faculty and is a walking distance from Obz Sqaure. Enjoy!

Name: Park Café
Address: 1056 Bill Peters Dr, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Keith Petty
Review: We were a party of 10 adults for breakfast. The staff were very friendly and welcoming. The food was very flavoursome and well presented. The waitrons were attentive and super efficient. The selection of cakes on offer are fresh and portion sizes are good. Very good value for money.  I will definitely be back again. I had the Avo, scrambled egg and blackened cherry tomatoes on toasted sourdough bread.

Name: Park Café
Address: 1056 Bill Peters Dr, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: #TMp Hashtag Travel Marketplace (#TMp)
Review:
The cheesecake here was an absolute delight. Its creamy texture melted effortlessly in my mouth, with just the right balance of sweetness and richness. The crust had a perfect crunch, providing a lovely contrast to the smooth filling. Paired with a warm cup of tea, it made for a perfect afternoon treat. The portion size was generous without being overwhelming, leaving me both satisfied and craving just a little more. The serene outdoor ambiance added to the overall experience, making this a spot I'd definitely visit again.

Would highly recommend ( The park cafe in Green Point Cape Town) this place if you’re in the mood for some excellent cheesecake and a peaceful setting!

Name: Park Café
Address: 1056 Bill Peters Dr, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Larry Liu
Review:
Cute, spacious and sunny cafe located inside the green point park.

There is a small selection of food available. Several juices and coffee as well.

Food is reasonably priced. Staff is friendly and lots of seating areas.
