The burger is extremely tasty. It has all the classic ingredients. Scones are fresh. Hotdog is okay, I’d recommend the burger over the hotdog.

Seems to be wheelchair friendly and parents can watch their kids play in the grass field in front.

Name: Park Café
Address: 1056 Bill Peters Dr, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Leigh Margoc
Review: The food and atmosphere was great. Our waitress forgot that we ordered coffee. The food came out relatively quick and was amazing. When I asked for the bill she said she will bring it, 15 minutes later I asked again for the bill and then waited 10 more minutes. When I stood up to ask for the bill for the third time then I finally got a reaction. It's a pity that I had to down grade my rating due to a waitress, because this place is a full on gem.

Name: Park Café
Address: 1056 Bill Peters Dr, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Anzhelika Petrova
Review: the first time I came with my 9 month old baby, sat down at a table where there was a high chair, laid out my things and went to change my son’s diaper; upon returning, I saw that the high chair had been taken away from my table to the next table. It was as unpleasant as possible, to which the waiters said that I didn’t warn them that I was taking a high chair and gave it to another child 🤷🏼‍♀️ the second time they brought me overcooked dish... but thanks, they changed it

Name: Stodels Cafe Bellville
Address: 81 Eversdal Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Debi.R
Review:
The staff is very friendly and welcoming. The café is tidy, and area is clean. We didn't consume food here, but bought a take-away juice and 350ml water. I feel that R56 for this is a TOTAL rip -off.
One bitten, twice shy.
I will be sure to pack my own liquids at the next Stodels vissit.

Name: Stodels Cafe Bellville
Address: 81 Eversdal Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Clarissa Le Sar
Review:
Absolutely beautiful, calming environment to visit for the family. Plenty children area available as well and lovely selection of ALL your garden and plant needs.
Lovely restaurant at the far of the plant area offering food with Devine fresh delicious produce.

Name: Stodels Cafe Bellville
Address: 81 Eversdal Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Yolande Booth
Review: Food was average. Milkshakes was okay. Service was slow so if they are busy I would not want to go there. They play area for the kids area great

Name: Stodels Cafe Bellville
Address: 81 Eversdal Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Benita Faiers
Review: Thank you to the staff at Stodels Cafe for their friendly and efficient service - all the time. I am a regular visitor to the coffee shop and love Caroline and her ladies. The food is good but the apple pie and cream are to die for! After an absence due to knee surgery, Aisha organized a helper to do my plant shopping for me on Saturday while i chilled at the coffee shop.  Aisha is a gem and an assett to the company. Always cheerful and friendly, no matter how busy she is. She knows my friend and I are regulars and always checks in with a kind and welcoming word. We love doing Stodels - such a warm and welcoming atmosphere!

Name: Stodels Cafe Bellville
Address: 81 Eversdal Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Tim Blake
Review: I visited garden centers and nurseries in many countries and rate Stodels as world class. An excellent experience and nice professional garden center. The restaurant is a wonderful bonus.

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: clinton van loggerenberg
Review: Lovely spot. Great beer and awesome trails. Would have given this 5 stars. BUT, please, sort out your ordering system. No one wants to queue for 25 minutes to order a beer refill. How about a bar only queue? Or let the waitrons take orders and payment. You will sell twice as much and your customers will be happier too.

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Tara-Lee Bezuidenhout
Review:
Beautiful play area and setting for kids! It's enclosed and has everything you need to have a great time with busy little ones. Well maintained grass, swings, jungle gym, slide, sand pit with toys provided, trees for shade and picnic benches inside the actual play area which is nice.

It was windy in Cape Town today but this area was quite well protected and pleasant.

Deluxe coffee and carrot cake from The Dairy Shed is 👌. It would be great if they could add more vegetarian options to their menu.

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Marc De Decker
Review:
Authentic farm experience, with uniquely rustic and not yet commercialized approached that is true to it’s centuries old heritage. Awesome trails for any skill of mountainbiker but no trail running folks! Food is great and the coffee is usually fantastic.

EDIT: NO DOGS ALLOWED!

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Audrey Baird
Review: The weather was lovely today, ideal to stop by and enjoy some fresh air, at this farm setting. We enjoyed their cappuccino and fresh, delicious, moist carrot cake!

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Chantel Pienaar
Review: Lovely, just lovely. Rustic. Love the massive enclosed play area. Awesome coffee. Beautiful views. Tranquil.

Name: Koffi Terapi
Address: Shop 18F, 1st Floor, Willowbridge Mall, Carl Cronje Dr, Tygervalley, Cape Town
Author: Only Me
Review: Been there a on few occasions now over a few months and I find the manager to be a bit stiff and more friendly towards the regular visitors he knows by name which is sad. I feel that one should treat ALL customers in a friendly manner, not just your daily / weekly visitors. The cheesecake varieties are refreshing and tasty but the chocolate cake is dry, I've had this twice now (gave it a second chance), I would not recommend. Overall it was pretty average as reflected in the score.

Name: Koffi Terapi
Address: Shop 18F, 1st Floor, Willowbridge Mall, Carl Cronje Dr, Tygervalley, Cape Town
Author: Elana van der Kolff
Review: Beautiful café with delicious coffee and cake. There's seating inside and outside with a jungle gym for kiddies. I had a bacon & egg on sourdough that was very oily, next time I'll choose something else or stick to something sweet.

Name: Koffi Terapi
Address: Shop 18F, 1st Floor, Willowbridge Mall, Carl Cronje Dr, Tygervalley, Cape Town
Author: Jonel Engelbrecht
Review: Perfect place especially for a rainy day to have some coffee and cake or a light lunch. Service was amazing. Food was fantastic. It's a beautiful place. Mesmerizing. We had 2 meals and 3 juices. The bill was barely R300. Prices are very reasonable considering the portion you get. It's really amazing. 15 / 10 recommend.

Name: Koffi Terapi
Address: Shop 18F, 1st Floor, Willowbridge Mall, Carl Cronje Dr, Tygervalley, Cape Town
Author: Henk van Milligen
Review: Koffi Terapi provided an exceptional dining experience during our recent visit. The service was exemplary, marked by attentive and welcoming staff. The culinary offerings surpassed our expectations, particularly the Southern style chicken burger, which was a standout. Additionally, their selection of coffee and cakes was delightful, each item demonstrating the same high quality as the main courses. This establishment is highly recommended for its overall excellence in both service and cuisine.

Name: Koffi Terapi
Address: Shop 18F, 1st Floor, Willowbridge Mall, Carl Cronje Dr, Tygervalley, Cape Town
Author: Lizelle Liebenberg
Review: Had the best waiter, Gracious. He was very accommodating. Very busy restaurant but service was still good and food was delish! We sat on the outside patio and enjoyed the view. Will definately visit again!!

Name: Cafe Puka.
Address: 2 Brunswick Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Natalie Miller
Review: THIS PLACE WAS SO NICE AND SUPER AFFORDABLE! It’s located on the corner of Brunswick and it is such a quiet area, no cars coming by, so quiet and lovey to sit on the patio. Shady spots for sure so you can eat in complete peace. The prices are so affordable and the quality is so good. The service is so kind and it really feels like a local gem. So nice for a quick bite or even take your laptop with, they are so chilled. so nice for families or a friend meet up or even a breakfast date. Loved it and can’t wait to be back!

Name: Cafe Puka.
Address: 2 Brunswick Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Kshethra Naidoo
Review:
We popped by Cafe Puka, having heard about this hidden gem through the grapevine.

We had such a wonderful brunch, and we're welcomed in like family from the moment we walked in.

The Trainsmash wrap was tremendous and delicious! I thoroughly enjoyed the Vietnamese Coffee.

Charming local cafe with a delicious, varied, and well priced menu. Thank you to Michael and his team for making us feel so welcome and for such a lovely meal.

Name: Cafe Puka.
Address: 2 Brunswick Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: O A
Review: Nothing tasted good that I ordered. The hash brown was soaked in oil, the hazelnut cappuccino had a weird taste, and the croissant had too much oil in it. The staff was friendly.

Name: Cafe Puka.
Address: 2 Brunswick Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Shannon Dubay
Review: Food is yummy and generous. Lots of exciting options. Staff is SO friendly. Such a small hidden gem with a lovely view of table mountain!!

Name: Cafe Puka.
Address: 2 Brunswick Rd, Tamboerskloof, Cape Town
Author: Anastasia Brukman
Review:
Fantastic service, generous portions (we had scrambled egg on the biggest croissant I’ve ever eaten 🥰).
Excellent quality coffee. A real gem in Tamboerskloof.

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: K Bourn
Review: Had a slice of their Cheese cake. Anyone reading this review that's a cheese cake fan - THIS IS HANDS DOWN THE BEST YOU WILL EVER ENJOY! I highly recommend you try it. Words can't describe it. Definitely the new #1 ranked on my 'best cheese cake list'.

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Charlton Bryce Reimers
Review:
This place is great! I love it, BUT today there was a guy on his phone, watching reels at FULL VOLUME.
People keep getting up from their tables and the staff had not asked him to be respectful to others by lowering his volume or using earphones. This is unacceptable on so many social levels.  As I wrote this I had noise-cancelling headphones on and I could still hear him.

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Fraser Hewson
Review:
Just a bit underwhelmed. No parking other than pay parking which is not great if youbare getting something to go. R10 for 10 mins. Not the fault of Nood, but something to take into consideration.
Also, coffee cup for Cappuccino was not filled full. Maybe this is picky, but at R34, I'd like a full cup.

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Chanelle Samaai
Review: Such a vibe. We always return because of the most friendly people ever. The food is absolutely to die for. We always have the Eggs Benedict. It really is the BEST one in Cape Town, if not SA. Thanks for the most awesome vibes ever. ✨️ 💞

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Matthew Fridjhon
Review: Easily the best service, I have ever had. Incredibly good waiters and kind and positive staff all around. Delicious and simple food with an encapsulating and relaxing atmosphere. Highly recommend as just an experience to receive high quality service from high quality people.

Name: Vida e Caffè Edward street Bellville
Address: 112 bloemhof building Corner of Bloemhof Rd &, Edward Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Nicci Beket
Review: Close to home and for sure my favorite Vida! Small but inviting. Food, coffees and smoothies are great BUT their Chai latte is by far the best I have ever had, and trust me, I've been on a Chai mission for the best! Had to get me some for home use!

Name: Vida e Caffè Edward street Bellville
Address: 112 bloemhof building Corner of Bloemhof Rd &, Edward Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Jessica Daniels
Review: My favourite Vida by far! Not the biggest branch, but the coffee is always PERFECT and the toasties are delicious and hot. Whoever makes the cappuccino here is the best bestest of the best :)

Name: Vida e Caffè Edward street Bellville
Address: 112 bloemhof building Corner of Bloemhof Rd &, Edward Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Mind Bodysoul
Review: Learn to clean tables before a next person takes seating. A fresh wipe is always welcome. Hygiene is an advantage. I often observe this here. Next clients enter, table not wiped. It may look clean, I saw the before clients left. New clients arrived. Increase your attentiveness to this particularity. Thank you

Name: Vida e Caffè Edward street Bellville
Address: 112 bloemhof building Corner of Bloemhof Rd &, Edward Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Tara Khai
Review:
The absolute worst Vida branch to visit, especially if you're a person of colour. My colleague and I, both Vida regulars and people of colour, were ready to order and waiting patiently in line. Suddenly, this entitled white guy breezes right in, cuts the queue in front of us, and has the audacity to LAUGH about it!  He tells the cashier, "I just cut in line!" while digging in his wallet, and guess what?  The cashier LAUGHS with him and takes his order without batting an eyelid. The whole thing was so incredibly disrespectful, it left us speechless.  We watched the entire thing unfold, and it was clear the cashier saw exactly what happened.  Yet, there was no attempt to address it, no "let the people who were here first order," nothing.  Honestly, it was pathetic.

I've always been a Vida fan but it seems like this particular branch is bending over backwards to make sure their white customers are happy FIRST, no matter how rude they are. Needless to say, we walked right out. This is not the inclusive and welcoming environment I've come to expect from Vida - we got better service from the Tygervalley mall branch.

Name: Vida e Caffè Edward street Bellville
Address: 112 bloemhof building Corner of Bloemhof Rd &, Edward Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Thuli Nyoka
Review: Quality is abysmal. The first red cappuccino they made me had dairy milk, luckily I asked before drinking. Then the second was made in such a rush that it just tasted like tea flavored water. Disappointing.

Name: Frank's Coffee & Bagel Bar
Address: 36 E Lake Dr, Marina Da Gama, Cape Town
Author: Vasti M.
Review:
Frank's is a cute little place.
Service was friendly, and the kind young lady, Jody, removed one of those spicy caterpillars from our area, we didn't even notice it. Observant and attentive. Although I do believe a little short-staffed, seeing as we did wait a while for service.
The coffee is on point.
Bagels, The Cure, and the Frank. Both the toppings were generous. The bacon on the cure was dry. All in all, still a very pleasant experience.  :)

Name: Frank's Coffee & Bagel Bar
Address: 36 E Lake Dr, Marina Da Gama, Cape Town
Author: Jess Kipling
Review:
We had a pleasant breakfast here.

The staff were great, the coffee was hot and the venue was beautiful! We had the Benedict bagels and a coffee each. Possibly not the best bagels in town but great for a small local spot. For an affordable breakfast we definitely got our money's worth.

I was eyeing out all the other goodies they had and I'll be going back for a croissant and a slice of cake next time too!

Name: Frank's Coffee & Bagel Bar
Address: 36 E Lake Dr, Marina Da Gama, Cape Town
Author: Gemma F
Review: Incredible service and delicious food. A big variety of tasty breakfasts to go along with a really good coffee. The carrot cake is also delicious. The portions are big and a good value for money. The staff were there for our every need.

Name: Frank's Coffee & Bagel Bar
Address: 36 E Lake Dr, Marina Da Gama, Cape Town
Author: Kyle Ackermann
Review: Awesome brekkie and lunch spot nestled away in the Marina de Garma. Very cool owners that were extremely friendly and helpful. My partner and I visited for their live vinyl dj set on Friday evening. We enjoyed beer on tap and aperol Spritz along with a delicious calamari starter and risotto balls which we had never tasted before but were delicious. Heaps of parking available, great atmosphere and plenty of freshly baked goods available. Definitely worth a visit if you're in the Southern Suburbs looking to get away from the busy spots.

Name: Frank's Coffee & Bagel Bar
Address: 36 E Lake Dr, Marina Da Gama, Cape Town
Author: Günther Smit
Review:
One of the best, if not THE BEST croissant I have ever had...

The wait is a bit long, but if you're in the area with time to spare, Frank's is a absolute Gem.

Also, they serve TRUTH coffee, which is absolutely delicious.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Glengariff
Address: 229a Main Rd, Three Anchor Bay, Cape Town
Author: Maryam Valodia
Review:
It’s a Wednesday morning the air is foggy and smells of ocean mist.
My entrance is embraced by a warm hug of the gas heater burning brightly.
The staff wore bright smiles and were so attentive to my needs I felt like the only customer, yet the place was fully seated.

I had a matcha latte with oat milk to get my creative juices flowing and it hit the spot but that’s not even the best part…
The Rockstar Bagel!! Succulent chicken grilled to perfection, every bite was mouthwatering and fresh.
I had a super productive day at Shift Glengariff got 4hrs of work done which was more than I planned for the day but it was just the perfect setting. My new favourite Shift Bar ❤️

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Glengariff
Address: 229a Main Rd, Three Anchor Bay, Cape Town
Author: Jesse Lee Heath
Review:
This coffee shop is a true gem for remote working, catch ups with friends or alone time. The staff are incredibly friendly and helpful & the coffee is great! The best food options in my opinion: Salmon Benedict, Omelette 2, The Club bagel, Monte Cristo sandwich. I have a sweet tooth so I often order an iced Honeybadger (coffee drink).

We’ve also ordered takeaways (free delivery within 2km I think?) and they were just as tasty!

P.S. they’re dog friendly too 🥹

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Glengariff
Address: 229a Main Rd, Three Anchor Bay, Cape Town
Author: Marco Fischer
Review: Overall it was nice. Went as a group of 5 with a small kid (unfortunately no kids toys available but the gave us some paper and pen to draw). They had the first coffee order a bit mixed up but they sorted it out after and gave a new one for free. The breakfast was in my opinion a bit too basic, the bread overtoasted (hard) and my wife's egg whites watery. The rest (Omelettes and banana bread) were good. Overall good but they could slightly improve, or maybe it just wasn't to my own taste. The coffee was delicious. The inside is a bit dark but on the street side it was nicer to sit.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Glengariff
Address: 229a Main Rd, Three Anchor Bay, Cape Town
Author: Mansour Al-Essa
Review:
Egg bendict is wunderful selection
the breakfast is great with a great host welcoming visitors
I come again

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Glengariff
Address: 229a Main Rd, Three Anchor Bay, Cape Town
Author: Garry Oldfield
Review: Best little coffee shop we’ve been to in ages!  Great coffee (including decaf for me 😁…ya ya).  Amazing food on offer and a great atmosphere to wind down, or sit and get some work done (headphone’s recommended).  Pet friendly which is great too!  Great management m, very attentive and excellent waiting on tables 👌☕️ NB! We had already started eating…the photo’s dont do their food justice! 👌👌👌 Try their French Toast!  VERY generous portion too!

Name: Starlings Locale
Address: 4 Mark Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Madison Oosthuysen
Review: So so hospitable especially of my dogs! I love it there & highly recommend the Avo Pesto toast

Name: Starlings Locale
Address: 4 Mark Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: SIMON AINSWORTH TAYLOR
Review:
Decided to try Starlings Locale on Saturday morning after Google searching for breakfast spots.

What a hidden little gem  this place is.
Off down a little residential side street its hidden away.
Went inside to be greeted by a very friendly and welcoming Walter and offered a table.
Decor is simple yet comfortable.
I started with a cappuccino, ..... very nice, great coffee.
All served in Le Creuset cups and saucers.
Cutlery was all super clean, something I am in the habit of checking.
The menu was presented with not a huge choice, it included  healthy offerings and more, but more than enough to whet most appetites.
I ordered the poached eggs with extra mushrooms and bacon 🥓.
My breakfast arrived shortly after and looked amazing, 5 rashers of bacon and a volumous amount of mushrooms, with 2 perfectly poached eggs and two slices of sourdough bread.
The bacon was top notch, (and I had heard others comment on this as I was eating), the mushrooms very tasty and as I said great medium poached eggs.
There was enough bacon and mushrooms to cover the second slice of bread.
I ordered a second cappucino as the first just wasn't enough to quench my coffee thirst !
Great service from Walter, amazing coffee from Fabrice.." the coffee Maestro " and not forgetting Happiness and Lusanda making magic in the kitchen. Thank you guys, nice to meet you all
This unassuming place is well worth a visit, well priced and away from the hustle and bustle.
A great spot for a meeting with coffee and good food.
I rate this 8 out of 10 and will definately be back.
Just a note, parking is limited and the local traffic constabulary are on the hunt. I stupidly parked on a yellow line, thinking it was Saturday and would be ok..... It cost me R 500 extra 😒
Besides that it was a great morning.

Name: Starlings Locale
Address: 4 Mark Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Christiaan Cloete
Review:
Waiters were super friendly
Food was really good and fresh
Drinks were amazing I would recommend the ice coffee
Parking was a mission but it still was amazing walking in the streets
I would recommend anyone here it’s lovely atmosphere
Wi-Fi is available
Good working environment

Name: Starlings Locale
Address: 4 Mark Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Ashlin S
Review: The food is always consistently good. An easy decision to make when you aren't sure where to get breakfast or lunch. The staff is super friendly and quick to help.

Name: Starlings Locale
Address: 4 Mark Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Teegan Foster
Review: My boyfriend and i visited this sweet spot for a quick brekkie. It was past breakfast time but our waiter Fabrice made an exception to let us order some yummy breakfast. The food was so tasty and well served. A major thanks to Fabrice, your humor and polite attitude is appreciated. We will definitely be back!!

Name: Bootlegger - Harrington Street
Address: The Harrington, 50 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Elouise Van Wyk
Review:
I recently had lunch at Bootlegger Coffee Company in Harrington street and it was a pleasant experience.

I had the tomato soup, which was one of three soups of the day. This tomato soup, paired with a pesto and mozzarella toasted sandwich, is my favorite from Bootlegger’s menu, and I having enjoyed it at other locations before. The staff was very accommodating, preparing a cheese-only toasted sandwich for my friend. My partner had the club sandwich, which he also enjoyed. They also have a display with ready-to-go sandwiches and pastries. A good amount of seating is available. Lovely area to sit and enjoy a coffee while looking out on the street. You do have to use a bathroom outside the restaurant. It seems like a communal bathroom for all the shops in the building that this Bootlegger branch is located in. The bathroom was not very clean.

The service was good, and the location is convenient, right near a plant store, a bike store, and an array of other cafe’s, bars, and shops. The place was decently clean, the staff were friendly, and the service was good. Overall, a nice spot for a relaxed meal or quick coffee break. Bootlegger’s prices are also fairly good in comparison to many other cafe’s and restaurants in the area.

Name: Bootlegger - Harrington Street
Address: The Harrington, 50 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Polina Ulanova
Review: Great place! Delicious food and coffee, fast service, nice vibes. Will definitely come back again

Name: Bootlegger - Harrington Street
Address: The Harrington, 50 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Love God
Review: Nice food but veeeery slow service

Name: Bootlegger - Harrington Street
Address: The Harrington, 50 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Gcobisa Nokwabuza
Review: I had the chicken strips for lunch... the potion was nice and feeling as it comes with a potion of chips. I would recommend the chicken strips delish 👌 . I would also recommend trying their dirt Chai, it the best

Name: Bootlegger - Harrington Street
Address: The Harrington, 50 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Lester C
Review: Pancakes with bacon for breakfast was spectacular! My wife says the coffee was perfect. Lots of indoor and outdoor seating. A fabulous way to start our day off in the city. 😎

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Robyn-Leigh Daniels
Review:
The restaurant's setup was far from ideal. There was construction happening right behind our table, and the tables were so close together that it was impossible not to overhear other diners' conversations. While the food came quickly and was very nice (the best part), the service and atmosphere were uncomfortable, to say the least.

Once our food arrived, no one checked on us. We wanted to get a takeaway box and the bill, but it took 20 minutes to get any assistance. During this time, waiters passed by, even knocking into us without an apology. When we finally received the takeaway box, it was broken, but we didn't even bother asking for another one.

The service staff seemed most cheerful when we were leaving. The overall experience was disappointing, with certain undertones in the environment that made the visit feel unwelcoming.

I would rather go to the one in Muizenberg, a much better atmosphere.

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Waxed Personalised Beauty
Review: I’ve been searching for months for a coffee shop to work from, meet my clients or just pop in for a quick cappuccino. Hans and Lloyd in Newlands has ticked all the boxes for me and has become my ultimate favourite. They are central making it easy to travel to and from the southern suburbs. Their breakfast dishes are an absolute must and they serve a nice warm cappuccino for the coffee lovers. Also if you’re feeling for a ginger shot they got you covered. I’ve visited over two days to make sure I wasn’t dreaming! Definitely will be back 😁

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Hendrickse
Review:
A really nice venue with inside and outside seating. The food was pricey for the quantity however still very tasty. The service was unfortunately very slow and poor. We had to leave our table to find a waiter on more than one occasion after waiting a while and not having any check in on us. We had seats right at the back.
There is street parking as well as a small paid parking area (see times on the boards)

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Suzanne Everaerts
Review: We go here often and it’s a lovely restaurant with great food.

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Hein De Vries
Review:
Hans & Lloyd's feels like snuggling into the cozy embrace of a vintage coffee sack--if hessian weren't itchy,  and premium coffee beans were cotton balls. They serve a rich, full-bodied brew that is high on my list of yummiest coffees in Cape Town.

I can't comment on the entire menu, but if all of it is as good as Lloyd's Vegetarian Breakfast, you can't go wrong. The chunky mushrooms were incredible.

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Marius Kriel
Review:
I had a delightful experience at The Ginger Tree. From the moment I stepped in until I left, the hospitality was outstanding. Though small, the café is cozy with a calming atmosphere and charming decor.

The gentleman running the store is incredibly hospitable and engaging. They offer a variety of espresso-based drinks, listed upon entry, and a few sandwiches, though there’s no formal food menu. I highly recommend the "everything sandwich"—it’s worth a visit just for that!

The coffee is very flavorful, and they also offer takeaways if your in a rush. With ample parking and outdoor seating, The Ginger Tree is a local staple and the perfect hangout spot. I’ll definitely be returning!

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Van Niekerk
Review: Searched for a coffee shop near me whilst in the area for an appointment and Ginger Tree popped up. What a beautiful environment with incredibly yummy coffee and homemade toasties. If I lived closer I’d be here daily.😉

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Samihah Ebrahim
Review: Wonderful, friendly service! So homey and comfortable. Delicious drinks and cake! 😍

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Malcolm Campbell
Review: Beautiful place, good atmosphere, great service, amazing food. Definitely worth a revisit.

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Wium Brosens
Review: Such a lovely coffee shop with great ambiance, friendly owner and very tasty home baked cakes! The ice coffee is a must try and variours other delicious snacks. Excellent coffee and won't break the bank either. Highly recommended for coffee lovers and something delicious on the side.

Name: Nest Deli
Address: Uitsig, Constantia, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Lauren Smith
Review: We had lunch here a few ago on a glorious Friday afternoon, one of those perfect winter days. Good is well-presented and tasty, but my quinoa and chickpea salad came minus the chickpeas! I was distracted with all the chatter and only realised halfway through when my friend enquired how my salad was. I was offered a discount of 20% which I didn’t think went far enough, however, the manager was most apologetic and did offer me half portion to take home instead. Beautiful setting, I’ll definitely be back.

Name: Nest Deli
Address: Uitsig, Constantia, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Ingrid Helgeson-Peters
Review: Amazing venue in the most exquisite setting! Menu caters for all. Our best outing as a family! Definitely won't disappoint! Side mention cheese board is a WINNER! Enjoy a walk in the exquisite rose garden after your meal.

Name: Nest Deli
Address: Uitsig, Constantia, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Luiz Felipe Gasparini
Review: Great food, we had breakfast my family with my kids. Beautiful landscape

Name: Nest Deli
Address: Uitsig, Constantia, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Brian Goemans
Review: Glorious setting for good food and friends. Always worth a visit, as well as the other attractions at Uitsig. Garden is stunning 🤩

Name: Nest Deli
Address: Uitsig, Constantia, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Hennie Basson
Review: Only a visit with seniors. Bit disappointing because not all the units were operational. One thing that stands out is the beautiful well maintained gardens. Wow and even more so with the beautiful mountain views giving you a calm and peaceful experience. Would like to visit when the gardens are in full bloom.

Name: Bootlegger - Kenilworth
Address: Pam Golding Centre, 323 Main Road G05, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Alvin Fredericks
Review: Have had a few bad experiences here unfortunately. The manager was good, and assisted after I went to speak to him. I sent my eggs back twice, and on the second time the waiter told me: "I will see if they give you another egg". The egg never arrived and my wife's food came after the rest of us had finished eating. The manager didn't charge for my wife's meal and was very apologetic.

Name: Bootlegger - Kenilworth
Address: Pam Golding Centre, 323 Main Road G05, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Alexandr Steinberg
Review: Arrived to at least three nice tables at booths, with plug points, all empty but marked 'Reserved'. They were vacant for the better part of an hour. When an esteemed patron arrived to occupy one of the reserved tables, he was greeted with all manner of joy by the waiter. A cliquy environment not suitable to the remote worker passer by. The Cove down the road is much better for remote working and, while still neighbourly in the best way, welcoming -- will go there next time.

Name: Bootlegger - Kenilworth
Address: Pam Golding Centre, 323 Main Road G05, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Stefan Green
Review: Great spot. Fine food and good music. Nice Wi-Fi. Manager was great

Name: Bootlegger - Kenilworth
Address: Pam Golding Centre, 323 Main Road G05, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Naidoo
Review: I went today to do some some work and grab some brunch. Spacious table and available plug points, but not available at all the table. WiFi only available when you order food, but that’s understandable. Service was good and they always checked up to see that all was good. The food was good but can be improved on. I had the crumbed chicken burger. I think it could have improved with a bit more sauce so that it isn’t so dry. Overall pleasant experience. Would go again. The tapas menu looked good

Name: Bootlegger - Kenilworth
Address: Pam Golding Centre, 323 Main Road G05, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Enid Son
Review: Good service.  I like the flexibility of building your own breakfast. I had the sesame bagel with Avo.  Hubby had the bunless burger.   The idea of serving cuppucino in glass tumblers just doesn't work for me - I really need that handle on a mug because my fingers can't handle direct heat. Apart from that I do enjoy   Bootleggers.

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ashley Booysen
Review: Was my 1st time beiing here, staff was very friendly I must say including the owner was very hands on. We came for the Dillion Olifant comedy nite, wow wot a vibe it was and Dillion was so funny I almost pulled a stomach muscle.  I had the Nelson burger with chips , everything was freshly made and pricing is very good if not too good. Thanks for a wonderfull nite indeed.

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Gary Simpson
Review: A real little gem of a coffee shop, really embodies what Obs is, genuine places that really go the extra mile to provide good service and a good product. Has a relaxed feeling to it, friendly staff and good coffee. They have power during loadshedding and offer a decent selection of food, pictures of the menu included with this review.

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Adam Steed
Review: Ground culture is an awesome spot!! Such lekker vibes and good food. I’ve been to a few comedy nights there and it is always a good time! Highly recommend 🤩

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Kyle Waterston
Review: Such a lovely, rustic and authentic coffee shop in Obs! ☕️. Definitely worth a visit. Love the vibey, laid-back tunes that create the perfect working atmosphere 🎶. The staff are so friendly and positive, always making my day! Love the close proximity to AFDA and 20% student discount, which make lunches here wonderful 😋. Credit to owners Shelley and Sebastian for all their hard work behind the scenes 😀. You don’t want to miss this hidden gem. Will definitely be back soon 🙃🙌🏻.

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Cheree Roelofse
Review: I ordered food from here for delivering and it was some of the absolute best burgers I've ever had. I ordered a Brekkie Bun, and this is coming from someone who doesn't usually like eggs, but the Brekkie Bun is definitely now one of my all time favour burgers. I also had the Nelson burger with fries, which was just as good. There is no shortage of patty and overall it is just amazing, even the fries are good, fluffy yet crispy. I can't wait to order here again.

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Sven Tietz
Review: Dude, there’s a puppy menu. Dude, a full Doggie menu with Puppachinos and Tail Waggers and lots of great dishes and treats for dogs. Would come here just for the dog menu, love it so much. The space was also great, service was friendly, coffee was ok-ish or ok enough, nice place to pop in and do some work, and normally it vibes nicely but they were kind enough to turn it down a bit for my zoom meeting. Definitely recommended… and bring your Furbaby!! ❤️🐕

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Clementine ?
Review: I ate brunch with a few friends and the service was magnificent! We were served by Georgina and she was super attentive, before I even ordered another cup of water it was already being brought to me. The food was great as well! I got a flapjack tower with hash browns and bacon. The hazel nut latte was amazing as well!

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Nigel Teasdale
Review: Four of us had brunch here today, and it was a great experience. The café is attractive and has a pleasant relaxed atmosphere. Service was polite and friendly, and the food was not only very tasty, but also beautifully presented. Highlt recommended.

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Roger Mullens
Review: Cute place to enjoy a lovely breakfast or lunch. I enjoyed the French Toast and a cappucino. The service was incredible. The cappucino was good and the lityle heart cookie was a nice touch. The presentation of the French Toast was stunning, and the taste was great! Can't wait to go back and try more of the offerings! The venue has both inside and outside seating available.

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Shanice Dee Page
Review:
Honestly the cutest little roadside cafe!

The atmosphere is great! Lesley, my waiter, super friendly and quick service! Very affordable food, drinks and treats.

The food is delish and don’t even get me started on the baked cheesecake 😫🤌🏼 Shout out to you Honeybun.

Loved my first brekkie in CT with y’all.

Name: Bootlegger - Claremont
Address: 21 Dreyer Street Shop 04, Sunclare Building, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Michael Streatfield
Review: Thought I try this place and catch the winter sun on the deck. Unfortunately had a disappointing service experience. Seated by manager, but the Waitress ignored me for ages. Eventually I enlisted someone in another section to help me. (Thanks R. for the help). Food was delicious but arrived with an unwelcome visitor -  a hair on top. Sigh - should have stuck with my usual spot

Name: Bootlegger - Claremont
Address: 21 Dreyer Street Shop 04, Sunclare Building, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Bran Wood
Review:
Great place for a good coffee 👌🏼
Walking distance to loads of other shops so this makes a great pit stop for a bite to eat.

Name: Bootlegger - Claremont
Address: 21 Dreyer Street Shop 04, Sunclare Building, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Freddie Vaas
Review: My waiter Robyn- Lee was exceptional! She provided top-notch service, attentive and friendly without being intrusive. Orders were accurate, and food was delivered promptly. Robyn went above and beyond to ensure my  dining experience was exceptional. Her knowledge of the menu and recommendations were spot on. Her patience throughout my indecisiveness was appreciated.Kudos to Robyn for a job well done! 5 stars and I’ll be back!"

Name: Bootlegger - Claremont
Address: 21 Dreyer Street Shop 04, Sunclare Building, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Bernadette Dix
Review:
Far too many laptops hogging the tables for hours and barely ordering any food or drink over the 3-4 hours some of them are there for. They should be seated at the bar along the window or move to the back section when done eating.
It’s a restaurant, not a library.
We no longer frequent this particular establishment because there are never any decent tables, we were once asked to be seated at the bar area along the window which is clearly marked for laptops. Ridiculous.

Name: Bootlegger - Claremont
Address: 21 Dreyer Street Shop 04, Sunclare Building, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Dean Ferreira
Review: Amazing service, great food and vibe

Name: Parks Edge / Cafeen
Address: 41 3RD Ave, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Michelle Macleod
Review:
A lovely coffee shop with so much character along with the great food. You can choose to sit inside or undercover outside. There is a lovely view of the park and the mountain.
There is a good variety of breakfast options as well as lunch, good cake options too! Parking is along the road.

Name: Parks Edge / Cafeen
Address: 41 3RD Ave, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Mark Rouse
Review: A lovely little coffee shop with a great food menu too. Lots of character and friendly staff. Tables outside with views of the park or inside.

Name: Parks Edge / Cafeen
Address: 41 3RD Ave, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Samantha Longbottom
Review: My partner and I went to Parks Edge cafeen for breakfast for my birthday and it did not disappoint. The prices are reasonable,  the food is good and the atmosphere is one for Saturday or Sunday serenity.  Bring your doggo/children for a walk in the park across the road after filling your belly with the best that Parks Edge Cafeen has to offer. Tiny street parking available.

Name: Parks Edge / Cafeen
Address: 41 3RD Ave, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Wendy-Joy Floris
Review: We had the strangest experience at Parks Edge this morning. We had a small breakfast and coffee and split the bill 50/50 as we always do... And were told we're not allowed to. Why ever not?

Name: Parks Edge / Cafeen
Address: 41 3RD Ave, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Samantha Peters
Review:
Had breakfast here on Saturday and the food was sensational. Great value for money, the portions are generous, the food is well presented and came out quickly despite it being quite busy.
The coffee was delicious too. I would highly recommend this restaurant, the views are fantastic and location is ideal.

Cant wait to go back and try the Eggs Benedict!

Name: Café Dynamis Coffee Shop
Address: c/o Paul Kruger &, Hibiscus Way, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Johan Keulder
Review: Hidden jewel between all the medical facilities with delicious light meals, very reasonably priced. I can recommend the chicken mayo toastie.

Name: Café Dynamis Coffee Shop
Address: c/o Paul Kruger &, Hibiscus Way, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Charl Landsberg
Review: This is the nicest little coffee shop where I waited for my mom while she was with her physio next door. Lovely people. Amazing atmosphere. Amazing coffee. Amazing cheesecake.

Name: Café Dynamis Coffee Shop
Address: c/o Paul Kruger &, Hibiscus Way, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Miss M Polley
Review: So lovely!! Amazing, friendly staff and a beautiful setting. I just looked for the nearest coffee shop to me, and I am so happy it brought me here.

Name: Café Dynamis Coffee Shop
Address: c/o Paul Kruger &, Hibiscus Way, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Tarn Walton
Review: Pancakes were very dry and they mixed up our order. The cappuccino was generous but coffee was quite bitter.

Name: Café Dynamis Coffee Shop
Address: c/o Paul Kruger &, Hibiscus Way, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Eleanor Kerkman
Review: We went as a group of 20 ladies. I really likes the decor. The service was good. When the food arrived, it was beautifully presented, it was not and everybody got what they ordered. Coffee was amazing. Thanks for an wonderful breakfast!

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Engela van der Merwe
Review: The coffee was out of this world! The staff, atmosphere and food are absolutely amazing! Great place for you and your furry friends to stop after a nice walk at the Deer Park, which is just up the road.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Angie Lawrence
Review: We had such a great experience. The staff are so friendly and warm - They offered us blankets for our baby (who was sleeping when we got there), as well as toys when he woke up. The waiters and manager were very attentive to our every need. The food was also fantastic - we enjoyed everything we ordered, and the coffee is delicious. The atmosphere is so relaxing too - We had a great time and stayed longer than planned because it was so nice there.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Eduard Zehrt
Review:
A very pleasant experience overall.

The food was good - very tasty with decent portions. The service was memorable for being exceptionall friendly. The atmosphere was lovely. The wall art, the plants and the warm soft lighting make for a very inviting atmosphere.

I hope to return to this somewhat hidden gem very soon.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Anjulé Smith
Review:
Management was so kind to host our group for a birthday party. While other venues wanted to charge venue hire or impose a minimum spend per person, Shift Espresso Bar in Deer Park made our group feel welcome, let every guest order off the full menu, and accommodated the kids (and one puppy!) inside, away from the rain. The staff even managed to keep separate tabs for all the accounts and there were zero issues by the time we had to pay.

It's also a fab place to work with great music playlists all day. Super friendly and helpful staff, great food and great service made us feel right at home!

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Trudy Botha
Review: Awesome views, great coffee selections,  delicious sweet treats, healthy alfresco breakfast and lunches, great atmosphere,  friendly staff , well done John Brown and staff on a delightful experience and awesome vibe, whether it's a coffee on the run, a Sunday leisurely breakfast, a mid week office break or a Saturday shake me up!!!!

Name: The Mill & Press Cafe - Masons Press building
Address: Masons Press, Woodlands Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Brandon
Review:
At R85 the falafel burger with a very generous helping of superb sweet potato fries will keep any vegan coming back again and again.
Like an earlier review said, the lady on the till could have been friendlier but maybe she's shy..

Name: The Mill & Press Cafe - Masons Press building
Address: Masons Press, Woodlands Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Khoreesha
Review: I have to express how disappointed I am lately by the inconsistency of what I usually order. Some days I am charged extra for adding something, other days I don’t. I know what I usually order. Very disappointed. Some staff members aren’t as friendly anymore either and notice a difference in treatment to other customers. Not a pleasant feeling to work in the building and feel I need to rather buy lunch elsewhere. Might have to find a new lunch spot. I spend money here but for this sort of treatment, I’ll support another business instead

Name: The Mill & Press Cafe - Masons Press building
Address: Masons Press, Woodlands Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Curly Lifestyle
Review: The food is fresh and absolutely delicious. We took our toddler who is a very picky eater with us and she ate all my food. The fresh squeezed juice was phenomenal. A must try.

Name: The Mill & Press Cafe - Masons Press building
Address: Masons Press, Woodlands Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Karl Hammerschmidt
Review: A very regular customer and never complained. They messed up my take away order yet again... which I realized when I got back. I obviously couldn't go back straight away so I took a photo and went back a few days later as it has happened one too many times now. Owner laughed and said there is no way they are refunding the items that were missed unless I come back straight away. Not really practical - or a great way to treat customers.

Name: The Mill & Press Cafe - Masons Press building
Address: Masons Press, Woodlands Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Michael Hanslo
Review: Lovely little takeaway / sit-down eatery, serving the local businesses wonderfully tasty meals. Coffee is great, portion sizes are well matched to price, and the menu specials rotate quite often.

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Em Alaryani
Review:
One of the best coffee shops in Cape Town is an exceptional place by all measures. It features a historical ambiance inspired by the series "Game of Thrones" adding a unique charm. The staff are extremely friendly and their coffee, along with the biscuits and cakes is unparalleled.

The aroma of coffee fills the place, making the visit a unique sensory experience. The prices are very reasonable compared to the quality of the place and the flavor of the coffee.

I highly recommend visiting this coffee shop, and I will certainly return to it repeatedly whenever I am in Cape Town.

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Özdemir Seyhun Dede
Review: The best coffee shop in the world. Owner is an exceptional human. Coffee and sweet bites are top notch

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Lauren C
Review:
This is honestly the best coffee I've ever had, I cannot recommend this place enough. You can tell this shop loves their coffee and their passion shows.
The atmosphere is perfect by the river. (Unfortunately dried up at the moment, perfect nonetheless)

Definitely worth a visit and worth a drive to visit, as we do.

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: jaclyn susan
Review: The selection of coffees and treats are world class.. its a little peace of heaven.  You could be in a log cabin in Europe or north America..  love the vibe and of course the service.  The chocolate Lome is my favourire

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Dave Anton
Review: Brilliant find in Houtbay!! Coffee roasted on site and was delicious. You must have a sweet treat as well as the cakes are Exceptional. The orange almond flour cake was so full of favour and probably the best example we ever had.

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Kevin Beh
Review: Stumbled upon this gem of a cafe whilst out and about in the early morning. Beautiful coffees and an amazing breakfast of eggs and avo on toast. Service is top notch with friendly and welcoming baristas and staff. Great job guys. Special thanks to Wisdom for his"wise" suggestions on the menu items.

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Sophie van Rijen
Review: THE BEST coffee in town! Love their smooth oat cappuccino and the staff is super friendly! In our 2 weeks holiday we visited 5 times for coffee! 5+ stars!! 🌟

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Ira de Luxo
Review:
The restaurant is very good I loved having breakfast
Top 10

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Emma Ellis-Brown
Review: BEST COFFEE SHOP IN CAPE TOWN🔥🔥🔥been coming here for 7 years🥹 amaaaaazing coffee! Delicious food!! Wonderful atmosphere and people ! It’s the place to be☕️

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Stephanie Blomkamp
Review: Beautiful souls work here and their energy makes it a wonderful experience. Whether a quick coffee or a delicious meal it’s a versatile happy place for all & everything. Ground Art Caffe even has a backgammon board, and for those in the know the boys who run the show are the ones to beat. The place, the people, the food are all pure gold. I’ve been going for years and it feels like family.

Name: Cafe Latino N1 City
Address: N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Ellen Langenhoven
Review:
I once sat at Cafe Latino and the waiter gave better service to another customer than he did me. I didn't feel too happy with the service he gave me.

The other time I sat there, the waitress and the manager served me, and their service was top tier. So, I'm not sure how to rate the service. I guess the second time made up for the first time.

Their food is amazing. I can't complain about the food.

The atmosphere is amazing as well. However, because of my sore back, I would love a more comfortable, warmer seating... but that's just me. Other than that, it's fine just as it is.

Name: Cafe Latino N1 City
Address: N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Lana Wright
Review: I was here last 3 years ago and then again last week, their waiters are professional, eager to be helpful and the food is lovely and value for money, I had a hard time  choosing off the menu as it offers a wide variety.  I'll be back when I'm in the area again.

Name: Cafe Latino N1 City
Address: N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Thato Chiloane
Review: The food always slaps especially their breakfast👌

Name: Cafe Latino N1 City
Address: N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: frank holder
Review: Great chef..thank you for good food , I am not a smoker and sat at back of cafe' and smoke was quite strong when waiters came out out of smoking area

Name: Cafe Latino N1 City
Address: N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Rene Mostert
Review: This place makes THEEE BEST filled pancakes! There are a variety sweet and savory to choose from. The portions are generous especially with their cake.

Name: Reload Flamingo Square Centre
Address: Shop 10 Flamingo Square Centre Corner of Blaauwberg Road, Study St, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Madiba Mashao
Review:
This is by far the best coffee shop/ restaurant I've ever worked in. Because of this, I always start my day at Reload 🥰

Food: Very affordable food and drinks. Perfect for dates and work meetings without breaking the bank.
They bake their own bagels and bread, so always fresh food 🤤. PS: I even buy their bagels on Saturday morning for breakfast at home.

Service: The staff is always friendly and willing to help. Very humble and accommodating manager as well.

Atmosphere: Never too loud, hence I take my virtual meetings here. The staff will not bother you while on a call.

Just one of those hidden gems in tableview ☺️

Enjoy

Name: Reload Flamingo Square Centre
Address: Shop 10 Flamingo Square Centre Corner of Blaauwberg Road, Study St, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Bella Be
Review: Conveniently located near our home, we frequently pop into this spot for a quick brunch. While I haven't always been a fan of their menu offerings, I must admit, their creamy chicken soup won me over from the first spoonful. The outdoor ambiance is particularly charming, offering both indoor and outdoor seating options. While the service can be a tad sluggish if you're in a hurry, the staff's friendly demeanor more than makes up for it. Plus, the prices are wallet-friendly, making it a reliable choice for a casual meal.

Name: Reload Flamingo Square Centre
Address: Shop 10 Flamingo Square Centre Corner of Blaauwberg Road, Study St, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Kylah Brown
Review:
Reload is a true hidden gem, from the moment you step inside you are greeted with the irresistible aroma of freshly brewed coffee and the welcoming smiles of the staff.

What sets Reload apart is its simple yet soulful menu, featuring an array of homemade dishes that are bursting with flavor and made with love. Each dish is crafted with precision and passion by the talented chefs in the kitchen, whether you are in the mood for a hearty breakfast or a light lunch, you'll find something to satisfy your cravings here!

Reload will always be my favorite spot to grab breakfast or lunch!

Name: Reload Flamingo Square Centre
Address: Shop 10 Flamingo Square Centre Corner of Blaauwberg Road, Study St, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Dylan Stuart
Review:
As always, the staff, food and lovely garden surrounds make for an excellent afternoon to wind down after a day in the office. Of course having a full liquor license makes this more appealing.

Although situated in the heart of a busy shopping mall, the atmosphere in the garden gives you a sense of escape be it for a short moment. The garden is a pet friendly establishment and I have yet to have a bad experience there.

Well done to management and staff. 10/10

Name: Reload Flamingo Square Centre
Address: Shop 10 Flamingo Square Centre Corner of Blaauwberg Road, Study St, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Marisa Botes
Review:
Enjoyed this experience visiting Reload at Flamingo Square Centre. The service was amazing, and their Red Cuppachino is Amazing ♡ i will definitely go visit them again to grab something to eat. The atmosphere is very chilling and relaxing, and it is definitely worth the visit for a quick meeting or some lunch or dinner.
☆☆☆☆☆

Name: Café de la Monté
Address: Cafe de la Monte, Shop 17 Pinehurst Centre, Cnr Brakenfell Boulevard & Pinehurst Drive, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Bran Wood
Review: If you’re ever in the mall and need a break from all the shopping, head straight to Café de la Monté. This little gem has the most amazing food and baked goods. I’m talking drool-worthy pastries and super tasty sandwiches. It’s the perfect spot to grab a bite or enjoy a relaxing coffee.

Name: Café de la Monté
Address: Cafe de la Monte, Shop 17 Pinehurst Centre, Cnr Brakenfell Boulevard & Pinehurst Drive, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Chloe Georgia Green
Review: Nice little coffee spot. Just haven’t had the greatest service, depending on where you sit, it seems that the corner tables get forgotten inside the restaurant, as I’ve been about 6 times and whenever I’m seated in the corner, I struggle to get service. Other than that, the food is good and the cakes are fantastic.

Name: Café de la Monté
Address: Cafe de la Monte, Shop 17 Pinehurst Centre, Cnr Brakenfell Boulevard & Pinehurst Drive, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Eliné Imrie (Len)
Review: I am truly impressed :) Thank you for the great service! The interior is very welcoming, with a  a warm feel and some lovely wall decals. The food wasn't bad, the cake was very nice and the coffee isn't too badly priced. They also offer a Sunday roast special. The staff are very friendly and go the extra mile to ensure that you have a good experience. Children are welcome (there is a little play area for them). Well done guys

Name: Café de la Monté
Address: Cafe de la Monte, Shop 17 Pinehurst Centre, Cnr Brakenfell Boulevard & Pinehurst Drive, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Tani T
Review:
Great home cooked meals.
Their Milkshakes are just as popular.
Friendly staff. Laid-back atmosphere.
They serve home cooked meals every day of the week as well as on Sundays. a full Sunday lunch is available for either sit-down or takeaway options. See their weekly menu or ask to be added to their WhatsApp group where you can pre-order.

Name: Café de la Monté
Address: Cafe de la Monte, Shop 17 Pinehurst Centre, Cnr Brakenfell Boulevard & Pinehurst Drive, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Ellen Imrie
Review:
Didn't had cake this time although the cakes looked delicious. They got better since last time I've visited.

Good friendly service and delicious cake. A good variety to choose from too.

Name: Kanéla Café
Address: 78 Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Nthabiseng Matolo
Review: Whether you’re looking for an intimate time with family, a partner or friends with lovely atmosphere then this is the place for you. With a great variety of vegan options, lovely staff and they even have throw blankets for those chilly days. The place is kid and dog friendly and it’s 2 restaurants so you an option if you’re looking for meat and the likes

Name: Kanéla Café
Address: 78 Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Shubha Bhat
Review:
If you visit this place, you have to sit outside, such a beautiful view. Their coffee with a hint of cinnamon is amazing.

Sandwiches are such a common breakfast in South Africa. I like that they had something a little different, like their winter menu. Their winter menu had soups and pasta dish. Keep things different, Kanela ✨️.

Food and services are excellent.  Very friendly staff.

Name: Kanéla Café
Address: 78 Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Ксения Л.
Review: atmospheric cafe, cozy courtyard. the atmosphere is very pleasant. i went here to drink coffee. i didn't like the coffee, it was very bitter. the service also leaves much to be desired. i won't be back

Name: Kanéla Café
Address: 78 Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Skylar Ava Louw
Review: I recently visited Kanela and had an amazing experience! The coffee and food was exceptional, and the friendly service created such a welcoming environment. I highly recommend Kanela to anyone looking for a great cup of coffee and an amazing spot to get some work done or catch up with a friend. 5 stars and can't wait to go back!

Name: Kanéla Café
Address: 78 Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Felix Heuer
Review: One of the best breakfast places in Cape Town, great seating options in the shady courtyard. Outstanding service and many delicious vegan options on the menu. Can highly recommend.

Name: Bootlegger - Durbanville
Address: Shop 13 & 14 The Village Square C/o Oxford &, Queen St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Star Scribe
Review: The staff were friendly, professional, and attentive. The coffee was very good, and the muffin was delicious. It was a good place to spend an early morning hour.

Name: Bootlegger - Durbanville
Address: Shop 13 & 14 The Village Square C/o Oxford &, Queen St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Jacques Cronjé
Review: Definitely my new favourite breakfast spot.  Service was quick and efficient without being overbearing.  Delicious iced coffee with the BootBun was the perfect way to start the morning.  Portion to price ratio is spot on.

Name: Bootlegger - Durbanville
Address: Shop 13 & 14 The Village Square C/o Oxford &, Queen St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Kayle Devine
Review: Great Atmosphere, Amazing coffee, Food a bit on the pricey side of things especially in comparison to other similar restaurants in the area but none the less delicious. Service was good too. Will definitely be back :)

Name: Bootlegger - Durbanville
Address: Shop 13 & 14 The Village Square C/o Oxford &, Queen St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Amy Cooper
Review: I’ve been coming to the Bootleggers Durbanville since it opened, and not once have i EVER had a bad experience! The food, service, absolutely everything is always amazing!! The owner, Nicola, is always so lovely and welcoming to all her guest's, such a lovely lady with the biggest smile - i will never stop coming to this place!

Name: Bootlegger - Durbanville
Address: Shop 13 & 14 The Village Square C/o Oxford &, Queen St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Trish Storbeck
Review:
The outdoor seating is lovely on a warm day, with some nice shade and cover from the wind. It's also quiter outside without the noise of the coffee machines. I had wanted to order a milkshake, but they'd run out of ice-cream, though they were expecting a delivery soon. So I decided on an iced mocha instead, which was delicious. To eat, I had the Buttermilk Flapjack Stack with bacon - it was so good and beautifully presented, and a very decent portion size (I couldn’t finish it).

The servers were friendly and helpful. And the atmosphere was nice and chilled.

Name: Bean Tree Cafe (Observatory)
Address: Corner Of Browning And Main Road, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Jawahir Hoosain
Review: It's an amazing place to come to if you are looking for divine food, awesome service, great prices and a fabulous atmosphere. Thank you guys so much for being so helpful and amazing. This deserves a five star review and it's my go to place for an awesome breakfast. Eating that delicious food really brightened my entire day. Definitely recommend to anyone who wants the best experience at a restaurant.

Name: Bean Tree Cafe (Observatory)
Address: Corner Of Browning And Main Road, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Thato Daumas
Review:
The food here is so lovely. Just a bit light on the salt. But there’s salt on the table so that’s not an issue really.

I had the very berry tea with hibiscus and rosehip oooooh! 👌🏾 lovely. This was the best breakfast I’ve had in a whiiiiiiile. Ooooh. 🥰🥰🥰

The staff was just alright tbh. Not overly welcoming neither adversarial nor neglectful. I didn’t have a problem with them. Didn’t love them either. So meh.

Cleanliness is average. Acceptable.

The atmosphere is also okay.

Pricing is also decent. All in all great really.

I gave them a 5 because I really like it there. It’s comfortable and I really cant fault it. I had a goooooood filling and fresh breakfast.

Name: Bean Tree Cafe (Observatory)
Address: Corner Of Browning And Main Road, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Dawood Jawoodien
Review: Lovely breakfast. Very tasty and nice portion size. Was definitely one of my best breakfasts in a long time. The venue was Clean and the coffee very nice too.

Name: Bean Tree Cafe (Observatory)
Address: Corner Of Browning And Main Road, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Raeesa F
Review:
The pizza was very nice.
The worst was the banana pancake. It was bitter and was not fried properly. Would not recommend it

Name: Bean Tree Cafe (Observatory)
Address: Corner Of Browning And Main Road, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Aneeqah Abrahams
Review:
friendly staff!
food was exceptional!
definitely my new favorite spot!

Name: Lime Tree Cafe
Address: Shop 8, Sherwood Centre, Corner Children's Way and Dreyersdal Road, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Barbara Newport
Review:
As a carer I took my client (who uses a walker and her behavior is erratic) for brunch. I didn't eat, but she had a decent meal. The restaurant was busy (it's clearly a popular restaurant!) and as a result the service was a bit slow. My client had words with the manager, but she handled it exceptionally well and my client was content afterwards. Thanks Lesley, and also for your assistance when I realized I'd misplaced my credit card. It was a happy ending after we found it.
Definitely recommended .

Name: Lime Tree Cafe
Address: Shop 8, Sherwood Centre, Corner Children's Way and Dreyersdal Road, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Margaret Dugmore
Review:
We had a very delicious coffee and snack today.
Not our first visit and the experience was as always excellent.
I especially love the plating of food at Lime Tree Cafe. Our food was delicious.

Name: Lime Tree Cafe
Address: Shop 8, Sherwood Centre, Corner Children's Way and Dreyersdal Road, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Enid Son
Review:
We went for a "blunch" (early lunch) after a walk in Tokai Forest.   We had their classic burger which was so delicious. The croissant with salmon and cream cheese was generously filled.  Hubby had the pastrami with smoked chicken and a whole lot of toppings - also delicious. Great coffee,milk shakes and iced coffee. I loved my freshly squeezed orange and carrot juice with ginger.  Their cakes looked good as well - but this is for next time.
They are doggy friendly too which is such a bonus for us as we took our dogs to the forest.  Our waitress was efficient and friendly.    Definitely worthy of a return visit when we are out that way.

Name: Lime Tree Cafe
Address: Shop 8, Sherwood Centre, Corner Children's Way and Dreyersdal Road, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Hendrickse
Review:
What a fantastic hidden gem of a restaurant.
The venue is nice and the the food is good and well priced. The service of the food was a bit slow, granted they were full. Parking is free out front. I would highly recommend.

Name: Lime Tree Cafe
Address: Shop 8, Sherwood Centre, Corner Children's Way and Dreyersdal Road, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Willem Coetsee
Review: Loved this unexpected gem of a place. We are not from area and typed on Google for a place to eat… this one got the top spot… good find! Great burgers and vibe…

Name: Brick Lane Eatery Century City
Address: The Quays, 142 Park Ln, Century City, Cape Town
Author: ThePigThing
Review:
I enjoyed my time here. It was such a nice, small, cozy cafe to have a meal with the family. The flatbread pizzas were absolutely divine. The half price special on a Friday is worth going for! Our waiter was a lovely gentleman who served us eagerly, respectfully and in an efficient pace.

You will definitely be seeing me again! Such a great restaurant 😃

Name: Brick Lane Eatery Century City
Address: The Quays, 142 Park Ln, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Jade Heneke
Review:
Serene, private outdoor setting provides the perfect atmosphere for the delicious food. Had the savory pancakes with a poached egg, bacon, avo, and some incredible tomato chutney. As well as the breakfast bagel.
Servers were amazing, checking up often and ensuring you have everything you need. Service was quick and food came out hot. I cannot fault this place, did not want to leave, and I look forward to my next visit!

Name: Brick Lane Eatery Century City
Address: The Quays, 142 Park Ln, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Clive Anthony
Review: What a cool spot away from the hustle of Canal Walk Shopping Centre. A real little oasis. The staff makes you feel like you’re coming home - Really friendly. The food also tastes like home - delicious. The prices are good so you can have some dessert too. We had a very good experience and I highly recommend. A well deserved 5 star ⭐️

Name: Brick Lane Eatery Century City
Address: The Quays, 142 Park Ln, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Steve
Review:
Favorite place to have breakfast in century city. Staff are very friendly and the food is great (and affordable)!

The location is a little secluded and often has a few pets around which add up to a great atmosphere

Name: Brick Lane Eatery Century City
Address: The Quays, 142 Park Ln, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Laetitia mb
Review:
13.06.2024

My Best friend took me out for lunch and I enjoyed it even more than when I had been before.
We always have a beer shandy, delicious as always.
The food was sublime. I enjoyed every mouthful. Can’t wait to go back for the nachos

Name: Eden Café Restaurant
Address: Eden on the Bay Centre, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Audrey Baird
Review: On Monday, 7 October 2024, we enjoyed a delicious meal, while appreciating the wonderful weather and spectacular views. Service from our friendly waiter was great and we received exactly what we ordered. My seeded chicken mayo panini was fresh and tasty. They have great coffee too!

Name: Eden Café Restaurant
Address: Eden on the Bay Centre, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: C M
Review: Can I give this place an extra star. Such a great place we always come here and get breakfast. Great food and the view the relaxing friendly people. If by chance you are near by try it out its well worth a visit. The picture is of the beach you view from your table and your right on the beach. Great place

Name: Eden Café Restaurant
Address: Eden on the Bay Centre, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Lee-Anne Curtis-Cox
Review:
We absolutely love this little cafe at Eden on the Bay, and we are clearly not the only ones! It is a very popular spot any time from 11h00.

The manager always seems to be on duty, and the regular waiters are incredibly efficient, and orders are placed quickly, and the food arrives soon after.

Great menu, fantastic coffee.

We are never disappointed.

Name: Eden Café Restaurant
Address: Eden on the Bay Centre, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Caddie Hart
Review:
You can’t beat a location with a sea view!
Service was good, although they weren’t too busy. Food was tasty- I had the mozzarella bagel. It’s definitely not a proper American style bagel, more bread-like but was good nonetheless

Name: Eden Café Restaurant
Address: Eden on the Bay Centre, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Darren Ford
Review:
Our favourite breakfast spot in Blouberg. The food is always delicious and fresh.
The staff are incredibly friendly and go out of their way to make sure that you enjoy your visit. It overlooks the spectacular kite surfing beach and is pet friendly which is always a plus! A good way to start your day.

Name: Bootlegger - Bakoven
Address: 38 Victoria Road, Bakoven, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Izod Man
Review: Great breakfast place! I stopped  in today and tried their Bootegger with a large cappuccino. Everything was delicious. The only thing I could to think to make this 5 star would be the staffing to button up more on their service skills to ensure they are offering the best experience to the patrons. One gentleman, his name is Roy, upon entry was personable but the waitress may need some training on “offering service with a smile” approach. Greet with a smile, offering to assist, answering questions and thanking for stopping by. Otherwise, a convenient spot in Cape Quarters if you have other business there to take care of.

Name: Bootlegger - Bakoven
Address: 38 Victoria Road, Bakoven, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: tamara delbrooke-jones
Review: Best way to start my Saturday morning is with a Coffee from Bootlegger at Bakoven before hitting a run or walk! Then some brunch on the way back🤗🔥 Friendly and quick service and quality food and coffee. I couldn’t ask for better!

Name: Bootlegger - Bakoven
Address: 38 Victoria Road, Bakoven, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Veronika
Review: I had a flat white with oat milk, and a strawberry smoothie. Both amazing! Very friendly staff. I got offered a taste test of a product they are selling - coffee nut butter

Name: Bootlegger - Bakoven
Address: 38 Victoria Road, Bakoven, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Aran Nathanson
Review: It's on a busy road, so there's a lot of traffic, but the food is great and so is the staff

Name: Bootlegger - Bakoven
Address: 38 Victoria Road, Bakoven, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: sumarie schreiner
Review: I've been a regular at this coffee shop for a couple of years now, and it's truly become my second home. The coffee here is simply the best—rich, flavorful, and absolutely addictive! The food is consistently excellent and packed with flavour. But what really sets this place apart are the warm smiles and outstanding service...every single time! Each visit feels like catching up with old friends, and it's the kind of welcoming atmosphere that keeps me coming back day after day!

Name: Cafe Nouveau, Pinelands
Address: Central Building, 7a Central Square, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Waseem Moolla
Review: Had a Great experience. Abongile and Tadiwa were simply excellent. Kind and nurturing towards my kid. Food was great. Over all top class. High recommendation from me.

Name: Cafe Nouveau, Pinelands
Address: Central Building, 7a Central Square, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: KIM RUTGERS
Review: A very cute and quaint place. Love the decor and goodies for sale. However, not really a warm welcome and no fond farewell. Such an awesome location bring next to the renowned Italian restaurant. Long wait food (salad) and the (small) Cobb salad (R125) should be called a BLT salad.

Name: Cafe Nouveau, Pinelands
Address: Central Building, 7a Central Square, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Carol Byett
Review:
Friendly and personal service from the owner the couple on the occasions we have visited. Good atmosphere with soothing music that doesn't say hurry up and go to me.
The menu isn't extensive but enough variety to chose from & the food is delicious and well presented and prepared. I opted not to have chips with my main meal and was offered salad instead that was nice and fresh. I opted for dessert today - delicious apple strudel served with cream - such a generous portion that I only managed to eat half of it.
We will be back 😋

Name: Cafe Nouveau, Pinelands
Address: Central Building, 7a Central Square, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Paul Smith
Review: What a quaint and cheerful establishment. I highly recommend the fact that everyone was talking to each other and not glued to cellphones. Refreshing!

Name: Cafe Nouveau, Pinelands
Address: Central Building, 7a Central Square, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Ash46
Review:
Nice chilled vibe, with good chilled music.
Great service from the moment you walk in, Imtiyaaz is a star.
Food was good and prices very reasonable. We had the chicken tikka sandwich and chicken wrap, both came with rustic fries.
Will definitely come back for dinner

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Ludwig Otto
Review: Strong wifi, not loud at all very nice space to work remotely,  with great coffee and very tasty food. The owner is very hands on. The barista was on point with his cappuccino. Ample parking in a quiet neighborhood.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Ava Peattie
Review:
Service was slow, waitress forgot my drinks order. Portion size is amazing, but quality of food could be of a higher standard. I ordered the butternut, feta, avo, cucumber sandwich on rye, bit bland nothing special, but great combination. The chai freezer was really great and refreashing.

Will definatly give this resturant another go if Im in the area!

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Dima Zamisa
Review: Wonderful Cafe for your meeting with friends and family outing.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: A W
Review:
Wonderful, strong, unpretentious coffee at great prices. Visit on a Sunday morning to have the place all to yourself.

Feb 2020 update: Flat white is still R25 and good! R39 Rise and Shine breakfast is good value too if you're peckish but not ravenous.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Khanyi Zulu
Review: It's a nice local café. The service is very warm ,but the muffins we got came in plastic packaging. Would've been nice to have them fresh or served heated.

Name: Dario's Cafe’
Address: 30 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Julia Kallweit
Review:
Such a warm and welcoming place. Very kind staff, checking in on you all the time. We came with a little one in a pram and wasn’t an issue at all to let her sleep.

If you got kids, they even got a playground, with swings a trampoline etc
