Food was great, I can recommend for breakfast “Dario’s Choice” with 2 extra poached eggs. Presentation isn’t fabulous and food was good, same as the coffee.

Easy to get parking, just not the most serene setting right by the road, but a nice busy social spot.

We’ll be back 🙌

Name: Dario's Cafe’
Address: 30 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Kirstin Wiedow
Review: Locals spot with a really good emmentaler and gherkin toasties option. Some heaters outside and lovely staff. The bolognese and ravioli pasta was OK.

Name: Dario's Cafe’
Address: 30 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Wayne Adriaanse
Review: Been here probably twice before, went for a quick breakfast, was greeted by Dario as if he had known me for 10 years. Very welcoming and comfortable environment. Was also asked to stay for a Friday morning grappa.

Name: Dario's Cafe’
Address: 30 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Russell Brown
Review: Fantastic coffee and amazing service with real smiles and humor peppered with Dario's serious fist pump. A bit noisy sometimes, however, I love it, every time. Highly recommended, the coffee is reliable, and that is important to me.

Name: Dario's Cafe’
Address: 30 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Erin Ferber
Review:
Vegan friendly with menu clearly label. I enjoyed a cappuccino and one of the vegan burgers. Both were tasty!

Cute local spot in Hout Bay.

Name: The Woodstock Hub
Address: 170 Victoria Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Jesse Gillis
Review:
This has to be my new favorite spot. Kelvin Corner is basically a shared work space office catered towards the film and media industry. With gear hire downstairs, meeting rooms upstairs and desks. Great vibe and the staff that work there are all amazing kind people. They also have 5g internet available. Greta kitchen and coffee bar, I had a chicken wrap for lunch and was blown away about how nice it was.
I can highly recommend this place if you are looking for an office space or even just a chill space in week. Relaxed atmosphere.

Name: The Woodstock Hub
Address: 170 Victoria Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Timmi CPV
Review:
Need a place to work?
Hotdesks @ R150 pd
150mb internet speed, and a Cafe so you don't need to leave for lunch.

Did I mention there is roof access as well.

Name: The Woodstock Hub
Address: 170 Victoria Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: G P
Review:
Rarely do I give a negative review. I took a detour for office this morning because of a crash on M5 and thought to be blown away by the coffee as this location is highly rated.

Unfortunately, there is no branding with "Woodstock Hub", you've to check the google street view, trust your instincts and double check with the staff to confirm that this is "THE" place. In my case, when I checked-in, it was dead as a dodo, décor was artistically eclectic (and the only positive I could find) but way too run down and there was no one manning the coffee counter. When the hapless barista eventually came in, he indicated that they are yet to open which is contrary to their timings on google page (0730 am to 0600 pm) even though I came in at 1000 AM. Needless to say, I was utterly disappointed for a blatant disregard of potential clientele, no apology whatsoever.
Given the cold shoulder I experienced here today, I may never return again.

Of what I observed, I do not think owners care about this place as either the hours on google page are wrong and haven't been corrected or the staff's communication skills aren't sufficient to inform customers of coincidental exceptions.

Moda Deli just a stone's throw away was my coffee savior again today.

Name: The Woodstock Hub
Address: 170 Victoria Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Daniela Franca Joffe
Review: I have been working here for the past few weeks and I cannot recommend it highly enough. The space is light- and sun-filled and beautifully curated, with high ceilings and a general feeling of spaciousness and tranquility. The staff are warm and attentive and, most importantly, real. Best of all, the hot desks are affordable and accessible compared to most in the city.

Name: The Woodstock Hub
Address: 170 Victoria Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Cecile Du Preez
Review: Very nice space to meet...

Name: Cafe Amazing
Address: 10 Induland Cres, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Lenah Pakati

Name: Cafe Amazing
Address: 10 Induland Cres, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Sumaya Hendricks

Name: Cafe Amazing
Address: 10 Induland Cres, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: george moosa

Name: Cafe Amazing
Address: 10 Induland Cres, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: shinaaz adjiet

Name: Peak Cafe
Address: 72 Main Rd, Fish Hoek, Cape Town
Author: Smiley 1715
Review: What a cute and extremely modern yet artistic little cafe. I wish I lived closer so I could visit daily. Our waiter was extremely efficient and charming. I love the interior design, and the food was just as amazing. I highly recommend stopping by if you are ever in the area.

Name: Peak Cafe
Address: 72 Main Rd, Fish Hoek, Cape Town
Author: Johan Fourie
Review: Made a quick stop for coffee. We were so amazed by this place that we stayed for breakfast. Something is the menu that you tick what you want. Their christmas mince pies are really the best. As a tourist, I wish I could have that close by where I stay.  We enjoyed the food and even bought a few other items from the second hand section. The big windows that are opened create a nice vibe. Can highly recommend this place. Their is something unexpected and unique to experience in the bathroom. Go an visit them to experience that 😉

Name: Peak Cafe
Address: 72 Main Rd, Fish Hoek, Cape Town
Author: Stephanie Scott
Review: Had a delicious breakfast at this restaurant. Food was delicious and very affordable and it was a lovely morning🌸

Name: Peak Cafe
Address: 72 Main Rd, Fish Hoek, Cape Town
Author: Dr. Jane White
Review: Great little place to meet for coffee and breakfast. Had the best cup of latte.

Name: Peak Cafe
Address: 72 Main Rd, Fish Hoek, Cape Town
Author: Veronica Wolmarans
Review:
The waiter made several mistakes to our order, I was kind enough to eat the order that was placed in front of me by this insecure waiter just to find when I made payment in cash he stole a further R15 from an already generous more than 10% tip looking at me hoping I'd miss it!

Horrible experience!

To top this off the lady sitting behind us proceeded to take a picture of my daughter's glasses to show it sparkles in the lighting.... Who does that? In todays day and age ... Take pictures of other people's children... without asking and doing so with a smile.

Name: The Collective Hout Bay & Cafe Collective
Address: 35 Main Rd, Scott Estate, Cape Town
Author: Mapula Mhlanga
Review: I loved the warmth I felt from the moment we entered the place. It was a Friday night and it felt like one big family dinner. I enjoyed their Chinese cabbage and filo pastry from their Haverst table and my daughter recommended their Lemon meringue for dessert of which I enjoyed so much the sweet and sour taste combination was perfect.

Name: The Collective Hout Bay & Cafe Collective
Address: 35 Main Rd, Scott Estate, Cape Town
Author: Eben Smith
Review:
This is an absolute must for anyone! An amazing experience from the moment we arrived. The outdoor seating area is tastefully decorated and surrounded by small shops selling arts and crafts. There is even a delightful children's area where they can paint and entertain themselves.
The food was absolutely divine! I highly recommend trying the Harvest Table. All dishes are served buffet-style while Chef Tam was close-by to answer any questions. Everything was cooked to perfection and I have never tasted better sweet potatoes anywhere in the world.
I would recommend booking ahead of arrival.

Name: The Collective Hout Bay & Cafe Collective
Address: 35 Main Rd, Scott Estate, Cape Town
Author: Rethabile Mashala
Review: A delightful outdoor lunch buffet experience. The chef is such a warm and welcoming person. Loved every bit of the experience.

Name: The Collective Hout Bay & Cafe Collective
Address: 35 Main Rd, Scott Estate, Cape Town
Author: Woon Peng Ziady
Review:
A lovely spot for the soul. The cafe is in the courtyard with lovely architecture and tasteful decorations by the owners.

Great service with a smile, excellent baked goods and cake. Lunch is The Harvest Table (go at 12noon when it starts, why wait?) where you select from multiple dishes and pay by weight. I always enjoy the salads by lovely Chef Tamalyn and her protein is always cooked just right. Lots of vegetarian options too.

Shop at The Collective (Old Post Office building) with it's many nooks and crannies as you discover local artisanal goods. My go-to place for birthday gifts. Support local, enjoy local.

Name: The Collective Hout Bay & Cafe Collective
Address: 35 Main Rd, Scott Estate, Cape Town
Author: Anneke Frank
Review:
Absolutely amazing!😍
It was everything we hoped it would be.
We felt welcome from the moment we arrived and ofcourse had to try all the food that was put out😅
Everything was just so tasty, my favorite was definitely the lamb chops and stuffed chicken although everything was fresh and tasty.🥰🥰
The staff along with the owners are all lovely people and engaged with us quite a bit.😄
The entire look and feel of the restaurant was relaxing.
We definitely recommend The Collective and can't wait to go back. 🤩

Name: Mynt Cafe
Address: 31 Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Kyle Grovers
Review:
Very friendly service, pizza and sushi was delicious. If you are a lover of sushi, they have an All day sushi special and very affordable, happy hour is also good when it’s busy you would probably only be able to order like 3-4 cocktails, other than that, it’s worth coming back to.

View and scenery is amazing, good music and atmosphere

Name: Mynt Cafe
Address: 31 Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Melanie N-P
Review:
Little can be said about the view other than perfect, situated on the iconic camps bay stretch. The restaurant has a funky appearance combining different art & styles.
Food was flavorful & well prepared, especially the starter wings & gnocchi. The restaurant seems to offer quite a few specials & deals but being handed numerous menus & getting told all the specials can be quite overwhelming. Unfortunately mocktails were not part of the happy hour special which is downer. We also were not sure on who exactly was our waiter/ress & there were so many different people checking on us that we often thought fellow guests were staff. Whilst the waitron staff have aprons the "management" staff looked like guests & it was hard to know who to ask for help as we waited for some time to get our bill.
Some work needed to refine the customer experience but definitely worth a visit.

Name: Mynt Cafe
Address: 31 Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Raquel
Review: Highly recommend! Caribbean shake was great! Prawns/Calamari and Cesar Salad were delicious! Nicely open to get fresh air from the ocean with stunning views of the boardwalk and beach. Felt comfortable with their COVID precautions - sanitizing, temp checks, and masks on correctly over staffs nose.

Name: Mynt Cafe
Address: 31 Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Darryl Jordaan
Review: This was a phenomenal experience. The atmosphere was great. Very relaxed and jovial. The food was very good and a large portion. My wife could not finish her full meal. Guess who ate the rest. Besides that, we had great live Jazzy music of days gone by but still alive today.  Definitely a venue for a relaxed and exciting time to enjoy your meal and listen to great music when the musician is available.  Believe it or not the prices are more than reasonable. Give it a shot in Camps Bay.

Name: Mynt Cafe
Address: 31 Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Lucille Ripepi
Review: What an Incredible Experience with Louis and Tracey. The Vibe and the Atmosphere even before we had anything to drink and or eat. The conversation and the mere fact that they honestly gave us their opinion on what we should try. And yes their suggestions were spot on. Then you get to the cocktails that made you crave more and we did. To having our wine in a stand with ice and we were offered to top up was really truly something you see in movies. Then the food and the presentation. Eating with your eyes before it touches your lips. Omgee my bff and could have spent the entire day there. Thanks to the incredible Team that made our Experience truly one for the books. We will definitely be coming back for more.

Name: Ohana Cafe
Address: 23 Main Rd, Kalk Bay, Cape Town
Author: Sybz Solomons
Review: Lovely spot with a sea view in Kalk Bay. Pet friendly, and laid back atmosphere. Typical deep south feel, with friendly people.. stunning flower arrangements. Very good choice of healthy juices on the menu. A mean cup of coffee as well. Outside area has a beachy feel.

Name: Ohana Cafe
Address: 23 Main Rd, Kalk Bay, Cape Town
Author: Vannette Viljoen
Review: An amazing ocean view light meal space. Covered and open air seating with a view of the ocean and train that passes by. Service is good but slow, which falls into the whole "Slaapstad" theme of things. Take your time, take a breath, have a meal and enjoy the view. I love this spot!!

Name: Ohana Cafe
Address: 23 Main Rd, Kalk Bay, Cape Town
Author: Lone Rucksack
Review: Absolute gem of a spot. I went early Sunday morning as the team was opening and got the coveted little table in the outdoor area with the view of the sea and lighthouse. The service was stellar and the best I’ve had here in South Africa. I had the eggs benny with a rosti base (highly recommend) and a flat white with oat milk. Will definitely be back!

Name: Ohana Cafe
Address: 23 Main Rd, Kalk Bay, Cape Town
Author: Amy Thorne
Review: Vegetable tart was excellent, as was the “harvest table” salad deal. All beautiful salads; aubergine bulgur terrine, cumin roast carrot and onion, braised pork, grilled courgette and yogurt dressing…. Service was excellent too. Added bonus is being able to see whales while you eat

Name: Ohana Cafe
Address: 23 Main Rd, Kalk Bay, Cape Town
Author: Jacki Sands
Review: Lovely understated sea-side setting, with a calm, relaxing atmosphere. Staff are wonderful, friendly and suit the vibe. The food was delicious, especially the famous Ohana bun - a must-have, whether there, or to go.

Name: Deer Park Café
Address: 2 Deerpark Dr W, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Evan Swart
Review: The Cafe is situated in a great place with stunning views outside. Food and service was good although a bit slow at times on father's day possibly due to the amount of people in and out. So can be quiet busy at times although the nice thing was that we could make a reservation. Great place if you have kids as the play park is a good place to run off the food after with the kids.

Name: Deer Park Café
Address: 2 Deerpark Dr W, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Ripe Avocado
Review: Lovely café for brunch on a sunny day with floor-to-ceiling windows for natural light and tasteful decor. The back of the restaurant opens up onto a large grassy garden with a playground where dogs and children can come and go as they please. Food was pretty good with several vegan and vegetarian options. My only suggestion would be to add labels to the menu to help people with dietary restrictions see what they can have.

Name: Deer Park Café
Address: 2 Deerpark Dr W, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Kerri A (Kez)
Review:
Firstly, I would  like to thank Timothy for the great service with a broad smile. Plus, all the other staff were extremely pleasant and accommodating. Timothy had recommended the Lamb curry, which a number of other guests were enjoining. But I felt for another favourite of mine, and I was definitely  not disappointed! The Butternut, Sage & Ricotta Lasagne, with side salad, had my taste buds begging for more.

Secondly, can we please mention the beautiful setting. I love dining when I feel like I am immersed in nature. Plus, it's a great place for the folks and kids!

Ps. Rumour has it that the hot chocolate is a decadent nutty chocolate delight.

Name: Deer Park Café
Address: 2 Deerpark Dr W, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Rick Rohan
Review: Good location next to a small park with ample space for children to play. Dog friendly, the doggos can enjoy the park. Coming to the food, we had breakfast, which was good but not worth what we paid for it.

Name: Deer Park Café
Address: 2 Deerpark Dr W, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Lulu E
Review: The place is very cute and aesthetic. Cool vibes. The service was excellent and these are the best nachos l’ve ever had. Definitely one of my favourite spots on CT

Name: Vovo Telo Bakery & Café
Address: and, Constantia Village Shopping Centre, Shop 17B Corner Constantia Main, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Tegan Kelly
Review: A great cafe with wonderful variety and the staff are always very friendly and welcoming! Be sure to check out some of the pantry items they have on sale or the freshly baked bread that they make that you buy to take home (that is, if you get there early enough 😉). Being Vovo Telo, the food is always delicious too!

Name: Vovo Telo Bakery & Café
Address: and, Constantia Village Shopping Centre, Shop 17B Corner Constantia Main, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Richard Morris
Review: I did only pop in late at 17h00 for a quick  chocolate Croissant and cinnamon cronut which were stale but possibly accepted due to last stock. I felt that R41.00 for a flat white is a little pricy when one compares it with other coffee shops where they average around R35.00 The store did have an interesting selection of x2 brands of Kombcha. Service was friendly.

Name: Vovo Telo Bakery & Café
Address: and, Constantia Village Shopping Centre, Shop 17B Corner Constantia Main, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Sven Agnew
Review:
I’ve been a regular and consistent customer for this lovely little Cafe since the beginning and I keep coming back because of the consistent quality of food and service.

The team at Vovo Telo clearly work hard and take pride in delivering great service and excellent meals at good value and it shows.

This place is a gem.

Name: Vovo Telo Bakery & Café
Address: and, Constantia Village Shopping Centre, Shop 17B Corner Constantia Main, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: Kelly Herring
Review: After running around to many different bakeries asking if they could make a cake for my mom (over the public holiday weekend) and having no success, the beautiful staff at Vovo Telo came to my rescue! Fiona was so warm and helpful from the moment we started talking to her, offering to help in any way she could. She made arrangements calling her boss and the head baker to try and work something out for us. Lizzie - who was so friendly and lovely - stepped in to finish up the final touches of the cake process, coordinating with us, adding in a few extra goodies to decorate the cake with, and making it such an incredibly smooth process! A massive thank you to the head baker for coming through for us and making the most delicious cake!!! Every guest kept saying how yummy it was, and we fully agree. We took it home and decorated it ourselves but there is no way I could’ve made a cake half as amazing as she did! Thank you thank you THANK YOU! We are so grateful and my mom was so very happy 😊❤️

Name: Vovo Telo Bakery & Café
Address: and, Constantia Village Shopping Centre, Shop 17B Corner Constantia Main, Spaanschemat River Rd, Constantia, Cape Town
Author: G P
Review: Good coffee experience. Bought a freshly baked bread which turned out nice. If not for a fly in their display section I would have given 5 stars.

Name: Cafe du Cap
Address: Shop 1, Kildare Centre Corner Kildare road and, Main St, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Satish Gupta
Review: If you looking for quick bite or coffee to sip, this is Stylish and above many restaurants when considering their size overall. Comfy sitting and just the right music and volume, elegant servers and presentation. They sure do go the mile, it was my first visit will return again. Downside is limited seating, tables tightly spaced. Not for proper dinning or a place where expect good space.

Name: Cafe du Cap
Address: Shop 1, Kildare Centre Corner Kildare road and, Main St, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Yuri Bhaga
Review: Great little spot just down the road from Cavendish . Very fast service ? Food came out fast fresh and tasty . Accommodations were made for us and service was friendly . Nice spot for a light meal of breakfast .

Name: Cafe du Cap
Address: Shop 1, Kildare Centre Corner Kildare road and, Main St, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Enid Son
Review: A lovely spot in Newlands for delicious breakfast.  Slight mountain view - comfortable seating on the patio.  Friendly service.

Name: Cafe du Cap
Address: Shop 1, Kildare Centre Corner Kildare road and, Main St, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: jaclyn susan
Review: A little place in heaven in Newlands.. Its a great place to go during the week for a glass of wine and a spot of dinner. The service can be improved.. there was only one waiter on duty.. my orange cake was very dry and I was promised by the waitor that I was going to get served cream/ it never came which was disappointing.   I waited quite a while to get topped up with Beverages and suggestions for more things to eat and drink.. the waitor should up sale more and offer suggestions.. other than the service,  I really liked the place..

Name: Cafe du Cap
Address: Shop 1, Kildare Centre Corner Kildare road and, Main St, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Joseph Green
Review: Really cute venue at super reasonable prices. Atmosphere felt very curated in the outside space. Food offering was very tasty and fresh with a good variety of options. Would be a good location for a wholesome date- 10/10 would recommend

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Mitch
Review:
Excellent tea shop! This is a proper tea shop that steeps based on temperature and time. The sencha fukuyu was very nice.
The atmosphere is excellent, tons of tea accessories to purchase. And a massive list of loose leaf tea to go.
I will definitely be back!

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Amanda Rinquest
Review:
Quiet and unassuming little tea shop with a huge selection of teas to purchase, and a few things other vegetarian things to buy.

It can be overwhelming but the staff help you decide based on what you feel like snacking on. Really worth a visit!

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Anastasia Coulbanis
Review: What a different way to spend a morning! Tea tasting! Extremely knowledgeable, amazing service an such a peaceful atmosphere. Definitely going back!

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Nazeema Khan
Review: Great little spot to relax and enjoy a selection of any tea you can imagine  - think black, white, green, fruits - and some options for coffee lovers. Their cakes are absolutely divine (with vegan options), and even though they have a limited food menu, it is well worth it. The shop has a variety of goods and utensils for sale. Service is above expectations. Do yourself a favor and experience a little bit of magic and relaxation.

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Brian Claassen
Review:
This Teacafe is an absolute gem. I received the warmest greeting and the owner actually remembered me even though I had only been in once before, many weeks prior.

Their tea selection is simple awe inspiring and extremely well priced. I had the omelette which was delicious and its tea pairing.

My girlfriend has insisted that I bring her along on my next visit, which will hopefully be very soon.

Customer service at its absolute best!!

Name: Enmasse Espresso & Tea Merchants
Address: V&A Waterfront, Shop 6, The Watershed, 17 Dock Rd, Cape Town
Author: David Ding
Review: Good offerings and explanation. While I drank many different types of tea, roobois isn't my type, but the shop presented a variety of ways for me to understand it. Biology, taste, texture, samples, you name it. Good setup, education, and description wins this rating.

Name: Enmasse Espresso & Tea Merchants
Address: V&A Waterfront, Shop 6, The Watershed, 17 Dock Rd, Cape Town
Author: benaya simeon
Review: Amazing tea collection, and the staff also super helpful to explain every single tea variant, glad to get tea here.

Name: Enmasse Espresso & Tea Merchants
Address: V&A Waterfront, Shop 6, The Watershed, 17 Dock Rd, Cape Town
Author: Zintle Mlomo
Review: In the hustle and bustle of v&a, I enjoyed a very relaxing jasmin queen tea. There are also interesting tea mixtures available for sale and I took home the pinotage rooibos tea, which I absolutely enjoy. Will return to try out their other flavours. Would recommend to tea lovers.

Name: Enmasse Espresso & Tea Merchants
Address: V&A Waterfront, Shop 6, The Watershed, 17 Dock Rd, Cape Town
Author: J MS (Celestine TLS)
Review: Stopped for a couple of flat whites whilst walking through the V&A shed. Tea looked amazing but the coffee was a bit meh. Lovely service but I’d go for tea over coffee in the future.

Name: Enmasse Espresso & Tea Merchants
Address: V&A Waterfront, Shop 6, The Watershed, 17 Dock Rd, Cape Town
Author: Justin Boober
Review: Hours are wrong/ closing early, doesn't accept amex, won't make a sandwich,, sell me me spicy teas with no rooibos. Your  smile is nice you're too good to work at this tea company. Get a job selling expensive clothes or make up

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Billy du Plessis
Review:
Came for lunch and had starters and mains—absolutely delicious! We shared three dishes of dim sum for starters, and everything was fresh, homemade, and delicious. The atmosphere was unmatched as we had a beautiful scenic lunch in the garden. Not a fancy place, but very pretty and homey all the same. The owner was very friendly and chatty, knowledgeable, and the service was very fast.

Onto mains, we ordered ramen and Peking duck. The duck was not on the menu and seemed like a secret gem—all you have to do is ask for it.

The pork ramen was super flavorful, the meat succulent and delicious. It had all the right ingredients, and the noodles were out of this world.

The Peking duck could be from Beijing, that's how enjoyable it was, with good sides and the meat was super nice as well.

They had homemade rooibos ice tea that was flawless.

Overall, a superb meal with great atmosphere

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Tahreef Fransch
Review:
I recently visited this gem in Hout bay with a group of friends. The experience was incredibly memorable in a calm and serene environment even when they were quite busy.

Stand out moments of the day would definitely be the staff hospitality and humour, food quality as well as the environment.

The staff has an incredible knowledge on the food accompanied by their humour and ability to create a connection with the group. By the end of the experience you would likely spend your time either laughing or speechless because of how good the food is.

I ended up trying the orange chicken which left me speechless with how fresh the ingredients were, how tender the chicken was and how refreshing the meal was as a whole.

I also ended up trying some of the chicken chow mein. The portion size and the dishes taste had me jealous of my friend’s insight of the menu, wishing I had ordered another dish to take home.

They also offer ceramic painting with quite a variety of colours and unique objects for your imagination. The environment and atmosphere created a more inviting environment without the hustle and bustle that I’ve found at similar experiences. Leading me to put this on the top of my list.

Lastly to end off we tried a portion of their ‘bow ties’. A delicious and light pastry coated with in a layer of sugar syrup to end off the meal  on a really high note.

Overall this was a stand out experience with an increasingly set of staff and the food quality to match. I would definitely recommend it to anyone visiting the area.

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: alba mir mora
Review: Order the soup dumplings and the braised pork with rice by Uber. To begin with in my life I had tried a drier and tasteless dumpling, it barely had any filling and it didn't have any soup either, so very bad. But the worst part is when I open my rice I realize that it stinks of cinnamon, which I am allergic to, it is not specified anywhere, it is not like the photo and besides, I have never ordered pork in an Asian restaurant and it has come with those species, then I called the restaurant to see if there is something we can do and they tell me no problem, we can make a change, I should order something else and they will come to take the pork and bring me the new dish, I wait for 1 hour to bring me the food, when suddenly the manager calls me with a condescending tone to tell me that my food is on the way but that I will have to pay for it, I explain to her that that is not what we had agreed on, that I am not going to pay two times, on top of that they are not cheap prices... nothing, that is not the case, that the food is already served and that I am going to have to pay... I have told her that there is no way I pay twice, so nothing here I'm left without eating... and with a dish that stinks and could have killed me.

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Emma Foster
Review:
Our waiter created such an amazing experience for us, we are not familiar with the cuisine and they guided us through the menu and gave great recommendations.

The food was great quality, super flavourful and fresh!
They also offer vegan options.

They are dog friendly in the outdoor seating area, which is amazing for dog owners.
I would really recommend this to anyone looking to have a good experience with flavourful Asian cuisine.

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Violet Jade
Review:
Good food but I won’t go back. Honestly I’m getting tired of Asian restaurants like this reaping off of people’s ignorance and not knowing where Asian grocery stores are. I thought the baos and dumplings would be hand made.. but it’s the same ones I buy and steam at home.

R65 for a bit of white rabbits? It’s the same thing for a big bag…

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Stephan Maritz
Review:
We ordered a chocolate and a match bubble tea, and flavours were perfect.

Many of the menu items weren't available, and drinks weren't cooled but that's what loadshedding is doing to businesses.

I'll be back again for sure!

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Chantel Mlilwana
Review:
I love the way you listen to my kids and exceptional 💕.!!!!!
You guys really know your customers

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: That _weirdio
Review: We ordered a mango and peach blast and they were amazing. Cold, refreshing and the bobba added a great texture. The services was kind and answered all my stupid questions. I loved this restaurant and I would go again

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Anpan
Review:
Huge sizes, taste amazing
Best taro milk tea ever tasted🤞🏻

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Elzaan Liebenberg
Review: Been there on 4 different occasions and always closed

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Richard Morris
Review: Great atmosphere and sales assistant made for the experience. Sampling of the teas sold was fun to enable one to compare and so varied that we ended up walking away with x3 boxes. Well done and a great store.

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Jonas Meister
Review: Have ordered tea online from Enmasse several times now. They sell the best Earl Grey tea that I've found anywhere in SA. It's very good and online ordering and delivery has been hassle-free. Can recommend.

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Lisa Henderson
Review: Green roobios and the green roobios blends are amazing !

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Dr Gerald Matiko
Review: Attractive and delightful!!

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Hemal Patel
Review: Best teas, ever.

Name: Topqualitea South Africa (Pty) Ltd
Address: 14 Rowallan St, West Beach, Cape Town
Author: Petula Baart
Review: What a nice experience to visit this establishment,, learned a lot about the different flavors,, the owner was welcoming and friendly,, good atmosphere. Will definitely visit again.

Name: Topqualitea South Africa (Pty) Ltd
Address: 14 Rowallan St, West Beach, Cape Town
Author: Yvette Rowland

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Sultan
Review: Kiosk located towards the entrance of the mall.  A variety of items on the menu and the staff were eager to assist with recommendations. The original thai milk with brown sugar boba was delicious.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Liv G
Review:
This was our first experience with the Ben's Bubble Tea brand and it was incredibly disappointing.

The staff seemed annoyed that we were there (around 5 PM, so nowhere near closing) and didn't greet. While my group of 4 looked at the menu and I'd informed the person that took eventually took our order that we were still deciding (we were the only customers) she kept pressing to take the order leaving us feeling rushed and pressured.

We placed our orders for 4 of the flavours and received warmer than room temperature beverages (the milk had been left on the counter) that were more akin to a warm drinks that had gotten cold as opposed to something pleasant and refreshing.

The beverage topped with caramelised sugar had a melted cup that leaked from the moment it was handed to us. The server could have opted to tell us this but said nothing, and this resulted in a significant portion spilling out of the cup before we'd even reached the parking lot.

From the shoddy service, a melted plastic cup and drinks served at an unappetising temperature, this wasn't the experience we were hoping for at all.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Naeemah
Review:
This is my favourite bubble tea store! The fruity drinks are so refreshing and the brown sugar bliss is perfectly sweet and decadent.

The staff is incredibly friendly and it's always such a pleasure seeing them. They are attentive to taking my order, patient with explaining the different drinks and prepares my order perfectly.

Thank you to the KC staff for always providing great service :) See you very soon!

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Sian
Review: Amazing bubble tea and mochi!!🧋😍 my fave is the Brown Sugar Bliss (25%) and the Taro mochi! I order often from UberEats or go there when I'm in KC and it always tastes great!

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Ayesha Patel
Review: The 1st time we tried bens bubble tea the experience was great . And the other times as well. The reason for my 3 stars is one of your staff. She really needs training. My 2 girls bought them boba(11, 6) mind you they always buy themselves at other boba stores and don't get ignored like they got from your staff. They waited for there drinks until my husband realized 2 other customers got there drinks and my babies still standing there waiting on there drinks  they other one comes with a attitude making there drinks... On another occasion we went and the same cashier blatantly ignored us. We walked away then she looks at us. I'll attach a picture of the same lady with and extreme bad attitude. I won't ever visit this koisk again.

Name: Pola
Address: Main Rd, Scott Estate, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Demmers Teahouse
Address: Shop A3, Old Cape Quarter, 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Amanda Lan
Review: The best bubble tea. Perfectly balanced in flavour. This was the first bubble tea shop. Sorry guys this has the authentic recipe and flavour. I will definitely be returning.

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: CHRISTINA RUWONA
Review:
I recently visited  your Waterfront shop and my experience was nothing short of delightful. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted by a calming and cozy atmosphere and Lovely Team
The variety of teas available at this shop is impressive. They offer a wide range of choices, from classic black and green teas to unique  blends and seasonal specialties. I particularly enjoyed your Taro Time
I would like to Say Thank you to Beaulah and Team
Keep up the work

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Fayhoon Faisal
Review: Boba place in V&A waterfront 😚 has a nice variety of options 👀 The mango drink and milk tea are great 😆❤️ Tapioca pearls are always perfect so soft and squishy 😁

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Jutika Krongmongkolkul
Review:
went there almost everyday during my stay in cape town. good location inside v&a shopping mall.
reasonable price and the staff made as per order correctly.
no seats available there as it was only for buying to-go.

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Taahirah Chafeker
Review: I've been going to Royal Tea most Sunday's where Alfred or Mervielle serves us. They have made the place so welcoming and inviting. I look forward to conversing with them on Sunday's. They are helpful and make your day brighter, even during winter months. Keep up the good work and the warmth!

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Rozana Kamish
Review:
Royal Tea bubble tea shop sells refreshing cold tea with a difference, new to Cape Town as I have had it in Taiwan many years ago. Flavors and ingredient selection caters for all.
This tea shop is situated in Cavendish Square, inside The Egg store, a trendy business that sells various companies products, with clothing and health products and trendy food stores.
I tried a large matcha tea with bubbles and it was good. I would recommend honey in it.
I took the large size as I wanted the reusable cup that came with it, so cool. So much to drink. The strawberry and pine bubble tea was also refreshing. Loved loved loved the service. Those guys working their are friendly and happy to assist if you struggling to decide what to order. Next time I'm trying Taro. Had it before at another store, still a favorite.

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Ondela Mgumane
Review: I had a fantastic first bubble tea experience at this store , the staff was welcoming and they know their products very well and recommended me to try the oreo dairy delight .wow amazing

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Faizel Manuels
Review: Overall this would be the best service and smelling bubble tea we've had so far. Wish I knew the guy who helped us. He added to the experience by being an amazing employee. Sometimes you just need some kindness in the way of service to make you spend more money at a place. Thanks for that hire

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Kelly du Raan
Review: I had the most amazing Juice there called “All Inclusive” and it was delicious. The oreo boba drink is also amazing. Definitely recommend trying some drinks out here.

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: SHANAAZ PRINS
Review: The service is really good and super friendly. The drinks are made right in front of you, which is really interesting to watch. One of my top recommendations for sure.

Name: Rhodes Cottage Museum and Tea Room
Address: 246 Main Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Fazlyn Petersen
Review: If you're looking for a quaint restaurant with a beautiful sea view, you should try this place. I went to enjoy brunch with my friend and it was lovely catching up outside. The staff was friendly and polite. The menu is limited but the scones were delicious. They also have high tea on Sundays.

Name: Rhodes Cottage Museum and Tea Room
Address: 246 Main Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Skylar Abrahams
Review: Free entry, affordable, and absolutely beautiful. The scones specifically are homemade, delicious, and come in messy heart-shapes, which feels really nostalgic.

Name: Rhodes Cottage Museum and Tea Room
Address: 246 Main Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Johan Fourie
Review: Enjoyed nice cup of tea and scones on senior citizens discount day. Friendly service. The tearoom is very popular, booking a table can be recommended.

Name: Rhodes Cottage Museum and Tea Room
Address: 246 Main Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Janine Brown
Review: Was a wonderful way to celebrate my mother's birthday. The setting is gorgeous, the high tea beautiful and service was very friendly. Can't wait to go back again.

Name: Rhodes Cottage Museum and Tea Room
Address: 246 Main Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Jiehad Mallum
Review:
Parking is free on the main road.Outdoor shaded setting with ocean view and sea breeze.Each table seats 4.wifi available during your visit.Food is halal and also reasonably priced.Paid R100 - lunch for two,which consisted of Malay rotis,"slaai",tasty chicken curry,garnished with fresh danya.
Roshana will take care if your needs while you visit.Payment by card is an option.
Nextime will try their scones and hot beverages iA.
There is no alcohol served on this property.

Name: Ibhongo Lethu :-)
Address: Ndlulamthi St, Town 3, Cape Town
Author: Ntombi Pukwana

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Shakira Ontong
Review: Not what I was expecting. The area is a bit dodge and the interior needs a bit of work. Great place for young people to get together to enjoy hooka and milkshakes. Otherwise, the food was delicious, we had the steak pizza(a bit spicy) and chicken Tikka masala. Portions were enough for two people to share if you are not that hungry and prices are reasonable. Will definitely go back again but will sit at the non smoking section(didn't know about it until we left) next time as the smoke was a bit too much for me...

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Taufeeq Laattoe
Review: Prior to Covid I frequented Arabian nights weekly , however now not often at all . Decided to visit for a pipe and milkshake and it was not disappointing at all. For me they still have the best pipes always smoking so smooth . Their milkshakes are still top notch . Was great to be back for a chill session with friends .

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Shaheeda Crombie
Review: From what I could see it looks like a typical shisha spot. You pay for your hookah. they have a menu. my daughter had her 18th birthday there but I didnt stay. just checked the place out and left. by 1st glance it looked pretty decent.

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Mogamat Razien Boyes
Review: Boba Pearls are nice and sweet. Great texture and quality. I had the bar one flavour. Flavour is good. I enjoyed my boba tea. I will definitely recommend! Its better than driving all the way to Canal walk or Kenilworth. The random printed cartoon characters on the lid is a good gesture. Support Local 😀

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Noel Adams
Review: Place was nice and cosy. The place does need some TLC renovations though but was a lovely experience. Food was Devine and hooka pipes was not bad, had a broad range of flavours I never even knew existed.

Name: Tea and Tattered Pages
Address: 10 Chenoweth St, Durbanville
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Tlc wellness shop only online perchase
Address: 7 Jessica Rd, Valhalla Park, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Fynkos
Address: Gate 2 Kirstenbosch Botanical Gardens, Rhodes Drive Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Johan Steyn
Review:
Unfortunately not a nice experience as the cheesecake that we ordered was said to have been a baked cheesecake but it was soft and cold. Hardly tasted like  cheesecake at all, and definitely not baked properly as there was no crust with texture, it was soft all the way through (including the crust) similar to a fridge cheesecake.

The picture makes it look delicious.

Very nice venue after a good walk around the botanical gardens, we were just disappointed with our order.

Name: Fynkos
Address: Gate 2 Kirstenbosch Botanical Gardens, Rhodes Drive Newlands, Cape Town
Author: H Al Suwaidi
Review: Service is really bad, the waitress took a simple order of 3 French Fries and veg soup but took forever to bring the food and at the end she her self decide to change the soup without even informing us. After calling her (the white waitress) and informing her that the soup is not what we have ordered she admitted that yes she decided to bring us black bean soup onstead of veg soup without coming back to us.  Also when the French Fries arrived was cold so obviously it was ready long time but it was not served to us.

Name: Fynkos
Address: Gate 2 Kirstenbosch Botanical Gardens, Rhodes Drive Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Alice Greider
Review: The picnic is such a great idea to take with you - really large portions though. One portion would easily serve two people. Came all packed for us in a tote bag. Crisps, a fizzy drink, a piece of granary bread, small pitas, and then a collection of meats salad falafel hummus guacamole and finally three little cakes.

Name: Fynkos
Address: Gate 2 Kirstenbosch Botanical Gardens, Rhodes Drive Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Lauren van Noort
Review:
I hadn't visited the tea room at Gate 2 in about 15 years but visited earlier this week - what a lovely experience! From the initial greeting and chat whilst being seated, to the quick service and absolutely delicious meal - everything was spot on! Thank you Fynkos for such a positive experience.

I would recommend getting there a bit before traditional lunch time if you are on a schedule - I sat down at about 12pm and my food was served very quickly, but the restaurant filled up fast towards 1pm. The food prices also seemed a little expensive as a local but the meal was worth the treat!

Name: Fynkos
Address: Gate 2 Kirstenbosch Botanical Gardens, Rhodes Drive Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Nerina Naik
Review: Delightful stop for tea and a bite to eat between exploring. The high tea setting was lovely with many options for us veggies, fresh lovely salad with our toasty with generous portions

Name: MERAKI Coffee house
Address: 2 Devon Rd, Yorkshire Estate, Cape Town
Author: Raeez Nackerdien
Review: Gorgeous space, barista was on point. My flat white was perfect. Milk temperature hit just right and well balanced shot pulled. They also have sandwiches and good value combo’s

Name: MERAKI Coffee house
Address: 2 Devon Rd, Yorkshire Estate, Cape Town
Author: Adrian Carelse
Review: Cosy little coffee shop located in Lansdowne. Offering all your favourites from lattes to cappuccinos and espressos. Friendly and warm. the coffee on the go is a much welcomed convenience.

Name: Sāan Artisanal Teas
Address: Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Test1234567
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Grant Swanepoel
Review: I love Tiki Tonga. I ran in this morning early with a big order and they were friendly, welcoming and professional. The atmosphere is cosy but upbeat. The coffee is excellent and quick and of course you can buy their signature coffee beans and pods to make at home. Highly recommended!

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: BH
Review: Small place with a small menu but very well crafted. The cappuccino was creamy and smooth. My eggs cheese and bacon sandwich was ok. It's a good option for a quick stop and go type of bite.

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Jordan West
Review: We were driving to go play hockey at UCT and needed a coffee and didn’t want to stop at the petrol station. We then found Tiki Tonga. Was such a great experience - service was fantastic but the coffee was even better. Will definitely be back and get food next time. Thanks to the Tiki Tonga team

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Jody Wyngaard
Review:
What a great spot for a delicious iced coffee and some cake of the day.

The gentleman working there was able to tell us about the different filter and bean coffees available, and some of the story behind Tiki Tonga. Would certainly recommend.

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: carla Combink
Review:
Found this little coffee  shop.
First just stopped for a coffee. But then  returned later to have  something  to eat and drink. Ordered a toasted sandwich with egg, bacon and cheese. It was really tasty...Coffee so creamy.
Staff was so friendly and helpfull.
Thank-you to them
Also had awesome  background  music. Will visit  again

Name: Langham House Coffees
Address: 59B Long St, Central, Cape Town
Author: Janet van Aswegen
Review: Cute little coffee cafe on Long Street. You can sit inside or on the pavement amidst the bustle of the passing people, cyclists and traffic.

Name: Langham House Coffees
Address: 59B Long St, Central, Cape Town
Author: Ignacio H
Review: Petit Cafe with great breakfast menu. Also great options to satisfy a sweet tooth.

Name: Langham House Coffees
Address: 59B Long St, Central, Cape Town
Author: siva pillay
Review: Friendly staff and owner. Very hands on. Very good cheese and ham omelette. Excellent Coffee. Definitely going back!

Name: Langham House Coffees
Address: 59B Long St, Central, Cape Town
Author: Ari'anna Kuit
Review:
It is what other reviews say, absolute gem of a little spot. The atmosphere makes me want to stay longer than I always plan too lol. I have yet to venture into their breakfasts but I know ALL their baked goods are just fire, they're AMAAAAAAA-ZIIIIIIING. I feel it's a crime leaving without at least a biscuit or Romany cream.... good lawd have mercy on my waist. lol
And service is always friendly. 😇❤️

Name: Langham House Coffees
Address: 59B Long St, Central, Cape Town
Author: Nick Dempers
Review: Great place to have coffee and/or breakfast or a quick bite in Cape Town.

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Siobhan S
Review:
had the most amazing bubble tea experience at Bubble Up Boutique Bubble! The owner made some fantastic recommendations, and every sip was delightful. If you’re a bubble tea lover, this place is a must-visit. 🍹✨

#BubbleTeaLover #Durbanville #HiddenGems #BubbleUpBoutiqueBubble

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Rakkooni USA
Review: Calling all geeks, nerds, weebs, muggles and even everyone in between! This is the pit stop for you! The selection of bubble tea is HUGE! I love their caffeine-free rooibos options. They have all the classics as well as the ability to mix all kinds of exotic varieties. Talk about a hole in the wall! I have to go here every time I am remotely near the area, lol. Amazing quality!

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Lisa Roberts
Review: A favourite after-school treat! Lovely staff and super owner. Taro and tapioca bubble tea is amazing 🧋💜

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Donald Stockhall
Review:
Great little place with friendly staff.
Just a note to the owners to consider, if you open at 09:00 for business on a Monday, then ensure you have all your ingredients ready for people arriving at 09:00 to order.
Last week we couldn’t get what we came for because the tapioca wasn’t cooked yet.
Today we arrived at 14:00 and again couldn’t get what we came for because there was no tapioca available. It’s really disappointing.
They still get five stars because their product is really good and they really are super friendly. Go give them a try.

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Lizelle De Bruin
Review: The bubble tea was delicious as a treat. I had Rooibos with Blood orange and Turkish delight

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Sabrina Roberts
Review:
The “Tareo” IS AMAZING! I fully recommend it! It’s a 10/10 drink for R58 (reasonably priced compared to other boba tea shops) with chewy, soft and fresh tapioca that was a delight. The drink as a whole was creamy and sweet, yet not overly so, and there were enough sweet, biscuity Oreo crumbs to make every every gulp a pleasure.

For R54, I also tried the Iced Tea and Lemonade drink with cherry popping boba. It was bitter-sweet and very refreshing… but I didn’t like the somewhat artificial-tasting cherry boba. I’d recommend another boba favour. Overall it had a 7/10 from me.

The lady working there is genuine and patient, very smiley. There is seating outside and it is lovely and cool, and wasn’t busy at all at 3pm when we went there. I WILL be back for more of their Signature milk tea drinks!

It’s located on the upper floor of the mall, on the outside veranda on your right as you ascend the elevator. Was a bit tricky to find so I hope this helps someone.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Kayla J
Review: The Mango and Passion Fruit Bubble Tea was very refreshing and tasty. I doesn't taste like the fake syrup most places use but the fruity tea taste that makes it so great!

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: William H
Review: Came a few minutes after they had closed but they opened the door for us and let us order🥹 Owners were super kind and even complimented my shoes! The drinks itself are pretty good too for their price. Would definitely recommend.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Mysterious Gem
Review:
Staff were friendly, place smelt like Boba and was clean.
There is a store closer to me but the service there is terrible. Was worth the drive

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: David Hicks

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Chelsea Zive
Review: I’ve loved every moment here, definitely the best susu although I recommend they change their confusing slogan. Live loved by others, live loving others. This eliminates confusion and is more sensible which enhances the experience in this institute.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Yasmin Rehder
Review: Love stopping by after work to grab a bubble tea. My fav brand/franchise regarding taste and flavor of bubble tea. They also have another branch in Canal Walk that I visit. Love the brown sugar milk tea 🧋 Definitely worth paying an extra R5 for a large size. Absolutely delicious and highly recommended.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Samuel Christov
Review: Ordered off uber eats and my lemon honey tea with strawberry pops. I found a floating black foreign object in the still sealed drink container. I have sent a message of complaint where someone promptly responded. I received a message where they stated that they will be in touch to get involved in the issue. It's been a while now and no one has sent any kind of contact. Hence the review.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Jessica
Review: The drink was refreshing and not too sweet just the way I wanted. They have a lot of flavors to choose from. Glad I tried it.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Jutika Krongmongkolkul
Review: nice and colorful decorations. staffs were friendly. had a few menus of their bubble tea such as classic bubble milk tea, taro milk tea, strawberry slush and all tasted great!

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Shanoa Madeira
Review: She has very attentive with my mother. The massages were very good. My mom could walk very well because of her hip pain but after her massages she was feeling very fresh. Totally recommend coming!!!

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Ryan Muller
Review: We had no idea prior visiting Rosetta's today; asthma can be handled and brought under control in such a short space of time. Apart from her knowledgeable information pertaining to Chinese Medicine, the Gua Sha performed, did absolute wonders. Upon entering, Theresa struggled with breathing, but shortly after, she was 90% back to her real self. I'd recommend Rosetta Health anytime.

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Norman John
Review:
One of our favourite small local businesses that my wife and I enjoy to support because they stock products that you will not find in a large chain supermarket. Many of the items have been imported from Asia. They also stock perishable and refrigerated items that are sourced locally, are of a high quality and reasonably priced.

The proprietors, Rose and May are super friendly and helpful. A welcoming smile from either of them when I walk in is the highlight of my day! My wife on occasion will go for a massage in their parlour which is adjoined to the shop. We cannot wait until we go back to stock up on more of the specialties that they have in store for us.

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Carla Whitehead
Review: Excellent Chinese pressure point massage. Friendly staff and nangamso gave me the extra hard massage I asked for finding every single knot. Will definitely go back and highly recommend if you are looking for a pressure point massage

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Aaron Van heerden
Review:
By far the best massaging and cupping sessions.

Been going to them for awhile now with lower back problems. Once done with the sessions I'm able to move And go about my day with no issues .

Staff is very kind and generous too

10 out 10 and definitely will recommend them

Name: TreeHouse Seventeen Coffee Shop
Address: 261 Breakwater Blvd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Ronier Zalnieriunas
Review:
Super cute little coffee truck with very friendly service. I recommend this place for the vibe and location by the sea front.

The espresso was a bit on the bitter and slightly watery side for my taste. Though very smooth. It’s also the cheapest espresso I’ve had in Cape Town at R20.

Name: TreeHouse Seventeen Coffee Shop
Address: 261 Breakwater Blvd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Vukile Mkhala
Review:
Place in a perfect sport for guests.
Prompt service and
Delicious snack and good  quality coffee.
Wifi connection delivery service

Name: TreeHouse Seventeen Coffee Shop
Address: 261 Breakwater Blvd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Tanith Valentine
Review: Amazing little coffee shop, with amazing coffee and amazing service !!!!

Name: TreeHouse Seventeen Coffee Shop
Address: 261 Breakwater Blvd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: sudley armien
Review: Staff member so rude telling me i MUST ask someone for change an i can go buy somewhere else .rude and bad attitude

Name: TreeHouse Seventeen Coffee Shop
Address: 261 Breakwater Blvd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Sumaya Seedat
Review: Awesome coffee and awesome service

Name: Delhi House Cafe
Address: 87-93 Victoria Rd, Grassy Park, Cape Town
Author: Shaheed Martin
Review: Friendly family run Cafe on the corner of 6th Avenue and Victoria Road Grassy Park. They also offer very cheap cellphone repairs.

Name: Delhi House Cafe
Address: 87-93 Victoria Rd, Grassy Park, Cape Town
Author: Shaun Clarke
Review: Great place to shop

Name: Delhi House Cafe
Address: 87-93 Victoria Rd, Grassy Park, Cape Town
Author: Lucian Davis

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Mustaqeem Abrahams
Review: Honestly my first worse experience ever. I’ve bought boba tea a lot by this place for a long time now and today my wife and I decided to buy again. My wife ordered a chocolate milk tea with strawberry boba and I ordered a taro tea with pudding boba and honestly the worst I’ve ever had. My wife’s strawberry boba wasn’t even boba balls and my taro boba was like slime it was like snot in my mouth. Both her and I were very disappointed and did not finish it at all, we both took literally one sip and I spat mine immediately out, it was honestly disgusting  and was such a waste of money spent so much for such a waste.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Fadeelah Kenny
Review: Love the selection of fresh ingredients but their execution leaves much to be desired. When I ordered there was no one ahead of me but my poke bowl still took quite a while to make. I asked them twice to add less mayo than usual but it still came drenched in mayo and the soy sauce dressing. Spoilt a perfectly good poke bowl with so much dressing IMHO.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Atomic-Chicken
Review:
So I tried Taro Milk Boba Tea with Mango Bubbles for the first time today & I was pleasantly surprised! 😁 I added some sugar and ice to this tastebud sensation, but you have the option to leave it out. You can customise your drink as you wish, which I think is super awesome. ☑️☑️☑️
My 3 star rating is aimed at the staff who honestly didn't seem very interested in their job. I asked for a suggestion and I might as well have asked for the world! They are NOT professional and definitely NOT friendly. 👎
I will go in search of another TeAwesome and try some of their other drinks on their menu. This one will never see me again.
you will find them in the food court area.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Samuel Christov
Review: The service was quick and the Drinks that we ordered where fantastic. I love all the different flavors that where available as well as the fact that they also let us choose our own ingredients to make the drink feel like our own custom beverage.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Jasmine Wong
Review: Amazing that they have bubble tea in Cape Town. They have all the classic flavours including taro, chocolate and coffee. The milk teas are the best options. Love that you are able to customized options like: sugar, ice and add ins. If you know, you know you have to add tapioca balls. Wish they were fresher though.

Name: Sankofa House
Address: City Center, 4 Vredenburg Ln, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: sandra
Review: I love the food and their shakes, they are really gorgeous and helpful.🫶🏼

Name: Sankofa House
Address: City Center, 4 Vredenburg Ln, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Elsie
Review: The sandwiches are amazing, really friendly atmosphere. Thanks Sankofa House ; )

Name: Sankofa House
Address: City Center, 4 Vredenburg Ln, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Ally Kumbuka
Review: Great food and amazing fresh juices! I loved it

Name: Sankofa House
Address: City Center, 4 Vredenburg Ln, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Joseph Bibwireyesu
Review: Best food best drinks

Name: vida e caffè FPG House
Address: FPG House, 3 Kiepersol Cl, Plattekloof, Cape Town
Author: Laschwin David's
Review: I highly recommend! Besides the food and drinks being amazing, thank you Thabang, Nasha & Shagon for always putting on a smile and delivering exceptional service!

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Adele Botes
Review: Most serene experience ever! I could sit there forever whilst comfortably sipping a warm cup of coffee. The view is simply astonishing. My new favourite place to go to.

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Chanel King
Review: Our absolute favourite spot with the best view and the best Baristas! Very close to our hearts ♥️☕️

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Lee-Anne Curtis-Cox
Review:
This well run cafe makes great coffee (and quickly) along with some amazing filled croissants! Customer service is warm and efficient.

Ask to sit in the "secret" garden space if you wish to enjoy your coffee with a view!

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: heather williams
Review: Hidden gem of a place. The affogato was perfectly made and the carrot cake delightful. You order at the little upstairs cafe where there are one or two seats and then go downstairs into the garden area to enjoy it or just get coffee to go. Not wheelchair friendly. Could use a few umbrellas for shade.

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Astrid Schon
Review: These cakes are just delicious... they are next level decadent. Don't be fooled by the size... we cut them into 4 and it is the perfect bit of yumminess with a coffee or a tea.  Best cheesecake this side of the bay and the croissants are so good, you are missing out if you don't try them.

Name: The House of Machines
Address: 84 Shortmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Hannelie Hartman Sanders
Review: What a lovely evening - Tuesdays are open mic evenings - good quality live music and even some poetry.

Name: The House of Machines
Address: 84 Shortmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Andrés
Review: Best pub in town, craft beer, live music, good vibes!

Name: The House of Machines
Address: 84 Shortmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Dewald Reinke
Review: I had heard a lot of good things about the House of Machines so when I got the opportunity to visit, I immediately grabbed it. This quaint bar is located on a small road with some outside seating on the street as well as inside. The atmosphere in the bar is carried all the way from its exciting design and décor to the staff - for example, they keep their house brand liquors in what looked like barrels. The staff were very friendly and made us feel special by finding out what we like and then suggesting drinks based on that. We started off with a coffee, which was very tasty, and then proceeded to grab some beers. They had some local crafts available on tap including a beer that was brewed exclusively for them. Next time I visit, which I definitely will be doing, I will sample some of their cocktails which seem to be very popular. If you love quaint bars, this is a must visit for you.

Name: The House of Machines
Address: 84 Shortmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: MIKE BORGIA
Review: Amazing staff, solid food. I had an incredible live show experience there. I can't wait to come and play there again. The people in general are what made it great.

Name: The House of Machines
Address: 84 Shortmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Lee-Anne Curtis-Cox
Review:
I've absolutely loved this spot since 2014! Whether it's listening to Tuesday night's Songwriters Fest, or Friday and Saturday's live music performances, or just to pop in to enjoy the best Old Fashioned in town, House of Machines is the place to be.

We always receive excellent service from the bar staff, most who have been working there for years!

Their boerie rolls are to die for!

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Dewald Reinke
Review: Susu offers a wide variety of bubble teas that can definitely be overwhelming for first-timers. Luckily, they have recommendations for their top sellers and even a fun spin-the-wheel option to try something new. I tried their Bubble Tea Latte Macha which was freshly made and tasty. Although I loved the shop's style and the wide variety of options on the menu, the staff could be a bit friendlier. Overall, if you're craving quality bubble tea, give Susu a visit. They're easy to find in the Tygervalley Shopping Centre food court.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: lamise inglis
Review: Such a cute little stop for bubble tea! A huge range of options and they are yummy. They have vacuum sealed tops which prevent spills. There is a very cute photo booth with a light to snap a memorable pic or two. Prices are not too bad either.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Kylie Ndagijimana
Review: Staff was very friendly and the boba tea was amazing and reasonably priced. Would definitely visit again!

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Tasneem Patrick
Review: Visited the Tygervalley branch. Friendly-ish staff but I had to walk up to the counter to be served. They didn't call out for service like they did with the few people that went before me. Lady that called out order out upon completion wasn't very friendly. The bubble tea was GOOD though. You can watch how they prepare everything infront of you. No seats available as they're right across the food court that have ample space. They have 2 or 3 different settings available for you to take pics for your social media though. Medium R45, large priced at R50. A large selection of flavours available.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review: Went there on 4 November 2023. Good service, a variety of flavours. They're affordable and have cute social mefia stands. I'd recommend them.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Sultan
Review: A kiosk located inside of Sports Scene store in Canal Walk. A variety of items on the menu and the staff was eager to assist with recommendations. The original thai milk with brown sugar boba was delicious.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Fazlyn Petersen
Review: My kids have been nagging for bubble tea so I finally went to get some. The shop is inside Sportscene. The staff were friendly and polite. The caramel milk tea was very sweet though. If you order on Uber eats you can select how much sugar. Next time I'll ask them to make it 50%. My daughter enjoyed her chocolate slushie.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Jenny D
Review: Last time I was here, was told they don't do fresh milk latte. But it was quickly corrected.  This time they tell me they don't have fresh milk in stock, while we are in a mall with 4 grocery stores who sells milk, there's milk j  the fridge in store! I don't want to drink that palm oil podwer, so I guess I'm not ever coming back here

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Naidoo
Review:
Strangest location. It’s inside Sportscene but that have really good bubble tea and such a nice variety

I had the green apple tea with mango balls which was supper yummy. The passion fruit ice tea with mango balls was my favorite. I am yet to try their milk tea selection.

The staff is friendly and the service is fast.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Siobhan
Review:
MoMo is located inside Sportscene it is not a standalone store.

They seem to be quite popular as there was a large group of people waiting for their orders when I got there, and I waited around 15-20 minutes for my order. But it was definitely worth the wait.
You need to have a rough idea of what it is that you would like to order, as the guy who takes your order is not very friendly or helpful. Even after expressing that it was my first time visiting the store.

It would also be great if they had pictures on their menu, it would make the ordering process so much easier.

All in all, still worth it. The bubble tea was great, I ordered two different flavours (a milky tea and a fruity one) and I thoroughly enjoyed both.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review:
Went there on the morning of 11 February 2024. It's a small shop so once you order you have to stand outside if it gets busy. The shop looks cute. Note that the Summer Series sign outside is just a sign and they don't offer those flavours (I think it was a Christmas special).

We ordered the Chocolate Slush with grass jelly and the Japanese green tea slush with peach pops. It was deliciously refreshing in the heat. They charge extra for toppings like tapioka pearls. The prices are reasonable.

The wait is questionable though. They weren't busy when we ordered.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: chane bezuidenhout
Review:
I love Susu bubble tea (usually) and have visited Susu Waterkant many times. My absolute favorite is the jasmine milk tea with brown sugar boaba. I was so excited when a Susu opened up closer to us. We've ordered bubble tea twice from here ( Bothasig). Both times the tea was so weak. It was basically like drinking watery milk. First time I got 50% sugar, and it was barely sweet. Second time I got 75% sugar, it tasted exactly like the 1st one. I tasted my boyfriends bubble tea with also 75% sugar, this was very very sweet. Clearly not the same 75%.. It's been very disappointing so far.

Cant even compare this tea to the other Susu.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Trinity Bisschoff
Review: We come here ALOT and my usual experiences here are amazing with lovely ladies and they make the atmosphere so welcoming and pleasant, but today, the 12th of august, at 17:30 there was one guy that was working there and he served with absolute 0% passion or energy he wasn’t very welcoming and he waited so long to say hello to us we had to eventually say hello. And when I got my boba the top of the lid was creased and it was almost like he didn’t care…

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Andisiwe Wu
Review:
Tonight I was craving for Boba Tea and we didn't want to drive out far to Canal Walk or any other places. So luckily for Google Maps we were directed to Susu Bothasig.

We ordered our usuals Brown sugar Matcha with Tapioca and Custard, second drink Slushy Apple with Lychee Pop and Pineapple Coconut Jelly like we do at CW, but I've noticed a slight change in texture, it's not as thick as in milky as it should be, was a bit more watery. However the taste and recipe is still exactly the same, I must say Bothasig prepares their Tapioca better than CW! Dense, soft, sweet and Chewy!

Staff are super bubbly and helpful deciding a third drink for my difficult mother for not having a sweet tongue, but they definitely nailed the expectations! My mother finished the drink! Mango Jasmine Tea with tapioca and grass jelly, most importantly, *No sugar, Less sweetness*

Not too bad for a new "pop up" shop, will definitely be back!

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Kim-Louise Rousseau - Personal Gmail
Review: Friendly service and great bubble tea. And it's nice to have a boba shop in the northern suburbs. Atmosphere is hard to comment on since it is a tiny stall more than it is a shop.

Name: Taro Tea Co
Address: 31 Harbour Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Clifford Moore
Review: Very nice bubble tea and a nice selection of flavours off tea and bubbles

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Ieremia Oliver
Review: Absolutely horrible service, I’ve had the same bad experiences with one of the lady’s at the front counter she’s incredibly unprofessional and rude. I asked for my boba sugar free and she stares at me like I just spoke some foreign language, I repeat myself and she doesn’t say anything and when I got my boba it wasn’t even sugar free! I’ve gone a couple times and every time it’s a bad experience. BEN you need to train or staff better or just get better staff because I certainly won’t be coming back anytime soon.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Rachel Gomes
Review:
I love love love the way you guys make boba tea! You can actually taste the matcha and not watery as the other places that make boba tea. I like how the tapioca balls are always fresh and you guys never disappoint.
