Address: Shop 80B, Otto du Plessis Dr, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Andrew Robert Lusk
Review: This is the best coffee I have had in over 5 years. As a chef I am very impressed

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Ieremia Oliver
Review: Absolutely horrible service, I’ve had the same bad experiences with one of the lady’s at the front counter she’s incredibly unprofessional and rude. I asked for my boba sugar free and she stares at me like I just spoke some foreign language, I repeat myself and she doesn’t say anything and when I got my boba it wasn’t even sugar free! I’ve gone a couple times and every time it’s a bad experience. BEN you need to train or staff better or just get better staff because I certainly won’t be coming back anytime soon.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Rachel Gomes
Review:
I love love love the way you guys make boba tea! You can actually taste the matcha and not watery as the other places that make boba tea. I like how the tapioca balls are always fresh and you guys never disappoint.
Big ups to the lady's that take pride in their work and thank you so much Aneeqah! You are a star ✨

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Liz “Toxic_Mercy” Marinus
Review: Great customer service, vibes was great all of them were happy Cheerful and laughing all round. I got myself a matcha milk tea. It was very delicious but don't recommend if you dont like grass tasting drinks. Would 100% recommend the restaurant exp was great.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Najuwah
Review: Second time going here. Amazing bubble tea. The staff is friendly and the service is quick. Small seating area would've been nice if there were more space to sit. The kids love the experience of bubble tea, so bring the whole family. The kiosk is clean and a bit hidden if you are not aware. I say they should move up to the food court where there's more space and seating. Definitely recommend trying

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: C De Jager
Review: I’m a bubble tea connoisseur and their brown sugar bliss was really good! The boba was very fresh. It would be nice to see the staff wearing gloves for sanitary reasons because it appears to be a very hands-on process and with winter coming a lot of people are sick. I asked for my milk tea to be cold as I saw Ben’s Bubble Tea now offers both hot and cold and the staff member I was talking to was very confused as to why I was specifying cold and insisted that they only serve cold. Would appreciate a friendlier approach from staff but otherwise really nice milk tea :)

Name: Topqualitea South Africa (Pty) Ltd
Address: 14 Rowallan St, West Beach, Cape Town
Author: Petula Baart
Review: What a nice experience to visit this establishment,, learned a lot about the different flavors,, the owner was welcoming and friendly,, good atmosphere. Will definitely visit again.

Name: Topqualitea South Africa (Pty) Ltd
Address: 14 Rowallan St, West Beach, Cape Town
Author: Yvette Rowland

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Sabrina Roberts
Review:
The “Tareo” IS AMAZING! I fully recommend it! It’s a 10/10 drink for R58 (reasonably priced compared to other boba tea shops) with chewy, soft and fresh tapioca that was a delight. The drink as a whole was creamy and sweet, yet not overly so, and there were enough sweet, biscuity Oreo crumbs to make every every gulp a pleasure.

For R54, I also tried the Iced Tea and Lemonade drink with cherry popping boba. It was bitter-sweet and very refreshing… but I didn’t like the somewhat artificial-tasting cherry boba. I’d recommend another boba favour. Overall it had a 7/10 from me.

The lady working there is genuine and patient, very smiley. There is seating outside and it is lovely and cool, and wasn’t busy at all at 3pm when we went there. I WILL be back for more of their Signature milk tea drinks!

It’s located on the upper floor of the mall, on the outside veranda on your right as you ascend the elevator. Was a bit tricky to find so I hope this helps someone.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Kayla J
Review: The Mango and Passion Fruit Bubble Tea was very refreshing and tasty. I doesn't taste like the fake syrup most places use but the fruity tea taste that makes it so great!

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: William H
Review: Came a few minutes after they had closed but they opened the door for us and let us order🥹 Owners were super kind and even complimented my shoes! The drinks itself are pretty good too for their price. Would definitely recommend.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Mysterious Gem
Review:
Staff were friendly, place smelt like Boba and was clean.
There is a store closer to me but the service there is terrible. Was worth the drive

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: David Hicks

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Richard Morris
Review: Great atmosphere and sales assistant made for the experience. Sampling of the teas sold was fun to enable one to compare and so varied that we ended up walking away with x3 boxes. Well done and a great store.

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Jonas Meister
Review: Have ordered tea online from Enmasse several times now. They sell the best Earl Grey tea that I've found anywhere in SA. It's very good and online ordering and delivery has been hassle-free. Can recommend.

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Lisa Henderson
Review: Green roobios and the green roobios blends are amazing !

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Dr Gerald Matiko
Review: Attractive and delightful!!

Name: Enmasse Tea Merchants
Address: 125 Hope Street, Gate 2 (parking & entrance at back of building), Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Hemal Patel
Review: Best teas, ever.

Name: Peacock Tea and Coffee
Address: Shop G11, Howard Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Alliston Ruiters
Review: Sorry Abt the review earlier I am editing the time of arrival at peacock coffee shop was 17.30 not 18.30 it was an error to the time. Good evening last night I went for a cup of coffee at the above mentioned store at the time stated wen I arrived there the stores door was ajar with a chair placed Infront of it I then placed my order went on an errand then returned at 17.40 to collect my order I took a sip of my coffee It seem to have a funny taste like the milk was sour I reported it to the assistant Shante who took it up with the manager on duty (the short old grey lady) telling her the customer says the milk is sour she blatantly ignored her she asked to replace it but arrogantly replied there is no coffee I then left the coffee which was paid for went to Woolworths to buy me another I was very upset with the service

Name: Peacock Tea and Coffee
Address: Shop G11, Howard Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Mean Aubergine
Review: Returned a mocha pot that didn’t seal properly. Initially I was abruptly told  ‘no slip - no refund. But bring it and they will take a look. ‘ I took it weeks ago. Never heard from them again. Not the best coffee. Not the best service and I’d warn anyone against purchasing any merchandise.

Name: Peacock Tea and Coffee
Address: Shop G11, Howard Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Michelle Macleod
Review: A great place to select your favorite coffee beans or get them ground.  There is such a big range to suit all tastes. There is also a wonderful selection of teas. If you are looking for a lovely unique mug for a gift, then this is the place. There is also a good selection of filter machines, coffee makers other gifts for the tea or coffee lover. Sit down and enjoy a cup with a enjoy with a small selection of pastries.  This store is on the ground level and is situated near Woolworths entrance. It is wheelchair friendly

Name: Peacock Tea and Coffee
Address: Shop G11, Howard Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Jade Van Niekerk - Greentree
Review: The most uncomfortable and inhospitable experience i have had in any store in a LONG time. The old woman that works there will ignore you completely. Highly DO NOT recommend!

Name: Peacock Tea and Coffee
Address: Shop G11, Howard Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Lauren Gormley
Review: Beware of their returns policy which will not grant you a refund. I have just returned a R500 coffee plunger to the Pinelands store and can only use store credit in return. Disappointing.

Name: Peacock Tea & Coffee
Address: Shop 71b, Sunnydale Rd, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: C Coelle
Review: Very unhelpful and rude staff. Denise slapped my hand away and called me a lair after I tried to exchange a espresso Maschine that had broken days after purchase. Shocking experience!

Name: Peacock Tea & Coffee
Address: Shop 71b, Sunnydale Rd, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Eve Oosthuizen
Review: Great coffee. Staff very friendly.

Name: Peacock Tea & Coffee
Address: Shop 71b, Sunnydale Rd, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: MM
Review: Great freshly roasted coffee, even some good South African beans when I last went

Name: Peacock Tea & Coffee
Address: Shop 71b, Sunnydale Rd, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Jay
Review: Good service, lovely coffee and treats, very good prices.

Name: Peacock Tea & Coffee
Address: Shop 71b, Sunnydale Rd, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Zaida Lourense
Review: 0ur stop after shopping best coffee ever...

Name: Serenitea Cafe
Address: V942+XG, Kommetjie, Cape Town
Author: Ragna Red
Review: What a beautiful peaceful place! The food is absolutely delightful, prepared with so much love and care. Fresh lettuce hand-picked from the garden and and they added such interesting spices! Lovely staff too, going out if their way to make this tranquil cafe/nursery vibe a wonderful experience for you. It has quickly become one if our favourite lunch spots! 5 stars for sure! 🌿🌸🌱

Name: Serenitea Cafe
Address: V942+XG, Kommetjie, Cape Town
Author: Alyssa Frank
Review: Just had breakfast here after a lovely beach horseback ride next door and it was so perfect! Set in an adorable garden/nursery on a quaint but lively farm & equestrian center amidst other restaurants and coffee shops, we were greeted by the super friendly owner who chatted with us and served us her super delicious shakshuka, smoothies, and coffee. We lounged in the garden in the shaded sun and just loved it. Highly recommend!

Name: Serenitea Cafe
Address: V942+XG, Kommetjie, Cape Town
Author: Louis
Review: Very friendly staff, very nice location looking over a small plant nursery. The selection of teas was great.

Name: Serenitea Cafe
Address: V942+XG, Kommetjie, Cape Town
Author: Marc Binder
Review: Lovely little oasis to relax and enjoy life. Great organic food created by a passionated host. Highly recommended.

Name: Serenitea Cafe
Address: V942+XG, Kommetjie, Cape Town
Author: Melany Brown
Review: We popped in to have a light lunch. Food and service was great. Worth a visit, and then wander around the nursery.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Reyaan Boltman
Review: This is a must try experience. Friendly staff. Beautiful Atmosphere. They have games on the tables like cards, jenga and naughts and crosses. The Bubble Tea is amazing and they have many flavors to choose from. I had passion fruit with mango.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: mehraaj hassan
Review: Been there twice but not the best Boba tea . Received order very fast like under 2min each. I suggest they take their time to make the Boba tea cos clearly making it that fast leaves it almost tasteless. decor is nice but atmosphere a bit boring.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: veronica allen wildeman
Review: Loved all the refresher drinks with boba bubbles! Beautiful setting. Atmosphere needs a bit of uplifting. Service is fast and efficient. Must still try the milk tea drinks.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: imohoosain
Review: I drove all the way there with my kids because the trading times online says they are open, only to arrive and discover they were closed. Me and my kids left feeling very disappointed. Please make sure your trading hours are updated online to avoid inconveniencing clients.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Maliqah Williams
Review: Disappointed that it’s the third time coming to the place and it being close . Please change your times / correct them it’s always wrong and people are left disappointed.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Dewald Reinke
Review: Susu offers a wide variety of bubble teas that can definitely be overwhelming for first-timers. Luckily, they have recommendations for their top sellers and even a fun spin-the-wheel option to try something new. I tried their Bubble Tea Latte Macha which was freshly made and tasty. Although I loved the shop's style and the wide variety of options on the menu, the staff could be a bit friendlier. Overall, if you're craving quality bubble tea, give Susu a visit. They're easy to find in the Tygervalley Shopping Centre food court.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: lamise inglis
Review: Such a cute little stop for bubble tea! A huge range of options and they are yummy. They have vacuum sealed tops which prevent spills. There is a very cute photo booth with a light to snap a memorable pic or two. Prices are not too bad either.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Kylie Ndagijimana
Review: Staff was very friendly and the boba tea was amazing and reasonably priced. Would definitely visit again!

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Tasneem Patrick
Review: Visited the Tygervalley branch. Friendly-ish staff but I had to walk up to the counter to be served. They didn't call out for service like they did with the few people that went before me. Lady that called out order out upon completion wasn't very friendly. The bubble tea was GOOD though. You can watch how they prepare everything infront of you. No seats available as they're right across the food court that have ample space. They have 2 or 3 different settings available for you to take pics for your social media though. Medium R45, large priced at R50. A large selection of flavours available.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review: Went there on 4 November 2023. Good service, a variety of flavours. They're affordable and have cute social mefia stands. I'd recommend them.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Larry Wayne Cupido
Review: This was one our favorite places to get sushi. It was always really good and prepared well. The last two times I ordered it was not as expected at all. The first time it was bad I thought okay might just be a isolated incident. Possibly the sushi guy was not there and someone had to stand in but now again... this is not the standard that attracted me to buy.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Thato Daumas
Review:
The place is okay but the furniture could be updated. The decor is very simple with some Chinese objects like fans and such.

I ordered the passion fruit and lemon 🍋 bubble tea. It was tasty and flavourful. I didn't eat anything because of my dietary restrictions.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Joleen Witbooi
Review: Ordered platter E for supper last night. Tasty and fresh sushi, would have liked the rice to be slightly softer but overall happy with the sushi. Platter includes chicken and veg spring rolls which were very nice. Will be back to try the rest of their menu.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Jacques louw
Review: Ordered sushi here a few times before. Was always good. Yesterday’s was not the same. Ordered platter E. Portion sizes were a lot smaller and not all the pieces were included as per the menu. Lady also dismissed this when asking about the missing pieces. Would not recommend.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Maojing Zhang
Review: Amazing food!love it 😍 I love sushi and so does my five-year-old daughter! This is a good choice🥰

Name: Northern Plateau Rooibos
Address: 378 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Slade Augustine
Review: Great, original rooibos Tea! . www.nprooibos.store

Name: Northern Plateau Rooibos
Address: 378 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Shuaib Bhawoodien
Review: The premier ROOIBOS experience in cape town

Name: Northern Plateau Rooibos
Address: 378 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Nadeem Davids
Review: Best rooibos in south africa! They even have free tasters available

Name: Northern Plateau Rooibos
Address: 378 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Mogamad Davids (Mogamad The Programmer)
Review: Good quality tea! My gran loves it

Name: Northern Plateau Rooibos
Address: 378 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Natalie Gabrielse
Review: Absolutely  delicious tea!!! Wonderful service from the store

Name: INGWE TEAS
Address: 3 Irene St, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Great P Photography

Name: Khoisan Gourmet
Address: Brooklyn, Cape Town
Author: Thabiet Gabier
Review: Always a pleasure visiting Khoisan Tea, wide range of excellent and quality teas, friendly faces, warm welcome and an EXTREMELY helpful receptionist!!! Exactly the experience I look for when choosing a supplier for my catering business. Khoisan Tea, YOU ROCK!!

Name: Khoisan Gourmet
Address: Brooklyn, Cape Town
Author: Janine Adams
Review: One taste and your'e hooked, what an excellent product range and the staff is always very kind and helpful. Will definitely recommend to everyone i know.

Name: Khoisan Gourmet
Address: Brooklyn, Cape Town
Author: Desmond Adams
Review:
Because its a family enviroment
and people of all race is welcome to purchase their wonderful variety of teas.

Name: Khoisan Gourmet
Address: Brooklyn, Cape Town
Author: Marcel Boraine
Review: Best tea around. Organic and fair trade with many flavours to treat tour tast buds with.

Name: Khoisan Gourmet
Address: Brooklyn, Cape Town
Author: Warren Jacobus
Review: Best tea I have ever had, and I had them all... well done khoi

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review:
Went there on the morning of 11 February 2024. It's a small shop so once you order you have to stand outside if it gets busy. The shop looks cute. Note that the Summer Series sign outside is just a sign and they don't offer those flavours (I think it was a Christmas special).

We ordered the Chocolate Slush with grass jelly and the Japanese green tea slush with peach pops. It was deliciously refreshing in the heat. They charge extra for toppings like tapioka pearls. The prices are reasonable.

The wait is questionable though. They weren't busy when we ordered.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: chane bezuidenhout
Review:
I love Susu bubble tea (usually) and have visited Susu Waterkant many times. My absolute favorite is the jasmine milk tea with brown sugar boaba. I was so excited when a Susu opened up closer to us. We've ordered bubble tea twice from here ( Bothasig). Both times the tea was so weak. It was basically like drinking watery milk. First time I got 50% sugar, and it was barely sweet. Second time I got 75% sugar, it tasted exactly like the 1st one. I tasted my boyfriends bubble tea with also 75% sugar, this was very very sweet. Clearly not the same 75%.. It's been very disappointing so far.

Cant even compare this tea to the other Susu.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Trinity Bisschoff
Review: We come here ALOT and my usual experiences here are amazing with lovely ladies and they make the atmosphere so welcoming and pleasant, but today, the 12th of august, at 17:30 there was one guy that was working there and he served with absolute 0% passion or energy he wasn’t very welcoming and he waited so long to say hello to us we had to eventually say hello. And when I got my boba the top of the lid was creased and it was almost like he didn’t care…

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Andisiwe Wu
Review:
Tonight I was craving for Boba Tea and we didn't want to drive out far to Canal Walk or any other places. So luckily for Google Maps we were directed to Susu Bothasig.

We ordered our usuals Brown sugar Matcha with Tapioca and Custard, second drink Slushy Apple with Lychee Pop and Pineapple Coconut Jelly like we do at CW, but I've noticed a slight change in texture, it's not as thick as in milky as it should be, was a bit more watery. However the taste and recipe is still exactly the same, I must say Bothasig prepares their Tapioca better than CW! Dense, soft, sweet and Chewy!

Staff are super bubbly and helpful deciding a third drink for my difficult mother for not having a sweet tongue, but they definitely nailed the expectations! My mother finished the drink! Mango Jasmine Tea with tapioca and grass jelly, most importantly, *No sugar, Less sweetness*

Not too bad for a new "pop up" shop, will definitely be back!

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Kim-Louise Rousseau - Personal Gmail
Review: Friendly service and great bubble tea. And it's nice to have a boba shop in the northern suburbs. Atmosphere is hard to comment on since it is a tiny stall more than it is a shop.

Name: Sāan Artisanal Teas
Address: Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Live Mart Bakery Cafe
Address: 13 Printers Way, Marconi Beam, Cape Town
Author: Robbi Ferreira
Review: Such a good experience. So much variety and lovely staff. They have Boba and food as well and can recommend what to choose based on what you like.

Name: Live Mart Bakery Cafe
Address: 13 Printers Way, Marconi Beam, Cape Town
Author: Johanna Vermeulen
Review: Fast, affordable and friendly people. I do recommend them

Name: Live Mart Bakery Cafe
Address: 13 Printers Way, Marconi Beam, Cape Town
Author: Atarah Rass
Review: Only one of the people know anything about what they're selling - like ability to give advice - but they're super friendly, they're more affordable than most of the other Asian markets, they have great hours and food is yummmmmy. Less sugar option on drinks is still quite sweet.

Name: Live Mart Bakery Cafe
Address: 13 Printers Way, Marconi Beam, Cape Town
Author: Yolanda Herrendoerfer
Review: The restaurant's food is warm and delicious. It's the perfect lunchtime meal.  The service was quick.

Name: Live Mart Bakery Cafe
Address: 13 Printers Way, Marconi Beam, Cape Town
Author: Jelena Jerling
Review: This place has the absolute best bubble tea ever! They also have a very lovely selection of snacks and bakery goods. Amazing selection of food to choose from. Highly recommended to go here and grab some asian food.

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Adele Botes
Review: Most serene experience ever! I could sit there forever whilst comfortably sipping a warm cup of coffee. The view is simply astonishing. My new favourite place to go to.

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Chanel King
Review: Our absolute favourite spot with the best view and the best Baristas! Very close to our hearts ♥️☕️

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Lee-Anne Curtis-Cox
Review:
This well run cafe makes great coffee (and quickly) along with some amazing filled croissants! Customer service is warm and efficient.

Ask to sit in the "secret" garden space if you wish to enjoy your coffee with a view!

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: heather williams
Review: Hidden gem of a place. The affogato was perfectly made and the carrot cake delightful. You order at the little upstairs cafe where there are one or two seats and then go downstairs into the garden area to enjoy it or just get coffee to go. Not wheelchair friendly. Could use a few umbrellas for shade.

Name: Lighthouse cafe Blouberg
Address: 47 Stadler Rd, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Astrid Schon
Review: These cakes are just delicious... they are next level decadent. Don't be fooled by the size... we cut them into 4 and it is the perfect bit of yumminess with a coffee or a tea.  Best cheesecake this side of the bay and the croissants are so good, you are missing out if you don't try them.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Basheerah Schroeder
Review: Ben’s bubble tea is a cute small store. They have delicious gourmet flavors and the usuals. Personally the strawberry and chocolate tastes like nesquik ( which tastes really good ) and their gourmet flavors are quite nice too such as the Biscoff ( jusy very sweet) but the best one is the chocnado. Would defs recommend if you’re a boba lover however I must say they always take quite long to make your order even when not as busy so place your order and go in a few other shops then come back if you’re in a hurry

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Raeesah
Review:
Best bubble tea I've had in a while 😋
I had the watermelon and strawberry fresh fruit tea with extra pineapple jelly . It was really refreshing and flavourful 🤩 I highly recommend trying their tea.

And a big plus is that they are fully halaal 👍🏼

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Crisscross Applesauce
Review: Was my first time experiencing Boba tea. Coffee flavor Boba tea was nice. A bit expensive considering I've never had Boba. I've mentioned to staff it was my first time that I've heard about Boba, and I ordered what I though was Boba tea, ended up paying R18 for a desert item (the description is very hard to read as it is small), but I would have expected staff to inform me of what it was considering that I have mentioned that I never had Boba tea.  After a while of sitting and had a look through the menu, my mom told me that she doesn't think it was tea that I've ordered. Eventually I ordered the boba tea. But furthermore, flavor was good.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: dragoneyes1ct
Review: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City. Quaint little store with lovely staff. I had the Brown Sugar Classic Bubble Tea. Having had Bubble Tea in China and Japan and in various locations internationally, in SA this is the closest thing to the original I have had. Delicious. Highly recommend

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Serie Ross
Review: The Boba was delicious and you can pick the sweetness of your boba the staff are nice which gives it a great atmosphere.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Mogammad Faisal Baulackey
Review: Really nice vibey store. Difficult to find. They are up the stairs, through the woolworths, right, then left. Amazing place to take pictures.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: lluwellen king
Review: Funky interior.. great drink

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Marvin Ngobeni
Review: Great tasting bubble tea with perfect blend of flavours and sweetness

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Ayesha **
Review: One of my daughters favs

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Tatenda Schaafsma

Name: Susu bubble tea Cavendish
Address: 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Fayhoon Faisal
Review:
Lovely branch of Susu 💐
You can find it near dischem on lower floor👀 Bargain books is also next to it
Highly recommend Mango Tornado !! best drink 😋

Name: Susu bubble tea Cavendish
Address: 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Aisha Walls
Review: The Boba is delicious and high quality, though I will say they are a bit stingy on the amount of tapioca they give. Other than that it's an overall good experience.

Name: Susu bubble tea Cavendish
Address: 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Yusraa M

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Fayhoon Faisal
Review:
Love this branch of Susu 😍🩷 The only one opening up to a very late time 🙌🏻
Thanks a lot 💐keep up the good work🫡

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Herman
Review: Susu is such a great addition to the Adelphi Galleria. My go-to is the standard milk tea with pearls. Zero sugar is needed because the pearls are quite sweet. I wish they had a Matcha milk tea option with pearls on their menu. It would be great if they traded longer hours over a weekend.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Stephen Frank
Review:
1st time at this establishment. What a huge DISAPPOINTMENT 😞. Service was appalling, the lady had absolutely no interest in helping or assisting.
We were the only customers and she ignored us for about 4 minutes continuing with menial tasks instead of assisting us.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Farrid Chiwoniso
Review: 1st time at this establishment. What a huge DISAPPOINTMENT 😞.  Service was appalling,  the lady had no interest in helping or assisting. Ordered Drinking "YOGURT" But got the most watery Drink. Nothing about the YOGURT was YOGURT,  just plain tea/ flavored water. Asked the lady if that was indeed YOGURT and she just showing any interest or anything said yes. NOT ON 👎🏾

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Chrismonia Voko
Review: I had my favourite treat of all time. Tasty Taro Milk tea 🧋 Vegan option and 30% sugar. Thank you Susu Bubble tea Sea Point for the best service.

Name: Dalxa Coffee Shop Known Qurbajoogta
Address: 38 Durban Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: John Mrtn
Review: There coffee was extremely nice but they could have better place

Name: Dalxa Coffee Shop Known Qurbajoogta
Address: 38 Durban Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Biba Abdi
Review: I love this please  is one of the best coffee shop in Belleville

Name: Dalxa Coffee Shop Known Qurbajoogta
Address: 38 Durban Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Abdull Osman
Review: I love the tea they make is 100% galgaduud shah

Name: Dalxa Coffee Shop Known Qurbajoogta
Address: 38 Durban Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: BASHIR MOHAMMED
Review: They make some delicious coffee

Name: Dalxa Coffee Shop Known Qurbajoogta
Address: 38 Durban Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Axmed Nuur Mohamed
Review: Is special for me. When I'm buying

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Fei Yang
Review: I ordered a cup of cappuccino, and it was very good. And I ordered eggs benedict, and it was really good too. Friendly service and door open from 6:45am. Very good for breakfast.

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Wentzel Muller
Review: Our running crew has been coming to Mischu early in the mornings once or twice a week over the past two years. Such incredible staff, service, coffee and food! Will always return. Thank you so much for always having us Mischu!

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Veronika
Review: A great café at Sea Point, with very kind staff, cozy place and nice atmosphere, tasty food and drinks! I've had a green juice, and it was amazing!

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Aud Quest
Review: Courteous and affable staff, loved the coffee and Eggs Benedict. Definitely recommend.

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Arthur Yang
Review: Very good coffee and food, friendly service, door open early in the morning, as early as 6:45am.

Name: Original T-Bag Designs Hout Bay
Address: Shop 1, Pam Arlene Place, Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Carmen Michelle Sonnenberg
Review: Looking forward to seeing you again at Easter. People always laugh at my bag. I'm an old bag from Africa. Interesting to meet the folks who work there. Beautiful items on display. Really worth a visit. See you at Easter this month.

Name: Original T-Bag Designs Hout Bay
Address: Shop 1, Pam Arlene Place, Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Elizabeth Webster
Review: Very very interesting and so worth supporting. Take your used teabags to them. (Don't try and discard the tea leaves.) This amazing setup uplifts the women in the local township, by employing them. They can work from home. A must visit. Amazing craft work.

Name: Original T-Bag Designs Hout Bay
Address: Shop 1, Pam Arlene Place, Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Jaco Beukman
Review: Unique artwork, fashion and household items made all from old/used tea bags and tea. Friendly staff also all with a very unique story to tell. Housed in some parts of the great Cape-Dutch Kronendal buildings. Small free parking outside, video telling their story played inside with a cup of tea (or coffee). Afterwards join a tour with any of the friendly staff members of the place to meet all the artists and manufacturers as they produce. Toilet available.

Name: Original T-Bag Designs Hout Bay
Address: Shop 1, Pam Arlene Place, Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: S Wilson
Review: I have been going to the Tea Bag company for years as I believe in what it is doing to help the community (or certainly used to). It has now been taken over by new owners and even has an outlet at The Waterfront so I do hope that it remains the amazing place it was and delivers benefits to the community. There was a reasonable display of items but some of them have been 're designed' and I am not sure for the better. I shall go back sometime and that will give me a better idea of how well the original concept is still alive and kicking.

Name: Original T-Bag Designs Hout Bay
Address: Shop 1, Pam Arlene Place, Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Andras S
Review:
Unique place worth travelling to see it.
It is not a charity but a working business involving people from the local township creating art.
Get your souvenirs and presents from their gift shop, every item is a bit different to be one of a kind.

Name: CAFE CHIFFON
Address: SHOGF007, Old Cape Quarter, 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Ксения Л.
Review: We bought two pieces of cake here to take with us and a candle in the shape of a bear.  The sponge cake with strawberry cream was delicious and well soaked, but the taste was too simple, I would have liked something else inside, maybe jam or mashed berries.  the cheesecake was very good.

Name: CAFE CHIFFON
Address: SHOGF007, Old Cape Quarter, 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Iguette Miya
Review:
I recently visited Cafe chiffon and had the pleasure of trying their treaty delights . The it  was not only delicious but also beautifully presented, fitting perfectly with the cafe's charming aesthetic. The atmosphere was warm and inviting, making it a delightful spot to relax and enjoy a treat.

The service was okay—not outstanding, but certainly not bad. The staff was polite, and the overall experience was smooth. Despite the average service, I would definitely return, as the ambiance and quality of the cake make it a place worth visiting again.

Name: CAFE CHIFFON
Address: SHOGF007, Old Cape Quarter, 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Vitesh Rampaul
Review: Heard about this place, had to try it out. Went there for drinks and cake on a Sunday. It was very quiet which I guess was nice... unfortunately our coconut milk drinks tasted funny and upon returning it to the counter, they confirmed it was off. Thankfully, though, new drinks were made. The edibles are insanely good.

Name: CAFE CHIFFON
Address: SHOGF007, Old Cape Quarter, 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: C. D.
Review: Lovely experience: their Japanese cheesecake was light and fluffy, the Caprese Danish was fresh and delicious if you're a savory fan. They've got some creative non-alcoholic drinks as well! Took home a slice of their basque cheesecake, which was divine. And their s'more Crookie was excellent! So much variety, definitely worth the visit!

Name: CAFE CHIFFON
Address: SHOGF007, Old Cape Quarter, 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Homarya Shaik
Review: Went based on recommendations and was so disappointed. It was empty when I got there so didn’t expect the cold greeting from the staff behind the counter, nevertheless ordered and expected fresh treats since it was so early. Instead the macaron was clearly taken out from the fridge and was a weird cream texture, not the way regular macarons are. Just not the place for me I think. Drink was okay

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Shanoa Madeira
Review: She has very attentive with my mother. The massages were very good. My mom could walk very well because of her hip pain but after her massages she was feeling very fresh. Totally recommend coming!!!

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Ryan Muller
Review: We had no idea prior visiting Rosetta's today; asthma can be handled and brought under control in such a short space of time. Apart from her knowledgeable information pertaining to Chinese Medicine, the Gua Sha performed, did absolute wonders. Upon entering, Theresa struggled with breathing, but shortly after, she was 90% back to her real self. I'd recommend Rosetta Health anytime.

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Norman John
Review:
One of our favourite small local businesses that my wife and I enjoy to support because they stock products that you will not find in a large chain supermarket. Many of the items have been imported from Asia. They also stock perishable and refrigerated items that are sourced locally, are of a high quality and reasonably priced.

The proprietors, Rose and May are super friendly and helpful. A welcoming smile from either of them when I walk in is the highlight of my day! My wife on occasion will go for a massage in their parlour which is adjoined to the shop. We cannot wait until we go back to stock up on more of the specialties that they have in store for us.

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Carla Whitehead
Review: Excellent Chinese pressure point massage. Friendly staff and nangamso gave me the extra hard massage I asked for finding every single knot. Will definitely go back and highly recommend if you are looking for a pressure point massage

Name: Rosetta Asian Healthy Store
Address: 46 C Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Aaron Van heerden
Review:
By far the best massaging and cupping sessions.

Been going to them for awhile now with lower back problems. Once done with the sessions I'm able to move And go about my day with no issues .

Staff is very kind and generous too

10 out 10 and definitely will recommend them

Name: Majestic Café
Address: 44 Beach Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Liberty Dimingo
Review: I ordered a fish and chips parcel on the 21 Dec 2024. There was more flour on the fish that the fish itself.  This is really disappointing especial for a place like that. The other day i ordered ice cream cone . My son dropped some of the ice cream on the car mate, when i wiped it off, i missed a spot only to find it days later when i was cleaning the vehicle , to my surprise, the cream had solidified. My question! what edible product was in the ice cream which would solidify instead of melting ?

Name: Majestic Café
Address: 44 Beach Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Stephanie A
Review: A lovely small family-owned shop by the beach - very generous portions of their snack boxes and cheap eats. Iconic heaping creamy ice cream. Cash or snapscan only although they have an ATM machine for cash withdrawals in-store.

Name: Majestic Café
Address: 44 Beach Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Penny Penny
Review: Amazing shop, oldest shop in Muizenburg, super friendly owners, Portuguese Fish and Chips, ice cream cones, just like in Portugal. Wonderful collection of vintage tins and Coke memorabilia. Rare beach front gem. Right on the beach, loads of parking.

Name: Majestic Café
Address: 44 Beach Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Henrik Daugbjerg
Review: When I am on tour with my tourists, I always try to make enough time to pop around for the most delicious soft serve on the continent. Have never failed. Friendly staff and the soft serve is just perfect.... creamy and tasty. Til we meet again

Name: Majestic Café
Address: 44 Beach Rd, Muizenberg, Cape Town
Author: Kiara Fernandes
Review: Very friendly staff here, everyone's helpful and even after a hot day, all you see is smiles. the ice cream here is honestly top class (usually the best after a nice surf). The fish and chips are ELITE, crispy and fresh fish, hot and crispy chips served with the best quality,calamari with the taste that tingles your tastebuds. The food here is packed with flavor and not to mention, the wonderful view opposite the café. one thing i like to do is usually get an ice cream here (there is a variation of chocolate and vanilla or even mixed) and walk along the beach. served soft and creamy! i definitely recommend this place to many people who enjoy a nice, creamy ice-cream, or even some fresh fish and chips.There is alot of other variations here other than just fish and chips, such as toasted sarmies, hamburgers and chiprolls.The prices here are a bargain especially regarding to the quality of the food served. Take a stop by The majestic café in Muizenberg!!!

Name: Susu
Address: The Pumphouse, 6 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: gustav gabel
Review: Very nice bubble tea

Name: Susu
Address: The Pumphouse, 6 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Widaad Daniels

Name: Susu
Address: The Pumphouse, 6 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Rafàh Lutta

Name: The Daily Coffee Café - Willowbridge Village
Address: Shop 10C,Willowbridge village, 39 Carl Cronje Dr, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Reynhard
Review: Picture says it all. Could get some work done whilst enjoying amazing food at a great place.

Name: The Daily Coffee Café - Willowbridge Village
Address: Shop 10C,Willowbridge village, 39 Carl Cronje Dr, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Es* Louw
Review:
Very nice and cool inside, specaily on a very warm day.  Peaceful  inside.
Coffeeshake could have more coffee flavor to my preferences.
Pancakes can be .... I would add butter or oil to the mixture to make it more light and less dry. Just my opion. .

Name: The Daily Coffee Café - Willowbridge Village
Address: Shop 10C,Willowbridge village, 39 Carl Cronje Dr, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Charl Esau
Review: Was hankering for a perfect poached egg and decided to pop in at this venue for the first time. Bacon & Eggs are my favourite food items, I don't care what time of day it is - I love myself some "breakfast". Found a comfortable quiet cosy spot, with Wifi. I enjoyed a really decent vanilla latte (bonus yummy custard biscotti) and hands-down the best value-for-money breakfast. Perfectly poached eggs and proper bacon (my partners in crime had theirs with potato rosti/on sourdough). Really glad to have found this spot. Thanks for an enjoyable breakfast (The Daily serves All-Day Breakfast). My kinda menu. Breakfast All-Day Everyday🙌

Name: The Daily Coffee Café - Willowbridge Village
Address: Shop 10C,Willowbridge village, 39 Carl Cronje Dr, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: MK Interiors
Review: We wanted to pass some time until the next appointment and walked into this little coffee shop. It is cosy, and the staff is friendly. The cappuccino was delicious, and we enjoyed the relaxing atmosphere. We will definitely want to try their food sometime. I will definitely suggest them for a quick business lunch or meeting.

Name: The Daily Coffee Café - Willowbridge Village
Address: Shop 10C,Willowbridge village, 39 Carl Cronje Dr, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Kareemah Abdurahman
Review: The food is quite mediocre, the wettest burger I've ever eaten, bland. My husband had a Nashville bagel, didn't have a drop of spice on it. You're only given one serviette, and when I asked for more, I only got one more, even though they serve the wettest burgers and bagels. No wet wipes. Service was meh, waitress was only there to take our order and bring food. Wasn't seen again until we asked for the bill. The manager heard me expressing my unhappiness but didn't bother coming to check on us. Overall poor food and poor service. Would not recommend.

Name: High Tea Tighty
Address: 60 Penlyn Ave, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Hamza Du Plessis
Review: Lady Zaheera is an artist, a pro and a wonderful person. I am working abroad in Saudi thus at times it gets a little tricky to impress my daughter. But by all means, lady Zaheera is here to impress and satisfy with her marvelous creation of beautiful pieces of eatable art. My daughter Nusaybah is literally jumping with joy and enjoying every moment of her special moment alongside this very special birthday cake along with extras. Thank you 💚💚💚

Name: High Tea Tighty
Address: 60 Penlyn Ave, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Clare Downie
Review: Had such a wonderful experience working with this team! They were able to put together a gorgeous cake with very little time! And it tastes insanely good 🤩🤩🤩

Name: High Tea Tighty
Address: 60 Penlyn Ave, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Pranav Ramkilawan
Review:
Had a custom cake made for my wife's 30th.
Looked amazing and their friendly staff made it easy to arrange & pick up.
We may have got more cake than we bargained for but that's not really a "problem" 😄

Name: High Tea Tighty
Address: 60 Penlyn Ave, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Elizabeth Bekker
Review: I've ordered cakes and cupcakes from Zaheera for my nephew's birthday for the last couple of years. Every time they looked simply amazing!! Whenever I approached her with an idea or concept she was very happy to realize it and each time time it was simply top top quality. I cannot tell you how impressed we were with the cakes and cupcakes - she's a baking magician! But not only do they look great they also taste fantastic. I live overseas and requested delivery for my family in Cape Town, so most of our correspondence has taken place over email - she is always very responsive and patient in her communication. I cannot recommend Zaheera enough!! I will definitely contact her for our future special occasions as well.

Name: High Tea Tighty
Address: 60 Penlyn Ave, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Kiera
Review: Wow - just wow! Zaheera is an expert in her craft. I had to organise a cake for my little cousin’s birthday and I was really struggling to do so from the UK. I found High Tea Tighty and contacted Zaheera who got back to me within hours. She communicated every step of the way, and made sure the cake was there early in the morning on the day of the party. Not only was the cake gorgeous (everyone was so impressed with it!) but I just really wanted to highlight Zaheera’s customer service. She was kind throughout and just really took the pressure off of everything. I am definitely going to be using her services for every future birthday! Thank you again, Zaheera!

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Ludwig Otto
Review: Strong wifi, not loud at all very nice space to work remotely,  with great coffee and very tasty food. The owner is very hands on. The barista was on point with his cappuccino. Ample parking in a quiet neighborhood.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Ava Peattie
Review:
Service was slow, waitress forgot my drinks order. Portion size is amazing, but quality of food could be of a higher standard. I ordered the butternut, feta, avo, cucumber sandwich on rye, bit bland nothing special, but great combination. The chai freezer was really great and refreashing.

Will definatly give this resturant another go if Im in the area!

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Dima Zamisa
Review: Wonderful Cafe for your meeting with friends and family outing.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: A W
Review:
Wonderful, strong, unpretentious coffee at great prices. Visit on a Sunday morning to have the place all to yourself.

Feb 2020 update: Flat white is still R25 and good! R39 Rise and Shine breakfast is good value too if you're peckish but not ravenous.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Khanyi Zulu
Review: It's a nice local café. The service is very warm ,but the muffins we got came in plastic packaging. Would've been nice to have them fresh or served heated.

Name: Ta Tea
Address: 37 Simonsberg Cres, Tafelsig, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: danielle krasniqi
Review:
My first high tea experience was made so incredible with the staff and ambiance at Mount Nelson.  Craig was thoughtful in explaining the tea selection to us and sharing details of tea ceremonies which we loved.  and our waiter Cheslyn was outstanding- I hope to see her immortalized in the menu book with a drawing of her smiling face by the time we come back for another visit!
The food, teas, and sweets were so good and we loved the hotel itself.  11/10 experience- hope we can make it back from NYC soon!

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Theran Pather
Review: Had the vegan High Tea experience. Some of the most interesting vegan foods I've ever eaten. A great selection of sweet and savory items for everyone. There is even a selection of cakes that you can also indulgence in as part of the experience. The highlight is, in fact, the tea selection. There are so many options to choose from, I personally enjoyed the Mount Nelson House blend for those who like a stronger, darker tea.

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Rene Mostert
Review: It was good but I do miss the long table with eats. The trays are fancy but do not give you the buffet feeling of abundance... Fot the price one would like abundance. The kids' tray was really cute. As always...the hotel and garden are absolutely amazing!

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Bradley Ford
Review: Highly recommend the High Tea option. The staff provides, and creates, a perfect experience. Don't be put off by the price, it's worth every cent.

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Amber Cohen
Review: OMG! This entire afternoon exceeded expectations. The service was the best I’ve experienced in Cape Town. It was such a brilliantly designed menu, both tea and food. The honey bush ice tea/punch was incredible. Better than any house cocktail I’ve ever had. The exterior and interior of the hotel itself is beautiful! Couldn’t believe how gorgeous in person it was. Thank you for a relaxing and filling afternoon.

Name: Wild and Ancient Natural Products
Address: 72 Loop St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Barry Tyson
Review: Top quality teas and the packaging makes for a perfect gift

Name: A Boutique Tea and Coffee Cafe
Address: 1 Welbevind Road Cnr, Valley Rd, Hout Bay`, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Ottery Hyper
Address: New Ottery Rd, Ottery, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Honest Chocolate Cafe
Address: 64A Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Tarcisio Borges
Review: A great spot for a coffee and treat. Honest Chocolate Café has a nice selection of chocolate cakes, cookies, and truffles. We tried the coffee and the double dark chocolate cake, and both were really good. The place has a cozy vibe and is worth a stop if you’re in the area and want something sweet.

Name: Honest Chocolate Cafe
Address: 64A Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Loren Ellis
Review:
I had one Intense Hot Chocolate, which is perfect for that person who loves a 70%-85% chocolate intensity. So delicious! Sip and Savour.
Secondly, we had a Biscoff Spread Hot Chocolate which is a nice in-betweener with a more milky-natural-sweet, warm cookie taste.
Friendly and helpful staff who give great advice and a cozy warm atmosphere with delightful music.
They are also vegan friendly.
A MUST visit in my books.

Name: Honest Chocolate Cafe
Address: 64A Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Review: Cozy elegant cafe. welcoming kind staff. And This is the best brownie I have ever tried also the coffee tastes great🤎

Name: Honest Chocolate Cafe
Address: 64A Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Bree
Review: Gorgeous place, love the interiors and patio! The staff are adorable and sweet, and the treats and coffee were delicious. We tried the chocolate cheese cake and buckwheat tart. 10/10!

Name: Honest Chocolate Cafe
Address: 64A Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Buyisiwe Shongwe
Review:
Quirky, curious and off the beaten track. We enjoyed all the hidden gems this cafe had to offer. The chocolate is artisanal and accessible. I assume it’s made is small batches because it really tasted special.
Friendly staff. Interesting and charming spot.

Name: Pola
Address: Main Rd, Scott Estate, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Caitlyn Ganesh
Review: The tomato soup was delicious and perfect for the cold Cape Town weather. The restaurant was packed on this particular Saturday evening, expected for Observatory though, but it took around 20mins or so just for my drinks order, and at least I got a knee blanket to embrace the cold. Will definitely return on my next trip to Cape Town and hopefully it will be warmer🙌🏽

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Lyndsey Petro
Review:
Always considered this more of a café, but was pleasantly surprised by the variety and yumminess of the menu.

Great spot for meetings and 'working from home'.

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Jain Halder
Review: The food was heartwarmingly good. Their selection of drinks ranged from a wide variety. The place is a little crowded but very warm and cozy thanks to their fire place. Definitely recommend coming here but only if it's not crowded.

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ziggy Ezulu
Review:
This definitely makes the Grade in the Cosy Chic Cafe category.
Beautiful interior with picturesque bar and functional wood-burning fireplace in the corner.
Tables by the window with the outside view- the best for just chilling and watching life pass by.
Went there in the evening and the ambiance was perfect - relaxed, good muted music, the buzz of conversation.
Friendly staff and good service.
Can definitely recommend this 😉👍

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ntando Sithole
Review: A great place to work and eat delicious food. The food is also affordable, the atmosphere were just right and the staff was very friendly. For the students, I highly recommend it for study dates. It's close to the UCT health science faculty and is a walking distance from Obz Sqaure. Enjoy!

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Afrika Lovers
Review:
Truth seemed to be a guarantee for great quality of food and beverages and amazing atmosphere. Where the latter still stands, the former is a bit shaky recently.

We ordered a free-range burger that was way too greasy, more than blend taste wise and somehow just not doing it.

The pastries (chocolate croissants) were better. The teas (lemon rooibos & earl grey) were good whereas the orange juice was not made from fresh oranges but concentrate.

Come on Truth you can do better. Almost a 2 stars for the food but we don’t want to be too harsh. Will be back soon to see if there are improvements…

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Milo
Review:
What a truly awesome experience! Walking by their little unassuming outside coffeebar, I asked for a seat and was absolutely astounded by what awaited inside! The setup is incredible, absolutely incredible. Awesome music, amazing fun staff, cool antiques, mechanical design, coffee roasting, pleasing aesthetic, delicious food and outstanding coffee. This place has it all!
Its been a minute since any shop has had such an effect on me. I went in with the intention of starting a book I had bought down the street but instead just stared in amazement at this wondrous place. There are so many neat things going on, apparently they also have fantastic breads and pastries but I won’t even get into that. My favorite was the rotating coffee bean stand and amazing server Siya ✊🏽💜
A tremendous thank you for this great coffee experience.

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Toni Henning
Review:
I thoroughly enjoyed the outing to Truth Coffee. Their coffee IS amazing. I had a Resurrection Latte, and my son had a flat white.
I really appreciate that they don't charge extra for milk substitutes. The food was extra yummy. The decor is unusual - everywhere you look - such interesting things. The menu is fun to read! I love the way the waiting staff dress, too. I have one little complaint. When we ordered, I asked if they have muffins. Our waiter said no and proceeded to indicate on the menu what I could choose from. Clearly, I was in the mood for something sweet or baked if I asked for a muffin, right? Only after our food arrived, I saw other patrons with cake. It would have been great if our waiter had told me that, while they don't have muffins, they have other baked goods. Luckily, the avo on toast was so delicious that I got over it. Haha! Next time I go, I will order from the bakery.

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Tarcisio Borges
Review: We absolutely loved Truth Coffee! The atmosphere is incredibly unique with its steampunk-inspired decor, making it feel like you’ve stepped into a different world. The coffee is truly exceptional—carefully roasted and served with great attention to detail. The staff is knowledgeable and friendly, guiding you through their impressive selection. It’s no wonder this spot has been recognized globally as one of the best coffee shops. Definitely a must-visit in Cape Town!

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Pete T (epetet)
Review: A funky atmosphere with fantastic coffee and unique food options.  More than just a touristy spot they have a great variety of coffees and bakery products as well as kitchen-made menu options.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Jon W (Anonymous)
Review: Quite an artsy spot, but you would struggle to find better coffee in CT! Parking across the road is free and plentiful. Prices are very reasonable. Just wish they sold decaf coffee.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Marlin
Review: Great little coffee shop that you should go and check out not just for the take away coffee. Bought of their houseblend beans and its really good. More than that I think its a nice space to just unwind a bit while having a coffee.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Marizanne van Wyk
Review: I had a great time at Boston Coffee! I had an iced coffee, and it was lovely. I’ve previously had other drinks there, and everything is delicious! The service is good and the people working there are so friendly. The vibe is really relaxed and nice. It was the first time I ate there, and the food was good. The only drawback for me personally, was that there is no option of brown bread. However, the bread they do give is very nice. Other than that, I loved everything.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Johan van Wyk
Review:
When I really want to treat myself with incredible coffee and very nice something small to eat, this is the place that I go to.
The variety of coffees is to write home about, the staff is very friendly and helpful and the atmosphere is always joyful.
It is a very popular business and there are always people, irrespective of the time of day.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Magdalena Rousseau
Review: I love - LOVE! - visiting Boston Coffee! Sometimes alone, sometimes with my 2 grandkids, sometime with friends. The coffee is excellent, and so is the food. And the vibe is mysterious, i.e. no words to describe it, but it is THERE! Well done, and keep it up!

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Hesham Abuelkher
Review: When the taste of coffee and excellent service come together, it's Mocha Coffee cafe

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Abduragiem Adams
Review: Hidden gem next to excellent meat market 😋

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: shireen adam
Review: Lovely surprise walking around not this coffee shop. Tucked away at the end of the parking lot. Wonderful hospitality and coffee. Will definitely be back.

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Aniesa Amijee
Review:
Excellent service and warm welcome by barista Ali and co-worker Abdul.
Coffee and hot chocolate was extra delicious.
Worth a visit.

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Abed Khaled
Review: One of the best cortado I taste ever

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Yolinda Badenhorst
Review: You come in as a stranger and don't leave like one. Hey Stranger, is a new kid on the block and I hope they stay here for long. Not many coffee shops with a small bite to eat. This place is for those in a hurry or just one who wants to sit down and enjoy a great cuppa. The shop is very clean and staff very friendly. They also have a loyalty card, for the regulars. 10th cup on the house.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Christina Pretorius
Review: Such a nice contrast to the grittiness of Long Street. Modern, clean, minimalist aesthetic with great, strong coffee and friendly service. We had the pecan tart and matcha white chocolate cake. Both were good, the only minor thing I would suggest is to warm the tart a bit so it’s easier to eat. I went back for a second coffee before I left and spoke to the owner and she was so nice and welcoming.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Ludwig van Rooyen
Review:
Finally a specialty coffee shop serving exclusively Father Coffee. One of the best Cortado’s I had in Cape Town. Using Heirloom Blend as the house blend. Clean and minimalistic interior. Wide selection of pastries available. Single origins and Almond and Oat milk alternatives available.

Father beans also sold here.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Illana Justus
Review: We were looking for a coffee shop whilst out in Town earlier today, and happened to stumble across Hey Stranger. What a pleasant experience. The staff are friendly. The coffee was good. The caramel cheesecake & red velvet cake were delicious too.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Nicole Pretorius
Review: My experience at Hey Stranger coffee collective was great, service was quick coffee and bun combo was oh so good, the decor was modern, each piece of furniture unique and very mod. Thomas and Natalia are wonderful people, I chatted with Thomas a little bit. The food came quickly and service was very friendly. A top notch coffee shop, a definite visit when you're in Town CBD you won't be disappointed a treat.

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Talisa Jane Du Bois
Review:
This five star review surprised even me. Deeply excited to see all these smaller cafe’s popping up and thriving around Cape Town. Beautiful aesthetics, good light (you know I’m a fan), GREAT coffee, and 10/10 service.

I’ve been back maybe 25 times since this original review - a coffee space that delivers flawlessly time and time again!

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Dr. KHAMIS ALMAZROOEI
Review: One of my favourite coffee shops in Cape Town. You can taste the passion and love of the coffee in each sip of coffee.

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Fariah Mayet
Review:
I'm so glad I stumbled across this place. I went in to ask if they sell Moka pot filters, but unfortunately, they didn’t. However, Grant was incredibly helpful and advised me on where I could find one. Since I was already there, I decided to try their coffee. I was absolutely blown away by the taste. Their baristas are clearly highly trained, and it shows in every cup :)

Not only is their coffee outstanding, but they also offer a selection of specialty coffee beans for purchase. Their subscription services are reasonably priced and a true gem. It’s safe to say they’ve gained a loyal customer in me

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: kimberley canning
Review:
Simple bru Melkbos is our current favourite coffee in CT and so we had so stop by to check out the new spot closer to home.

Coffee just as delish! Friendly staff in a contemporary space filled with their unique artwork - a different vibe from the seaside space we know but we are sure this location will be a success and will definitely be back for more coffee soon

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Stanley Biggs
Review: Excellent coffee. Super professional baristas that’s absolutely perfectionistic about quality, but remains super friendly, welcoming and human. I really love this space and I feel right at home there. They do great and interesting specialty coffee and they serve all the manual brews to perfection, but the thing that I keep on telling people to go try is a Flat White with their Alternative Blend: at this moment, I honestly believe that it is the finest house blend in Cape Town. It has sweetness and range that will satisfy all palates (and it’s great black too!)

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Yoveen H
Review:
A unique restaurant with a powerful social change aspect to it, combined with incredible food and excellent service.

Side note: the best Eggs Benedict in the Cape!

Well done ILC - keep up the phenomenal work!

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Benita Evans
Review: A wonderful gem of a coffee shop. Always great service and friendly staff. Seating available ioutside weather permitting, it's lovely to sit outside and enjoy a lovely coffee in the summer. In the winter the inside is usually packed, especially on a rainy day. The shelves are filled with delicious baked goods and pastries. They also roast their own coffee beans and will grind them if you prefer to buy ground coffee.

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Richard Hills
Review:
On this holiday we've made time to eat here three times so far, Unathi has been the lady who has looked after us on all our visits and is friendly and fantastic at her job.
I highly recommend the mushroom and egg on sour dough bread. The prices are very reasonable and the size of portions are very good.

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Willem Van Ryswyck
Review:
What an incredible business plan to have majority staff who are hard of hearing. Everyone was incredibly friendly and there were lots of smiles all round from the staff. On top of the the food was excellent and had a nice outside sitting area, with plugs inside if needed.

Went back and tried the pastries and can highly recommend! The carrot cake was delicious.

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Malaika
Review: So glad I found this bakery. I had a custard croissant and apple, carrot and ginger juice. The custard croissant was delicious and the juice was good. I can’t wait to go back and try the other baked goods.

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Antony Ribot
Review: Lovely barista and coffee. The muffins looked amazing but we didn’t have time to try them this time round. We’ll be back. The other regular customers were very friendly as well.

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Michael psaltis
Review:
Enjoyed the chia tea but there could be more seating area. Nice spot to stop for a quick coffee after a run or cycle.
Great friendly service

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Lindsay S
Review:
My love affair with coffee just never seems to end. That first sip just boosts my serotonin receptors.
Not everyone feels that way about the magical brew in a little cup.
Our taste buds all differ and with that our preference for coffee. Some might like a bold dark roast with bitter chocolatey overtones and others might like a smooth balanced coffee.

If you prefer a smooth, slightly acidic fruity coffee with a chocolatey finish, then Barista on Move will deliver.

I have had many good coffees and what makes Barista on Move so different, is the experience created by the passionate baristas. On arrival you are greeted with a friendly unpretentious smile followed by an inquiry into how you are doing. With me being a regular, it is just for me to confirm my preference - usually a flat white with a double shot of espresso.

The picture attached to the review shows what the perfect flat white should look like - velvety and creamy - absolute magic in a cup!!

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Anthony Goble
Review: Great spot for a quick coffee on the go. Friendly baristas serving good coffee with a smile. Note there is no indoor seating but the outdoor benches are under cover.

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Marsha Petersen
Review: Delish coffee. Opens up at 6am opposite the walking park. Quick cuppa before you get on with the day. Nothing in CT opens that early. I'm impressed. I need my coffee this early in the morning. Thank you for being open so early and having a wonderful cheerful barista. Keep it up.

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Jia Jin Low
Review:
Happy to see that single origin beans have finally made their way to Cape Town since my last visit. Was intending on hitting up Rosetta, Origin, and Espressolab to get a feel for the coffee scene here.

The cafe had a nice vibe, but the coffee was decidedly average by international standards (e.g. if you’ve visited top coffee joints in Japan, Australia, Singapore, etc. — this ain’t that). The macchiato was overextracted and too acidic, while the flat white didn’t show the promised “buttery” or “citrus peel” notes. It’s hard to say whether the issue was with the sourcing, roasting, or the shot pulling, but either way it didn’t blow my mind.

I do have to say though, that the coffee compares favourably with other cafes in Cape Town. But if you’re a tourist, it’s not worth a special trip to see.

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Ghalib Bin Ghalib
Review:
Updated review

Rosetta Roastery is a standout spot for anyone serious about coffee in Cape Town. The espresso, pour-over, and milk-based drinks are all crafted to perfection, with each cup highlighting the rich, nuanced flavors of their expertly roasted beans. Whether you're an espresso aficionado or prefer a delicate pour-over, you’ll be impressed by the attention to detail and quality.

In addition to the stellar coffee, their pastries are the perfect accompaniment. Freshly baked and delicious, they make a great snack to enjoy alongside your drink, whether you’re stopping by for a quick break or settling in for a relaxed morning.

If you’re looking for a place that pairs top-tier coffee with delightful pastries, Rosetta Roastery is an absolute must-visit in Cape Town.

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Jill Paslier
Review: I got some beans and a pastry to go. They were both absolutely incredible. I’ve never smelt or tasted coffee like this before. It was very unique and very delicious. One of the baristas helped me pick it out and they were very kind and gave me a sample as well.unfortunately I did not have time to visit this coffee shop again as the atmosphere was very nice inside and I would have loved to sit down for coffee as well. The beans are great. I can definitely recommend those for now. :-)

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Em Alaryani
Review: One of the best specialty coffee spots in Cape Town! The coffee is exceptional, the staff is friendly and knowledgeable, and the prices are very reasonable. Highly recommended!

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Ronier Zalnieriunas
Review:
Dropped by for a quick coffee and had a delightful experience.

The folks behind the bar know their coffees and take their craft seriously.

I tried the progressive option for my espresso and was a little disappointed. They immediately offered to remake the coffee and adjusted the extraction.

Extracting light roasts is difficult and they did well the second time round.

The espresso tonic (can’t recall if that was the name) was a super cool concept that I’d never tried before. Be try tasty as well.

The ambiance was relaxed and the bar beautiful.

Highly recommend a visit here if you like coffee.

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: የክርስቶስ ቤተ ክርስቲያን
Review:
Always great service lovely.very good people and best coffee, originally from Ethiopia. I love it the most.
እግዚአብሔር ይባርካችሁ

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: Tarik Abay
Review: organic and natural coffe with good service

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: nicolas carsten
Review: The food is okay however the parking and location is what had me worried the hole time and I was constantly thinking about my safety on the way out I got boxes in by 2 men that tried to steal my cell phone........be very careful and vigilant

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: Salman Mo
Review: Very clean and neat environment, love that taste of coffee. Excellent service

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: ABDIHAKIM HASAN ABDI Abdi
Review: I enjoy the taste of coffee.

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Shaheeda Crombie
Review: Just mediocre. The staff component handles everything well. The menu however, man talk about boring. I am going to say it but Vida and Kauai makes better drinks. Just a no for me.

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Izelke Gebhardt
Review:
Such convenience
The Seattle Coffee ☕️ Company is very conveniently situated outside Foodlovers so grab a coffee ☕️before or after your shopping 🛍.
Staff is friendly, you can sit down while taking in the smells 😁👍

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Anchilla Sobotker
Review: The best coffee ever. Thank you very much for the excellent customer service you provide. Keep up the great service you provide.

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Dietrich Schwarzer
Review: Very friendly staff. Excellent coffee

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Anne Burger
Review: Love going to this Seattle, it's just a little container style coffee bar outside of the Food Lover's Market. Whenever we go to buy groceries at that FLM we go get coffees first and sip on them while we shop. The people that work at this specific Seattle are also always super friendly, they like to make jokes and chat to their customers and I thoroughly enjoy that.
