Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Bruce Moore
Review: I've been here 10+ times.  Mostly Sunday mornings for post hike breakfast.  Great experience.  Lovely to be remembered. They know our coffee orders and what we order.  Food has always been good.  Coffee is strong.  Highly recommend.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Hendrickse
Review: A fantastic hidden gem in Cape Town, with fantastic food and hot beverages. There is plenty inside seating with a very spacious setting. Parking is limited on the street. Prince, our waiter, was fantastic and was extremely attentive.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Ronny Schäfer
Review: Great place. Very good coffee. Elegant, modern interior. Very nice service. Food was good, but the bagel was not my favorite one, too soft. Would absolutely recommend it for a coffee though and if you need a relaxing place to work.

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Edith Mirzaian
Review:
I’m so happy to have discovered this gem in Cape Town. I was here for 8 days and the hotel coffee wasn’t cutting it. Of course we explored the town in search of the coffee that was going to hit the spot. Shift did not disappoint. The coffee is the best we had in town, the green juice was also a favorite, and you enjoy these treats in the cool vibe atmosphere they’ve created. But the staff is the best. They are lovely people serving deliciousness.
I did not try the food, but the dishes going past me looked delectable.

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Buyisiwe Shongwe
Review:
Great place to have a coffee outside if the weather’s good. Service started off great but deteriorated over the course of our stay. May have just been a busy morning. The staff was friendly enough and fixed all the errors they were making.
The location gets the spot a nice rating otherwise it’s kinda average.

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Chantal H.
Review: During our stay in Cape Town we had breakfast at this place every day. The personal was amazing and very friendly! The food is good and de ambiance is very laid back and chill. Would defenitly recommand this place for everybody who likes good food and coffee to start your day of with a smile.

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Erick Mendoza
Review: Good spot to relax in friendly enviroment food for me was spot on i did scrabled eggs avo and toast and the avocado is actually real no a spread. Staff is friendly especially Pumi

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Milo
Review:
What a gorgeous coffee shop! The aesthetic is perfect, the service is excellent and warm and the coffee is wonderful. Its such a perfect experience, I made this my compulsory stop every morning!
Their food is so good too!
They have a give a book, take a book deal and I am proud to have left a work I truly enjoyed there. It’s an all-round great feeling here at shift.
If I lived here, I would want to work here 📚☕️⛰️🌊🦭☀️🌴

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Fei Yang
Review: I ordered a cup of cappuccino, and it was very good. And I ordered eggs benedict, and it was really good too. Friendly service and door open from 6:45am. Very good for breakfast.

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Wentzel Muller
Review: Our running crew has been coming to Mischu early in the mornings once or twice a week over the past two years. Such incredible staff, service, coffee and food! Will always return. Thank you so much for always having us Mischu!

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Veronika
Review: A great café at Sea Point, with very kind staff, cozy place and nice atmosphere, tasty food and drinks! I've had a green juice, and it was amazing!

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Aud Quest
Review: Courteous and affable staff, loved the coffee and Eggs Benedict. Definitely recommend.

Name: mischu coffee
Address: 85A Regent Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Arthur Yang
Review: Very good coffee and food, friendly service, door open early in the morning, as early as 6:45am.

Name: Bootlegger XS - Brackenfell Corner
Address: Paradys St, Brackenfell, Cape Town
Author: Des'Ree Nortje
Review: Lovely space, a bit small, but the atmosphere is great.

Name: Bootlegger XS - Brackenfell Corner
Address: Paradys St, Brackenfell, Cape Town
Author: D Welgemoed
Review: Bootlegger Brackenfell, the best! The best service, friendlyness and most awesome yummy food! The coffee is a definate day maker!!! Will see you soon!!!

Name: Bootlegger XS - Brackenfell Corner
Address: Paradys St, Brackenfell, Cape Town
Author: Hannes van Eeden
Review: I've been coming here since 2022, and there's a reason I return 3 or 4 times a week. The coffee is fantastic, and the food, especially the avo on toast. But the main reason I keep coming back - the staff. You guys are what makes this place a gem.

Name: Bootlegger XS - Brackenfell Corner
Address: Paradys St, Brackenfell, Cape Town
Author: Jorinda Coetzee
Review: Always friendly and know locals so well. Thanks for keeping the Brackenfell Teacher up and running with the best coffee around the block.

Name: Bootlegger XS - Brackenfell Corner
Address: Paradys St, Brackenfell, Cape Town
Author: Gillian Nunn
Review: I love popping around to out little Bootlegger for a coffee. Often just to get out and relax. The service is fantastic, the coffee is soothing and the food is always delicious and fresh.  A favorite with my family is the mini cheesecakes and the filled croissants.

Name: Motherland Coffee Cavendish
Address: Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Enid Son
Review: Great for a quick bite and cuppachino whilst out shopping in Cavendish.   Super friendly service.    Really good coffee.   The pastrami on rye is really delicious.

Name: Motherland Coffee Cavendish
Address: Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Heather Withers
Review: Very hard to knock this little gem. Ordered one tea and one coffee. The first thing that gets you is the comfort handle of the mug. Staff uber friendly, toasted chicken and avo sandwich very yummy and the brownie cheesecake is to die for. Definately will return.

Name: Motherland Coffee Cavendish
Address: Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Fayhoon Faisal
Review:
very small seating place , sandwich was too salty for me 🙁
i ordered hot chocolate and 3 cheese sandwich , the drink was good 👍🏻

Name: Motherland Coffee Cavendish
Address: Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Sithembiso Gugulethu Mbatha
Review: Lovely, cute place to catch up on some leisure reading, while enjoying a 3 cheese toasted bagel and a chai latte (my favourite)!

Name: Motherland Coffee Cavendish
Address: Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Tracy Bee
Review:
One of the best hidden gems in Cavendish.
The baristas are super friendly no matter what time of the AM you order - always positive and vibrant. Amazing service. Delish foods too.  Couldn’t recommend Motherland Coffee enough.

Name: Motherland Coffee St Georges Mall
Address: Mandela Rhodes Place, Wale St & Georges Mall, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: G P
Review:
Excellent coffee shop with distinct coffee flavor that stands out in the myriad of coffee shops/chains that Cape Town has to offer. Ambience is chill, decor is modern, spacious interiors, great barista, quick service.

I would recommend this place mainly for coffee as I didnt find the food options appealing enough.

Name: Motherland Coffee St Georges Mall
Address: Mandela Rhodes Place, Wale St & Georges Mall, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Benita Evans
Review: Great vibe in the mother city. Always a good place to grab a delicious coffee and croissant. You can sit and work or just relax while enjoying great coffee. Centrally located and very convenient for people working in the area.

Name: Motherland Coffee St Georges Mall
Address: Mandela Rhodes Place, Wale St & Georges Mall, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Amanda Weilenmann
Review: The farmers daughter and peanut butter smoothie are both very good. The peanut butter smoothie almost tasted like a liquid candy bar, but I'm not complaining. The food is also very good here. We enjoyed a breakfast wrap with avocado instead of bacon. It was fine but the humus toast was the winner - really fresh and delicious.

Name: Motherland Coffee St Georges Mall
Address: Mandela Rhodes Place, Wale St & Georges Mall, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Roger Mullens
Review: Nice place for coffee in the centre of Cape Town. They have a good arrange of breakfast and lunch items as well. Both indoor and outdoor seating are available  also interesting to know is that this is the gathering point for Free Walking atours through Cape Town, so it's perfect to grab a drink or bite before you start exploring the town.

Name: Motherland Coffee St Georges Mall
Address: Mandela Rhodes Place, Wale St & Georges Mall, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Gerhard Grundling
Review: By far the best place to have breakfast/brunch in the mother city. Great food, very quick service and the best atmosphere by far! Always a stop to make when visiting.

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Karen Schellhase
Review: Very nice atmosphere; surrounded by beautiful green plants this place is a little oasis in the city. Nice selection of coffees and teas. There is a nice, charming shop where you can buy local products.

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Kerusha Naidoo
Review:
Needed a quick lunch before going into the planetarium.
Service was amazing and our food arrived in minutes. I would love to thank them for this as we were in a bit of a rush. It truly meant alot to us.

I definitely recommend the almond croissant and also the cheese and pesto toasted sandwich. Everything was so buttery, it literally melts in your mouth.

The cafe overlooks the gardens where you can watch the beautiful birds and cute little squirrels as you enjoy your meal.

Would definitely recommend this place ❤️

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Konsti | Brandl Nutrition
Review:
Wow! Stunning place in the peaceful company gardens. The Cafe uses local products. The Cheesecake is amazing, the best i ever had! There is a beautiful garden where they harvest their own vegetables.

All in all a beautiful place to enjoy your time with friends or a good book ☺️

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: David Webster
Review:
We enjoyed one of the tea tasting of the local teas. The owner of the shop gave the class and lots of local history, background on how it was started, and herbal knowledge facts. Background included how she ended up with the tea cups we all drank out of. She even brought of a VOK plate. Great presentation and knowledge.
The tea was served with biscotti. Dunking the biscotti in the tea and listening to the stories. Made for a great time by all.
Of course by the end, you have to talk next door to small store and buy some of the delicious tea and trinkets.

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Mikha'il Hathey
Review:
Coffee at the Vine is also known as the Smallest Coffee Shop in Cape Town and this is definitely a coffee shop you should go check out.

I had the Carrot Cake and Iced Coffee and I would definitely recommend you try this out.

Service is excellent as you are at all times taken care of by the manager. The Restaurant is set in the small historical courtyard in Heritage Square.

If you go on the First Thursday of the month you will be delighted by the vibe as this is known as First Thursday’s so there tends to be bands playing live music in the courtyard at it just gives this place an awesome atmosphere.

Name: The Daily Coffee Café Burgundy
Address: Shop 37 Burgundy Square Shopping Centre, Cnr. Burgundy Drive and Tygervalley Road, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Kady De Villiers
Review: Love coming here for a quick coffee break. The drinks here are to die for. They have a wide variety to choose from and I cant wait to go back and try something new.

Name: The Daily Coffee Café Burgundy
Address: Shop 37 Burgundy Square Shopping Centre, Cnr. Burgundy Drive and Tygervalley Road, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: MJ
Review: Lovely cafe with outdoor seating that serves an all-day breakfast. A wide variety of options on the menu with good coffee. The tin cup is highly recommended. The coffee freezo is my go-to on a hot day. They serve a delicious homemade cookie to accompany the coffees so much so that we buy a bag of these cookies everytime we visit. They have a kiddies menu as well. Shout out to Emma who has served us a few times over the last 3 - 4 visits. We noted how much we loved Tabasco and were disappointed that the cafe didn't have it as an option. A week later we visited and what do you know, a bottle of Tabasco sauce was brought to the table! How's that for great service! Highly recommend a visit here.

Name: The Daily Coffee Café Burgundy
Address: Shop 37 Burgundy Square Shopping Centre, Cnr. Burgundy Drive and Tygervalley Road, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Michael De Wet
Review:
Really cool coffee shop, neat and affordable.

They really put a effort into the aesthetics of the place which makes it so cosy.

There is outside & inside seating available. The is also a work bench for those that need to respond to a quick mail as well.

Name: The Daily Coffee Café Burgundy
Address: Shop 37 Burgundy Square Shopping Centre, Cnr. Burgundy Drive and Tygervalley Road, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Anton Stoltz
Review: Best coffee in Town!! Great relaxed atmosphere. Tsitsi our waitres was very friendly & helped us so nicely.  We didn't eat anything but the table next to us got different cakes. It looked lovely & they really seemed to enjoy it. I've visited other 'The Daily Coffee Café's' and their food has been fantastic every time.

Name: The Daily Coffee Café Burgundy
Address: Shop 37 Burgundy Square Shopping Centre, Cnr. Burgundy Drive and Tygervalley Road, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Benn Dudley
Review:
Very nice food options. Very filling and tastful. Milkshakes are made with syrup not cacua, but are still decent.
They also have seating with wall plugs to charge during shedding🔌

Name: El Greeyo Coffee Roastery
Address: 9, Brickfield Park, 18 Brickfield Rd, Salt River, Cape Town
Author: Feyaza Khan
Review:
I’ve been to this place twice in two weeks, which I never do. It’s brilliant. They serve halal food and they’re the loveliest Cuban family running it. I’ve always talked about not being able to find good coffee here except for Mockha coffee in Durban, but this coffee is brilliant. The owner roasts and grinds it on premises, so the second time I came in the aroma wafted over with a lovely bit of white noise that my daughter loves, especially as she’s autistic. My husband described the food and feeling here as: “it feels like being overseas”.
And it does! It reminds me of our last trip to Barcelona where I found this random dive that served the most fresh and delicious prawn tacos.

And here it’s the same,  because everything is so fresh and tastes. It’s just so good and it’s in a totally random place in Salt River next to a linen store!

Also look at the prices on the menu - you could eat here everyday and be fine. Also, they have free Wi-Fi and a good co-working space upstairs. And since it seems most of their custom is delivery-based, it’s lovely and quiet when you sit in!

***Please have the iced coffee, it is insanely good.

Name: El Greeyo Coffee Roastery
Address: 9, Brickfield Park, 18 Brickfield Rd, Salt River, Cape Town
Author: Cayley Cammell
Review: The most delicious coffee in the area! I really enjoy bringing my laptop and working upstairs with a view of the mountain. Nice vibe! Plus it's dog friendly which is amazing

Name: El Greeyo Coffee Roastery
Address: 9, Brickfield Park, 18 Brickfield Rd, Salt River, Cape Town
Author: Mansour Al-Essa
Review:
It is a coffee with food serving
but the coffee is below expectation also the breakfast was below expectation
the coffee is not crafted well as long as the eggs is normally cooked with rigid toast

Name: El Greeyo Coffee Roastery
Address: 9, Brickfield Park, 18 Brickfield Rd, Salt River, Cape Town
Author: Joseph Souza
Review: Cuban coffee and music, most reasonable prices in the area, quick and courteous service. nice and simple café.

Name: El Greeyo Coffee Roastery
Address: 9, Brickfield Park, 18 Brickfield Rd, Salt River, Cape Town
Author: Kirsten Manuel
Review: Well well well, super rustic Cuba little cafe vibe when you you sit down and then.... You order! We just took something light but it was delicious. With the Cuban music flowing and the aromatic coffee fragrances hanging in the air, they do sometimes forget about you☺️😜 but all in all, wonderful spot

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Robyn-Leigh Daniels
Review:
The restaurant's setup was far from ideal. There was construction happening right behind our table, and the tables were so close together that it was impossible not to overhear other diners' conversations. While the food came quickly and was very nice (the best part), the service and atmosphere were uncomfortable, to say the least.

Once our food arrived, no one checked on us. We wanted to get a takeaway box and the bill, but it took 20 minutes to get any assistance. During this time, waiters passed by, even knocking into us without an apology. When we finally received the takeaway box, it was broken, but we didn't even bother asking for another one.

The service staff seemed most cheerful when we were leaving. The overall experience was disappointing, with certain undertones in the environment that made the visit feel unwelcoming.

I would rather go to the one in Muizenberg, a much better atmosphere.

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Waxed Personalised Beauty
Review: I’ve been searching for months for a coffee shop to work from, meet my clients or just pop in for a quick cappuccino. Hans and Lloyd in Newlands has ticked all the boxes for me and has become my ultimate favourite. They are central making it easy to travel to and from the southern suburbs. Their breakfast dishes are an absolute must and they serve a nice warm cappuccino for the coffee lovers. Also if you’re feeling for a ginger shot they got you covered. I’ve visited over two days to make sure I wasn’t dreaming! Definitely will be back 😁

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Hendrickse
Review:
A really nice venue with inside and outside seating. The food was pricey for the quantity however still very tasty. The service was unfortunately very slow and poor. We had to leave our table to find a waiter on more than one occasion after waiting a while and not having any check in on us. We had seats right at the back.
There is street parking as well as a small paid parking area (see times on the boards)

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Suzanne Everaerts
Review: We go here often and it’s a lovely restaurant with great food.

Name: The Hans & Lloyd Coffee Co | Newlands
Address: 28 Kildare Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Hein De Vries
Review:
Hans & Lloyd's feels like snuggling into the cozy embrace of a vintage coffee sack--if hessian weren't itchy,  and premium coffee beans were cotton balls. They serve a rich, full-bodied brew that is high on my list of yummiest coffees in Cape Town.

I can't comment on the entire menu, but if all of it is as good as Lloyd's Vegetarian Breakfast, you can't go wrong. The chunky mushrooms were incredible.

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Amanda Lan
Review: The best bubble tea. Perfectly balanced in flavour. This was the first bubble tea shop. Sorry guys this has the authentic recipe and flavour. I will definitely be returning.

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: CHRISTINA RUWONA
Review:
I recently visited  your Waterfront shop and my experience was nothing short of delightful. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted by a calming and cozy atmosphere and Lovely Team
The variety of teas available at this shop is impressive. They offer a wide range of choices, from classic black and green teas to unique  blends and seasonal specialties. I particularly enjoyed your Taro Time
I would like to Say Thank you to Beaulah and Team
Keep up the work

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Fayhoon Faisal
Review: Boba place in V&A waterfront 😚 has a nice variety of options 👀 The mango drink and milk tea are great 😆❤️ Tapioca pearls are always perfect so soft and squishy 😁

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Jutika Krongmongkolkul
Review:
went there almost everyday during my stay in cape town. good location inside v&a shopping mall.
reasonable price and the staff made as per order correctly.
no seats available there as it was only for buying to-go.

Name: Royal Tea V&A Waterfront
Address: Level one, V&A Waterfront, Victoria Wharf, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Taahirah Chafeker
Review: I've been going to Royal Tea most Sunday's where Alfred or Mervielle serves us. They have made the place so welcoming and inviting. I look forward to conversing with them on Sunday's. They are helpful and make your day brighter, even during winter months. Keep up the good work and the warmth!

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Chelsea Zive
Review: I’ve loved every moment here, definitely the best susu although I recommend they change their confusing slogan. Live loved by others, live loving others. This eliminates confusion and is more sensible which enhances the experience in this institute.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Yasmin Rehder
Review: Love stopping by after work to grab a bubble tea. My fav brand/franchise regarding taste and flavor of bubble tea. They also have another branch in Canal Walk that I visit. Love the brown sugar milk tea 🧋 Definitely worth paying an extra R5 for a large size. Absolutely delicious and highly recommended.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Samuel Christov
Review: Ordered off uber eats and my lemon honey tea with strawberry pops. I found a floating black foreign object in the still sealed drink container. I have sent a message of complaint where someone promptly responded. I received a message where they stated that they will be in touch to get involved in the issue. It's been a while now and no one has sent any kind of contact. Hence the review.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Jessica
Review: The drink was refreshing and not too sweet just the way I wanted. They have a lot of flavors to choose from. Glad I tried it.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Waterkant
Address: 72 Waterkant St, Green Point, Cape Town
Author: Jutika Krongmongkolkul
Review: nice and colorful decorations. staffs were friendly. had a few menus of their bubble tea such as classic bubble milk tea, taro milk tea, strawberry slush and all tasted great!

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Rozana Kamish
Review:
Royal Tea bubble tea shop sells refreshing cold tea with a difference, new to Cape Town as I have had it in Taiwan many years ago. Flavors and ingredient selection caters for all.
This tea shop is situated in Cavendish Square, inside The Egg store, a trendy business that sells various companies products, with clothing and health products and trendy food stores.
I tried a large matcha tea with bubbles and it was good. I would recommend honey in it.
I took the large size as I wanted the reusable cup that came with it, so cool. So much to drink. The strawberry and pine bubble tea was also refreshing. Loved loved loved the service. Those guys working their are friendly and happy to assist if you struggling to decide what to order. Next time I'm trying Taro. Had it before at another store, still a favorite.

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Ondela Mgumane
Review: I had a fantastic first bubble tea experience at this store , the staff was welcoming and they know their products very well and recommended me to try the oreo dairy delight .wow amazing

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Faizel Manuels
Review: Overall this would be the best service and smelling bubble tea we've had so far. Wish I knew the guy who helped us. He added to the experience by being an amazing employee. Sometimes you just need some kindness in the way of service to make you spend more money at a place. Thanks for that hire

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Kelly du Raan
Review: I had the most amazing Juice there called “All Inclusive” and it was delicious. The oreo boba drink is also amazing. Definitely recommend trying some drinks out here.

Name: Royal Tea Cavendish Square
Address: Shop F8 & F9, WE ARE EGG, Floor, Cavendish Square, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: SHANAAZ PRINS
Review: The service is really good and super friendly. The drinks are made right in front of you, which is really interesting to watch. One of my top recommendations for sure.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Sultan
Review: A kiosk located inside of Sports Scene store in Canal Walk. A variety of items on the menu and the staff was eager to assist with recommendations. The original thai milk with brown sugar boba was delicious.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Fazlyn Petersen
Review: My kids have been nagging for bubble tea so I finally went to get some. The shop is inside Sportscene. The staff were friendly and polite. The caramel milk tea was very sweet though. If you order on Uber eats you can select how much sugar. Next time I'll ask them to make it 50%. My daughter enjoyed her chocolate slushie.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Jenny D
Review: Last time I was here, was told they don't do fresh milk latte. But it was quickly corrected.  This time they tell me they don't have fresh milk in stock, while we are in a mall with 4 grocery stores who sells milk, there's milk j  the fridge in store! I don't want to drink that palm oil podwer, so I guess I'm not ever coming back here

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Naidoo
Review:
Strangest location. It’s inside Sportscene but that have really good bubble tea and such a nice variety

I had the green apple tea with mango balls which was supper yummy. The passion fruit ice tea with mango balls was my favorite. I am yet to try their milk tea selection.

The staff is friendly and the service is fast.

Name: SUSU Bubble Tea Canal Walk
Address: Shop 407A, Century Blvd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Siobhan
Review:
MoMo is located inside Sportscene it is not a standalone store.

They seem to be quite popular as there was a large group of people waiting for their orders when I got there, and I waited around 15-20 minutes for my order. But it was definitely worth the wait.
You need to have a rough idea of what it is that you would like to order, as the guy who takes your order is not very friendly or helpful. Even after expressing that it was my first time visiting the store.

It would also be great if they had pictures on their menu, it would make the ordering process so much easier.

All in all, still worth it. The bubble tea was great, I ordered two different flavours (a milky tea and a fruity one) and I thoroughly enjoyed both.

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Siobhan S
Review:
had the most amazing bubble tea experience at Bubble Up Boutique Bubble! The owner made some fantastic recommendations, and every sip was delightful. If you’re a bubble tea lover, this place is a must-visit. 🍹✨

#BubbleTeaLover #Durbanville #HiddenGems #BubbleUpBoutiqueBubble

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Rakkooni USA
Review: Calling all geeks, nerds, weebs, muggles and even everyone in between! This is the pit stop for you! The selection of bubble tea is HUGE! I love their caffeine-free rooibos options. They have all the classics as well as the ability to mix all kinds of exotic varieties. Talk about a hole in the wall! I have to go here every time I am remotely near the area, lol. Amazing quality!

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Lisa Roberts
Review: A favourite after-school treat! Lovely staff and super owner. Taro and tapioca bubble tea is amazing 🧋💜

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Donald Stockhall
Review:
Great little place with friendly staff.
Just a note to the owners to consider, if you open at 09:00 for business on a Monday, then ensure you have all your ingredients ready for people arriving at 09:00 to order.
Last week we couldn’t get what we came for because the tapioca wasn’t cooked yet.
Today we arrived at 14:00 and again couldn’t get what we came for because there was no tapioca available. It’s really disappointing.
They still get five stars because their product is really good and they really are super friendly. Go give them a try.

Name: Bubble Up Boutique Bubble Tea
Address: 3 Pampoenkraal Ln, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Lizelle De Bruin
Review: The bubble tea was delicious as a treat. I had Rooibos with Blood orange and Turkish delight

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Larry Wayne Cupido
Review: This was one our favorite places to get sushi. It was always really good and prepared well. The last two times I ordered it was not as expected at all. The first time it was bad I thought okay might just be a isolated incident. Possibly the sushi guy was not there and someone had to stand in but now again... this is not the standard that attracted me to buy.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Thato Daumas
Review:
The place is okay but the furniture could be updated. The decor is very simple with some Chinese objects like fans and such.

I ordered the passion fruit and lemon 🍋 bubble tea. It was tasty and flavourful. I didn't eat anything because of my dietary restrictions.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Joleen Witbooi
Review: Ordered platter E for supper last night. Tasty and fresh sushi, would have liked the rice to be slightly softer but overall happy with the sushi. Platter includes chicken and veg spring rolls which were very nice. Will be back to try the rest of their menu.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Jacques louw
Review: Ordered sushi here a few times before. Was always good. Yesterday’s was not the same. Ordered platter E. Portion sizes were a lot smaller and not all the pieces were included as per the menu. Lady also dismissed this when asking about the missing pieces. Would not recommend.

Name: Cape Sun Sushi Asian Cuisine Bubble Tea
Address: The New National Hotel Building ZA, Voortrekker Rd, Parow East, Cape Town
Author: Maojing Zhang
Review: Amazing food!love it 😍 I love sushi and so does my five-year-old daughter! This is a good choice🥰

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Shakira Ontong
Review: Not what I was expecting. The area is a bit dodge and the interior needs a bit of work. Great place for young people to get together to enjoy hooka and milkshakes. Otherwise, the food was delicious, we had the steak pizza(a bit spicy) and chicken Tikka masala. Portions were enough for two people to share if you are not that hungry and prices are reasonable. Will definitely go back again but will sit at the non smoking section(didn't know about it until we left) next time as the smoke was a bit too much for me...

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Taufeeq Laattoe
Review: Prior to Covid I frequented Arabian nights weekly , however now not often at all . Decided to visit for a pipe and milkshake and it was not disappointing at all. For me they still have the best pipes always smoking so smooth . Their milkshakes are still top notch . Was great to be back for a chill session with friends .

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Shaheeda Crombie
Review: From what I could see it looks like a typical shisha spot. You pay for your hookah. they have a menu. my daughter had her 18th birthday there but I didnt stay. just checked the place out and left. by 1st glance it looked pretty decent.

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Mogamat Razien Boyes
Review: Boba Pearls are nice and sweet. Great texture and quality. I had the bar one flavour. Flavour is good. I enjoyed my boba tea. I will definitely recommend! Its better than driving all the way to Canal walk or Kenilworth. The random printed cartoon characters on the lid is a good gesture. Support Local 😀

Name: Bobalogy
Address: 105 Kromboom Rd, Rondebosch East, Cape Town
Author: Noel Adams
Review: Place was nice and cosy. The place does need some TLC renovations though but was a lovely experience. Food was Devine and hooka pipes was not bad, had a broad range of flavours I never even knew existed.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Ieremia Oliver
Review: Absolutely horrible service, I’ve had the same bad experiences with one of the lady’s at the front counter she’s incredibly unprofessional and rude. I asked for my boba sugar free and she stares at me like I just spoke some foreign language, I repeat myself and she doesn’t say anything and when I got my boba it wasn’t even sugar free! I’ve gone a couple times and every time it’s a bad experience. BEN you need to train or staff better or just get better staff because I certainly won’t be coming back anytime soon.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Rachel Gomes
Review:
I love love love the way you guys make boba tea! You can actually taste the matcha and not watery as the other places that make boba tea. I like how the tapioca balls are always fresh and you guys never disappoint.
Big ups to the lady's that take pride in their work and thank you so much Aneeqah! You are a star ✨

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Liz “Toxic_Mercy” Marinus
Review: Great customer service, vibes was great all of them were happy Cheerful and laughing all round. I got myself a matcha milk tea. It was very delicious but don't recommend if you dont like grass tasting drinks. Would 100% recommend the restaurant exp was great.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: Najuwah
Review: Second time going here. Amazing bubble tea. The staff is friendly and the service is quick. Small seating area would've been nice if there were more space to sit. The kids love the experience of bubble tea, so bring the whole family. The kiosk is clean and a bit hidden if you are not aware. I say they should move up to the food court where there's more space and seating. Definitely recommend trying

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Blue Route Mall
Address: Shop 53A, Blue Route Mall, okai Rd, Tokai, Cape Town
Author: C De Jager
Review: I’m a bubble tea connoisseur and their brown sugar bliss was really good! The boba was very fresh. It would be nice to see the staff wearing gloves for sanitary reasons because it appears to be a very hands-on process and with winter coming a lot of people are sick. I asked for my milk tea to be cold as I saw Ben’s Bubble Tea now offers both hot and cold and the staff member I was talking to was very confused as to why I was specifying cold and insisted that they only serve cold. Would appreciate a friendlier approach from staff but otherwise really nice milk tea :)

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Sabrina Roberts
Review:
The “Tareo” IS AMAZING! I fully recommend it! It’s a 10/10 drink for R58 (reasonably priced compared to other boba tea shops) with chewy, soft and fresh tapioca that was a delight. The drink as a whole was creamy and sweet, yet not overly so, and there were enough sweet, biscuity Oreo crumbs to make every every gulp a pleasure.

For R54, I also tried the Iced Tea and Lemonade drink with cherry popping boba. It was bitter-sweet and very refreshing… but I didn’t like the somewhat artificial-tasting cherry boba. I’d recommend another boba favour. Overall it had a 7/10 from me.

The lady working there is genuine and patient, very smiley. There is seating outside and it is lovely and cool, and wasn’t busy at all at 3pm when we went there. I WILL be back for more of their Signature milk tea drinks!

It’s located on the upper floor of the mall, on the outside veranda on your right as you ascend the elevator. Was a bit tricky to find so I hope this helps someone.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Kayla J
Review: The Mango and Passion Fruit Bubble Tea was very refreshing and tasty. I doesn't taste like the fake syrup most places use but the fruity tea taste that makes it so great!

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: William H
Review: Came a few minutes after they had closed but they opened the door for us and let us order🥹 Owners were super kind and even complimented my shoes! The drinks itself are pretty good too for their price. Would definitely recommend.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Mysterious Gem
Review:
Staff were friendly, place smelt like Boba and was clean.
There is a store closer to me but the service there is terrible. Was worth the drive

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Rondebosch
Address: Shop 35a, 95 Main Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: David Hicks

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Dewald Reinke
Review: Susu offers a wide variety of bubble teas that can definitely be overwhelming for first-timers. Luckily, they have recommendations for their top sellers and even a fun spin-the-wheel option to try something new. I tried their Bubble Tea Latte Macha which was freshly made and tasty. Although I loved the shop's style and the wide variety of options on the menu, the staff could be a bit friendlier. Overall, if you're craving quality bubble tea, give Susu a visit. They're easy to find in the Tygervalley Shopping Centre food court.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: lamise inglis
Review: Such a cute little stop for bubble tea! A huge range of options and they are yummy. They have vacuum sealed tops which prevent spills. There is a very cute photo booth with a light to snap a memorable pic or two. Prices are not too bad either.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Kylie Ndagijimana
Review: Staff was very friendly and the boba tea was amazing and reasonably priced. Would definitely visit again!

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Tasneem Patrick
Review: Visited the Tygervalley branch. Friendly-ish staff but I had to walk up to the counter to be served. They didn't call out for service like they did with the few people that went before me. Lady that called out order out upon completion wasn't very friendly. The bubble tea was GOOD though. You can watch how they prepare everything infront of you. No seats available as they're right across the food court that have ample space. They have 2 or 3 different settings available for you to take pics for your social media though. Medium R45, large priced at R50. A large selection of flavours available.

Name: Susu Tygervalley
Address: LL 094, Tygervalley Centre, Willie Van Schoor, Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review: Went there on 4 November 2023. Good service, a variety of flavours. They're affordable and have cute social mefia stands. I'd recommend them.

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Stephan Maritz
Review:
We ordered a chocolate and a match bubble tea, and flavours were perfect.

Many of the menu items weren't available, and drinks weren't cooled but that's what loadshedding is doing to businesses.

I'll be back again for sure!

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Chantel Mlilwana
Review:
I love the way you listen to my kids and exceptional 💕.!!!!!
You guys really know your customers

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: That _weirdio
Review: We ordered a mango and peach blast and they were amazing. Cold, refreshing and the bobba added a great texture. The services was kind and answered all my stupid questions. I loved this restaurant and I would go again

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Anpan
Review:
Huge sizes, taste amazing
Best taro milk tea ever tasted🤞🏻

Name: Kings bubbletea
Address: Unit 2C, Falcon Crest, Tyger waterfront, Tyger Falls Blvd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Elzaan Liebenberg
Review: Been there on 4 different occasions and always closed

Name: Susu bubble tea Cavendish
Address: 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Fayhoon Faisal
Review:
Lovely branch of Susu 💐
You can find it near dischem on lower floor👀 Bargain books is also next to it
Highly recommend Mango Tornado !! best drink 😋

Name: Susu bubble tea Cavendish
Address: 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Aisha Walls
Review: The Boba is delicious and high quality, though I will say they are a bit stingy on the amount of tapioca they give. Other than that it's an overall good experience.

Name: Susu bubble tea Cavendish
Address: 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Yusraa M

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Basheerah Schroeder
Review: Ben’s bubble tea is a cute small store. They have delicious gourmet flavors and the usuals. Personally the strawberry and chocolate tastes like nesquik ( which tastes really good ) and their gourmet flavors are quite nice too such as the Biscoff ( jusy very sweet) but the best one is the chocnado. Would defs recommend if you’re a boba lover however I must say they always take quite long to make your order even when not as busy so place your order and go in a few other shops then come back if you’re in a hurry

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Raeesah
Review:
Best bubble tea I've had in a while 😋
I had the watermelon and strawberry fresh fruit tea with extra pineapple jelly . It was really refreshing and flavourful 🤩 I highly recommend trying their tea.

And a big plus is that they are fully halaal 👍🏼

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Crisscross Applesauce
Review: Was my first time experiencing Boba tea. Coffee flavor Boba tea was nice. A bit expensive considering I've never had Boba. I've mentioned to staff it was my first time that I've heard about Boba, and I ordered what I though was Boba tea, ended up paying R18 for a desert item (the description is very hard to read as it is small), but I would have expected staff to inform me of what it was considering that I have mentioned that I never had Boba tea.  After a while of sitting and had a look through the menu, my mom told me that she doesn't think it was tea that I've ordered. Eventually I ordered the boba tea. But furthermore, flavor was good.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: dragoneyes1ct
Review: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City. Quaint little store with lovely staff. I had the Brown Sugar Classic Bubble Tea. Having had Bubble Tea in China and Japan and in various locations internationally, in SA this is the closest thing to the original I have had. Delicious. Highly recommend

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea N1 City Mall
Address: Shop 69a, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Serie Ross
Review: The Boba was delicious and you can pick the sweetness of your boba the staff are nice which gives it a great atmosphere.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review:
Went there on the morning of 11 February 2024. It's a small shop so once you order you have to stand outside if it gets busy. The shop looks cute. Note that the Summer Series sign outside is just a sign and they don't offer those flavours (I think it was a Christmas special).

We ordered the Chocolate Slush with grass jelly and the Japanese green tea slush with peach pops. It was deliciously refreshing in the heat. They charge extra for toppings like tapioka pearls. The prices are reasonable.

The wait is questionable though. They weren't busy when we ordered.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: chane bezuidenhout
Review:
I love Susu bubble tea (usually) and have visited Susu Waterkant many times. My absolute favorite is the jasmine milk tea with brown sugar boaba. I was so excited when a Susu opened up closer to us. We've ordered bubble tea twice from here ( Bothasig). Both times the tea was so weak. It was basically like drinking watery milk. First time I got 50% sugar, and it was barely sweet. Second time I got 75% sugar, it tasted exactly like the 1st one. I tasted my boyfriends bubble tea with also 75% sugar, this was very very sweet. Clearly not the same 75%.. It's been very disappointing so far.

Cant even compare this tea to the other Susu.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Trinity Bisschoff
Review: We come here ALOT and my usual experiences here are amazing with lovely ladies and they make the atmosphere so welcoming and pleasant, but today, the 12th of august, at 17:30 there was one guy that was working there and he served with absolute 0% passion or energy he wasn’t very welcoming and he waited so long to say hello to us we had to eventually say hello. And when I got my boba the top of the lid was creased and it was almost like he didn’t care…

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Andisiwe Wu
Review:
Tonight I was craving for Boba Tea and we didn't want to drive out far to Canal Walk or any other places. So luckily for Google Maps we were directed to Susu Bothasig.

We ordered our usuals Brown sugar Matcha with Tapioca and Custard, second drink Slushy Apple with Lychee Pop and Pineapple Coconut Jelly like we do at CW, but I've noticed a slight change in texture, it's not as thick as in milky as it should be, was a bit more watery. However the taste and recipe is still exactly the same, I must say Bothasig prepares their Tapioca better than CW! Dense, soft, sweet and Chewy!

Staff are super bubbly and helpful deciding a third drink for my difficult mother for not having a sweet tongue, but they definitely nailed the expectations! My mother finished the drink! Mango Jasmine Tea with tapioca and grass jelly, most importantly, *No sugar, Less sweetness*

Not too bad for a new "pop up" shop, will definitely be back!

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Bothasig
Address: Tafelberg St, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Kim-Louise Rousseau - Personal Gmail
Review: Friendly service and great bubble tea. And it's nice to have a boba shop in the northern suburbs. Atmosphere is hard to comment on since it is a tiny stall more than it is a shop.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea
Address: Arum Rd, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Cindy D. Calvert

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Mogammad Faisal Baulackey
Review: Really nice vibey store. Difficult to find. They are up the stairs, through the woolworths, right, then left. Amazing place to take pictures.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: lluwellen king
Review: Funky interior.. great drink

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Marvin Ngobeni
Review: Great tasting bubble tea with perfect blend of flavours and sweetness

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Ayesha **
Review: One of my daughters favs

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Camps Bay
Address: Victoria Rd, Camps Bay, Cape Town
Author: Tatenda Schaafsma

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Sultan
Review: Kiosk located towards the entrance of the mall.  A variety of items on the menu and the staff were eager to assist with recommendations. The original thai milk with brown sugar boba was delicious.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Liv G
Review:
This was our first experience with the Ben's Bubble Tea brand and it was incredibly disappointing.

The staff seemed annoyed that we were there (around 5 PM, so nowhere near closing) and didn't greet. While my group of 4 looked at the menu and I'd informed the person that took eventually took our order that we were still deciding (we were the only customers) she kept pressing to take the order leaving us feeling rushed and pressured.

We placed our orders for 4 of the flavours and received warmer than room temperature beverages (the milk had been left on the counter) that were more akin to a warm drinks that had gotten cold as opposed to something pleasant and refreshing.

The beverage topped with caramelised sugar had a melted cup that leaked from the moment it was handed to us. The server could have opted to tell us this but said nothing, and this resulted in a significant portion spilling out of the cup before we'd even reached the parking lot.

From the shoddy service, a melted plastic cup and drinks served at an unappetising temperature, this wasn't the experience we were hoping for at all.

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Naeemah
Review:
This is my favourite bubble tea store! The fruity drinks are so refreshing and the brown sugar bliss is perfectly sweet and decadent.

The staff is incredibly friendly and it's always such a pleasure seeing them. They are attentive to taking my order, patient with explaining the different drinks and prepares my order perfectly.

Thank you to the KC staff for always providing great service :) See you very soon!

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Sian
Review: Amazing bubble tea and mochi!!🧋😍 my fave is the Brown Sugar Bliss (25%) and the Taro mochi! I order often from UberEats or go there when I'm in KC and it always tastes great!

Name: Ben's Bubble Tea Kenilworth Centre
Address: Kenilworth Centre, Kenilworth, Cape Town
Author: Ayesha Patel
Review: The 1st time we tried bens bubble tea the experience was great . And the other times as well. The reason for my 3 stars is one of your staff. She really needs training. My 2 girls bought them boba(11, 6) mind you they always buy themselves at other boba stores and don't get ignored like they got from your staff. They waited for there drinks until my husband realized 2 other customers got there drinks and my babies still standing there waiting on there drinks  they other one comes with a attitude making there drinks... On another occasion we went and the same cashier blatantly ignored us. We walked away then she looks at us. I'll attach a picture of the same lady with and extreme bad attitude. I won't ever visit this koisk again.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Reyaan Boltman
Review: This is a must try experience. Friendly staff. Beautiful Atmosphere. They have games on the tables like cards, jenga and naughts and crosses. The Bubble Tea is amazing and they have many flavors to choose from. I had passion fruit with mango.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: mehraaj hassan
Review: Been there twice but not the best Boba tea . Received order very fast like under 2min each. I suggest they take their time to make the Boba tea cos clearly making it that fast leaves it almost tasteless. decor is nice but atmosphere a bit boring.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: veronica allen wildeman
Review: Loved all the refresher drinks with boba bubbles! Beautiful setting. Atmosphere needs a bit of uplifting. Service is fast and efficient. Must still try the milk tea drinks.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: imohoosain
Review: I drove all the way there with my kids because the trading times online says they are open, only to arrive and discover they were closed. Me and my kids left feeling very disappointed. Please make sure your trading hours are updated online to avoid inconveniencing clients.

Name: Bobalicious cpt
Address: 350 Imam Haron Rd, Lansdowne, Cape Town
Author: Maliqah Williams
Review: Disappointed that it’s the third time coming to the place and it being close . Please change your times / correct them it’s always wrong and people are left disappointed.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Mustaqeem Abrahams
Review: Honestly my first worse experience ever. I’ve bought boba tea a lot by this place for a long time now and today my wife and I decided to buy again. My wife ordered a chocolate milk tea with strawberry boba and I ordered a taro tea with pudding boba and honestly the worst I’ve ever had. My wife’s strawberry boba wasn’t even boba balls and my taro boba was like slime it was like snot in my mouth. Both her and I were very disappointed and did not finish it at all, we both took literally one sip and I spat mine immediately out, it was honestly disgusting  and was such a waste of money spent so much for such a waste.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Fadeelah Kenny
Review: Love the selection of fresh ingredients but their execution leaves much to be desired. When I ordered there was no one ahead of me but my poke bowl still took quite a while to make. I asked them twice to add less mayo than usual but it still came drenched in mayo and the soy sauce dressing. Spoilt a perfectly good poke bowl with so much dressing IMHO.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Atomic-Chicken
Review:
So I tried Taro Milk Boba Tea with Mango Bubbles for the first time today & I was pleasantly surprised! 😁 I added some sugar and ice to this tastebud sensation, but you have the option to leave it out. You can customise your drink as you wish, which I think is super awesome. ☑️☑️☑️
My 3 star rating is aimed at the staff who honestly didn't seem very interested in their job. I asked for a suggestion and I might as well have asked for the world! They are NOT professional and definitely NOT friendly. 👎
I will go in search of another TeAwesome and try some of their other drinks on their menu. This one will never see me again.
you will find them in the food court area.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Samuel Christov
Review: The service was quick and the Drinks that we ordered where fantastic. I love all the different flavors that where available as well as the fact that they also let us choose our own ingredients to make the drink feel like our own custom beverage.

Name: TeAwesome
Address: Canal Walk, 490 Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Jasmine Wong
Review: Amazing that they have bubble tea in Cape Town. They have all the classic flavours including taro, chocolate and coffee. The milk teas are the best options. Love that you are able to customized options like: sugar, ice and add ins. If you know, you know you have to add tapioca balls. Wish they were fresher though.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Fayhoon Faisal
Review:
Love this branch of Susu 😍🩷 The only one opening up to a very late time 🙌🏻
Thanks a lot 💐keep up the good work🫡

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Herman
Review: Susu is such a great addition to the Adelphi Galleria. My go-to is the standard milk tea with pearls. Zero sugar is needed because the pearls are quite sweet. I wish they had a Matcha milk tea option with pearls on their menu. It would be great if they traded longer hours over a weekend.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Stephen Frank
Review:
1st time at this establishment. What a huge DISAPPOINTMENT 😞. Service was appalling, the lady had absolutely no interest in helping or assisting.
We were the only customers and she ignored us for about 4 minutes continuing with menial tasks instead of assisting us.

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Farrid Chiwoniso
Review: 1st time at this establishment. What a huge DISAPPOINTMENT 😞.  Service was appalling,  the lady had no interest in helping or assisting. Ordered Drinking "YOGURT" But got the most watery Drink. Nothing about the YOGURT was YOGURT,  just plain tea/ flavored water. Asked the lady if that was indeed YOGURT and she just showing any interest or anything said yes. NOT ON 👎🏾

Name: Susu Bubble Tea Sea Point
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Chrismonia Voko
Review: I had my favourite treat of all time. Tasty Taro Milk tea 🧋 Vegan option and 30% sugar. Thank you Susu Bubble tea Sea Point for the best service.

Name: J&T Coffee Shop
Address: 36 Wale St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Caitlyn Ganesh
Review: The tomato soup was delicious and perfect for the cold Cape Town weather. The restaurant was packed on this particular Saturday evening, expected for Observatory though, but it took around 20mins or so just for my drinks order, and at least I got a knee blanket to embrace the cold. Will definitely return on my next trip to Cape Town and hopefully it will be warmer🙌🏽

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Lyndsey Petro
Review:
Always considered this more of a café, but was pleasantly surprised by the variety and yumminess of the menu.

Great spot for meetings and 'working from home'.

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Jain Halder
Review: The food was heartwarmingly good. Their selection of drinks ranged from a wide variety. The place is a little crowded but very warm and cozy thanks to their fire place. Definitely recommend coming here but only if it's not crowded.

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ziggy Ezulu
Review:
This definitely makes the Grade in the Cosy Chic Cafe category.
Beautiful interior with picturesque bar and functional wood-burning fireplace in the corner.
Tables by the window with the outside view- the best for just chilling and watching life pass by.
Went there in the evening and the ambiance was perfect - relaxed, good muted music, the buzz of conversation.
Friendly staff and good service.
Can definitely recommend this 😉👍

Name: Cocoa Cha Chi
Address: Shop 20, Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ntando Sithole
Review: A great place to work and eat delicious food. The food is also affordable, the atmosphere were just right and the staff was very friendly. For the students, I highly recommend it for study dates. It's close to the UCT health science faculty and is a walking distance from Obz Sqaure. Enjoy!

Name: Chai Tea Room
Address: Shop 28 at Eden on the bay shopping cntr Cnr Otto Du Plessis, Sir David Baird Dr, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Ilse Van Der Poel
Review: Absolutely delightful little place.. A variety of teas and coffee as well as baked delicacies. The staff is nothing but beautiful friendly demeanor, etiquette is lovely, customer care and witty to say the least. Super relaxing atmosphere.. Definitely worth having a lovely cup of something 🙂

Name: Chai Tea Room
Address: Shop 28 at Eden on the bay shopping cntr Cnr Otto Du Plessis, Sir David Baird Dr, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Aasiyah Chafekar
Review: Loved the service, and the cakes. Ordered a muffin with my coffee. Friend had the cheesecake with the hibiscus green tea.

Name: Chai Tea Room
Address: Shop 28 at Eden on the bay shopping cntr Cnr Otto Du Plessis, Sir David Baird Dr, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Stacy Vermuelan
Review: Ordered 2 take away brownies a hot chocolate and a lemon meringue. Brownies were absolutely delicious but would definitely recommend someone checks the lemon meringue. It was extremely lemony and was not pleasant at all. Overall lovely service!

Name: Chai Tea Room
Address: Shop 28 at Eden on the bay shopping cntr Cnr Otto Du Plessis, Sir David Baird Dr, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Luke Duffield
Review:
Disappointing. I stopped in on Sunday around 2pm and ordered a double Bells on the rocks. The waiter only informed me when it came time to pay that the price on the menu had gone up just over 60%.
It's unprofessional. Wait staff should inform customers of any price change on the menu upon ordering. I will not be going back.

Name: Chai Tea Room
Address: Shop 28 at Eden on the bay shopping cntr Cnr Otto Du Plessis, Sir David Baird Dr, Bloubergstrand, Cape Town
Author: Andrea P
Review:
BEST & MOST BEAUTIFUL TEA ROOM! & yes they make great coffee too.
The most delicious treats and croissants. You are in good hands!

Name: The Dairy Den - Sea Point
Address: 148 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Meheshree Pillay
Review: We stopped here on our way to the beach. Cute little place, clean and hygienic and seating is available. We had the Caramel kitty dip and Belgian dark chocolate dip in charcoal sugar cone. This is our usual ice cream stop so we were satisfied and happy.

Name: The Dairy Den - Sea Point
Address: 148 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: johan jansen van rensburg
Review:
My third time here.Love the ice cream and love the dips and when I am not worrying about any calories I will return for their bubble waffle decadence.Truly a ice cream
Lovers haven.Welcome to my world!!

Name: The Dairy Den - Sea Point
Address: 148 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Evidence Sello Magaga
Review: A friendly stuff with a really welcoming smile and beautiful face. Tasty selection of doughnuts and ice creams and everything between! The space is quite small so don’t expect to lick and dine. It could be level up with a bigger outdoor space to enjoy your sweet treats but I get the idea - you’re meant to buy and leave. If you’re lucky and it’s not so busy you can find a chair and a table. Despite that, I’m definitely coming back!

Name: The Dairy Den - Sea Point
Address: 148 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Crystell Jean-Charles
Review:
Drove out at 8pm for ice cream and this place definitely did not disappoint us. They had some really amazing flavor combinations. There is something for everyone. They also serve coffee and some interesting donuts.

I was really impressed that they had a plant based selection too. Definitely a must visit.

Name: The Dairy Den - Sea Point
Address: 148 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Mohamed Khalid Khan
Review:
Imagine a quaint ice cream shop nestled on a charming busy street. The moment you step inside, a symphony of delightful aromas envelops you. The interior is adorned with pastel hues and whimsical decor, creating an atmosphere that transports you to a sweet wonderland. The shop's large windows allow natural light to dance upon gleaming countertops, showcasing an array of enticing ice cream flavors.

One standout flavor is the unicorn cone, boasting a rich and creamy texture that melts on your tongue. Each spoonful is a journey through a fragrant orchard, where the essence of vanilla pods embraces your senses. As you savor this indulgence, you'll discover subtle undertones of caramel that add a delightful complexity to the experience.

The ice cream is served in artisanal waffle cones, freshly made and wafting a buttery aroma. The shop's staff, adorned in retro uniforms, skillfully craft each cone with precision and care. The ambiance is heightened by the gentle hum of happy conversations and the occasional chime of a brass bell as the door swings open.

In this picturesque setting, enjoying a scoop of this exquisite ice cream becomes a sensory delight, blending taste and atmosphere into a memorable experience that lingers in your memory like a sweet dream.

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Mitch
Review:
Excellent tea shop! This is a proper tea shop that steeps based on temperature and time. The sencha fukuyu was very nice.
The atmosphere is excellent, tons of tea accessories to purchase. And a massive list of loose leaf tea to go.
I will definitely be back!

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Amanda Rinquest
Review:
Quiet and unassuming little tea shop with a huge selection of teas to purchase, and a few things other vegetarian things to buy.

It can be overwhelming but the staff help you decide based on what you feel like snacking on. Really worth a visit!

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Anastasia Coulbanis
Review: What a different way to spend a morning! Tea tasting! Extremely knowledgeable, amazing service an such a peaceful atmosphere. Definitely going back!

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Nazeema Khan
Review: Great little spot to relax and enjoy a selection of any tea you can imagine  - think black, white, green, fruits - and some options for coffee lovers. Their cakes are absolutely divine (with vegan options), and even though they have a limited food menu, it is well worth it. The shop has a variety of goods and utensils for sale. Service is above expectations. Do yourself a favor and experience a little bit of magic and relaxation.

Name: Nigiro Teacafe
Address: Nigiro Teacafe, 71 Gabriel Rd, Plumstead, Cape Town
Author: Brian Claassen
Review:
This Teacafe is an absolute gem. I received the warmest greeting and the owner actually remembered me even though I had only been in once before, many weeks prior.

Their tea selection is simple awe inspiring and extremely well priced. I had the omelette which was delicious and its tea pairing.

My girlfriend has insisted that I bring her along on my next visit, which will hopefully be very soon.

Customer service at its absolute best!!

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: K Bourn
Review: Had a slice of their Cheese cake. Anyone reading this review that's a cheese cake fan - THIS IS HANDS DOWN THE BEST YOU WILL EVER ENJOY! I highly recommend you try it. Words can't describe it. Definitely the new #1 ranked on my 'best cheese cake list'.

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Charlton Bryce Reimers
Review:
This place is great! I love it, BUT today there was a guy on his phone, watching reels at FULL VOLUME.
People keep getting up from their tables and the staff had not asked him to be respectful to others by lowering his volume or using earphones. This is unacceptable on so many social levels.  As I wrote this I had noise-cancelling headphones on and I could still hear him.

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Fraser Hewson
Review:
Just a bit underwhelmed. No parking other than pay parking which is not great if youbare getting something to go. R10 for 10 mins. Not the fault of Nood, but something to take into consideration.
Also, coffee cup for Cappuccino was not filled full. Maybe this is picky, but at R34, I'd like a full cup.

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Chanelle Samaai
Review: Such a vibe. We always return because of the most friendly people ever. The food is absolutely to die for. We always have the Eggs Benedict. It really is the BEST one in Cape Town, if not SA. Thanks for the most awesome vibes ever. ✨️ 💞

Name: Cafe Nood
Address: Quadrant Square. Shop, 3 Wilderness Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Matthew Fridjhon
Review: Easily the best service, I have ever had. Incredibly good waiters and kind and positive staff all around. Delicious and simple food with an encapsulating and relaxing atmosphere. Highly recommend as just an experience to receive high quality service from high quality people.

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Billy du Plessis
Review:
Came for lunch and had starters and mains—absolutely delicious! We shared three dishes of dim sum for starters, and everything was fresh, homemade, and delicious. The atmosphere was unmatched as we had a beautiful scenic lunch in the garden. Not a fancy place, but very pretty and homey all the same. The owner was very friendly and chatty, knowledgeable, and the service was very fast.

Onto mains, we ordered ramen and Peking duck. The duck was not on the menu and seemed like a secret gem—all you have to do is ask for it.

The pork ramen was super flavorful, the meat succulent and delicious. It had all the right ingredients, and the noodles were out of this world.

The Peking duck could be from Beijing, that's how enjoyable it was, with good sides and the meat was super nice as well.

They had homemade rooibos ice tea that was flawless.

Overall, a superb meal with great atmosphere

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Tahreef Fransch
Review:
I recently visited this gem in Hout bay with a group of friends. The experience was incredibly memorable in a calm and serene environment even when they were quite busy.

Stand out moments of the day would definitely be the staff hospitality and humour, food quality as well as the environment.

The staff has an incredible knowledge on the food accompanied by their humour and ability to create a connection with the group. By the end of the experience you would likely spend your time either laughing or speechless because of how good the food is.

I ended up trying the orange chicken which left me speechless with how fresh the ingredients were, how tender the chicken was and how refreshing the meal was as a whole.

I also ended up trying some of the chicken chow mein. The portion size and the dishes taste had me jealous of my friend’s insight of the menu, wishing I had ordered another dish to take home.

They also offer ceramic painting with quite a variety of colours and unique objects for your imagination. The environment and atmosphere created a more inviting environment without the hustle and bustle that I’ve found at similar experiences. Leading me to put this on the top of my list.

Lastly to end off we tried a portion of their ‘bow ties’. A delicious and light pastry coated with in a layer of sugar syrup to end off the meal  on a really high note.

Overall this was a stand out experience with an increasingly set of staff and the food quality to match. I would definitely recommend it to anyone visiting the area.

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: alba mir mora
Review: Order the soup dumplings and the braised pork with rice by Uber. To begin with in my life I had tried a drier and tasteless dumpling, it barely had any filling and it didn't have any soup either, so very bad. But the worst part is when I open my rice I realize that it stinks of cinnamon, which I am allergic to, it is not specified anywhere, it is not like the photo and besides, I have never ordered pork in an Asian restaurant and it has come with those species, then I called the restaurant to see if there is something we can do and they tell me no problem, we can make a change, I should order something else and they will come to take the pork and bring me the new dish, I wait for 1 hour to bring me the food, when suddenly the manager calls me with a condescending tone to tell me that my food is on the way but that I will have to pay for it, I explain to her that that is not what we had agreed on, that I am not going to pay two times, on top of that they are not cheap prices... nothing, that is not the case, that the food is already served and that I am going to have to pay... I have told her that there is no way I pay twice, so nothing here I'm left without eating... and with a dish that stinks and could have killed me.

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Emma Foster
Review:
Our waiter created such an amazing experience for us, we are not familiar with the cuisine and they guided us through the menu and gave great recommendations.

The food was great quality, super flavourful and fresh!
They also offer vegan options.

They are dog friendly in the outdoor seating area, which is amazing for dog owners.
I would really recommend this to anyone looking to have a good experience with flavourful Asian cuisine.

Name: Yesca's Teahouse
Address: 35 Main Rd, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Violet Jade
Review:
Good food but I won’t go back. Honestly I’m getting tired of Asian restaurants like this reaping off of people’s ignorance and not knowing where Asian grocery stores are. I thought the baos and dumplings would be hand made.. but it’s the same ones I buy and steam at home.

R65 for a bit of white rabbits? It’s the same thing for a big bag…

Name: Milky Lane
Address: 128 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Jonathan Cochrane
Review:
Waffles 1st time round were raw. Manager had attitude asking how raw...?
Music so loud we couldn't hear each other talk. Asked them to turn it down, they did for one song then extra louder. Songs were nice though.
Horrible eating with wooden knives and forks. Not very eco-friendly.
Still waiting for you to contact me....

Name: Milky Lane
Address: 128 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Muse&Alchemy
Review: An ice cream cake was ordered from Milky Lane Seapoint for a 9th birthday party, September,  2024. We ordered the crunchy ice cream cake. If you'd like bowls filled with melted ice cream, a disappointed birthday girl and complaints from everyone, including adults, then, by all means, order the Milky Lane ice cream cake. It was disgusting. Insipid banana and washed out chocolate flavoured ice cream was how my daughter ended her party. Thanks Milky Lane, you suck! Milky Lane has fallen from what it used to be.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: 128 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Lisa
Review: It gets very crowded at this place and there is very limited seatings.. The staff aren't very friendly and need more customer service training. The ice cream was nice and sweet though but the atmosphere was not very nice.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: 128 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Zunaid Isaacs
Review:
Myself and my family went to milky lane in sea point and the shop was very dirty ,when my wife told they lady at the till she did not acknowledge the uncleanliness of the shop. My granddaughter ordered a waffle and a soda and when we received it the waffle (which we have had many times before from milky lane) looked absolutely nothing like it should have and when she addressed it with the lady she insisted that the waffle was like that. When we decided to taste the waffle it proved to be bland and the chocolate mousse was more like sludge and was not edible at all. When we received our soda it was not what we ordered either. We then decided to give the food and the soda to a lady on the side of the road ,milky lane refused to let the lady sit in the restaurant and told her to leave , I do not understand how the restaurant is worried about a lady on the side of the road coming in and sitting there (when we bought her the food) when their shop was so dirty.  We then got her a take away and apologized to her.
The customer service and quality of the food was really not good at all. The lady we dealt with had a very bad attitude.
Not a good experience at all.☹️
And the only reason I gave them one star is because Google maps requires you to otherwise I wouldn't have even bothered with a star at all.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: 128 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Faizel Manuels
Review: I had a banana waffle which is not something new on the menu. Old favourites will always do. It wasn't crispy but okay. I had a oreo pudding one of the newer options.
