It was so hot I couldn't touch the glass, no warning that it's going to burn my hands or special holder, the tube glass it came in was too long as the ice cream doesn't mix well with the dense cake at the bottom and I ended up taking ice cream from the waffle to add in order to get it down. The bottom of the cake was burnt and the entire thing wasn't sweet or tasty. They didn't have the ice cream sandwich option either, I assume it was a stock issue. Overall it wasn't a great experience because I had to use a waffle to get down a bad tasting pudding lol I even use the waffle syrups to change the taste of the pudding. Strawberry worked :)

Name: Mango Ginger
Address: 27 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Lynn M
Review: Lovely, tasty lunch yesterday at Mango Ginger. The deal soup was divine and the wheat free chicken pie soo tasty. We also bought some sweetcorn, coconut and lemongrass soup for dinner and a ciabatta ....perfect for the cold last night. Yummy, yummy all around!!

Name: Mango Ginger
Address: 27 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Jain Halder
Review: I was served by the kindest lady who was so polite and hospitable. Absolutely loved their selection of drinks. She catered to my preferences and made me a new drink! It's such a calming place to wind down after a hectic day. Would definitely recommend!

Name: Mango Ginger
Address: 27 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Alexandra Cloete
Review:
We had some delicious breakfast options at Mango Ginger. The atmosphere is cozy and the decor makes me feel like I'm having lunch at my granny's house (in the best way possible).

The owner is sweet and attentive. I felt taken care of as a customer and I will definitely be back.

Name: Mango Ginger
Address: 27 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Colleen Hillman
Review: This is my favorite and best breakfast place in Cape Town! The food is incredibly delicious and the owner is the nicest woman! My husband used to go there when he lived in Cape Town 10 years ago and took me while we were on our honeymoon. We went three times in the four days we were there. It would have been all four but it is closed Sundays! I can’t say enough good things about Mango Ginger, it’s just the best all around, don’t miss it!!!

Name: Mango Ginger
Address: 27 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Herbalife - Rochelle De Swardt
Review: Best breakfast omlettes i have ever tasted. Warm friendly service. Most friendliest cosy venue. An absolute must to visit when in Cape Town. Mouth watering frozen meals for the on the go busy lifestyle that wants to eat a healthy good meal. Menu is excellent. My Birthday cake was an absolute hit my friends in Port Elizabeth also had pieces of my heavenly cake and they all cannot talk enought how devine it was. Thank you Fiona xxxx

Name: Gold Leaf
Address: Shop 16emporium center ZA, Sandown Rd, Parklands, Cape Town
Author: Pink White
Review: Ordered sweet and sour pork, chicken fried rice. Absolutely horrible. Waited for more than hour for the food and they were not busy. When we got home no sauce with sweet and sour pork, dry pork and tasted nothing like it should have. Chicken fried rice was tough and smelled and tasted off. Extremely disappointed. Waste of money. Wouldn’t recommend

Name: Gold Leaf
Address: Shop 16emporium center ZA, Sandown Rd, Parklands, Cape Town
Author: Shannon Jaques
Review: Ordered their sushi platter 5. The sushi was micro made a lot smaller than normal, I assume to save money. Very disappointing, used to order from them a lot and the quality and portion size has gone down significantly.

Name: Gold Leaf
Address: Shop 16emporium center ZA, Sandown Rd, Parklands, Cape Town
Author: Monz Thomas
Review:
DO NOT ORDER SUSHI!!!!
Worst sushi I've ever ordered. Salmon roses were so tiny, the 'platter' was more of a snack.  Platter on the menu said it came with Cucumber roses, but none were received. When asked why there were no Cucumber roses, was informed that they are no longer being made. This was not mentioned when the order was placed.
Ordered Crab and cheese springrolls as well which were thoroughly undercooked.

Name: Gold Leaf
Address: Shop 16emporium center ZA, Sandown Rd, Parklands, Cape Town
Author: Lynda Sparke
Review: One of my worst meals ever. Ordered the peppered the steak with noodles and the spicy beef and rice. Could not eat any of the meals both meals were old and disgusting. Would never eat another meal from this restaurant again

Name: Gold Leaf
Address: Shop 16emporium center ZA, Sandown Rd, Parklands, Cape Town
Author: Devon Loubser
Review:
I bought the Sweet and Sour Chicken, Beef Pad Thai and Tempura Prawn.

The sweet and sour chicken was very dry and came with an inadequate amount of sweet and sour sauce the dish was very doughy as the batter used was on the thicker side, the included vegetables are there only for decoration which is rather sad..

The Beef Pad Thai was okay not the worst and not the greatest..

The Tempura Prawn was also on the dry side and not enough sauce provided..

Name: Ying Tao Asia Xpress
Address: GrandWest Casino, 41, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Ruyterwacht, Cape Town
Author: tiffany desmore
Review: I RARELY rate 5 stars but the food and service here was amazing! The hot was actually hot! My lips went numb. The beef was so tender and I loved the chunky veg! I highly rec the caramel milk tea!

Name: Ying Tao Asia Xpress
Address: GrandWest Casino, 41, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Ruyterwacht, Cape Town
Author: Conlan Nagy
Review: Came at lunch time and ordered the chili cashew nut meal. The noodles were awesome, the people who work there were also friendly! Coming back for the noodles again soon!

Name: Ying Tao Asia Xpress
Address: GrandWest Casino, 41, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Ruyterwacht, Cape Town
Author: Mornique Mellem
Review: I had Boba tea. It was by far the worst I've had and I've tried many. It tastes like they took cheap Cylon tea and steeped it in cold water. The Tapioca was also a bit undercooked and then they added so much ice, it kept watering it down further.

Name: Ying Tao Asia Xpress
Address: GrandWest Casino, 41, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Ruyterwacht, Cape Town
Author: Devison Kamanga
Review:
I had the Beef Phuket Noodles (Mild).

Unfortunately I had to rush through this meal because I thought I was late to watch Kevin Hart. 😅 This meal is meant to be savoured and enjoyed. Each forkful bursts with flavour, heat and amazing sensation that leaves you wanting more until the very last bite.

I really wish they served this meal in a noodles box instead of a styrofoam packet and gave me noodles instead of a plastic fork. But otherwise that didn't take anything away from the taste, only the experience. I wish they served soju with this, it would be a very nice pairing.

And the speed with which this was ready was beyond what I expected. Great stuff guys! 👏🏾

Name: Ying Tao Asia Xpress
Address: GrandWest Casino, 41, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Ruyterwacht, Cape Town
Author: Corné Kotzee
Review: What an awesome experience! Me and my friends were amazed by all the boba tea and milk tea options I even had. 4 . And their dumplings were so good . I recommend this place to everybody

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Ludwig Otto
Review: Strong wifi, not loud at all very nice space to work remotely,  with great coffee and very tasty food. The owner is very hands on. The barista was on point with his cappuccino. Ample parking in a quiet neighborhood.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Ava Peattie
Review:
Service was slow, waitress forgot my drinks order. Portion size is amazing, but quality of food could be of a higher standard. I ordered the butternut, feta, avo, cucumber sandwich on rye, bit bland nothing special, but great combination. The chai freezer was really great and refreashing.

Will definatly give this resturant another go if Im in the area!

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Dima Zamisa
Review: Wonderful Cafe for your meeting with friends and family outing.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: A W
Review:
Wonderful, strong, unpretentious coffee at great prices. Visit on a Sunday morning to have the place all to yourself.

Feb 2020 update: Flat white is still R25 and good! R39 Rise and Shine breakfast is good value too if you're peckish but not ravenous.

Name: Social Bean Cafe
Address: 20 Station Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Khanyi Zulu
Review: It's a nice local café. The service is very warm ,but the muffins we got came in plastic packaging. Would've been nice to have them fresh or served heated.

Name: Simply Asia Pinelands
Address: Howard Centre, Forest Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: D L
Review: This is why your chef should use a hair net and not just a white cap. We saw that she didn't have a hair net while we waited for our take out. Lucky me, I got it in my 301. Gross. Really gross!

Name: Simply Asia Pinelands
Address: Howard Centre, Forest Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Taufeeq Laattoe
Review:
My friends and I had a late lunch here. We had sushi , Pad Thai , 514 and some dumplings. We were very disappointed with the Pad Thai , beef tasted stale and the dish seemed somewhat flavourless aside from the nut flavour one could taste.

The dumplings were decent. We all agreed it was nice and the sushi was decent as well. A nice portion and fresh salmon used .

Overall not a such a great experience.

Name: Simply Asia Pinelands
Address: Howard Centre, Forest Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: PRIYA PARBHOO
Review:
We went on Tuesday 10th September 2024. The food was great - many vegan/veg options.

However, we were disappointed that there was only one waitress (Ayanda). She was lovely, kind and patient but was overworked and managing the entire store alone. She was running between serving customers who were dining in, those who were ordering takeaways, dealing with kitchen staff and answering telephone calls. The manager on duty kept leaving the store (not sure what he was so busy doing) and did not seem bothered that after 30min of being seated in the restaurant, other customers had not been served  or attended to. One disgruntled customer walked out after complaining to Ayanda. PRIORITY WAS GIVEN MAINLY TO TAKEOUTS /UBER EATS / MR D, while customers in the restaurant waited forever for service.

The kitchen staff were so noisy and completely destroyed the ambience/atmosphere of the restaurant - compounded by the manager shouting orders to them from the main entrance.

Name: Simply Asia Pinelands
Address: Howard Centre, Forest Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: murielll
Review:
I ordered to take out.
Friendly staff, had to wait about 10 minutes for my order to be done.
I had the chili noodles number 514 with chicken and medium spicy.
The portion is a good amount, I could make 2 plates of noodles from the package once at home.
As a side I had the spring rolls with chicken. Which come with a sweet sauce on the side.

I would definitely order again and recommend this place!

Name: Simply Asia Pinelands
Address: Howard Centre, Forest Dr, Pinelands, Cape Town
Author: Faizel Manuels
Review: I ordered two of the same meals just with different noodles. The one had alot of chicken. The other not so much. Then I had a bone pierce my roof of my mouth. The one was drenched in liquid The other dry. Weirdest experience ever. Must say it was a painfully bad experience.

Name: Tiger's Milk Kloof Street
Address: 55 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Ron “Ronxrated” Petersen
Review: This place is amazing. Great food, the most energetic and friendly staff. The security even offered to tie my shoelaces. The vibe is great. Yiu guys deserve 10 stars

Name: Tiger's Milk Kloof Street
Address: 55 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Asive Asiphe
Review: I had an exceptional experience at the place being served by Sipho and the manager was very friendly, thanks Phelo. The ambiance of the place was amazing including the vibe.

Name: Tiger's Milk Kloof Street
Address: 55 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Justin Groenewald
Review: Food was good and they still had a Monday special in burgers and wine of the house, was worth a visit

Name: Tiger's Milk Kloof Street
Address: 55 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Lwandile Vara
Review: Great service from Phelo and  Nkosinathi we really had a great time.The place was very welcoming cool music nice place to chill at..Tigers will always be the best🔥

Name: Tiger's Milk Kloof Street
Address: 55 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Es* Louw
Review: We had to fall in here due to very poor service at a nearby restaurant.  We were very lucky  to get seated here.  Tigers Milk were extremely busy, and the service was very good.  The pizza's and burgers were terrific.

Name: Simply Asia Plattekloof
Address: Plattekloof Village Shopping Centre, Plattekloof Rd, Plattekloof, Cape Town
Author: Tracy-Lee Segal
Review: I ordered take out. When I opened the package the soup container fell apart and everything spilled. I then called the store and spoke to Apiwe he rudely brushed me off and said I should complain to the delivery partner. This was not an issue with the delivery partner but an issue with the quality of the packaging. The standard of this store has really dropped In The past few months.

Name: Simply Asia Plattekloof
Address: Plattekloof Village Shopping Centre, Plattekloof Rd, Plattekloof, Cape Town
Author: Karen Garner
Review: I tried the new 566 and it was delicious! This is my favourite place to meet up with a friend over good food 😋

Name: Simply Asia Plattekloof
Address: Plattekloof Village Shopping Centre, Plattekloof Rd, Plattekloof, Cape Town
Author: Wandi Lamprecht
Review: New menu meals are great! This simply Asia is one of the best ones!

Name: Simply Asia Plattekloof
Address: Plattekloof Village Shopping Centre, Plattekloof Rd, Plattekloof, Cape Town
Author: marelize tarr
Review: Great food and service, close to home…

Name: Simply Asia Plattekloof
Address: Plattekloof Village Shopping Centre, Plattekloof Rd, Plattekloof, Cape Town
Author: Uziel Romero
Review:
All I can say is the service is amazing! As soon as I entered I felt the calming atmosphere.  Felt at ease and everyone had a smile they know the meaning of teamwork. ติดตามการทำงานที่ดี  means keep up the good work .
Thank you all

Name: Kalok Sushi & Chinese Take Aways
Address: Verdi Blvd, Sonstraal Heights, Cape Town
Author: Michael Minnaar
Review:
What an hidden Gem, was greeted by staff when we walked in, staff member explained the menu and recommendations for the buy one get one free special.
The Bubble Tea was such a nice refreshing drink to have while waiting for the food which took 20min to prepare.
The Salmon Platter was amazing all the ingredients were fresh and portion size was perfect for a couple.
The Chinese food was warm and very tasteful with loads of flavour.
The hand roll was made with fresh rice.
The most impressive part of the order was the fresh ginger and fresh wasabi.
Packaging was extremely firm nothing fell apart or opened as we drove home.
Would recommend this restaurant to anyone who loves Chinese food and sushi.

Name: Kalok Sushi & Chinese Take Aways
Address: Verdi Blvd, Sonstraal Heights, Cape Town
Author: Morné de Kock
Review:
I recently ordered from Kalok,  and I was thoroughly impressed with the entire experience! As someone who enjoys quality sushi on the go, this place really delivers.

Ordering Experience:
The ordering process was smooth and easy. The menu was clear, with plenty of delicious options to choose from. My order was prepared quickly, and everything was ready for pickup right on time.

Packaging:
The packaging was top-notch. Each item was carefully packed, ensuring that nothing was squished or out of place. The presentation was impressive, especially for takeaway—everything looked just as beautiful as it would in a dine-in setting.

Food Quality:
The sushi was absolutely delicious! The fish was incredibly fresh, and each piece was expertly crafted. The rolls were flavorful and satisfying, with just the right amount of rice. I especially loved the Sashimi, which had a perfect balance of flavors. Even the wasabi and pickled ginger were of high quality, showing attention to detail.

Value:
For the quality of sushi you’re getting, the prices are very reasonable. They normally have two for one Specials. It’s great to know there’s a place that offers premium sushi without breaking the bank. The portions were generous, leaving me full and happy.

Overall:
Kalok is now my go-to spot for takeaway sushi. Whether you're in the mood for a quick lunch or a convenient dinner option, this place has you covered. I can't recommend it enough!

Name: Kalok Sushi & Chinese Take Aways
Address: Verdi Blvd, Sonstraal Heights, Cape Town
Author: Lelani Pelser
Review:
If I could give the staff a 10 out of 5 I would. I dropped my sushi by accident, before I could even pick the packet up one of the ladies was next to me insisting they remake my order. Never have I ever experienced that type of service from a takeaway shop.
Food was good. Will definitely be back!!!

Name: Kalok Sushi & Chinese Take Aways
Address: Verdi Blvd, Sonstraal Heights, Cape Town
Author: Justine Geffen
Review:
I went in to see their Buy 1 Get 1 special and was impressed that it applied to their sashimi, roses, specialist rolls etc. I ended up with six pieces of salmon sashimi, 16 tuna fashion sandwiches, and four salmon nigiri for just over R200. It was fresh, tasty, and good quality.

Really impressed and I'll be back.

Name: Kalok Sushi & Chinese Take Aways
Address: Verdi Blvd, Sonstraal Heights, Cape Town
Author: James W
Review: This was my first time and guaranteed last time. The food was terrible. Our prawn spring rolls tasted off as though the food expired. The pork chow mein lacked ANY flavor and was oily on top of that. We threw most of it away due to the poor quality. I'm not usually this harsh on a review, but the quality was just so bad. I wont go again.

Name: Simply Asia Paddocks
Address: Shop 21, The Paddocks Shopping Centre, Racecourse Rd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Clementine Moffett
Review: I've been here a few times, and had a similar experience each time. The service is extremely slow. It took over 40min for them to get my food ready, now I could understand if they were busy, but there was only 1 family in the restaurant that already had their food and maybe 2 Mr D drivers came to collect food... their portion sizes are also unreasonably small. In all my life, I've never had such small spring rolls and sushi. For the price I paid it was not worth it. I cannot even complain that their sushi was mostly rice, because there was very little rice. All served to me on a chipped plate. And the Boba ice tea that I got was mostly ice and the tea tasted like water because of how diluted it was. I will rather go to ocean basket next time if I want sushi.

Name: Simply Asia Paddocks
Address: Shop 21, The Paddocks Shopping Centre, Racecourse Rd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: LISA LOWTH
Review: We have eaten a lot of Sushi from Simply Asia (all over South Africa) as the quality is consistently good. However the sushi at The Paddock branch in Milnerton managed to exceeded all expectations today. Their sushi was exceptional, super fresh and simply delicious. This branch is definitely a jewel in the Simply Asia franchise crown 👑

Name: Simply Asia Paddocks
Address: Shop 21, The Paddocks Shopping Centre, Racecourse Rd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Samantha Jacobson
Review:
I had the most incredible sushi!! As Always!! It didn’t last long enough for me to take a photo of it! But love the Salmon Rainbow sushi, it’s the bomb!!
The soup was delicious 🤩

Name: Simply Asia Paddocks
Address: Shop 21, The Paddocks Shopping Centre, Racecourse Rd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: ROBLEY AUTO
Review: This simply Asia has the best tasting food in the country! Love going there! Would recommend it to anyone

Name: Simply Asia Paddocks
Address: Shop 21, The Paddocks Shopping Centre, Racecourse Rd, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Michelle Toua
Review: Super friendly and accommodating staff. We live in Plattekloof but drive past that branch to get our take aways from the Paddocks branch. Their food portions and quality is just so much better. Thank you team. Keep it up. 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟 🌟

Name: Simply Asia Glengarry
Address: Shop 01, Glengarry Shopping Centre, De Bron Rd, Brackenfell North, Cape Town
Author: Elize Greeff
Review: We ordered through Mr Delivery Simply Asian, Glen Garry. 1 x beef, 2 prawns with rice noodles. We asked standard. There is zero note of Chilli in these meals on mr Delivery's menu. We however received food that burnt our mouths - definitely not standard. We tried speaking to Simply Asia - no help.  We drove to the shop and got the manager - who let us stand more than a hour - still no assistance. He said we must speak to Mr D but fact is Mr D just deliver. If I wan not so annoyed I might have laughed. So there will be zero orders from our side ever again.

Name: Simply Asia Glengarry
Address: Shop 01, Glengarry Shopping Centre, De Bron Rd, Brackenfell North, Cape Town
Author: Jared Koning
Review: This is the worst Simply Asia. I went on Sunday, 8th September. The toilets were disgusting, smelling like poop with no air freshener. I thought the food was good, but I was wrong—I have been vomiting all day from food undercooked.

Name: Simply Asia Glengarry
Address: Shop 01, Glengarry Shopping Centre, De Bron Rd, Brackenfell North, Cape Town
Author: Andries Geldenhuys
Review: Had the new 565 Chicken, which was soooo aromatic and tasty! Will definitely have it again.

Name: Simply Asia Glengarry
Address: Shop 01, Glengarry Shopping Centre, De Bron Rd, Brackenfell North, Cape Town
Author: Reuben Kisten
Review: Pathetic service from this branch. Placed an order online at 19:05pm. It is now 20:43pm and the order is nowhere near ready. Absolutely appalled by this level of service. Will never order from this branch ever again and will be informing those around me about my experience.

Name: Simply Asia Glengarry
Address: Shop 01, Glengarry Shopping Centre, De Bron Rd, Brackenfell North, Cape Town
Author: SUKAWA Kim
Review:
Best simply Asia that I ever been to.
This place makes me happy.
I came to eat with my family and they were so kind.
Food 10 out of 10
Service 10 out of 10
Mood 10 out of 10

Name: Tjing Tjing House (Rooftop Bar, Torii Restaurant, Momiji Restaurant & Lounge)
Address: 165 Longmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Khiara Govender
Review:
This place is a vibe!

Let’s start with the staff—they’re beyond awesome! Super friendly, welcoming, and always up for a chat, they really make you feel like you’re part of the Tjing Tjing family.

And the food? AMAZING. Every dish is a flavor bomb that leaves you wanting more. Whether you’re trying something new or sticking to a favorite, you’re in for a treat. The creativity and quality in every bite are just off the charts.

Name: Tjing Tjing House (Rooftop Bar, Torii Restaurant, Momiji Restaurant & Lounge)
Address: 165 Longmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Vman Vendetta
Review:
It's seldom I experience a place with food of this calibre and an atmosphere that accentuates the experience to such an extent that I would highly recommend to anyone to take their significant others or friends to try their amazing Japanese cuisine.

The staff are wonderful and a friendly chap by the name of Luke gave us such wonderful service that we will be going back very soon for more.

This is one of the best spots in Capetown hands down because it offers three different floors with different atmospheres and cuisine that will take your pallets on a magic carpet ride.

I recall walking to the rest room and catching a glimpse of a very clean kitchen with hard working staff that have no trouble taking a quick second to indulge my compliments whilst handling a full house of patrons.

Thank you Tjing Thing you never disappoint and my partner and I cherish this place to celebrate our achievements.

Name: Tjing Tjing House (Rooftop Bar, Torii Restaurant, Momiji Restaurant & Lounge)
Address: 165 Longmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Willem Van Ryswyck
Review: The food is absolutely delicious, no doubt about that. While it is definitely on the pricey side, for the specific food cuisine offered, it is worth it. We ordered a bunch of starters which were filling enough that we didn’t feel the need for mains, and the drinks were great! There are also plenty of vegetarian options which is always appreciated. Can recommend the bao buns and rice balls.

Name: Tjing Tjing House (Rooftop Bar, Torii Restaurant, Momiji Restaurant & Lounge)
Address: 165 Longmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Jo V
Review: Nice vibe, we went on a Saturday evening and it was not full yet so found a nice table. We enjoyed our ramen, but not the best we’ve had. I loved their non-alcoholic cocktails and their cheesecake was also good, but not the authentic Japanese style you would expect. I will be back to try more dishes!

Name: Tjing Tjing House (Rooftop Bar, Torii Restaurant, Momiji Restaurant & Lounge)
Address: 165 Longmarket St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Heidi van Zyl
Review: Our cocktails were debatable! We couldn't drink them. But the food was good overall.

Name: Bubble & Swirl - Dessert Bar
Address: 51 Belgravia Rd, Athlone, Cape Town
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Akiya Sushi Axis
Address: ZA Cape Town Milnerton Shop 4 Axis Building 9, 9 Bridgeway, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Rene Carelse
Review: Lovely restaurant with an extensive menu. They offer sushi, Asian curries, hot pots, tofu steaks,  soups, salads, sake, and Asian cocktails, too.The coconut red curry was cooked to perfection. It comes with a side of rice or noodles and will fill you up. The hot pot is big enough for 2 people to share and is a popular dish here. The desserts had enough sweetness and flavour to satisfy us wholeheartedly. Service was really good too, the waiters know the menu well and offer knowledgeable recommendations. The owner is friendly and welcoming to all guests. Overall, we had a super experience and loved all meals.

Name: Akiya Sushi Axis
Address: ZA Cape Town Milnerton Shop 4 Axis Building 9, 9 Bridgeway, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Bronwin Losper
Review: Great atmosphere and service, from the moment we walked in. Place looks amazing with a cozy and natural feel. Waiters were on point with service, going the extra mile in explaining menu items. Sushi was nice and fresh and the overall great experience with the hotpot. Will definitely recommend and visit again.

Name: Akiya Sushi Axis
Address: ZA Cape Town Milnerton Shop 4 Axis Building 9, 9 Bridgeway, Century City, Cape Town
Author: VickiB
Review: Loved the experience here. We ordered the Japanese hot pot after being recommended by our server. She was spot on with it. Absolutely loved it. And the experience was amazing. Our service received was top notch. Definitely returning for more!

Name: Akiya Sushi Axis
Address: ZA Cape Town Milnerton Shop 4 Axis Building 9, 9 Bridgeway, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Lauren
Review: Beautiful interior and friendly staff. Highly reccommend the Tokyo Jazz Milk Tea. The portions are generous and the menu is extensive. The beef was slightly tough and chewy, but we will definitely be back!

Name: Akiya Sushi Axis
Address: ZA Cape Town Milnerton Shop 4 Axis Building 9, 9 Bridgeway, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Andrew
Review:
Went here today for the very first time and it was an awesome experience. The staff were very helpfull and the restuarant is very clean.

We had the Japanese hotpot and Miso Ramen and both were amazing. The portion size for what you pay is very good.

Definitely a must if you want proper and authentic Japanese cuisine.

Keep up the hight standards. I will definitely be coming back soon!

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Ashley Booysen
Review: Was my 1st time beiing here, staff was very friendly I must say including the owner was very hands on. We came for the Dillion Olifant comedy nite, wow wot a vibe it was and Dillion was so funny I almost pulled a stomach muscle.  I had the Nelson burger with chips , everything was freshly made and pricing is very good if not too good. Thanks for a wonderfull nite indeed.

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Gary Simpson
Review: A real little gem of a coffee shop, really embodies what Obs is, genuine places that really go the extra mile to provide good service and a good product. Has a relaxed feeling to it, friendly staff and good coffee. They have power during loadshedding and offer a decent selection of food, pictures of the menu included with this review.

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Adam Steed
Review: Ground culture is an awesome spot!! Such lekker vibes and good food. I’ve been to a few comedy nights there and it is always a good time! Highly recommend 🤩

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Kyle Waterston
Review: Such a lovely, rustic and authentic coffee shop in Obs! ☕️. Definitely worth a visit. Love the vibey, laid-back tunes that create the perfect working atmosphere 🎶. The staff are so friendly and positive, always making my day! Love the close proximity to AFDA and 20% student discount, which make lunches here wonderful 😋. Credit to owners Shelley and Sebastian for all their hard work behind the scenes 😀. You don’t want to miss this hidden gem. Will definitely be back soon 🙃🙌🏻.

Name: Ground Culture Cafe
Address: Shop 5, 170 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Cheree Roelofse
Review: I ordered food from here for delivering and it was some of the absolute best burgers I've ever had. I ordered a Brekkie Bun, and this is coming from someone who doesn't usually like eggs, but the Brekkie Bun is definitely now one of my all time favour burgers. I also had the Nelson burger with fries, which was just as good. There is no shortage of patty and overall it is just amazing, even the fries are good, fluffy yet crispy. I can't wait to order here again.

Name: Oblivion Bar & Kitchen
Address: 22 Chichester Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Clive Anthony
Review: This restaurant has never disappointed. The service is always good, the food, great. The atmosphere is chilled. Winter nights are great with the fireplace going and don’t forget to order a hot chocolate - It’s served with cream and flake, very indulgent. Our waitress, Tracy gave us super attentive service and made best recommendations. The board game was a nice finish to our meal. This restaurant is amazing and is very worthy of the 5 stars.

Name: Oblivion Bar & Kitchen
Address: 22 Chichester Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Zintle Mlomo
Review:
I don't like writing bad reviews, but I feel the need to tell others about my experience at Oblivion. The views of Table Mountain are great from the terrace. I had the everbloom cocktail, which was nice and well-mixed, and the traditional beef burger was okay. That's about all the "positive" experience I had.

The service leaves much to be desired. My first waiter sat me down with a menu then took off and never returned, that time it wasn't even busy. After 15min or so of waiting I got up and found another waiter to take my order, and the service started off okay - he took my order and it arrived not long after, then the vanishing commenced with him as well! I really wanted to explore their menu some more and try this other cocktail the second waiter recommended, but opted to go to another establishment for the rest of my afternoon.

As I was walking out I saw a group of waiters congregeted downstairs in what seemed like a meeting, but anyway some sort of hand-over or arrangement should have happend to not leave customers abandoned. Such a disappointing experience and will definitely NOT return nor recommend.

Name: Oblivion Bar & Kitchen
Address: 22 Chichester Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Chantel Balladon (AngelFace89)
Review:
This is one of my favourite places to go to for a post-office day drink or to just eat a pizza and appreciate the spectacular view upstairs.

The downstairs area is very intimate and very suitable for a cosy meal or coffee.

Upstairs has a roof covering and a stunning open view of the mountain - watching sunsets here is just so pretty.

Ample parking opposite Oblivion and conveniently located with easy access to main roads.

Super friendly staff and a great menu to choose from.

My favorite drink will have to be the Ice Dog (especially on a beautiful warm day).

Name: Oblivion Bar & Kitchen
Address: 22 Chichester Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Afrika Lovers
Review:
What a vibe this place is. Ashley our water helped us so beautifully and swiftly.

The Omelette with Bacon was huge and tasty. Take it with Ciabatta.

The Bacon & Brie Sandwich was ordered with some extra chicken. Very tasty. Also on Ciabatta.

The avocado toast with hummus and seeds was fresh and our favourite. Have it on sourdough. It’s beautiful.

Name: Oblivion Bar & Kitchen
Address: 22 Chichester Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Daleen Myburgh
Review:
Chilled, Easy and a good feeling.

Food is prepared very good. We had a pizza, amazing dough, just abit in need of taste.
Good presented and very good prices.

Coctails, are good aswell. Normal coctails all under R100 and then more special drinks are just a little bit higher.

Decor and atmosphere is very good. Games on some of the tables.
Upstairs 'roof top' area.

Will see me soon again.

Name: Jakura
Address: Shop FCK7, 193a Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Kamielah Davids
Review: The main issue here was the waitress/ cashier. We ordered spring rolls, soup dumplings and some sushi. We had to ask her for the wasabi and ginger as well as the spoons for the soup dumplings. She was not attentive and looked like she wanted to go home. The sushi was good but the soup dumplings were ok, it could have more soup in. When we were ready to pay the bill, she demanded that we tip her and said it was mandatory. We did not feel she deserved a tip as she literally did not give any exceptional service. The only thing she did was take our order and bring us our food. She was in her phone sending voice notes. We declined her demand for a tip and said no because we did not get any service. All of this happened today. Get staff that is passionate about the industry.

Name: Jakura
Address: Shop FCK7, 193a Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Arthur Dalbock
Review:
Review for Jakura Sushi, Cana Walk:

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

"Jakura Sushi at Cana Walk is a dream come true for anyone who loves a laid-back yet delicious dining experience. The place is all about convenience – you can just sit back and enjoy their round table offerings, which is a refreshing change from your typical restaurant setup. The staff are super friendly, creating a welcoming atmosphere where you feel right at home. They're always excited to introduce you to new items, making each visit a unique culinary adventure. Not to mention, the sushi here is fantastic and offers great value. It's the perfect spot for a casual, enjoyable meal without the fuss. Highly recommend for a chill sushi outing!"

Name: Jakura
Address: Shop FCK7, 193a Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Travis Kawabata
Review: We were pretty tired and looking for something quick to eat so we stopped here while we were at the mall.  The ramen was good, not great.  We also ordered some of the soup dumplings but they were also a miss.  It was reasonably priced and the restaurant itself was clean.

Name: Jakura
Address: Shop FCK7, 193a Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Mzukisi Mazula
Review: If you are buying a take away, they won't serve you fresh sushi. If you change your mind and want to sit in with your take away they will be upset. When you finally sit in you will learn that your sushi is old and it will break as you eat it. I was there on Saturday afternoon 11/05/2024 and experienced this and it was not nice.

Name: Jakura
Address: Shop FCK7, 193a Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Naomi Lazarus
Review: I really just wasted R360, never again. The ramen was tasteless, the buns were horrible, I literally GAVE the food away to a homeless person. I would have rather ate at KFC

Name: Korea Market
Address: 1st floor, St Peters Square, 423 Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Shahieda Ebrahim
Review: This market has a variety of imported Asian food items. Not everything is halal, but they do keep halal goods in seperate fridges. The restaurant, Gogi, is a must! I had the sweet and spicy chicken, fried dumplings and the chewy rice things. There's more I'd like to try, so I'll visit again. They were kind to give me a taster of kimchi- enjoyed it! This restaurant is fully halal!

Name: Korea Market
Address: 1st floor, St Peters Square, 423 Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Fehrzaana Karbelkar
Review: Very neat shop. Wide variety of products. What I really like is the clearly marked halaal products. They even sell halaal frozen products. There's a halaal restaurant attached to the shop.

Name: Korea Market
Address: 1st floor, St Peters Square, 423 Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Geraldine Ndlovu
Review:
Went to eat at Gogi, I tried Korean food there for the first time and it was amazing. Enjoyed every second of my time there. The waiters were friendly, the chairs were comfortable, korean music was playing throughout, would go again.

PS their Korean fried chicken is the best chicken I've ever tasted

Name: Korea Market
Address: 1st floor, St Peters Square, 423 Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review: Went there on the morning of 26 March 2023. Got a few snacks and drinks. They have a wide variety of noodles but I'm not a fan of noodles so I didn't get any. I'm excited to try what I bought. It's an affordable place and enough space to walk around. I saw various vegetable oils, sauces and mayonnaise.

Name: Korea Market
Address: 1st floor, St Peters Square, 423 Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Taahir Dawood
Review: Korea Market is an interesting experience. It is basically your local market, but with Korean items. The vending machine is cool because its like a new experience. Also, because we don't have those types of machines in Cape Town. The main attraction is actually the food. There's so much hype revolving around it. Either way, I tried a few of what they had to offer. It's not too bad. No alcohol or pork sold. I had the experience, but I wouldn't specifically return unless I needed to. However, check it out. You might enjoy it.

Name: KAUAI Canal Walk
Address: Shop 63, Canal Walk Shopping Centre, Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Matthew Coetzee
Review: Neat venue with and kind professional staff. They helped me choose a meal based off my discovery Kauai voucher. Mucho protein smoothie was perfect. I do feel the bbq chicken wrap could have had a little bit more chicken in.

Name: KAUAI Canal Walk
Address: Shop 63, Canal Walk Shopping Centre, Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Lifestyle By Princess
Review:
What a refreshing change!!! Not just the delicious smoothies...but also the service and quality of food!!

I frequent the various Kauais' in Johannesburg, and I am always left either disgusted or disappointed.

The restaurant was clean, the staff were friendly and the food was absolutely divine. The new menu really has included a variety of flavors making food for minorities so much more appealing.

Overall, this has definitely been a wonderful experience and I would 100% recommend it!!

Name: KAUAI Canal Walk
Address: Shop 63, Canal Walk Shopping Centre, Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Richard Morris
Review: The shop was very busy with most sitting with a coffee rather than at a table with a meal. There was a definately sense of urgency with staff and all were extremely friendly and attentive. As we stood up a staff member was immediately there to take our plate and wipe down the table. Very impressive sevice and neat and clean business, well done, we shall be back.

Name: KAUAI Canal Walk
Address: Shop 63, Canal Walk Shopping Centre, Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Naidoo
Review: Went to Kauai for lunch. Had a great time. You order what you want from the counter and then they bring it to you if you sitting down. I had the butta chicken bowl. Simply one of my favourite meals at Kauai and a healthy alternative. The food was great and the hot chocolate was good. Service was good and they have a nice seating area. I think they should try to wipe the seating areas down a bit more but otherwise lovely experience.

Name: KAUAI Canal Walk
Address: Shop 63, Canal Walk Shopping Centre, Century Blvd, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Obinna Okeke
Review: Kauai is right inside Century mall. Enjoyed a refreshing protein shake/drink but it took longer than expected before it was ready. Nevertheless, I enjoyed my drink. They have a lot of customers.

Name: Asami's House Rosmead
Address: 15 Carlton Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Rebecca Williams
Review: I have never been disappointed when ordering from Asami. Price's are low, portion sizes are MASSIVE, and taste is decent. Meat to veg to noodles is a good ratio/Batter to meat to noodles ratio is good. Staff is always friendly. Personal preference I like my sweet and sour sauce a bit more sweet but is absolutely fine as it is.

Name: Asami's House Rosmead
Address: 15 Carlton Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: QueenAries_EMZA
Review: Just ordered right now, don't waste your time with this place. Highly disappointed with what I recieved. Portion size is a joke for real, fashion sandwich is not even that.

Name: Asami's House Rosmead
Address: 15 Carlton Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Emile Liebenberg
Review:
I paid R300 on Uber eats to be served a whole bunch of rice. 90% of the platter was rice. Considering the price of this meal I can firmly say it’s not worth it.

NB: I ordered the 24 piece, but one get one.

Name: Asami's House Rosmead
Address: 15 Carlton Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Sakeenah Schroeder
Review:
Ordered on Uber eats as they do have nice combo deals.
Firstly this sushi gave myself and my husband food poisoning... the sushi had absolutely no flavour to it and some of the fish was probably off as it had a funny smell... The rice was absolutely tasteless and chewy...
AND my FAVOURITE part was finding HAIR INBETWEEN my California rolls and another drowning in mayo on the platter...
Needless to say I lost my appetite and threw the rest of my half eaten platter away. A waste of money and FOOD!

Name: Asami's House Rosmead
Address: 15 Carlton Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Leston Sylvester
Review: What an absolute gem found on Rosmead today! Good quality, friendly staff and amazing specials! I will definitely be seeing you again very soon!

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Sven Tietz
Review: Dude, there’s a puppy menu. Dude, a full Doggie menu with Puppachinos and Tail Waggers and lots of great dishes and treats for dogs. Would come here just for the dog menu, love it so much. The space was also great, service was friendly, coffee was ok-ish or ok enough, nice place to pop in and do some work, and normally it vibes nicely but they were kind enough to turn it down a bit for my zoom meeting. Definitely recommended… and bring your Furbaby!! ❤️🐕

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Clementine ?
Review: I ate brunch with a few friends and the service was magnificent! We were served by Georgina and she was super attentive, before I even ordered another cup of water it was already being brought to me. The food was great as well! I got a flapjack tower with hash browns and bacon. The hazel nut latte was amazing as well!

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Nigel Teasdale
Review: Four of us had brunch here today, and it was a great experience. The café is attractive and has a pleasant relaxed atmosphere. Service was polite and friendly, and the food was not only very tasty, but also beautifully presented. Highlt recommended.

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Roger Mullens
Review: Cute place to enjoy a lovely breakfast or lunch. I enjoyed the French Toast and a cappucino. The service was incredible. The cappucino was good and the lityle heart cookie was a nice touch. The presentation of the French Toast was stunning, and the taste was great! Can't wait to go back and try more of the offerings! The venue has both inside and outside seating available.

Name: Honeybun Café & Deli
Address: 107 Lower Main Rd, Observatory, Cape Town
Author: Shanice Dee Page
Review:
Honestly the cutest little roadside cafe!

The atmosphere is great! Lesley, my waiter, super friendly and quick service! Very affordable food, drinks and treats.

The food is delish and don’t even get me started on the baked cheesecake 😫🤌🏼 Shout out to you Honeybun.

Loved my first brekkie in CT with y’all.

Name: Tiger's Milk Century City
Address: 8 Kinetic Way, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Winston Rietmuller
Review:
Waited an hour for breakfast even though venue was not too full, sadly.
When food arrived, one meal had no mushrooms, indicated as included on the menu and the coffee was served cold but the waiter quickly sorted this out...
On the whole the relaxing atmosphere, tranquil location and with it being Father's Day, made for a more calm day even though we did have other plans for the day that were delayed...

Name: Tiger's Milk Century City
Address: 8 Kinetic Way, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Steven Manuel
Review:
Went last night for a mini celebration with my buddies for my new job. The convenience of location and positive past experiences made it an easy choice.

Decided to keep things light and ordered a lot of random things instead of 1 big meal each.

The food as always was great. The pizza was a bit soft, could have used a bit more time in the pizza oven to get that crunch and a bit of char I'm used to getting at Tiger's, BUT still good. Tried the AK47 fries for the first time, definitely a new go to for the table while the indecisive people take their sweet time to make up their mind (It's me, I'm the indecisive people 🙃).

Our server/waitress/waitron/food and drink delivery technician was great. Nice energy and got the order right first time. Overall an enjoyable experience. Would recommend.

I saw Wednesday night is quiz night... Are you pondering what I'm pondering?

Name: Tiger's Milk Century City
Address: 8 Kinetic Way, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Ntombi Mathonsi
Review: Always enjoy popping in Tigers milk, food is amazing and the cocktails are A class. Shout out to Charli! His Porn star martinis left me and my girl dazzled! :) too great! Really great to be in a place that delivers on your expectations!

Name: Tiger's Milk Century City
Address: 8 Kinetic Way, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Anas AlSubhi
Review:
One of the best in cape town

Nice place and lovely atmosphere with very delicious menu

Name: Tiger's Milk Century City
Address: 8 Kinetic Way, Century City, Cape Town
Author: Joanna Perumall
Review: I ordered the borderline burger on Uber eats. I waited 1,5 hours for my food to arrive after paying for priority delivery. The chicken patty was not southern fried but just fried. Very disappointed with the food and service.

Name: Checkers Century City
Address: Shop 11, Canal Walk Centre, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Danie van der Merwe
Review: Good experience shopping here and everything was fresh and well displayed. No long queues when I went either. Make sure to have their free loyalty card to qualify for some if the discounts. They are known for their excellent selection of wine.

Name: Checkers Century City
Address: Shop 11, Canal Walk Centre, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Cheree Roelofse
Review: Great store, with some of the freshest foods and an abundance of choices. But honestly I mostly go here for the sushi, Tony's sushi bar is located inside, prepared by Tony himself for some of the freshest and most delicious sushi I've ever had. There are ready to go platters available but I would definitely recommend taking the time to have a fresh one made. Not only does it taste better but it's actually cheaper for more sushi when you have a platter made instead of picking from the fridge. Checkers also offers a great variety of other  ready to go meals, has some great health options and a beautiful wine selection for those interested. Will definitely recommend this place and be back for more very soon.

Name: Checkers Century City
Address: Shop 11, Canal Walk Centre, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: ash gate
Review: This checkers is one of the best and the amount of items that is sold there is amazing. The customer service is fast quick and to the point. The fast food section is great and its so great that they made another section near the entrance. There is a Starbucks type thing in the front but I never used it. My only complaint and only one is that the rolls is packed weirdly in the packet. The rolls are fresh and bread is fresh the items are fastly made. 10/10 checkers honestly.

Name: Checkers Century City
Address: Shop 11, Canal Walk Centre, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: Cindy D. Calvert
Review: Wonderful shopping experience at Checkers in Canal Walk Shopping Centre, as ALWAYS! Got some fresh salads, made daily with all fresh ingredients, highly recommend! Shelves were fully stocked and the vibe in the store was really pleasant and welcoming. Fast efficient check-out no hassles.

Name: Checkers Century City
Address: Shop 11, Canal Walk Centre, Milnerton, Cape Town
Author: beth miss.watson
Review: Good quality items, large shop. It's always clean and well stocked. Tills are often busy, especially outside of normal working hours and on weekends. I can find nearly everything I need.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Engela van der Merwe
Review: The coffee was out of this world! The staff, atmosphere and food are absolutely amazing! Great place for you and your furry friends to stop after a nice walk at the Deer Park, which is just up the road.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Angie Lawrence
Review: We had such a great experience. The staff are so friendly and warm - They offered us blankets for our baby (who was sleeping when we got there), as well as toys when he woke up. The waiters and manager were very attentive to our every need. The food was also fantastic - we enjoyed everything we ordered, and the coffee is delicious. The atmosphere is so relaxing too - We had a great time and stayed longer than planned because it was so nice there.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Eduard Zehrt
Review:
A very pleasant experience overall.

The food was good - very tasty with decent portions. The service was memorable for being exceptionall friendly. The atmosphere was lovely. The wall art, the plants and the warm soft lighting make for a very inviting atmosphere.

I hope to return to this somewhat hidden gem very soon.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Anjulé Smith
Review:
Management was so kind to host our group for a birthday party. While other venues wanted to charge venue hire or impose a minimum spend per person, Shift Espresso Bar in Deer Park made our group feel welcome, let every guest order off the full menu, and accommodated the kids (and one puppy!) inside, away from the rain. The staff even managed to keep separate tabs for all the accounts and there were zero issues by the time we had to pay.

It's also a fab place to work with great music playlists all day. Super friendly and helpful staff, great food and great service made us feel right at home!

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Vredehoek
Address: 2 Deer Park Dr, Vredehoek, Cape Town
Author: Trudy Botha
Review: Awesome views, great coffee selections,  delicious sweet treats, healthy alfresco breakfast and lunches, great atmosphere,  friendly staff , well done John Brown and staff on a delightful experience and awesome vibe, whether it's a coffee on the run, a Sunday leisurely breakfast, a mid week office break or a Saturday shake me up!!!!

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop U25, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Bevan Abrahams
Review: Excellent service from Nande at Milky Lane N1 City. We thoroughly enjoyed our Sundaes. Quick and efficient service. High quality product.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop U25, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Johan van Wyk
Review:
We don't often go to Milky Lane, which is a mistake! They have the lekkerest waffels with properse soft serve ice cream and a variety of syrups.

Service was incredible and staff are friendliest!

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop U25, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Tasneem Patrick
Review: Ordered the new PS milkshake and aero caramel waffle. Supreme!!! ❤️. Service was efficient and there's plenty of seating available.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop U25, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: MUNERAH DAVIDS
Review: The ladies that help me and my husband and kids was amazing keep up the good work

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop U25, N1 City Mall, Louwtjie Rothman St, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Cobus Tromp
Review: Absolutely PATHETIC! Been coming here for years but the last 3 visits was bad, worse and then WILL NEVER COME BACK AGAIN!

Name: Akiya Sushi, Century Village
Address: Century City, Cape Town
Author: Rene Carelse
Review: Loved it. Super star menu/food and service. Our waiter was incredible,  we were a table of 8 and he made sure we were taken care of. The food was fresh and delicious.  Wonderful restaurant for casual mid week dining or celebrating special occasions. The owner was also hands on, being a lovely host to all the tables in the restaurant. Definitely worth visiting this place more often. They have a large sushi menu and hot meals menu with Chinese and Japanese dishes. Lots of vegetarian options too.

Name: Akiya Sushi, Century Village
Address: Century City, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review:
Went there for dinner on 21 May 2023 just after 3pm and they weren't full. Only one waitress was seen on the floor. During our stay we noticed that the asian lady was assisting with carrying dishes to various tables.
Due to this being our first time trying this establishment we asked the waitress what she would recommend us to try. In her defense, we didn't know what we felt like having and she thought she had to go through the entire menu with us. After bringing the drinks (boaba tea and mango jar) we decided to order. We ordered the Katsu Don chicken with miso soup and beef flank ramen. A few minutes later the waitress approached us asking us to repeat our order as the kitchen somehow lost our order.
We waited long for our food with me having to flag down the waitress to ask how long the food will be taking.
My miso soup came out first with the ramen. It was a while before the Katsu Don arrived. Whilst waiting we saw another waiter on the floor.
Safe to say we were not happy with our meals. The Katsu Don had overcooked rice. The broth of the ramen tasted like the packaged stock cubes and was very salty. The waitress offered us dessert as compensation for the wait, to which we declined.
When asking for the bill the Katsu Don wasn't added and I requested them to add it as I wasn't wanting a free meal. When paying I queried why there was a delay in the arrival of the food and the waitress responded that a lot of people left work during the week therefore they are short staffed. When I asked why the mango jar tasted acidic the waitress said she has no idea but the person who made it was new. She also mentioned that the managers are Chinese.

I would recommend this place to work on efficient communication from their staff. Perhaps the hotpot is what this place is well-known for but with such a variety of food to choose from, a bit of effort in making the food would do wonders. The portions were fine but the food itself didn't have much flavour to it.

Name: Akiya Sushi, Century Village
Address: Century City, Cape Town
Author: Casual Food Explorer
Review:
Flavourful fresh wholesome meals, surprisingly after reading many negative reviews, we felt extremely satisfied.  The restaurant was full, and all the meals that had been served looked amazing

Meals are large and can easily be shared.
Waiters friendly.
Waited a bit for our food, may have been due to staff transportation after taxi strikes.

Name: Akiya Sushi, Century Village
Address: Century City, Cape Town
Author: Melanie du Toit (Mel)
Review:
Some of the best sushi I've ever had! It's a great location with a beautiful view of table mountain.

This isn't a budget dining experience, but the sushi is definitely worth the steep price point. Prices range between R145-R175 for 8 pieces. But they did run a lunch special while we were there.

They serve bubble tea ranging from R45-R80 in different flavors.

I give the restaurant a 4 star rating, because the service didn't meet the quality of the food. The waitress was unfriendly and seemed irritated with us, even though we were the only diners in the place. They also had loud conversations at the front of the house, laughing and socializing. This took away from the relaxed, plant-filled atmosphere that is the restaurant's interior.

Name: Akiya Sushi, Century Village
Address: Century City, Cape Town
Author: Juan-Pierré Bruwer
Review:
I decided to give Akiya Sushi a try. At the time of writing, their Century Village branch offered a special for takeaway orders of buy-one-get-one-free. Their pace of making sushi is also quite impressive.

At home, the sushi received were spread over three plates and then ... the eating began. My goodness ... what succulent sushi! The sushi was of high quality and their takeaway special is very good value for money.

When in and around the area, and you have a craving for sushi, please do consider this place.

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: danielle krasniqi
Review:
My first high tea experience was made so incredible with the staff and ambiance at Mount Nelson.  Craig was thoughtful in explaining the tea selection to us and sharing details of tea ceremonies which we loved.  and our waiter Cheslyn was outstanding- I hope to see her immortalized in the menu book with a drawing of her smiling face by the time we come back for another visit!
The food, teas, and sweets were so good and we loved the hotel itself.  11/10 experience- hope we can make it back from NYC soon!

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Theran Pather
Review: Had the vegan High Tea experience. Some of the most interesting vegan foods I've ever eaten. A great selection of sweet and savory items for everyone. There is even a selection of cakes that you can also indulgence in as part of the experience. The highlight is, in fact, the tea selection. There are so many options to choose from, I personally enjoyed the Mount Nelson House blend for those who like a stronger, darker tea.

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Rene Mostert
Review: It was good but I do miss the long table with eats. The trays are fancy but do not give you the buffet feeling of abundance... Fot the price one would like abundance. The kids' tray was really cute. As always...the hotel and garden are absolutely amazing!

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Bradley Ford
Review: Highly recommend the High Tea option. The staff provides, and creates, a perfect experience. Don't be put off by the price, it's worth every cent.

Name: Afternoon Tea at Mount Nelson, A Belmond Hotel, Cape Town
Address: 76 Orange St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Amber Cohen
Review: OMG! This entire afternoon exceeded expectations. The service was the best I’ve experienced in Cape Town. It was such a brilliantly designed menu, both tea and food. The honey bush ice tea/punch was incredible. Better than any house cocktail I’ve ever had. The exterior and interior of the hotel itself is beautiful! Couldn’t believe how gorgeous in person it was. Thank you for a relaxing and filling afternoon.

Name: Aomori Sushi
Address: Corner of Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Tygervalley shopping center, Willie Van Schoor Dr, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Marius Kriel
Review: Great sushi take away spot inside Tygervallei mall.Sushi quality was good and not too expensive.Service was quick and friendly,my order was ready in less than 10 minutes.I had the prawn nigiri & salmon california rolls.I also had the Strawberry Milk Bubble tea which I would definitely recommend and a fortune cookie.

Name: Aomori Sushi
Address: Corner of Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Tygervalley shopping center, Willie Van Schoor Dr, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Annemi Marais
Review: Sushi was amazing. Freshly made and very tasteful. Will definitely recommend them!!! Thank you chef and your team!!!

Name: Aomori Sushi
Address: Corner of Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Tygervalley shopping center, Willie Van Schoor Dr, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Regina Luders
Review: Always enjoyed going there, and sitting at their counter to savour what I got, but suddenly new owners I believe, and the chairs they had at the counter are gone, and there is no longer a place to sit, and definitely don't want to sit in the adjacent big busy food hall. So, it seems like my favorite little stop for a quick bite to eat ay the mall has come to an end. Hope the chairs will be returned to the counter, and then I will gladly return too.

Name: Aomori Sushi
Address: Corner of Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Tygervalley shopping center, Willie Van Schoor Dr, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: B Chetty
Review: AOMORI makes absolutely the best sushi, the sizes are always constant and the freshest. I have been supporting them for years They are truly reliable and recommend to all sushi lovers. 👌👌👌

Name: Aomori Sushi
Address: Corner of Bill Bezuidenhout Ave, Tygervalley shopping center, Willie Van Schoor Dr, Bellville Park, Cape Town
Author: Chanelle Myers
Review: The best sushi I've ever had. I can't wait to go back.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop 13, GrandWest Casino and Entertainment World, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: T Newman
Review: Your coffee says brewed to make me feel good, but actually it's really poor tasting coffee?

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop 13, GrandWest Casino and Entertainment World, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Tumelo Machogo
Review:
Horrible experience.
Served frozen ice cream to a two year old and was told to wait for it to defrost.
Revolting.
First and definitely the last!

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop 13, GrandWest Casino and Entertainment World, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Daniel Kolony
Review: The ice-cream is always good thank you guys for the service 🥰😍

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop 13, GrandWest Casino and Entertainment World, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Roegshanah Bassier
Review: Excellent service from Gracia Kolony! Very friendly and welcoming!

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop 13, GrandWest Casino and Entertainment World, 1 Jakes Gerwel Dr, Goodwood, Cape Town
Author: Gustav Coetzee
Review: I got the one of the new sunny daes, but it was basically just a tiny bit of caramel and chocolate sauce, with cream and then a whole lot of vanilla ice cream. The lady that took our order also quickly got annoyed when we wanted to add something to our order. Truly disappointing and will never be returning.

Name: District Cafe
Address: 115 Roodebloem Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Mishkah Mahomed
Review: We dined at this fully halaal establishment and we had a wonderful time. The prawn starter was filled to the brim with prawns and the sauce was lovely. The dry-aged butter steak was rich and the freshness of the salad perfectly balanced it. The rib and brisket burger was hearty and spicy. I would definitely return.

Name: District Cafe
Address: 115 Roodebloem Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Fehrzaana Karbelkar
Review: Honestly amazing. We tried a variety of meat grills and it was done to perfection. I also had the chocolate cheese cake which was deeeelicious. The portion size of the cheese cake was very small though. Our waitress was super helpful without being intrusive. Pricey but worth it.

Name: District Cafe
Address: 115 Roodebloem Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Erin Rhoda
Review: The staff was very accommodating for our large group. Food very tasty and service on point. Had the lobster and prawn bagel and will definitely recommend it.

Name: District Cafe
Address: 115 Roodebloem Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Tasneem Patrick
Review: Visited today for something new. Had their famous breakfast and smoked salmon bagel. Bagel was abit dry, ordered carrot cake, the icing was too sweet. No cream cheese added to the icing for balance , not the traditional carrot cake icing. The cake itself was moist. Our waiter was friendly n accommodating. Lovely atmosphere. We'll be back to try their dinners on offer.

Name: District Cafe
Address: 115 Roodebloem Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Isa Isaacs
Review: First time here and I enjoyed a nice lunch with my wife. Both of us had the cheeseburger and chips. I ordered a side of onion rings which to be honest I feel should be part of the meal and not be charged separately for it. We both enjoyed our burgers and I followed it up with a mocktail strawberry daiquiri which was very good and fruity. Parking would be the only problem for most people but otherwise worth the visit

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop F94A, Cavendish Square Shopping Centre, Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Naidoo
Review:
Went this weekend for some ice creams. Always have a pleasant experience. The staff was friendly and the orders came out quickly.

Nice perk: They bring your waffles/ drinks to you when you sitting in the food court which is very nice.

I had the caramel cone with the flake. It was delicious and quite a big size.
I will be coming back

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop F94A, Cavendish Square Shopping Centre, Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: fauzia ibrahim
Review: This guy here was so rude to me and my kids and called us impatient for what when will it be our turn to order. Very poor service Milky Lane cavendish. So disheartening

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop F94A, Cavendish Square Shopping Centre, Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: SHANE Govender
Review: Visited the Milky Lane Cavendish  on Sunday after a very long time. Ordered 2 waffles and ice cream for the kids. I must say, the waffles was fantastic, the kids enjoyed their ice cream and service was in point. Thank you Milky Lane Cavendish for the awesome treats and service, well done. See you again soon

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop F94A, Cavendish Square Shopping Centre, Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: burger bums
Review: They don't have a smaller cones, a lady who seemed like she was one of the owners had an attitude, and did not allow me to pay with card even though my friend bought the same thing with card. And another man angrily shouted at me saying "we don't have smaller cones" even though I didn't hear him at first. The service is awful and the owners are so rude.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Shop F94A, Cavendish Square Shopping Centre, Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Zia Haffejee
Review: Was charged more for my order than the price appearing on the menu. My 'bar-one and banana' tub had no actual bar-one, and banana was scarce. RIP the milky lane of my youth

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Canal Walk, Shop FC001, Century Blvd, Century City
Author: English Rose
Review:
Went there on the morning of 19 May 2024 and ordered 2 waffles, the Bar One Bonanza and Oreo waffle. There wasn't anyone in front of us and the wait felt long.
We were advised to pour our own sauce.
Upon receiving our order which looked good, it didn't really taste all that good. It was OK, not great, just OK.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Canal Walk, Shop FC001, Century Blvd, Century City
Author: Cindy D. Calvert
Review: Milky lane is a true family favorite of ours! Quick, friendly service and the ice cream was top class! Highly recommend this!

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Canal Walk, Shop FC001, Century Blvd, Century City
Author: Ibtishaam Watson
Review: I have never in my life taste such nasty milkshake and low quality Waffles. These are one of my favorite desserts and it is absolutely horrible. I am not sure if this is the usual quality that milky lane provides but from what I can remember it never tasted like this.

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Canal Walk, Shop FC001, Century Blvd, Century City
Author: Sharmaine Govender
Review: I bought a triple chocolate mousse sundae and they put 1 drop of mousse in it. When i complained they added another drop and then took out all the chocolate sauce. When i complained again that this isn't how the dessert is made cos i buy it all the time in other branches the staff got angry and said that all the other branches are doing it wrong.  Last time i checked this is a franchise so it shouldn't matter where i but it from or whether it's sit down or take away.  It should be made the same. Unless you guys are making crazy savings by cutting back on your ingredients

Name: Milky Lane
Address: Canal Walk, Shop FC001, Century Blvd, Century City
Author: Johan Greyvenstein
Review:
R 216 Rand for these waffels with no waffers. Stale, cold. Driver obviously didnt care aswell,as you can see.

Obsurd. What a Joke

Name: Tiger's Milk V&A Waterfront
Address: Shop 261, V&A Waterfront, 19 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Thoriso Lelokoane
Review: We had a fantastic dining experience with great food and an amazing vibe. A special thank you to our waiter, Gift, for providing exceptional service throughout our meal. We thoroughly enjoyed every aspect of our visit and look forward to returning soon!

Name: Tiger's Milk V&A Waterfront
Address: Shop 261, V&A Waterfront, 19 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Sherwin Roodt
Review: First time eating at tigers milk with my family and it’s safe to say we will never eat here again. Especially at the waterfront branch. We waited 15 minutes for just drinks and 45 minutes for our food? We were a table of 8 and when we tried to communicate your staff that there are two tables of 4 open , they looked at us as if we were the problem with attitude. Our food finally came and was cold had to wait on our waiter after 10 minutes to go warm our food and an additional 20 minutes for our food to be warmed and brought back to us. Our waiter was pathetic we had to ask him for forks and knives and even sauce. Things that are suppose to be ok our table prior to arrival. For the price you pay it’s not worth it in the end if the service and food is poor.

Name: Tiger's Milk V&A Waterfront
Address: Shop 261, V&A Waterfront, 19 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Kim Sutter
Review:
It was a great experience like allways at Tiger's Milk.
The service was great, the food was delicious ans the drinks very good
Thank you for having us :)

Name: Tiger's Milk V&A Waterfront
Address: Shop 261, V&A Waterfront, 19 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Lia Mia
Review: We really love this place! We had amazing seats with a beautiful view. Also our food and the drinks were super delicious. We will definitely come back and highly recommend this restaurant. Many thanks also to the excellent service.

Name: Tiger's Milk V&A Waterfront
Address: Shop 261, V&A Waterfront, 19 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Anita Ndongeni
Review:
My waiter was Litha . I liked his vibe and he was always around to check up on us. I give him alone 5 stars.

I would like the restaurant itself to improve on getting nice music because it was a bit quiet

Name: Coffee on Orange
Address: Corner of 64 Orange and, 2 Rheede St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Cameron Luke Peters
Review: One of the most stylish and homely places in the whole Kloof Street district. A little confused in its half-Italian, half-Russian decor and menu but it's unique and the staff know you and love you after one visit. Because it's in-set in an apartment block on Rheede Street, it doesn't get nearly enough love! It's a co-working spot par excellence with an incredible Napoleon Cake. Bring your friends and your mamas and papas x

Name: Coffee on Orange
Address: Corner of 64 Orange and, 2 Rheede St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Hiroko Todoroki
Review: Great coffee shop with excellent customer service! I absolutely loved their iced mocha with almond milk and vegan carrot cake. It’s SO much better than Starbucks! The chocolate chip cookie was tasty as well. Happiness and the rest of the staff always greeted us with smiles and were very welcoming every time we visited. My boyfriend and I loved the retro decor and vibe of the coffee shop too. Coffee on Orange is a gem in Cape Town that I highly recommend. The shop has indoor and outdoor seating.

Name: Coffee on Orange
Address: Corner of 64 Orange and, 2 Rheede St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Gabriela Pacheco
Review: My favorite coffee in cape town! Amazing coffee/breakfast, really nice vibe, fast internet (if you need to get some work done) and the best staff (it’s impossible to talk to Happiness and not leave with a better vibe)! Strongly recommend.

Name: Coffee on Orange
Address: Corner of 64 Orange and, 2 Rheede St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Tarryn Bento
Review: I thoroughly enjoyed my experience here. The staff were lovely and the coffee was delish.

Name: Coffee on Orange
Address: Corner of 64 Orange and, 2 Rheede St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Ryan Berelowitz
Review: My morning coffee. Coffee is excellent with even better customer service. Atmosphere is perfect for a morning coffee stop to clear the mind before work.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Kate M
Review: Nicely decorated, friendly service, good wi-fi, delicious coffee! I think these are basically what you look for in any coffee shop!

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Celeste Engelbrecht
Review: Ordered a toasted sandwich. Don’t think it was near a toaster. No butter or mayo, it was bone dry and cold. No love went into this food.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Bruce Moore
Review: I've been here 10+ times.  Mostly Sunday mornings for post hike breakfast.  Great experience.  Lovely to be remembered. They know our coffee orders and what we order.  Food has always been good.  Coffee is strong.  Highly recommend.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Hendrickse
Review: A fantastic hidden gem in Cape Town, with fantastic food and hot beverages. There is plenty inside seating with a very spacious setting. Parking is limited on the street. Prince, our waiter, was fantastic and was extremely attentive.

Name: Shift Espresso Bar | Kloof Street
Address: 100 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Ronny Schäfer
Review: Great place. Very good coffee. Elegant, modern interior. Very nice service. Food was good, but the bagel was not my favorite one, too soft. Would absolutely recommend it for a coffee though and if you need a relaxing place to work.

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Jia Jin Low
Review:
Happy to see that single origin beans have finally made their way to Cape Town since my last visit. Was intending on hitting up Rosetta, Origin, and Espressolab to get a feel for the coffee scene here.

The cafe had a nice vibe, but the coffee was decidedly average by international standards (e.g. if you’ve visited top coffee joints in Japan, Australia, Singapore, etc. — this ain’t that). The macchiato was overextracted and too acidic, while the flat white didn’t show the promised “buttery” or “citrus peel” notes. It’s hard to say whether the issue was with the sourcing, roasting, or the shot pulling, but either way it didn’t blow my mind.

I do have to say though, that the coffee compares favourably with other cafes in Cape Town. But if you’re a tourist, it’s not worth a special trip to see.

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Ghalib Bin Ghalib
Review:
Updated review

Rosetta Roastery is a standout spot for anyone serious about coffee in Cape Town. The espresso, pour-over, and milk-based drinks are all crafted to perfection, with each cup highlighting the rich, nuanced flavors of their expertly roasted beans. Whether you're an espresso aficionado or prefer a delicate pour-over, you’ll be impressed by the attention to detail and quality.

In addition to the stellar coffee, their pastries are the perfect accompaniment. Freshly baked and delicious, they make a great snack to enjoy alongside your drink, whether you’re stopping by for a quick break or settling in for a relaxed morning.

If you’re looking for a place that pairs top-tier coffee with delightful pastries, Rosetta Roastery is an absolute must-visit in Cape Town.

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Jill Paslier
Review: I got some beans and a pastry to go. They were both absolutely incredible. I’ve never smelt or tasted coffee like this before. It was very unique and very delicious. One of the baristas helped me pick it out and they were very kind and gave me a sample as well.unfortunately I did not have time to visit this coffee shop again as the atmosphere was very nice inside and I would have loved to sit down for coffee as well. The beans are great. I can definitely recommend those for now. :-)

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Em Alaryani
Review: One of the best specialty coffee spots in Cape Town! The coffee is exceptional, the staff is friendly and knowledgeable, and the prices are very reasonable. Highly recommended!

Name: Rosetta Roastery Cafe (Bree Street)
Address: 101 Bree St, CBD, Cape Town
Author: Ronier Zalnieriunas
Review:
Dropped by for a quick coffee and had a delightful experience.

The folks behind the bar know their coffees and take their craft seriously.

I tried the progressive option for my espresso and was a little disappointed. They immediately offered to remake the coffee and adjusted the extraction.

Extracting light roasts is difficult and they did well the second time round.
