The espresso tonic (can’t recall if that was the name) was a super cool concept that I’d never tried before. Be try tasty as well.

The ambiance was relaxed and the bar beautiful.

Highly recommend a visit here if you like coffee.

Name: Espressolab Woodstock
Address: 375 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Mohammed Halwani
Review: The service here is incredibly good! Staff are wonderful people and so friendly. This makes me want to visit all the other branches!

Name: Espressolab Woodstock
Address: 375 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Clemens Nickel
Review: Nice place with a great atmosphere, friendly staff and a creative and tasty selection of coffeebased drinks and great regular coffee/espresso. Also the Sandwiches are delicious and the cakes look amazing. I will come back here anytime.

Name: Espressolab Woodstock
Address: 375 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Sander Hoogendoorn
Review: Excellent espresso bar, really strong espresso, good foods.

Name: Espressolab Woodstock
Address: 375 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Frank Pablo Sorin
Review:
Nice and tasty coffee variations! The shop is designed like a lab and it matches perfect with the coffee. But the ambient feels cold.
Tastes interesting.
Staff was in good mood.
We had a nice stay here.

Name: Espressolab Woodstock
Address: 375 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Richard Morris
Review: One of my favourite slick and modern coffee shops in the Old Biscuit Mill. I love the modern clean line contrast against the coffee bags and the architecture of the building and seating outdoors.

Name: George Coffee Bar
Address: 3 Marine Cir, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Louise Mac Donnell
Review: Super friendly crowd, best hot coffee on a chilly Sunday, thanks!

Name: George Coffee Bar
Address: 3 Marine Cir, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Wail Tag
Review: I've been a regular at George Coffee Bar for a year now, and I've always loved their coffee. It has a unique taste that reminds me of my Mediterranean roots, which is something I truly appreciate. However, I've noticed that the quality of the coffee has declined recently. I'm not sure if it's due to a change in the beans or perhaps the barista's skills, but I hope it improves soon because I really enjoy coming here.

Name: George Coffee Bar
Address: 3 Marine Cir, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Nina Blank
Review:
Café Latte and Mocha was great! 😊
Barista guy wasn’t that motivated 😅

Name: George Coffee Bar
Address: 3 Marine Cir, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Shae Bella
Review: 10/10 coffee and baristas , am the biggest lover of a large hazelnut iced latte , my only issue is I ride a motorbike and can’t carry it home with me, or don’t have the chance to come through to you , so my only request is to please please PLEASE put yourself on Uber eats or mr delivery 🙏 I will love you forever and I’m pretty sure it’ll do well

Name: George Coffee Bar
Address: 3 Marine Cir, Table View, Cape Town
Author: Vito Wiechmann
Review:
If you want the friendliest experience with baristas amazing coffee with an excellent grab-and-go concept with even better prices!
Thank me later!

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Talisa Jane Du Bois
Review:
This five star review surprised even me. Deeply excited to see all these smaller cafe’s popping up and thriving around Cape Town. Beautiful aesthetics, good light (you know I’m a fan), GREAT coffee, and 10/10 service.

I’ve been back maybe 25 times since this original review - a coffee space that delivers flawlessly time and time again!

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Dr. KHAMIS ALMAZROOEI
Review: One of my favourite coffee shops in Cape Town. You can taste the passion and love of the coffee in each sip of coffee.

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Fariah Mayet
Review:
I'm so glad I stumbled across this place. I went in to ask if they sell Moka pot filters, but unfortunately, they didn’t. However, Grant was incredibly helpful and advised me on where I could find one. Since I was already there, I decided to try their coffee. I was absolutely blown away by the taste. Their baristas are clearly highly trained, and it shows in every cup :)

Not only is their coffee outstanding, but they also offer a selection of specialty coffee beans for purchase. Their subscription services are reasonably priced and a true gem. It’s safe to say they’ve gained a loyal customer in me

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: kimberley canning
Review:
Simple bru Melkbos is our current favourite coffee in CT and so we had so stop by to check out the new spot closer to home.

Coffee just as delish! Friendly staff in a contemporary space filled with their unique artwork - a different vibe from the seaside space we know but we are sure this location will be a success and will definitely be back for more coffee soon

Name: Simple bru Coffee Co. | Harrington | Specialty coffee cafe
Address: 99 Harrington St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Stanley Biggs
Review: Excellent coffee. Super professional baristas that’s absolutely perfectionistic about quality, but remains super friendly, welcoming and human. I really love this space and I feel right at home there. They do great and interesting specialty coffee and they serve all the manual brews to perfection, but the thing that I keep on telling people to go try is a Flat White with their Alternative Blend: at this moment, I honestly believe that it is the finest house blend in Cape Town. It has sweetness and range that will satisfy all palates (and it’s great black too!)

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Bo-kaap
Address: 81 Rose St, Schotsche Kloof, Cape Town
Author: G P
Review:
Had heard a lot about this outlet and I was not disappointed at all. A great quality coffee cup on a beautiful autumn morning leaves an indelible mark on your caffeine memories.
It is a very small outlet with a couple of chairs, bench inside and a bench outside. Max is an awesome barista and whilst I was there Bob Marley's top track was playing in the background, he was also gracious enough to click my pics.
I had a regular cappuccino after asking Max for suggestions, it was one of the finest cappuccino's I've had after Heaven's coffee. The Coffee blend used in the Cap was majorly Brazilian and some portions of Gautemalan, Ethiopian (AFAIR), it smelled and tasted heavenly, there is a lady called Wadia selling samosas on the pavement just outside the shop and the pairing of samosas with Coffee was Divine.
If you're in the Bokaap area, please do try this gem of a place with an open mind. I bet you'll not be disappointed.
Max/Deluxe team, you've earned a patron now.

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Bo-kaap
Address: 81 Rose St, Schotsche Kloof, Cape Town
Author: Deniz Yildiz
Review: My favorite coffee shop in Cape Town. Consistently great coffee, great views, amazing service & in a nice part of town. Perfect hideaway for my daily caffeine fix

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Bo-kaap
Address: 81 Rose St, Schotsche Kloof, Cape Town
Author: Melrose Rodriguez
Review: Super tasty coffee!! Highly recommend the coffee here. Cool, warm staff and cute little chill spot with a nice view

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Bo-kaap
Address: 81 Rose St, Schotsche Kloof, Cape Town
Author: CV
Review: Excellent coffee. There is a nice little hook in the back to enjoy your coffee. Flat white is great. I love the fact that they have proper tableware, not only paper cups.

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Bo-kaap
Address: 81 Rose St, Schotsche Kloof, Cape Town
Author: Pierre Spence
Review: Max is great, and he also makes a great coffee.

Name: Pang Specialty Coffee
Address: 93 Wale St, Cape Town
Author: Rowyn Coetzee
Review:
NEW LOCATION!!!

Pang's Speciality Coffee is now in a new location on Wale Street, CBD. This coffee shop has a wholesome space right at the entrance of Bo-Kaap, and this space is curated by beautiful staff who serve coffee with only the best vibes.

Definitely a place to visit if you are needing some caffeine and lekker vibes!

Name: Pang Specialty Coffee
Address: 93 Wale St, Cape Town
Author: Jamie Uria
Review: Amazing coffee, super cool staff and awesome thrifting! 10/10 recommend such a unique experience for Cape Town!

Name: Pang Specialty Coffee
Address: 93 Wale St, Cape Town
Author: Shanice Da Silva
Review:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Pang’s Speciality Coffee is a fantastic coffee shop that truly feels like being at home. The cozy atmosphere, friendly staff, and excellent coffee make it a perfect spot to relax and unwind. The decor is warm and inviting, creating a comfortable space to enjoy your coffee and catch up on some chats.

Whether you're meeting friends or looking for a place to work, Pang’s is the ideal choice.

Highly recommend!

---

Name: Pang Specialty Coffee
Address: 93 Wale St, Cape Town
Author: Brendan Pitzer
Review:
A coffee stop you will not regret making. Pang’s very personable and genuine nature helps you ‘feel at home’ while enjoying a great cup of specialty coffee.

I would recommend trying it at least once. And you will ‘feel’ how different it is from any other coffee shop you have been to. It is a favorite, for all of the right reasons.

Name: Pang Specialty Coffee
Address: 93 Wale St, Cape Town
Author: David Zamora Sandoval
Review:
What a place to visit, Pang the Man behind the Coffee shop he is a professional Barista, you will be delight with his coffee and personality.

Welcoming place for work as provides Wifi, bicycle parking and soon will be a bike shop in the back of the coffee shop sponsored by Rookcycle.

Thanks pang for the hospitality and the coffee.

Name: George’s Coffee
Address: 29 Buitenkant St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Johan Brits
Review:
Joel is the best Barista in the City.
Friendly en offer the special for R40 Cappuccino and Muffin.

Name: George’s Coffee
Address: 29 Buitenkant St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Brendan Keogh
Review:
Great coffee, old school full cream flavour that warms your soul, that and the amazing welcoming charm of the team behind the counter.
Best koulourakia biscuits in town. (Don't tell my gran I said that)

Name: George’s Coffee
Address: 29 Buitenkant St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Anthony Redpath
Review:
Great deal for your morning coffee. ☕
Flat white R28
Coffee muffin combo R40

Surrounded by police buying coffee 🚔👮‍♂️

Name: George’s Coffee
Address: 29 Buitenkant St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Vito Wiechmann
Review: Amazing vibe being being in the heart of town with loads around to explore! Why not get a takeaway coffee from Georges and get exploring

Name: George’s Coffee
Address: 29 Buitenkant St, District Six, Cape Town
Author: Greg Schuurman
Review:
The little coffee shop that packs a big punch!
Super friendly service from the team who take care to make you feel very special. An outstanding black Americano really pepped me up for my meeting. Check out their student specials too (bring your student card along!)

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Kloof Street.
Address: 8 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Carol & Mike Aleta
Review: Lovely spot for a cup of great coffee. If you meet Colin, wish him happy birthday! We bought beans, both house blend and decaffeinated, ground for an Aeropress. This morning the coffee was perfect.

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Kloof Street.
Address: 8 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Charnell Ryan
Review: Deluxe Coffeeworks offers a cozy retreat in the heart of the city, boasting a diverse selection of expertly roasted coffee blends. With a commitment to sustainability and friendly, attentive service, it's the perfect spot to indulge in your caffeine fix or unwind with a decadent hot chocolate. Don't miss out on this gem for a memorable coffee experience.

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Kloof Street.
Address: 8 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Mbali Rademeyer
Review: This is definitely the place to be. I had a great time. The coffee sold there was top tier and not to mention their friendly staff . The atmosphere there is amazing. I'm coming back here, guaranteed.

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Kloof Street.
Address: 8 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Bailey Desmarais
Review: Going to this coffee spot is one of my favourite parts of my day. The baristas have become friends and the atmosphere is like home! Highly recommend grabbing your daily dose on metal lane 🖤☕️🎱🏁🐈‍⬛

Name: Deluxe Coffeeworks Kloof Street.
Address: 8 Kloof St, Gardens, Cape Town
Author: Robert Uys
Review: One of the best coffee stops in the city. It is unassuming and hidden away but with a seemingly loyal client base. The barista's are friendly and relatable. The coffee is absolutely fantastic.

Name: The Coffee Guy Noordhoek
Address: The Noordhoek Garden Emporium Cnr of Main and, Katzenellenbogen St, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Di Bulloch
Review:
Had a delicious breakfast roll with tasty bacon, fried egg perfectly cooked with cheese and tomato. Well presented on a fresh roll. I couldn't fault it.
Sat outside in the courtyard in the sun. Coffee was perfect. Looking forward to trying the new menu soon.
Staff are super friendly and I highly recommend that you pay them a visit.

Name: The Coffee Guy Noordhoek
Address: The Noordhoek Garden Emporium Cnr of Main and, Katzenellenbogen St, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Michelle de Jong
Review: Great spot for a pit stop with delicious coffee. Nice views and cool tunes 😎

Name: The Coffee Guy Noordhoek
Address: The Noordhoek Garden Emporium Cnr of Main and, Katzenellenbogen St, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Cape town Cannabis guide Cape town Cannabis guide
Review: Coffee Guy Noordhoek: Delicious coffee, cozy atmosphere, friendly staff. A must-visit for coffee enthusiasts in the area!"

Name: The Coffee Guy Noordhoek
Address: The Noordhoek Garden Emporium Cnr of Main and, Katzenellenbogen St, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Ronier Zalnieriunas
Review:
Good coffee. Stopped by for an espresso while driving through town. Smooth texture; balanced acidity and bitterness.

Super relaxed vibes and very friendly service. Highly recommend a coffee here if you’re going through Noordhoek.

Name: The Coffee Guy Noordhoek
Address: The Noordhoek Garden Emporium Cnr of Main and, Katzenellenbogen St, Noordhoek, Cape Town
Author: Carol Millward
Review: Comfortable, and shielded (from the breeze) venue to enjoy a meal and shop for shrubs, trees and plants. Relaxed service on hand.

Name: Infusion Coffee Shop _ Cape Town
Address: Corner Giel Basson &, Frans Conradie Dr, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Pierre de Proft
Review: 10/10 Amazing service, top quality coffee, great food! Infusion Coffee Shop we salute you for setting the bar so high!

Name: Infusion Coffee Shop _ Cape Town
Address: Corner Giel Basson &, Frans Conradie Dr, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Tanya Ellis
Review: I ordered the chicken and corn soup! It was so creamy and divine, I can’t wait to order my next cup!

Name: Infusion Coffee Shop _ Cape Town
Address: Corner Giel Basson &, Frans Conradie Dr, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Kerri-Ann Burt
Review: Super friendly service and delicious coffee! Just what is needed to start the day!

Name: Infusion Coffee Shop _ Cape Town
Address: Corner Giel Basson &, Frans Conradie Dr, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Ashlene Hughes
Review:
Best Coffee! Simply love their Cafe latte, cappuccino and Chai latte! Muffins are awesome! My absolute favorite are their bacon and cheese toasted sandwiches and their infamous pepper steak pies! Absolutely delish!

Service from their friendly staff is always great and they always go out of their way to ensure their customers are happy and satisfied.

Do yourself a favor and drop by ✨

Name: Infusion Coffee Shop _ Cape Town
Address: Corner Giel Basson &, Frans Conradie Dr, N1 City, Cape Town
Author: Dylan Subramany
Review: Amazing coffee one of my favorites now and a friendly face! Cant wait for you to get your sit down coffee shop!

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Yolinda Badenhorst
Review: You come in as a stranger and don't leave like one. Hey Stranger, is a new kid on the block and I hope they stay here for long. Not many coffee shops with a small bite to eat. This place is for those in a hurry or just one who wants to sit down and enjoy a great cuppa. The shop is very clean and staff very friendly. They also have a loyalty card, for the regulars. 10th cup on the house.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Christina Pretorius
Review: Such a nice contrast to the grittiness of Long Street. Modern, clean, minimalist aesthetic with great, strong coffee and friendly service. We had the pecan tart and matcha white chocolate cake. Both were good, the only minor thing I would suggest is to warm the tart a bit so it’s easier to eat. I went back for a second coffee before I left and spoke to the owner and she was so nice and welcoming.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Ludwig van Rooyen
Review:
Finally a specialty coffee shop serving exclusively Father Coffee. One of the best Cortado’s I had in Cape Town. Using Heirloom Blend as the house blend. Clean and minimalistic interior. Wide selection of pastries available. Single origins and Almond and Oat milk alternatives available.

Father beans also sold here.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Illana Justus
Review: We were looking for a coffee shop whilst out in Town earlier today, and happened to stumble across Hey Stranger. What a pleasant experience. The staff are friendly. The coffee was good. The caramel cheesecake & red velvet cake were delicious too.

Name: Hey Stranger Coffee Collective
Address: 172 Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Nicole Pretorius
Review: My experience at Hey Stranger coffee collective was great, service was quick coffee and bun combo was oh so good, the decor was modern, each piece of furniture unique and very mod. Thomas and Natalia are wonderful people, I chatted with Thomas a little bit. The food came quickly and service was very friendly. A top notch coffee shop, a definite visit when you're in Town CBD you won't be disappointed a treat.

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Grant Swanepoel
Review: I love Tiki Tonga. I ran in this morning early with a big order and they were friendly, welcoming and professional. The atmosphere is cosy but upbeat. The coffee is excellent and quick and of course you can buy their signature coffee beans and pods to make at home. Highly recommended!

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: BH
Review: Small place with a small menu but very well crafted. The cappuccino was creamy and smooth. My eggs cheese and bacon sandwich was ok. It's a good option for a quick stop and go type of bite.

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Jordan West
Review: We were driving to go play hockey at UCT and needed a coffee and didn’t want to stop at the petrol station. We then found Tiki Tonga. Was such a great experience - service was fantastic but the coffee was even better. Will definitely be back and get food next time. Thanks to the Tiki Tonga team

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: Jody Wyngaard
Review:
What a great spot for a delicious iced coffee and some cake of the day.

The gentleman working there was able to tell us about the different filter and bean coffees available, and some of the story behind Tiki Tonga. Would certainly recommend.

Name: Tiki Tonga Coffee Roasters - Rondebosch
Address: Shop 3, Atlas House Corner Main &, Grotto Rd, Rondebosch, Cape Town
Author: carla Combink
Review:
Found this little coffee  shop.
First just stopped for a coffee. But then  returned later to have  something  to eat and drink. Ordered a toasted sandwich with egg, bacon and cheese. It was really tasty...Coffee so creamy.
Staff was so friendly and helpfull.
Thank-you to them
Also had awesome  background  music. Will visit  again

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Hesham Abuelkher
Review: When the taste of coffee and excellent service come together, it's Mocha Coffee cafe

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Abduragiem Adams
Review: Hidden gem next to excellent meat market 😋

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: shireen adam
Review: Lovely surprise walking around not this coffee shop. Tucked away at the end of the parking lot. Wonderful hospitality and coffee. Will definitely be back.

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Aniesa Amijee
Review:
Excellent service and warm welcome by barista Ali and co-worker Abdul.
Coffee and hot chocolate was extra delicious.
Worth a visit.

Name: MOCHA COFFEE
Address: Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Abed Khaled
Review: One of the best cortado I taste ever

Name: Nulyfe Coffee Co.
Address: Maynard Mall Wynberg Main Rd, M4, Cape Town
Author: Eliot Katandika
Review: Did a barista course at Nulyfe and it was exceptional, definitely worth it. and I’m glad to say I’m now currently employed because of Nulyfe Coffee Co.

Name: Nulyfe Coffee Co.
Address: Maynard Mall Wynberg Main Rd, M4, Cape Town
Author: Roberto Batson
Review: I really loved the experience at Nulyfe. It was fun and educative. Thank sensei Sam

Name: Nulyfe Coffee Co.
Address: Maynard Mall Wynberg Main Rd, M4, Cape Town
Author: Irene Mataki
Review: I had a very great experience with nulyfe coffee where I was trained to become a barista and now I am a qualified barista all thanks to Charles and Samkelo

Name: Nulyfe Coffee Co.
Address: Maynard Mall Wynberg Main Rd, M4, Cape Town
Author: Firmino Banert
Review: I really loved the experience at Nulyfe. It was fun and educative. Thank sensei Sam

Name: Nulyfe Coffee Co.
Address: Maynard Mall Wynberg Main Rd, M4, Cape Town
Author: Shirin Khan
Review:
Nulyfe coffee the best taste ever ...and best service .
All best for your business in future..keep it up...

Kind regards

From Food Spot Maynard Mall Wynberg

Name: Platō Coffee - Sea Point
Address: 120 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Fransceska Pretorius
Review: The main thing I go back for is the service! Their coffee is good too, I suppose - jokes! I’ve had normal lattes, chai freezos, dirty chais and flirty chais (my favourite and absolutely top recommended), and every time I leave satisfied. One of my favourite places ever - I even snagged a free oats cookie when I joined their loyalty program! Even if coffee isn’t your thing, they’ve got many other alternatives.

Name: Platō Coffee - Sea Point
Address: 120 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Dylan Rosser
Review:
So tried Plato for the first time today. Let's just say if you like coffee flavoured milk then this is your spot. However if you want to enjoy a decent coffee with flavour then avoid this branch. Not sure if all are like this. I had a flat white which had very little coffee flavour and lacked any body. My partner tried the cafe mocha which I tried and this was also disappointing.

Service wise started off good. Wasn't to busy, the cashier greets you and the regulars that pop in.

However when it came to getting our order 2 other patrons who ordered after us got their drinks first (they were loyalty customers, so maybe that's why, double standards maybe?) only after asking where our order was did they realised they skilled us.

The place needs some work aswell, the ceiling area has peeling paint, the coffee lid and sugar section needs a wipe down. When you have white surfaces you really need to keep ontop of keeping it clean.

Will try another branch, but unlikely to go back to this branch.

Name: Platō Coffee - Sea Point
Address: 120 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Aly Nell
Review: Walkable location to collect a coffee and take it to the promenade for a stroll! Extremely friendly barista’s and always willing to give their knowledge on drinks. Got my usual - Medium Iced Latte with hazelnut 😋

Name: Platō Coffee - Sea Point
Address: 120 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Sven Tietz
Review: I’m such a groupie for this brand. Been to several of their locations and Plato coffee never disappoints (which is unusual for a group). The sea point location is no different- small, great vibe, stunning coffee - worth going out of your way for. Ps it’s ’front of house’ for a gym at the back - fun 🤩

Name: Platō Coffee - Sea Point
Address: 120 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Melanie Zayat
Review:
The coffee was good but it took ages (no apology included) We told them we were sitting in and they still gave us paper cups. The bites were good but no cutlery or plates so we had to have it in the only napkin they gave us.
It’s good for a coffee to go, but wouldn’t recommend sitting in.

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Marius Kriel
Review:
I had a delightful experience at The Ginger Tree. From the moment I stepped in until I left, the hospitality was outstanding. Though small, the café is cozy with a calming atmosphere and charming decor.

The gentleman running the store is incredibly hospitable and engaging. They offer a variety of espresso-based drinks, listed upon entry, and a few sandwiches, though there’s no formal food menu. I highly recommend the "everything sandwich"—it’s worth a visit just for that!

The coffee is very flavorful, and they also offer takeaways if your in a rush. With ample parking and outdoor seating, The Ginger Tree is a local staple and the perfect hangout spot. I’ll definitely be returning!

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Melissa Van Niekerk
Review: Searched for a coffee shop near me whilst in the area for an appointment and Ginger Tree popped up. What a beautiful environment with incredibly yummy coffee and homemade toasties. If I lived closer I’d be here daily.😉

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Samihah Ebrahim
Review: Wonderful, friendly service! So homey and comfortable. Delicious drinks and cake! 😍

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Malcolm Campbell
Review: Beautiful place, good atmosphere, great service, amazing food. Definitely worth a revisit.

Name: Ginger Tree Coffee
Address: 63 Friesland St, Parow, Cape Town
Author: Wium Brosens
Review: Such a lovely coffee shop with great ambiance, friendly owner and very tasty home baked cakes! The ice coffee is a must try and variours other delicious snacks. Excellent coffee and won't break the bank either. Highly recommended for coffee lovers and something delicious on the side.

Name: Pauline's
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Veronika
Review: The scrambled eggs on a sourdough with avocado was exactly what I was craving! So tasty, good price, and amazing service. Nice coffee, and free water with that. The best café in Cape Town!

Name: Pauline's
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Dr. Champ Payne
Review:
Pauline’s is an absolute treasure of a coffee shop! Their baked goods are nothing short of divine—freshly made, rich in flavor, and with a perfect balance of sweetness. Whether it’s their buttery cinnamon buns or decadent pastries, every bite feels like a special treat.

The coffee at Pauline’s is simply amazing. Each cup is brewed with care, full of bold flavors, and always served in the most delightful way. It’s clear they take pride in every pour, making it a perfect spot for coffee lovers.

And let’s not forget their sparkling water on tap—such a unique touch! It’s refreshing and adds to the overall fantastic experience. Pauline’s really has it all—great atmosphere, top-quality delicacies, and a perfect place to relax and enjoy. Highly recommended!

Name: Pauline's
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Ксения Л.
Review: didn't understand what it was.  Either I was unlucky with everything at once, or the reviews were bought.  This is a complete disappointment.  I ordered a cappuccino and scramble with toast and bacon.  The cappuccino was terribly sour, the worst coffee I've had in Cape Town.  The food was absolutely tasteless, bland, no seasoning or salt, just fried eggs and bacon.  I cook much better at home and the food from the supermarket tastes better than what was prepared here.  On top of that the toast was burnt and dry and the bacon was even more burnt, it was literally like charcoal.  I'm shocked, I relied on the reviews, but in the end I got indigestion and heartburn 🤢 p.s.  the only plus was the staff was friendly.

Name: Pauline's
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Adeeba Isaacs
Review: Coffee was great. My daughter’s eggs were medium as requested. If I were to make a recommendation is to serve the pancakes warm or atleast warm syrup. Pancakes was served room temp. Warm pancakes and syrup will make the experience even better! I will be back as I have not tried the cinnamon and cardamom buns which looked delish!

Name: Pauline's
Address: 277 Main Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Jo V
Review: Such a gem! Love this place. The decor is so beautiful, contemporary and makes you feel like you are in another world. The staff are super friendly, the baked goods are so tasty. They make the cardamon buns fresh every morning, they sell out pretty quickly. Highly recommend! It’s a small cafe with a lot of seating outside and some window seats on the side, world class! They Dont have decaf coffee, but they have an alternative that is very nice and tastes like nutty coffee.

Name: Corp Cafe Portside
Address: 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Weyers
Review: Awesome coffee, friendly staff and their food offerings was exquisitely prepared

Name: Corp Cafe Portside
Address: 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Lauren Ross
Review: I absolutely love this Cafe. I'm a regular customer.They are always friendly staff, great coffee and great service. However my rating is based in my experience I had today 4 January 2022. I came into the Cafe just after 3 because the door was open. The gentleman that always makes the coffee and a lady was there. When I placed my order the gentleman gave a loud sigh and said "we're closed". The lady then went on to say "this is the last week that we're closing at 3". I apologized humbly and profusely and said that I didn't know that they are closed. I didn't feel good after that reaction from the staff. I'm not a difficult customer and I know what it's like when it's closing time. Cashing up needs to happen and all those things however there was no sign up saying that the cafe is closed and the door was wide open. I feel very disappointed after that experience. I will continue to support the cafe but I just wanted to express my disappointment because in that very moment, I did not feel like a valued customer.😔

Name: Corp Cafe Portside
Address: 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Lezanne Maritz
Review: Amazing Coffee, chilled atmosphere and Friendly Staff. I would definitely go there again.

Name: Corp Cafe Portside
Address: 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Nia Taljaard
Review: This really wasn't a very nice experience. The service was very bad and slow. We all had burgers and most of our patties were burnt. Would not recommend.

Name: Corp Cafe Portside
Address: 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Erica Maling (Erockstar9)
Review: Lovely food and coffee, friendly staff, nice atmosphere, great place

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Afrika Lovers
Review:
Truth seemed to be a guarantee for great quality of food and beverages and amazing atmosphere. Where the latter still stands, the former is a bit shaky recently.

We ordered a free-range burger that was way too greasy, more than blend taste wise and somehow just not doing it.

The pastries (chocolate croissants) were better. The teas (lemon rooibos & earl grey) were good whereas the orange juice was not made from fresh oranges but concentrate.

Come on Truth you can do better. Almost a 2 stars for the food but we don’t want to be too harsh. Will be back soon to see if there are improvements…

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Milo
Review:
What a truly awesome experience! Walking by their little unassuming outside coffeebar, I asked for a seat and was absolutely astounded by what awaited inside! The setup is incredible, absolutely incredible. Awesome music, amazing fun staff, cool antiques, mechanical design, coffee roasting, pleasing aesthetic, delicious food and outstanding coffee. This place has it all!
Its been a minute since any shop has had such an effect on me. I went in with the intention of starting a book I had bought down the street but instead just stared in amazement at this wondrous place. There are so many neat things going on, apparently they also have fantastic breads and pastries but I won’t even get into that. My favorite was the rotating coffee bean stand and amazing server Siya ✊🏽💜
A tremendous thank you for this great coffee experience.

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Toni Henning
Review:
I thoroughly enjoyed the outing to Truth Coffee. Their coffee IS amazing. I had a Resurrection Latte, and my son had a flat white.
I really appreciate that they don't charge extra for milk substitutes. The food was extra yummy. The decor is unusual - everywhere you look - such interesting things. The menu is fun to read! I love the way the waiting staff dress, too. I have one little complaint. When we ordered, I asked if they have muffins. Our waiter said no and proceeded to indicate on the menu what I could choose from. Clearly, I was in the mood for something sweet or baked if I asked for a muffin, right? Only after our food arrived, I saw other patrons with cake. It would have been great if our waiter had told me that, while they don't have muffins, they have other baked goods. Luckily, the avo on toast was so delicious that I got over it. Haha! Next time I go, I will order from the bakery.

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Tarcisio Borges
Review: We absolutely loved Truth Coffee! The atmosphere is incredibly unique with its steampunk-inspired decor, making it feel like you’ve stepped into a different world. The coffee is truly exceptional—carefully roasted and served with great attention to detail. The staff is knowledgeable and friendly, guiding you through their impressive selection. It’s no wonder this spot has been recognized globally as one of the best coffee shops. Definitely a must-visit in Cape Town!

Name: Truth Coffee Roasting
Address: 36 Buitenkant St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Pete T (epetet)
Review: A funky atmosphere with fantastic coffee and unique food options.  More than just a touristy spot they have a great variety of coffees and bakery products as well as kitchen-made menu options.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Jon W (Anonymous)
Review: Quite an artsy spot, but you would struggle to find better coffee in CT! Parking across the road is free and plentiful. Prices are very reasonable. Just wish they sold decaf coffee.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Marlin
Review: Great little coffee shop that you should go and check out not just for the take away coffee. Bought of their houseblend beans and its really good. More than that I think its a nice space to just unwind a bit while having a coffee.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Marizanne van Wyk
Review: I had a great time at Boston Coffee! I had an iced coffee, and it was lovely. I’ve previously had other drinks there, and everything is delicious! The service is good and the people working there are so friendly. The vibe is really relaxed and nice. It was the first time I ate there, and the food was good. The only drawback for me personally, was that there is no option of brown bread. However, the bread they do give is very nice. Other than that, I loved everything.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Johan van Wyk
Review:
When I really want to treat myself with incredible coffee and very nice something small to eat, this is the place that I go to.
The variety of coffees is to write home about, the staff is very friendly and helpful and the atmosphere is always joyful.
It is a very popular business and there are always people, irrespective of the time of day.

Name: Boston Coffee Roasters
Address: Cleveland St, Boston, Cape Town
Author: Magdalena Rousseau
Review: I love - LOVE! - visiting Boston Coffee! Sometimes alone, sometimes with my 2 grandkids, sometime with friends. The coffee is excellent, and so is the food. And the vibe is mysterious, i.e. no words to describe it, but it is THERE! Well done, and keep it up!

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Yoveen H
Review:
A unique restaurant with a powerful social change aspect to it, combined with incredible food and excellent service.

Side note: the best Eggs Benedict in the Cape!

Well done ILC - keep up the phenomenal work!

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Benita Evans
Review: A wonderful gem of a coffee shop. Always great service and friendly staff. Seating available ioutside weather permitting, it's lovely to sit outside and enjoy a lovely coffee in the summer. In the winter the inside is usually packed, especially on a rainy day. The shelves are filled with delicious baked goods and pastries. They also roast their own coffee beans and will grind them if you prefer to buy ground coffee.

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Richard Hills
Review:
On this holiday we've made time to eat here three times so far, Unathi has been the lady who has looked after us on all our visits and is friendly and fantastic at her job.
I highly recommend the mushroom and egg on sour dough bread. The prices are very reasonable and the size of portions are very good.

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Willem Van Ryswyck
Review:
What an incredible business plan to have majority staff who are hard of hearing. Everyone was incredibly friendly and there were lots of smiles all round from the staff. On top of the the food was excellent and had a nice outside sitting area, with plugs inside if needed.

Went back and tried the pastries and can highly recommend! The carrot cake was delicious.

Name: I Love Coffee Roastery and Deli Claremont
Address: 103 Garfield Rd, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Malaika
Review: So glad I found this bakery. I had a custard croissant and apple, carrot and ginger juice. The custard croissant was delicious and the juice was good. I can’t wait to go back and try the other baked goods.

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Antony Ribot
Review: Lovely barista and coffee. The muffins looked amazing but we didn’t have time to try them this time round. We’ll be back. The other regular customers were very friendly as well.

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Michael psaltis
Review:
Enjoyed the chia tea but there could be more seating area. Nice spot to stop for a quick coffee after a run or cycle.
Great friendly service

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Lindsay S
Review:
My love affair with coffee just never seems to end. That first sip just boosts my serotonin receptors.
Not everyone feels that way about the magical brew in a little cup.
Our taste buds all differ and with that our preference for coffee. Some might like a bold dark roast with bitter chocolatey overtones and others might like a smooth balanced coffee.

If you prefer a smooth, slightly acidic fruity coffee with a chocolatey finish, then Barista on Move will deliver.

I have had many good coffees and what makes Barista on Move so different, is the experience created by the passionate baristas. On arrival you are greeted with a friendly unpretentious smile followed by an inquiry into how you are doing. With me being a regular, it is just for me to confirm my preference - usually a flat white with a double shot of espresso.

The picture attached to the review shows what the perfect flat white should look like - velvety and creamy - absolute magic in a cup!!

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Anthony Goble
Review: Great spot for a quick coffee on the go. Friendly baristas serving good coffee with a smile. Note there is no indoor seating but the outdoor benches are under cover.

Name: BOM Coffee
Address: 90c Monte Vista Blvd, Monte Vista, Cape Town
Author: Marsha Petersen
Review: Delish coffee. Opens up at 6am opposite the walking park. Quick cuppa before you get on with the day. Nothing in CT opens that early. I'm impressed. I need my coffee this early in the morning. Thank you for being open so early and having a wonderful cheerful barista. Keep it up.

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: የክርስቶስ ቤተ ክርስቲያን
Review:
Always great service lovely.very good people and best coffee, originally from Ethiopia. I love it the most.
እግዚአብሔር ይባርካችሁ

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: Tarik Abay
Review: organic and natural coffe with good service

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: nicolas carsten
Review: The food is okay however the parking and location is what had me worried the hole time and I was constantly thinking about my safety on the way out I got boxes in by 2 men that tried to steal my cell phone........be very careful and vigilant

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: Salman Mo
Review: Very clean and neat environment, love that taste of coffee. Excellent service

Name: Zoe Coffee Zone
Address: 12 Vrede St, Bellville Central, Cape Town
Author: ABDIHAKIM HASAN ABDI Abdi
Review: I enjoy the taste of coffee.

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Shaheeda Crombie
Review: Just mediocre. The staff component handles everything well. The menu however, man talk about boring. I am going to say it but Vida and Kauai makes better drinks. Just a no for me.

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Izelke Gebhardt
Review:
Such convenience
The Seattle Coffee ☕️ Company is very conveniently situated outside Foodlovers so grab a coffee ☕️before or after your shopping 🛍.
Staff is friendly, you can sit down while taking in the smells 😁👍

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Anchilla Sobotker
Review: The best coffee ever. Thank you very much for the excellent customer service you provide. Keep up the great service you provide.

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Dietrich Schwarzer
Review: Very friendly staff. Excellent coffee

Name: Seattle Coffee
Address: ERF11516 Main Rd, Dreyersdal, Cape Town
Author: Anne Burger
Review: Love going to this Seattle, it's just a little container style coffee bar outside of the Food Lover's Market. Whenever we go to buy groceries at that FLM we go get coffees first and sip on them while we shop. The people that work at this specific Seattle are also always super friendly, they like to make jokes and chat to their customers and I thoroughly enjoy that. There are a few seats if you want to sit and enjoy your coffee, but it is outside so weather permitting. Plenty of parking available. Would recommend.

Name: Naked coffee
Address: 2 Wisbeach Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Paul Viljoen
Review: There's not a lot of places with better coffee. Consistency in the quality of their coffee. Super nice service as well. One of my favourite places!

Name: Naked coffee
Address: 2 Wisbeach Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Marizanne van Wyk
Review: My family and I loved breakfast at Naked Coffee! The vibe was nice and the food was absolutely delicious. Everything on the menu sounded great, so it was difficult to choose what to order! I’ll definitely be back to try some of the other dishes too!

Name: Naked coffee
Address: 2 Wisbeach Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: De Du
Review:
They don't seem to maintain a door list, so we almost lost our place just because the waiter thought we were here after a couple of young ladies. Luckily the manager was paying attention.

The food is so good though. I'll happily endure subpar service for it. Got a magic coffee that was absolutely brilliant. The breakfast stack was divine.

Edit: just realised that it is a public holiday today, so they are probably way busier than they usually are. Bringing service score back up.

Name: Naked coffee
Address: 2 Wisbeach Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Anna “Anthek13” Kaufmann
Review: Very nice café! We had the couscous salad, smashed avo bread and a slice of banana bread. Everything was really good! I like the vibe here- some of the people are working, some come together after their gym session and some guys just grab a coffee.

Name: Naked coffee
Address: 2 Wisbeach Rd, Sea Point, Cape Town
Author: Jessie Jeftha
Review:
You know it was good when you leave a review, the minute you walk out of the establishment.

Naked is great if you need a chilled but cool place to work. I was lucky enough to grab a corner table, which I think is the best seat in the house. I had a good view of the space and could tap on my laptop peacefully. The coffee was good, the New York buttermilk pancakes were yummy and fluffy. I will definitely be back for brunch. And naturally, I'll be back when I need a space to work. The staff are great and friendly and not in your face at all. I loved the background music, it's my kinda vibe.

I'm so proud of myself for finding this gem.🤭

Name: Bara Coffee
Address: 24 Gladstone St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Siobhan S
Review: I enjoyed cup of coffee. The ambiance is pleasant and the presentation is visually appealing, making it a decent choice for a quick meeting or catch-up. However, for those looking to work remotely or need a spot to settle in and focus, this venue may not be the best fit. It lacks some of the key elements that remote workers typically look for, like more structured seating options or work-friendly features.

Name: Bara Coffee
Address: 24 Gladstone St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Reynhard
Review:
Quick coffee meeting ? Waiting for the kids to finish school? Or maybe a place to do some work. This coffee shop enables all of that and you to get your kick for the day.

Music is surprisingly of good taste and not too loud. Staff are very friendly and coffee is great. Food can be better but it isn’t a restaurant so don’t expect 5 star quality.

Name: Bara Coffee
Address: 24 Gladstone St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Hayley M Holesgrove
Review: What a lovely place for students to meet before or after school.  Coffee is good. Too early for me to have a sourdough toastie.  Did not feel like a bun so early.

Name: Bara Coffee
Address: 24 Gladstone St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Jenske Roukema
Review:
Coffee was decent (I had the cappuccino). The croissant toastie was delicious.
The service is acceptable and low key. Perfect if you want to converse with friends or even work without constant interruptions.

Name: Bara Coffee
Address: 24 Gladstone St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Kate Rolfe
Review:
Bara is my favourite coffee shop in Durbanville!

The staff are so welcoming and friendly! It is so nice to see how they bond on a deeper level with their customers and regulars!

Best toasties, coffee and rusks around !

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Ghalib Bin Ghalib
Review:
Origin Coffee Roasting in Cape Town is a fantastic spot not only for exceptional **coffee** but also for a great **breakfast**. Their **espresso**, **pour-over**, and **milk-based** drinks are consistently excellent, showcasing a range of flavors that highlight the care they put into their roasting process. Plus, their variety of **single-origin** options is perfect for those who love exploring unique flavors from different regions.

I also had breakfast here, and the food was just as impressive. Whether you're in the mood for something hearty or light, their menu offers delicious options to pair perfectly with your coffee. The overall experience makes Origin Coffee a go-to for both coffee lovers and those looking for a satisfying meal to start the day right!

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Ammar Alaidarous
Review: Awesome service, great atmosphere,  fresh food and tasty coffee. Their Benedict got a twist of beetroot,  the pabka is out of this world 🌎,  and the Portuguese tart is so smooth and delicious. They kept asking us if we're happy 😊 we were extremely happy having our breakfast there, and thanks Anni for taking care of us..

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Katie Pugh
Review: Love this coffee shop, awesome single origin coffee to try. The staff recommended an off menu Peruvian bean that was superb using a chemex brew. They also have great breakfast options as well, my husband said the almond croissant is one of the best he’s had and I loved the omelette with the meatball and local fontina cheese. Highly recommend a visit here.

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Radesh N
Review: If i could explain it, I would say it has a cool industrial look and a New York City kinda feel to it. Very interesting menu and lots to choose from. Really good coffee ( but i forgot to take a pic ) and my wife enjoyed her tea. The muesli with fruits were very refreshing. We also enjoyed the eggs benedict and the chicken, avo & mayo bagel. Service is very quick. Would definitely recommend for breakfast/ brunch and for coffee lovers.

Name: Origin Coffee Roasting
Address: 28 Hudson St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Stevel Marc
Review: I’ve been here a few times. I love this café. It has a great atmosphere. The staff is friendly and eager to help, and the food is tasty.

Name: Kamili Coffee on Long
Address: 29 Shortmarket St &, Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Jill Paslier
Review: I picked up some beans from this coffee shop twice because they were so great. I picked up a large bag to take back home with me since I’m visiting from out of town. The staff was super friendly and hopeful, and the coffee is delicious!

Name: Kamili Coffee on Long
Address: 29 Shortmarket St &, Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Ayesha Madan
Review: Our first taste of capetown and it was yummy. Found this trying to find the bathroom and found kamili. Charming service, great prices and good cappuccinos!

Name: Kamili Coffee on Long
Address: 29 Shortmarket St &, Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Mansour Al-Essa
Review:
The coffee is seemed good with extra flavor and good beans
I like it much than roseta coffee near this coffee

Name: Kamili Coffee on Long
Address: 29 Shortmarket St &, Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: T L
Review:
Convenient place to grab coffee after taking a walking tour. The walking tour meets here as well, so gets busy before and after tours.

A free beverage is included with the purchase of a bag of coffee beans. Friendly staff. USB charging ports and AC adapter units at every table.

Name: Kamili Coffee on Long
Address: 29 Shortmarket St &, Long St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Riccardo Tosetto Photography
Review: After a big lunch we needed a great coffee, from the outside the place seemed nice and actually it was! A really nice barman made us an Amarula Coffee, maybe bit strong since it was 2:30pm but we are on  holiday, who cares?! The coffee was perfect, really really good! Highly recommended!

Name: Halō Coffee
Address: In the foyer of, 117 Strand St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: yolonda johnson
Review: Absolutely My New Favourite Coffee Shop in Cape Town 😍 Mfundo & Rob our Waiters on the ball, the service Tara and I got the last three days was amazing! Gonna Miss you guys The Best Sourdough Sandwiches, not forgetting the amazing crunchies😊👌Chum thank you so much till we back in CT.

Name: Halō Coffee
Address: In the foyer of, 117 Strand St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Jonas “Safariguide Southafrica” Sandstrom
Review:
This place has been here for a long time!
Nice idea mixing a fashion jeans  shop with  Aesoap products and a café
But it needs a bit of face-lift ..Everything ok but feels like a " fat cat"
No entusiasm anymore.

Name: Halō Coffee
Address: In the foyer of, 117 Strand St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Jenna Massey
Review: A lovely spot for a Saturday morning coffee. It was so good we decided to come back the next week. The staff were so wonderful, friendly and even remembered our names and asked us about our plans from the previous week. If you haven’t been to Halō it needs to be next on your list.

Name: Halō Coffee
Address: In the foyer of, 117 Strand St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Tara Johnson
Review: Absolutely amazing ! Feels like home , friendly staff and amazing coffee and food 🥰

Name: Halō Coffee
Address: In the foyer of, 117 Strand St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Frederik Herholdt
Review: Visiting Halō was a delight – their coffee is truly a work of art, with every cup bursting with rich, unforgettable flavour. The friendly Halō crew made me feel right at home in their cozy, inviting space. I loved relaxing and connecting with others while enjoying my coffee. A must-visit for anyone who loves great coffee and good vibes!

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Em Alaryani
Review:
One of the best coffee shops in Cape Town is an exceptional place by all measures. It features a historical ambiance inspired by the series "Game of Thrones" adding a unique charm. The staff are extremely friendly and their coffee, along with the biscuits and cakes is unparalleled.

The aroma of coffee fills the place, making the visit a unique sensory experience. The prices are very reasonable compared to the quality of the place and the flavor of the coffee.

I highly recommend visiting this coffee shop, and I will certainly return to it repeatedly whenever I am in Cape Town.

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Özdemir Seyhun Dede
Review: The best coffee shop in the world. Owner is an exceptional human. Coffee and sweet bites are top notch

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Lauren C
Review:
This is honestly the best coffee I've ever had, I cannot recommend this place enough. You can tell this shop loves their coffee and their passion shows.
The atmosphere is perfect by the river. (Unfortunately dried up at the moment, perfect nonetheless)

Definitely worth a visit and worth a drive to visit, as we do.

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: jaclyn susan
Review: The selection of coffees and treats are world class.. its a little peace of heaven.  You could be in a log cabin in Europe or north America..  love the vibe and of course the service.  The chocolate Lome is my favourire

Name: Houtbay Coffee
Address: Mainstream Shopping Centre, Cnr Main and, Princess St, Hout Bay, Cape Town
Author: Dave Anton
Review: Brilliant find in Houtbay!! Coffee roasted on site and was delicious. You must have a sweet treat as well as the cakes are Exceptional. The orange almond flour cake was so full of favour and probably the best example we ever had.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 163 Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Njabulo Timothy Phiwinhlanhla Dube
Review: Very good place to grab a snack and also fill up on Gas. The staff is so helpful. The place is clean and it's well organized. I bought a cup of Americano coffee and had a small muffin. The muffins tasted freshly baked and very delicious. There is not enough parking space, just 5 parking bays. So for parking, it's first come first serve. But there is a nice bench outside for incase you'd love to sit outside and enjoy the meal.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 163 Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Eleanora Bresler
Review: Most delicious coffee. Love the 24 hr Seattle in the garage along Buitenkant St. Coffee consistently good across all stores. Carrot cake is superb. Love the seattle freeze.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 163 Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Stephen Olivier
Review: Excellent coffee and you get exactly what you order. Would just go to Food lovers Market to have their coffee

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 163 Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Mogamat Sedick
Review: There coffees and teas are to die for even the smoothies are revitalizing. The smell of the coffee that's freshly made draws you in, as you walk by the shop, wanting you to come in and sit down and have a cup of coffee. The service is excellent to detail of the customers pallet, and the biscuits, crunch bars, brownies you name it are so fresh you swear they make  new batches everyday

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 163 Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Karel Khan
Review: My favourite coffee place but sometimes one of the staff not so friendly.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Cavendish Square
Address: L44C, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Werner
Review: What a disappointment that the Carob Streamer is no longer available. The staff wasn't even apologetic about it being listed as an available option but yet withdrawn. Must admit Constantia Seattle staff are much friendlier. Food for thought. Customers queue at their doors, unlike Cavendish that is inside a mall.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Cavendish Square
Address: L44C, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Kes Addinall
Review: The worst Seattle experience I have ever had. I get coffee every morning and the other branches I have gone to have always been incredible. Today, I decided to go to this branch and not only was the staff not friendly, the were unhelpful. I asked if they had any sleeves left for the coffee cup as there weren't any, they did have a look but instead of telling me the didn't have any, they just carried on like I wasn't even there. I stood there waiting for a while before eventually just walking out. My coffee is also not great. So overall, incredibly unhappy with this experience.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Cavendish Square
Address: L44C, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Lauren Smith
Review: Still my favourite coffee spot in Claremont after many, many moons! Excellent service and the staff are exceptional. Great vibe with a fun tribe! Grab a wide range of quick snack options from cakes and biscuits to muffins, croissants and muesli cups. Best coffee everrrr.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Cavendish Square
Address: L44C, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Raoul A
Review: Great place for a quick bite to eat or a great coffee. Quick service and friendly staff. It is at the bottom floor in Cavendish Square.  At the foot of the escalators in the centre of the mall.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Cavendish Square
Address: L44C, 1 Dreyer St, Claremont, Cape Town
Author: Julia Tshibanda Sharp
Review: This is the best place we go for coffee. When it's 7am in the morning to do letter writing or to gain some energy when we do cart witnessing from 7-9:30 or house to house from 10-12pm. Seattle gives us the best coffee that fills our needs. It's like our 2nd home!

Name: Levé Coffee and Deli
Address: Brackenfell Blvd, Vredekloof, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review:
Went there for breakfast on the morning of 7 July 2024. It was raining and we didn't have a reservation. Parking wasn't a problem as there is parking outside of the entrance.

Friendly staff, reasonable priced menu and delicious food. The wait wasn't long either. They have inside and outside seating with a small shop too.

We couldn't even finish our food that's how good it was.

It's a really cute coffee shop and a must try if you're in the area. We enjoyed our stay.

Name: Levé Coffee and Deli
Address: Brackenfell Blvd, Vredekloof, Cape Town
Author: Rosie Whittaker
Review: We Wanted to find somewhere new and close for breakfast as tired of all the usual places so my daughter had seen Leve when she drove past and told us to try it.   What a gem.... the manager served us and allowed us to relax and enjoy the surroundings. No rush to be in and out. Food fabulous (big English breakfast)well priced. Can't wait to taste the healthy options which they advised they also serve.  Will most definitely be a regular.

Name: Levé Coffee and Deli
Address: Brackenfell Blvd, Vredekloof, Cape Town
Author: Justin Rezant
Review: Coffee is top class, excellent flavour. You will wait a while for your food as its a family business but it will be worth it. Located next to and in a nursery. No better view while enjoying your coffee

Name: Levé Coffee and Deli
Address: Brackenfell Blvd, Vredekloof, Cape Town
Author: Tatiana Thaele
Review: Such a beautiful, tranquil little coffee shop and nursery. A real hidden gem in the neighbourhood. Lovely service and food that reminds you of home. This will be a regular spot for us!

Name: Levé Coffee and Deli
Address: Brackenfell Blvd, Vredekloof, Cape Town
Author: Havashini Pillay
Review: Such a cute little space! Food and coffee were great! The staff were so nice. Great experience.

Name: Heaven Coffee Shop
Address: 38 Burg St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Rene Carelse
Review: Awesome coffee spot, really delicious coffee! The batista is super friendly and welcomes everyone with a huge smile. They have a small selection of pastries too, I had the nut brownie which was moist and tasted really good. The outdoor seating is comfortable and you get to view the gorgeous Greenmarket Square Market while you enjoy your fresh brew. 😀

Name: Heaven Coffee Shop
Address: 38 Burg St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Jelanie Robertson
Review:
I attended a funeral at the church and saw that they had a coffee shop. So when my daughter came to visit I had to take her to this special church and the coffee shop.
The cappuccino and hot chocolate were of the greatest that we’ve ever had!
Thanks to Mondli and Khoza that made our experience one thst will remain in our hearts for a long time to come…
We will surely visit again!

Name: Heaven Coffee Shop
Address: 38 Burg St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: aobing Zeng
Review:
Nestled within a beautiful church in Cape Town, Heaven Coffee Shop offers a truly unique and soul-stirring experience for coffee lovers and those seeking a moment of tranquility. This extraordinary establishment seamlessly blends the sacred atmosphere of the church with the inviting aroma of freshly brewed coffee, creating a haven for both the body and the spirit.
天堂咖啡，很值得！🔥🔥🥰🥰

Name: Heaven Coffee Shop
Address: 38 Burg St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Jill Paslier
Review: Very nice little coffee shop connected to an active church. I thought it was a really neat idea. The barista was very friendly and helpful. unfortunately they do not sell beans at this location, but he recommended a few other places around town where I can get some quality coffee beans. I also enjoyed a cappuccino from heaven, and it was great!

Name: Heaven Coffee Shop
Address: 38 Burg St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: M
Review: my only concern is that the (plant based) milks are not kept in a refrigerator. that makes it go bad quickly in warm weather. but otherwise a nice little place, good music and fine for a coffee to go. :)

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 1 Link Rd, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Tarrin Loxton
Review:
There is nothing quite like getting my early morning coffee fix at Seattle Bothasig on my way to work.

Jimmy and the team always brighten your bluest morning.

They know my order, they are always friendly to each and every customer. They bring the best vibe and make the best coffee ☕️

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 1 Link Rd, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Ingrid Jeannot
Review:
I am addicted to the sugar-free seattle freeze.
BUT apart from the coffee drink, the location is great, and most importantly, Lutho and Jimmy are the most fun guys. They make you feel welcome, always cherry and helpful.

When you stop by for a coffee, send my regards.....:)

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 1 Link Rd, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Natalie Hubbard
Review: Friendly baristas, who serve good coffee. A relaxed place to drink a coffee in the morning, or do a take away.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 1 Link Rd, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: English Rose
Review: Went there on the morning of 29 July 2023 for a hot chocolate and a cappuccino. It was raining but we could stand under the roof. Friendly staff, fast service. Looks neat, clean and comfy.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 1 Link Rd, Bothasig, Cape Town
Author: Liezel Van Zyl
Review: Not food the coffee is great plus they have loyalty cards.

Name: Kin Coffee Purveyors
Address: 19 Pasita St, Rosenpark, Cape Town
Author: Marlin
Review: Tried this new coffee shop and can recommend not only for the great coffee and fresh biscuit bit also friendly staff. Got welcomed by Etienne, the espresso cookies we had was delicious. Their take-away coffee is some of the best i've had. Go out and give them a try and spread the word.

Name: Kin Coffee Purveyors
Address: 19 Pasita St, Rosenpark, Cape Town
Author: John-Marc van Wyk
Review: My father and I both had Great coffee! And my dad had a freshly baked croissant which he said was absolutely delicious! Great atmosphere as well definitely going back!

Name: Kin Coffee Purveyors
Address: 19 Pasita St, Rosenpark, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Delmage
Review: Amazing coffee, friendly staff and great atmosphere!

Name: Kin Coffee Purveyors
Address: 19 Pasita St, Rosenpark, Cape Town
Author: Hayley M Holesgrove
Review: Amazing coffee. Served perfectly.   Why settle for any other coffee when you can have Kin Coffee?

Name: Kin Coffee Purveyors
Address: 19 Pasita St, Rosenpark, Cape Town
Author: Bertho Marais6
Review:
The best combination!

Great coffee, awesome sandwich and a mind shattering pecan nut slice which you have to try.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 261 Durban Rd, Bo Oakdale, Cape Town
Author: Rita van Eck
Review:
Super friendly baristas that always brew excellent coffee.

This is the original Seattle that converted me into a loyal follower nearly a decade ago. And they've still got that special something after all these years.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 261 Durban Rd, Bo Oakdale, Cape Town
Author: Joshua Venter
Review: I've been drinking Seattle for years now and have never been disappointed until now. First of all the barista was nowhere to be found for like 10min..Then she came and took my order and as I got to the counter to pay my coffee was done...like how. Anyway I took it and left and when I tasted it I found that she never even poured the shot of espresso in my drink(Cafe Mocha) plus it was lukewarm. She steamed the milk too much and just mixed the powder and not even well cause it came out in clumps when I drank from it in the car. I've been drinking a Mocha from Seattle for ages now and know the taste and temperature and just the quality man!!!...I never write bad reviews but this time I felt cheated and that's why I'm writing. Very disappointed but won't return to this branch. Word of advice watch the barista make your coffee, especially when there body language is just of someone who doesn't want to be there.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 261 Durban Rd, Bo Oakdale, Cape Town
Author: Engela Naudé
Review: The coffee was standard (a bit hotter than usual) but the experience with the Sunday staff was not as good as it normally is with Seattle Coffee staff. Communication was not clear and the atmosphere was quite unfriendly.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 261 Durban Rd, Bo Oakdale, Cape Town
Author: Marina van Greuning
Review: Seattle Coffee is simply the best coffee, and all their baristas are friendly and knows the art of coffee making!

Name: Seattle Coffee Company
Address: 261 Durban Rd, Bo Oakdale, Cape Town
Author: Christopher Absolon
Review: Good all-around service. The food is nice. Nice convenient spot and quick in and out.

Name: Coffee & Wood Bellville
Address: 23 Northumberland Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Darren
Review: What a gem of a coffee shop. Enjoy some yester year nostalgia. Apart from a good breakfast and superb coffees, there are retro furniture and bags for sale. Desks, trunks and tables at good prices.

Name: Coffee & Wood Bellville
Address: 23 Northumberland Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Blue Mountain Shuttles
Review: Amazing little place we stumbled upon while waiting for our car. Cosy atmosphere, friendly staff and fabulous food. The coffee was fantastic

Name: Coffee & Wood Bellville
Address: 23 Northumberland Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Theresa Slawers
Review: A little gem off the busy hussel track. Such a cute elegant shop.  Pastries were divine and the coffee of the best I have had in the North.  Staff was friendly and the place was so peaceful.  A must visit.

Name: Coffee & Wood Bellville
Address: 23 Northumberland Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Senti
Review: Always great coffee, personally love their iced coffee

Name: Coffee & Wood Bellville
Address: 23 Northumberland Rd, Bellville, Cape Town
Author: Candice Newton
Review: Hidden away down the alley, this little coffee shop is great. Filled with classic furniture pieces, historic images and art for sale. Best part is the Bean There coffee, free wifi and brownies. Had fun trying our luck at the Chinese puzzle box.

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Kevin Beh
Review: Stumbled upon this gem of a cafe whilst out and about in the early morning. Beautiful coffees and an amazing breakfast of eggs and avo on toast. Service is top notch with friendly and welcoming baristas and staff. Great job guys. Special thanks to Wisdom for his"wise" suggestions on the menu items.

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Sophie van Rijen
Review: THE BEST coffee in town! Love their smooth oat cappuccino and the staff is super friendly! In our 2 weeks holiday we visited 5 times for coffee! 5+ stars!! 🌟

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Ira de Luxo
Review:
The restaurant is very good I loved having breakfast
Top 10

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Emma Ellis-Brown
Review: BEST COFFEE SHOP IN CAPE TOWN🔥🔥🔥been coming here for 7 years🥹 amaaaaazing coffee! Delicious food!! Wonderful atmosphere and people ! It’s the place to be☕️

Name: Ground Art Caffe
Address: 160 Strand St, De Waterkant, Cape Town
Author: Stephanie Blomkamp
Review: Beautiful souls work here and their energy makes it a wonderful experience. Whether a quick coffee or a delicious meal it’s a versatile happy place for all & everything. Ground Art Caffe even has a backgammon board, and for those in the know the boys who run the show are the ones to beat. The place, the people, the food are all pure gold. I’ve been going for years and it feels like family.

Name: Crankshaft Cafe Classic Car Coffee Bar
Address: 120a Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Erika S.
Review: Love the classic cars and the feel of the place. Has classic music playing in the background while enjoying the view of the classic cars. The portion sizes for the coffee are great. Loved the Chococino. Also, it's right by the colorful house street. Really convenient after checking that street out

Name: Crankshaft Cafe Classic Car Coffee Bar
Address: 120a Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Ira de Luxo
Review: It's not just having a coffee, it's also enjoying a beautiful place full of relics, my desire to get out of there with a Rolls Royce or a Jaguar beautiful cars of categories of the years 40,50,60,70,80,90, 2000, everything beautiful the desire to work and have much more money to come and get a car is too much... a classic coffee the best in Cape Town

Name: Crankshaft Cafe Classic Car Coffee Bar
Address: 120a Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Inge Venter
Review: We loved this vibe. Tons of classic cars mixed with good food. The freshly squeezed OJ was a definite must. We loved watching all the classics. My boyfriend is wayy more into the car thing than I am but I was there for the food and we left bellies full and very satisfied.

Name: Crankshaft Cafe Classic Car Coffee Bar
Address: 120a Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: John Glancy
Review: What a great find!  Easy to miss if you don’t know it’s there but definitely worth a trip. Great coffee & pastries and wonderful service in a beautiful classic/exotic car showroom.

Name: Crankshaft Cafe Classic Car Coffee Bar
Address: 120a Buitengracht St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Ward Misdorp
Review: Great breakfast and a petrol heads delight with all the classy vintage cars !

Name: Fire Truck Coffee 🔥🚒
Address: Newlands Forest Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Marius Joubert
Review: Great staff, well trained baristas, milk tempered perfectly for top-notch flat whites, perfect setting in Newlands Forest, great system to buy a token for the firemen, someone having a bad day and some others. See you again soon.

Name: Fire Truck Coffee 🔥🚒
Address: Newlands Forest Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Chantal Du Chenne
Review:
Thank you FireTruck coffee Newlands for gifting CT with vibrant positive community building attitude 🤩
Lorraine and her Team know how to deliver five star customer service! The Coffee is superb, the snack options are bountiful & delish, and the ‘paid it forward’ tokens for our Cape Town Fire fighters reflect the overall attitude of this incredible new business.

Name: Fire Truck Coffee 🔥🚒
Address: Newlands Forest Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Lesley Ann Kohne
Review: Had the best coffee at this little gem yesterday. We were a large group of cycling ladies and the Barista's managed our order swiftly and with a smile. DIVINE, home baked goodies too. 🌼

Name: Fire Truck Coffee 🔥🚒
Address: Newlands Forest Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: kayla keet
Review: Only been there once and I am already in love with this place. The service is warm and friendly, guaranteed to put a smile on your face :) the beverages are DELICIOUS and they have a great variety of options to choose from. The only negative thing I can say is that it all sounds so good that it’s too hard to choose haha. Highly recommend 🙌

Name: Fire Truck Coffee 🔥🚒
Address: Newlands Forest Rd, Newlands, Cape Town
Author: Dr. Rainy Dube
Review: Perfect location, amazing coffee(hazelnut honey latte) and such amazing service!

Name: Dose coffee bar
Address: 354 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Magda Rautenbach
Review: The coffee and the avo toast was very good and the baked goods looked delicious as well. The art in the shop is mainly beaded animals and it is really very well made. The service was good and I'll definitely go back again to taste their other goodies. Well woth a visit.

Name: Dose coffee bar
Address: 354 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Nadia Samsodien
Review:
This experience was super delightful ❤🥐☕

Thank you Lisa and colleagues for the amazing coffee, courtesy and professionalism, which is highly recommended🏆

Let's support local business and help each other and the economy 🇿🇦 to become strong again 🤝

Name: Dose coffee bar
Address: 354 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: v m
Review: I love this shop. I love everyone who works here. They are great people. They make you feel comfortable and welcomed. The vibes are nice. I LOVE their carrot cake and their coffee! They have the BEST food EVER!

Name: Dose coffee bar
Address: 354 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: Kearabetswe Moopelo
Review: Theeeee Best sandwiches on the best bread, great cheese and delicious ingredients. Wow love at first bite. This is my favorite joint in CPT. Soooo much love for Dose Coffee, clean neat serving and a beautiful space

Name: Dose coffee bar
Address: 354 Albert Rd, Woodstock, Cape Town
Author: lisa mutenga
Review: Coffee here is great. I will definitely come back. Baked goods and friendly service was a plus. They had Wifi I managed to do some work.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Camps Bay
Address: 1 Rontree Ave, Bakoven, Cape Town
Author: Darwin Sakala
Review: Noli the Barister is just something else..very good service.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Camps Bay
Address: 1 Rontree Ave, Bakoven, Cape Town
Author: Jardin Roestorff
Review: Quick stop coffee shop inside Freshstop convenience store at Astron petrol station.

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Camps Bay
Address: 1 Rontree Ave, Bakoven, Cape Town
Author: Bonginkosi nqayi
Review: I've seen they are good

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Camps Bay
Address: 1 Rontree Ave, Bakoven, Cape Town
Author: Brendan Gradwell

Name: Seattle Coffee Company Camps Bay
Address: 1 Rontree Ave, Bakoven, Cape Town
Author: Jade Manasse

Name: Coffee @ The Happi Hub
Address: 3 Estmil Rd, Elfindale, Cape Town
Author: Kate Gibbons
Review:
Best coffee in town! The Barista, Brian is so kind and friendly.

What a great space to get out of the house and make use of their hot desks all the while getting a wonderful flat white. What an inspiring space with loads of beautiful things to look at. I will definitely be visiting this space on the reg.

Name: Coffee @ The Happi Hub
Address: 3 Estmil Rd, Elfindale, Cape Town
Author: Jean-Pierre Da Fonseca
Review: Great spot and delicious coffee, I often stop to get my daily perk on the way to work. Brian, the barista is awesome. Even better for the ladies, is that there is a nail bar there as well, so you can get coffee and do your nails in one stop #yourewelcome

Name: Coffee @ The Happi Hub
Address: 3 Estmil Rd, Elfindale, Cape Town
Author: Jaime Montgomery
