Review: I was never a coffee person but the coffee at The Happy Hub has converted me ! Delicious coffee made by the legend Barista Brian! 10/10 recommend !

Name: Coffee @ The Happi Hub
Address: 3 Estmil Rd, Elfindale, Cape Town
Author: Kim Kotze
Review: Vibe is fun and full of life, definitely the best coffee in town and an experience on its own. Love the pink 🌸😎

Name: Coffee @ The Happi Hub
Address: 3 Estmil Rd, Elfindale, Cape Town
Author: Anja Dippenaar
Review: Love this creative space! The coffee is amazing and Quirk Beauty is surprising and fun.

Name: Square Root Coffee Diep River (Halaal)
Address: 242 Main Rd, Diep River, Cape Town
Author: Grant Durr
Review: Was walking around in the area while waiting for new tires to be fitted and stumbled across this little coffee shop on main road. I ordered a latte which was fantastic and continued my walk. The coffee was so good that on the way back I stopped back again to get a flat white to enjoy on the way home! I can highly recommend Square Root Coffee to anyone in the area who would like to enjoy a great coffee and perhaps something light to eat.

Name: Square Root Coffee Diep River (Halaal)
Address: 242 Main Rd, Diep River, Cape Town
Author: Angela Kavallieratos
Review: Great and very well priced coffee. Had a delicious little milt tart too

Name: Square Root Coffee Diep River (Halaal)
Address: 242 Main Rd, Diep River, Cape Town
Author: Samantha Glaister
Review: Love my morning Lattes! Best coffee Latte in Diep River. They make a mean avo toastie! Wonderful personalised service. The staff are and service is fantastic as well.

Name: Square Root Coffee Diep River (Halaal)
Address: 242 Main Rd, Diep River, Cape Town
Author: Sally Mitchell
Review: I was out and about in the Bergvliet area today on a delivery.  On the way out, I spotted Square Root.  I was feeling hungry, queasy and tired, so decided to stop.  I avoid gluten and gingerly asked the gentleman if he had anything I could eat....I was pleasantly surprised!  Yesssss, there were these giant gluten free cookies (3 different kinds), oh man, the joy!  There were also little packs of nuts, yay!  And the cuppuccino was divine.  Best of all, the gentleman served me with a smile and made my day.  Thank you, will make a point of coming back again when I'm in the area

Name: Square Root Coffee Diep River (Halaal)
Address: 242 Main Rd, Diep River, Cape Town
Author: Michelle Meyer
Review: Discovered this one by accident and what a gem! A cute spot with friendly service, good coffee and enjoyed the croissant!

Name: Jakes Bakes Coffee Shop
Address: 49 Main Rd, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Barbara Newport
Review: The 2 ladies working today were so friendly and helpful (I was with a client who is mobility impaired). We had a really good breakfast and delicious Danish apple pastry. The éclairs and croissants looked great, so I'll be back!! A little gem this place is. Very cosy and homely.

Name: Jakes Bakes Coffee Shop
Address: 49 Main Rd, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Leeanne Marquard
Review: This is the best little coffee shop .. I highly recommend it.  The food is made by the owner her self.  And you can see that she takes pride in quality of what she is serving you . 10 out of 10

Name: Jakes Bakes Coffee Shop
Address: 49 Main Rd, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Edwin Adams
Review:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Had such an incredible experience at Jakes Bakes.. The food surpassed our expectations, and the atmosphere was simply amazing.. Its Cosy and welcoming.. We had a fantastic time and really can't wait to come back. Highly recommend checking it out! 🌟Bring you family and friends you will not be disappointed.

Name: Jakes Bakes Coffee Shop
Address: 49 Main Rd, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Dominique Theron
Review: This was definitely one of the nicest little coffee shops we have been to. The service was incredible and the food was made to perfection. A HUGE thank you to our waitress who made us feel like we were royalty. We cannot wait to come back!

Name: Jakes Bakes Coffee Shop
Address: 49 Main Rd, Bergvliet, Cape Town
Author: Carmen Gamieldien
Review: Had an amazing breakfast celebration at Jakes Bakes Coffee Shop in Bergvliet with friends to surprise a friend turning 40. The staff made it extra special, adding personal touches and creating a warm atmosphere. The food was delicious, and the vibe was incredible. Highly recommended! #BirthdayBreakfast #Recommended #GreatVibes #DeliciousFood#Stem

Name: TREAT Coffee Roasters
Address: Shop 5, Spiro's Centre Corner Wellington and Main Roads, Cape Town
Author: Delton Prins
Review: Cute little place for a cuppa and a sandwich. They guys here are so nice and seems like you just want to stay here and know them all as if you a regular customer. Everyone treated so good.

Name: TREAT Coffee Roasters
Address: Shop 5, Spiro's Centre Corner Wellington and Main Roads, Cape Town
Author: Abené Verreynne
Review: This is such a lovely café. The two gentlemen behind the counter are so friendly. The toasted sandwiches are amazing and the iced coffee is the best I've had

Name: TREAT Coffee Roasters
Address: Shop 5, Spiro's Centre Corner Wellington and Main Roads, Cape Town
Author: Nicola Stevenson
Review: Delicious coffee, the friendliest staff and a great vibe. Couldn’t ask for more!

Name: TREAT Coffee Roasters
Address: Shop 5, Spiro's Centre Corner Wellington and Main Roads, Cape Town
Author: Deborah Mulholland (DEBZ MULHOLLAND)
Review:
FANTASTIC breakfast rolls and the BEST coffee
Friendly staff
Definitely be back there!

Name: TREAT Coffee Roasters
Address: Shop 5, Spiro's Centre Corner Wellington and Main Roads, Cape Town
Author: Julia Truter
Review: the flat white i had was beyond amazing. definitely 5 stars. friendly people.

Name: Aya’s
Address: 17A Riebeek St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Zakariya Baker
Review: Been there twice, and the experience has always been wonderful. I am very much into my coffees, and the quality of the coffee here is exceptional. This morning's cappuccino must have been one of the most chocolatey coffees I have ever had. Beyond the coffee, the owner is extremely friendly and welcoming - his personality is part of the experience! Skip the franchise coffee, come get a proper and expertly brewed cup here!

Name: Aya’s
Address: 17A Riebeek St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Hajiera Carlie
Review:
Don't let the size fool .... good coffee... good price ... and an absolutely friendly dude serving it ...

My 1st time trying them ... Will definitely be back ! ...

Name: Aya’s
Address: 17A Riebeek St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Sumesh Chetty
Review:
Great coffee. Great service. Aya is wonderful.
No place to sit. This is strictly to go. My favourite coffee in Cape Town.

Name: Aya’s
Address: 17A Riebeek St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Tiago Bonifacio
Review: Awesome coffee, friendly interactions, and such a thrifty little store. Was totally worth the walk!

Name: Aya’s
Address: 17A Riebeek St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Nandipha Lloyd
Review: Thanks for the amazing coffee and great service Aya, will definitely come by for my second cup

Name: Seattle Coffee Co. Village Square
Address: Suite2, 34 Oxford St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Chad Ryan de Lange
Review: I would love to rate this Seattle 5 stars. The coffee is great but they never inform me when I’ve accrued my complimentary loyalty coffee. I asked and they said they can only check after my payment. This must be wrong. What’s the point of loyalty points if I have to fight to use them. I ordered two coffees this morning and  AFTER payment I have a free coffee to redeem and earned two points towards my next coffee. Please help me understand why this happens.

Name: Seattle Coffee Co. Village Square
Address: Suite2, 34 Oxford St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Josh Taylor
Review: I am a regular. The past couple times have been terrible experiences. For starters, the barista constantly rings up the order completely wrong. To be frank I don’t know how it’s possible for her to mess up a simple order. Secondly as a barista you should know that if a customer uses honey in a cold drink you need to melt it first. I get the same order each time and they usually do it. The past few times however they didn’t and we’ll look at this. I’m basically sucking on honey. Unbelievable for a Seattle coffee. Incredibly disappointing

Name: Seattle Coffee Co. Village Square
Address: Suite2, 34 Oxford St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Meghan Basson
Review: The staff was very friendly and helpful and they made sure the coffees were pretty enough that you actually feel guilty to drink it :p very impressed with this branch!

Name: Seattle Coffee Co. Village Square
Address: Suite2, 34 Oxford St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Christian Mills
Review: Always quick and easy service here, it's my partner and I's go to spot for coffee. Seattle freeze is always, and the butterscotch latte is amazing. Staff are also always friendly and helpful if you have questions.

Name: Seattle Coffee Co. Village Square
Address: Suite2, 34 Oxford St, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Kent Weyers
Review:
The staff here are always super friendly and energetic.

The coffee is good, one of my favourite places to grab a coffee and their hint cookies are amazing.

Name: Blessings Coffee and Creative
Address: 2 11th Ave, Melkbosstrand, Cape Town
Author: Lana Wright
Review: A quaint lovely old house turned into a restaurant, antique, art, coffee, tea, cake and artisan bread, book club, charity, creative lessons and get together spot for the locals.

Name: Blessings Coffee and Creative
Address: 2 11th Ave, Melkbosstrand, Cape Town
Author: Niki Köhne (NikiDesign Studio)
Review: Wonderful little coffee and gift shop in Melkbosstrand. Craft workshops and a love community get together location.

Name: Blessings Coffee and Creative
Address: 2 11th Ave, Melkbosstrand, Cape Town
Author: Chanélle Labuschagne
Review: What a lovely hidden gem. The fireplace sets for a cozy atmosphere. Very friendly hostess and waitress. The pancakes, which was so beautifully wrapped, was divine especially as it was a cold and rainy day. I thoroughly enjoyed it there, you will see me again.

Name: Blessings Coffee and Creative
Address: 2 11th Ave, Melkbosstrand, Cape Town
Author: Raymond Bezuidenhout
Review: A quaint and pleasant place. Food and service are good. I would recommend a visit. Not expensive

Name: Blessings Coffee and Creative
Address: 2 11th Ave, Melkbosstrand, Cape Town
Author: Liezel Schoen
Review: We visited for an art exhibition, we didn't stay to eat as out time was limited but the smells that came from the kitchen was amazing. Next time we will definitely stop for breakfast or lunch.

Name: Uber Coffee (now Smak Coffee Co.)
Address: Unit 2, Inospace Island Works Extension, 22 Cumberland Rd, Paarden Eiland, Cape Town
Author: Emma Blaikie
Review: What a find! Had been to buy some shoes in neighbouring unit and googled coffee near me - Uber came up and we gave it a try - fabulous tasting coffee - Katia was fantastic showed us around the roastery - if in the area do yourself a favour and pop in!

Name: Uber Coffee (now Smak Coffee Co.)
Address: Unit 2, Inospace Island Works Extension, 22 Cumberland Rd, Paarden Eiland, Cape Town
Author: Jaine Hannath
Review:
Delicious Ùber coffee - beans, pods and ground espresso - delivered to my door within 5 hours of placing my order. That is just awesome client service.
Looking forward to trying the Uber Coffee 70% chocolate that I also ordered off Uber site.

Thank you!

Name: Uber Coffee (now Smak Coffee Co.)
Address: Unit 2, Inospace Island Works Extension, 22 Cumberland Rd, Paarden Eiland, Cape Town
Author: Stephen Gohl
Review: total waste of Money! I bought a large bag of their 100 capsules, and either there is a design problem with their pods or a bad batch, but the bottom of the pods are solid plastic covered in foil, that the Nespresso machine can not penetrate!! i have to sit with a sharp pointy knife to make my own holes in the bottom of the pod to get the water to flow through, and even then its no guarantee you will get coffee out.. i have tested now with various other brands and it is ONLY the Uber pods that the machine will not pierce the bottom to get hot water to flow through.!

Name: Uber Coffee (now Smak Coffee Co.)
Address: Unit 2, Inospace Island Works Extension, 22 Cumberland Rd, Paarden Eiland, Cape Town
Author: Tamryn Hansen
Review: Great coffee! Really nice to taste a different brand to Nespresso! Excellent service and staff is professional. Highly recommended 👍🏻

Name: Uber Coffee (now Smak Coffee Co.)
Address: Unit 2, Inospace Island Works Extension, 22 Cumberland Rd, Paarden Eiland, Cape Town
Author: sophie Pagés
Review: Really good coffee, one of my friend make me discover this blend and it's perfect for bean to cup machine. They also have a really nice instant coffee for camping in style :)

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Edith Mirzaian
Review:
I’m so happy to have discovered this gem in Cape Town. I was here for 8 days and the hotel coffee wasn’t cutting it. Of course we explored the town in search of the coffee that was going to hit the spot. Shift did not disappoint. The coffee is the best we had in town, the green juice was also a favorite, and you enjoy these treats in the cool vibe atmosphere they’ve created. But the staff is the best. They are lovely people serving deliciousness.
I did not try the food, but the dishes going past me looked delectable.

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Buyisiwe Shongwe
Review:
Great place to have a coffee outside if the weather’s good. Service started off great but deteriorated over the course of our stay. May have just been a busy morning. The staff was friendly enough and fixed all the errors they were making.
The location gets the spot a nice rating otherwise it’s kinda average.

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Chantal H.
Review: During our stay in Cape Town we had breakfast at this place every day. The personal was amazing and very friendly! The food is good and de ambiance is very laid back and chill. Would defenitly recommand this place for everybody who likes good food and coffee to start your day of with a smile.

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Erick Mendoza
Review: Good spot to relax in friendly enviroment food for me was spot on i did scrabled eggs avo and toast and the avocado is actually real no a spread. Staff is friendly especially Pumi

Name: Shift Espresso bar | Waterway House
Address: 4 Dock Rd, Victoria & Alfred Waterfront, Cape Town
Author: Milo
Review:
What a gorgeous coffee shop! The aesthetic is perfect, the service is excellent and warm and the coffee is wonderful. Its such a perfect experience, I made this my compulsory stop every morning!
Their food is so good too!
They have a give a book, take a book deal and I am proud to have left a work I truly enjoyed there. It’s an all-round great feeling here at shift.
If I lived here, I would want to work here 📚☕️⛰️🌊🦭☀️🌴

Name: Manja's
Address: Loop St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Bianca Lucas
Review:
Stopped here for an early morning coffee. I was beyond impress with this little cafe. The quality of the coffee is outstanding with beautiful, sily foam on top. I ordered the coffee and muffin combo @R35 and it was the perfect start to my day.
Service is quick and super friendly. Thanks a lot- I will be back.

Name: Manja's
Address: Loop St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Anthony B
Review: Stopped by for a coffee, excellent service, staff were very friendly and the menu is really cheap for breakfast snacks and the alcoholic beverages are also well priced. I even got some free little cookies, will definitely come back again if I need a breakfast, coffee or an after work drink. :)

Name: Manja's
Address: Loop St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Aslam Saal
Review: Brilliant place, honored working with them, fabrication of the tables and back counter floating units

Name: Manja's
Address: Loop St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Sonja Wende
Review: Awesome place was there yesterday staff superfriendly and good food for the small pocket. You can even buy lekker bread, tinned food for the quick 2 plate stove at home and Italian spaghettis. Felt very at home.

Name: Manja's
Address: Loop St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Leendert Haasbroek
Review: Good coffee and small eats. Never closes. Smoking allowed.

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Karen Schellhase
Review: Very nice atmosphere; surrounded by beautiful green plants this place is a little oasis in the city. Nice selection of coffees and teas. There is a nice, charming shop where you can buy local products.

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Kerusha Naidoo
Review:
Needed a quick lunch before going into the planetarium.
Service was amazing and our food arrived in minutes. I would love to thank them for this as we were in a bit of a rush. It truly meant alot to us.

I definitely recommend the almond croissant and also the cheese and pesto toasted sandwich. Everything was so buttery, it literally melts in your mouth.

The cafe overlooks the gardens where you can watch the beautiful birds and cute little squirrels as you enjoy your meal.

Would definitely recommend this place ❤️

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Konsti | Brandl Nutrition
Review:
Wow! Stunning place in the peaceful company gardens. The Cafe uses local products. The Cheesecake is amazing, the best i ever had! There is a beautiful garden where they harvest their own vegetables.

All in all a beautiful place to enjoy your time with friends or a good book ☺️

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: David Webster
Review:
We enjoyed one of the tea tasting of the local teas. The owner of the shop gave the class and lots of local history, background on how it was started, and herbal knowledge facts. Background included how she ended up with the tea cups we all drank out of. She even brought of a VOK plate. Great presentation and knowledge.
The tea was served with biscotti. Dunking the biscotti in the tea and listening to the stories. Made for a great time by all.
Of course by the end, you have to talk next door to small store and buy some of the delicious tea and trinkets.

Name: Coffee At The Vine
Address: 17 Queen Victoria St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Mikha'il Hathey
Review:
Coffee at the Vine is also known as the Smallest Coffee Shop in Cape Town and this is definitely a coffee shop you should go check out.

I had the Carrot Cake and Iced Coffee and I would definitely recommend you try this out.

Service is excellent as you are at all times taken care of by the manager. The Restaurant is set in the small historical courtyard in Heritage Square.

If you go on the First Thursday of the month you will be delighted by the vibe as this is known as First Thursday’s so there tends to be bands playing live music in the courtyard at it just gives this place an awesome atmosphere.

Name: Beanos Coffee Shop
Address: 150 Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Ali Sayed
Review: Great coffee, when I went there wasn't a milk alternative option. But great.

Name: Beanos Coffee Shop
Address: 150 Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Tauriq Gamildien
Review: The first coffee shop I’ve experienced to charge R8 extra for honey when I’ve ordered a red cappuccino. Pretty disappointing

Name: Beanos Coffee Shop
Address: 150 Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Yusuf Omar
Review: What a pleasant surprise. A small restaurant that you could easily miss when you drive by. Cosy, unpretentious and genuine quality that bigger franchises can learn from. The food was excellent and great value for money. When did you ever have a toasted steak sandwich with whole tender pieces of steak!!!

Name: Beanos Coffee Shop
Address: 150 Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Shakira Wagiet
Review:
Food is decent. Service was terrible, we arrived here on a Saturday at about 12:10. It seemed like they didn’t even want to allow us in because apparently they close at 1pm. Firstly, this isn’t stated online. But regardless, we arrived just after 12 and they were already carrying on about closing time. Eventually they let us in, we sit down order our food etc. Once our food is ready, instead of bringing our food to our table, they packed it in takeaway bags, placed it on the front counter and told us it was ready. Keep in mind this was at 12:30 or so , not even their closing time yet but they’re basically trying to get us out. Once we explained that we would like to sit down and eat our food, they bring out mine and my daughters food on one plate. Didn’t even bother to give us our own plate. We had to awkwardly share a plate. I guess it would
take up too much time to just give us separate plates. Maybe the extra dishes didn’t allow them to close up at exactly 1pm. We were done and paid before 1pm but the entire experience felt rushed and they cared more about closing than about customer service. Super disappointed.

Name: Beanos Coffee Shop
Address: 150 Wetton Rd, Wetton, Cape Town
Author: Sharon Isaacs
Review:
This hangout is 5 minutes walk from my home.
The service is excellent.
The food is good.
It is one of my favourite places to enjoy a light meal.

I wish I had more time to go there.

Name: Ninety 9 Coffee company
Address: 75 Johnston Rd, Rylands, Cape Town
Author: Zaahied Karriem
Review: I always stop here before or after a long day, always a good atmosphere with friendly staff, my favourite thing to get are the hot chocolate, sanwhiches, banana loaf slices and of late my girls say its their new favourite spot for milkshakes , better then other places

Name: Ninety 9 Coffee company
Address: 75 Johnston Rd, Rylands, Cape Town
Author: Raeeza Li Li Jansen
Review: The service was great and very friendly staff and owner. The waffles was so soft and good including the slushi was delicious

Name: Ninety 9 Coffee company
Address: 75 Johnston Rd, Rylands, Cape Town
Author: zee
Review: This is my new favourite on the go coffee place! The Turkish delight hot chocolate has to be my favourite and those freshly made chocolate croissants in the morning, mouth watering! 🤤

Name: Ninety 9 Coffee company
Address: 75 Johnston Rd, Rylands, Cape Town
Author: Mohammed Allie Foflonker
Review: Great atmosphere. Amazing coffee and shakes. Quite a variety of items available. Pricing are reasonable. Atmosphere is nice and safe.

Name: Ninety 9 Coffee company
Address: 75 Johnston Rd, Rylands, Cape Town
Author: Fehrzaana Karbelkar
Review: Really friendly service. R30 for a smallish sized capuccino. Not really the best capuccino  I've had. Cake pop was yummy. Convenient location.

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: clinton van loggerenberg
Review: Lovely spot. Great beer and awesome trails. Would have given this 5 stars. BUT, please, sort out your ordering system. No one wants to queue for 25 minutes to order a beer refill. How about a bar only queue? Or let the waitrons take orders and payment. You will sell twice as much and your customers will be happier too.

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Tara-Lee Bezuidenhout
Review:
Beautiful play area and setting for kids! It's enclosed and has everything you need to have a great time with busy little ones. Well maintained grass, swings, jungle gym, slide, sand pit with toys provided, trees for shade and picnic benches inside the actual play area which is nice.

It was windy in Cape Town today but this area was quite well protected and pleasant.

Deluxe coffee and carrot cake from The Dairy Shed is 👌. It would be great if they could add more vegetarian options to their menu.

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Marc De Decker
Review:
Authentic farm experience, with uniquely rustic and not yet commercialized approached that is true to it’s centuries old heritage. Awesome trails for any skill of mountainbiker but no trail running folks! Food is great and the coffee is usually fantastic.

EDIT: NO DOGS ALLOWED!

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Audrey Baird
Review: The weather was lovely today, ideal to stop by and enjoy some fresh air, at this farm setting. We enjoyed their cappuccino and fresh, delicious, moist carrot cake!

Name: Coffee@theDairyShed
Address: Contermanskloof farm, Contermanskloof Road, Durbanville, Cape Town
Author: Chantel Pienaar
Review: Lovely, just lovely. Rustic. Love the massive enclosed play area. Awesome coffee. Beautiful views. Tranquil.

Name: KOFI Portside
Address: Shop 7, Portside Tower, 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: James Mikkelson
Review:
Get my daily brew on the way to work here.. one of the best coffees you can get at the portside end of bree street.

Friendly staff, clean fashionable place, some outside seating too.

Maybe a little background music or something would be cool.

Name: KOFI Portside
Address: Shop 7, Portside Tower, 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Trin Anomaly
Review: I had a mocha, and my friend had a red cappuccino. I quite enjoyed it, and so did he. Would go back to try their cortado.

Name: KOFI Portside
Address: Shop 7, Portside Tower, 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Sikhu N
Review: Great coffee, simple yet aesthetically pleasing setup with good lighting for video calls. A great location for ‘office for the day’. You can check my reel of this location on Tiktok user: sikhucooks

Name: KOFI Portside
Address: Shop 7, Portside Tower, 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Valhalla Reeves
Review: I got here at 8am on the 16th Mar 2024! It said it opens at 7am.....The staff told me they only open at 8:30am! I would guess lack of management allows staff to dictate times, waist precious time, and open when it suits them!

Name: KOFI Portside
Address: Shop 7, Portside Tower, 4 Bree St, Cape Town City Centre, Cape Town
Author: Jane Rushton
Review: Delicious coffee and such lovely staff!
